definitions.en: written abbreviation for in my opinion: used when you tell someone your opinion, for example in an email
definitions.zh: 以我之见，依我看（用于电子邮件等中，in my opinion的缩写）

definitions.en: to fill something or someone with a particular feeling, quality, or idea

examples.en: His poetry is imbued with deep, religious feeling.
examples.zh: 他的诗歌充满了深厚的宗教情感。

definitions.en: to make a word or phrase shorter by using only the first letters of each word

examples.en: abbreviate something as "Chief Executive Officer" is abbreviated as "CEO".
examples.zh: Chief Executive Officer（首席执行官）缩写为CEO。

examples.en: abbreviate something to "Daniel" is often abbreviated to "Dan".
examples.zh: Daniel常简称为Dan。

examples.en: There are many different ways to abbreviate the name "Elizabeth".
It's normal to abbreviate a lot of words when writing text messages.
"Centimetres", is generally abbreviated to "cm" without any full stop after it.

definitions.en: a short form of a word or phrase

examples.en: "ITV" is the abbreviation for "Independent Television".
examples.zh: ITV是Independent Television（独立电视台）的缩写。

examples.en: There is a list of abbreviations near the beginning of the book.
What does the abbreviation WHO stand for?
Please use the abbreviation "cm" for "centimetres", without any full stop after it.
Sorry - could you explain what some of these abbreviations mean in your letter?
We generally use the abbreviation "MP" instead of writing out "Member of Parliament" in full.

definitions.en: If you can't abide someone or something, you dislike them very much:

examples.en: I can't abide her.
examples.zh: 我真受不了她。

definitions.en: to live or stay somewhere

examples.en: He abided in the wilderness for forty days.
examples.zh: 他在荒野中度过了40天。

definitions.en: different from what is usual or average, especially in a way that is bad
definitions.zh: 反常的；异常的；变态的（尤指不好的）

examples.en: abnormal behaviour/weather/conditions
examples.zh: 反常的行为／天气／情况

examples.en: Tests revealed some abnormal skin cells.
examples.zh: 检验结果表明一些皮肤细胞异常。

examples.en: They said that the delay to our flight was due to abnormal bad weather conditions.
It isn't abnormal to have spots at your age.
Abnormal levels of radiation have been recorded in the area.
It's abnormal to have temperatures in the 30s here at this time of year.
Because he has to work abnormal hours, Jack doesn't often eat with the rest of the family.

definitions.en: made shorter by having some details or less important information removed

examples.en: the abridged edition/version of her novel
examples.zh: 她的小说的删节版

examples.en: The abridged article was first published in 2010.
examples.zh: 这篇文章的节选首次发表于2010年。

examples.en: This is an abridged and adapted version of an unpublished draft.
The book is an abridged account of the official US Government report.
There is no room for etymologies in an abridged dictionary.

definitions.en: in a sudden, unexpected, and sometimes unpleasant way

examples.en: He stood up abruptly and went outside.
examples.zh: 他猛地站起身，向外面走去。

examples.en: The talks ended abruptly when one of the delegations walked out in protest.
examples.zh: 当一个代表团离场抗议时，会谈突然结束了。

examples.en: The call ended abruptly.
She came back five minutes late from lunch and was abruptly dismissed.
The age of the dinosaurs ended abruptly about 65 million years ago.

definitions.en: in a way that seems rude and unfriendly, especially by using very few words when talking

examples.en: "I don't care what you think," Audrey said abruptly.
examples.zh: “我不在乎你怎么想，”奥黛丽粗鲁地说。

examples.en: I know I sometimes speak and act abruptly.
examples.zh: 我知道我有时说话和动作都很唐突。

examples.en: He spoke more abruptly than he intended.
"Why is this house so cold?" he asked abruptly.
It annoyed me that he spoke so abruptly.

definitions.en: to take something in, especially gradually

examples.en: Plants absorb carbon dioxide.
examples.zh: 植物吸收二氧化碳。

examples.en: In cold climates, houses need to have walls that will absorb heat.
examples.zh: 在气候寒冷的地方，房屋的墙壁必须能吸收热量。

examples.en: Towels absorb moisture.
examples.zh: 毛巾吸收水汽。

examples.en: The drug is quickly absorbed into the bloodstream.
examples.zh: 药物迅速被血液吸收流遍全身。

examples.en: Our countryside is increasingly being absorbed by/into the large cities.
examples.zh: 我们越来越多的农村地区正被大城市蚕食掉。

examples.en: The barrier absorbed the main impact of the crash.
examples.zh: 碰撞产生的大部分冲击力被防撞墙吸收了。

examples.en: I'm so tired, my poor befuddled brain can't absorb any more.
For running on hard roads, you need shoes with extra cushioning to absorb the shock.
From an early age, children absorb everything going on around them.
He was so absorbed in his work, he hadn't realized everyone else had gone home.
Vitamin D is absorbed by the body after exposure to sunshine.

definitions.en: to understand facts or ideas completely and remember them

examples.en: It's hard to absorb so much information.
examples.zh: 掌握如此大量信息非常困难。

definitions.en: to take up someone's attention completely
definitions.zh: （使）完全吸引住；（使）全神贯注于…

examples.en: The project has absorbed her for several years.
examples.zh: 好几年她都全神贯注于这项工程中。

definitions.en: if a business absorbs the cost of something, it pays that cost easily

examples.en: The school has absorbed most of the expenses so far, but it may have to offer fewer places next year to reduce costs.
examples.zh: 这间学校目前能够承担大部分的费用，可是明年可能不得不通过减少招生降低开支。

definitions.en: if one company absorbs another company, it takes control of it and they become one company

examples.en: Telecorp Holdings absorbed its Spanish subsidiary into its British headquarters.
examples.zh: 泰利控股将其西班牙子公司并入英国总部。

definitions.en: in a stupid or unreasonable way, or to an unreasonable degree

examples.en: You're behaving absurdly.
examples.zh: 你的行为很荒谬。

examples.en: It was absurdly expensive.
examples.zh: 它贵得离谱。

examples.en: The chances of winning the lottery are absurdly low.
Some of his decisions seem absurdly removed from common sense.
Despite her absurdly busy schedule, she made time to visit her fans.

definitions.en: to use something for the wrong purpose in a way that is harmful or morally wrong
definitions.zh: （尤指为个人私利而）滥用;妄用

examples.en: She is continually abusing her position/authority by getting other people to do things for her.
examples.zh: 她不断滥用职权／权力，让别人替她做事。

examples.en: I never expected that he would abuse the trust I placed in him.
examples.zh: 我从未料到他会利用我对他的信任。

examples.en: He fears that his brother may be abusing alcohol.
If you abuse your body now, you'll pay the price when you're older.
The Mayor and his cohorts have abused their positions of power.
In a landmark case/decision, the Governor pardoned a woman convicted of killing her husband, who had physically abused her.
People who have been abused as children often experience feelings/a sense of worthlessness.
The minister was sacked for abusing power for his personal gain.

definitions.en: to treat someone cruelly or violently

examples.en: sexually abuse The therapist works with children who have been sexually abused.
physically abuse The dog showed signs of being physically abused.
Men who abuse their partners are good at portraying themselves as the real victims.

definitions.en: to speak to someone rudely or cruelly

examples.en: verbally abuse The athlete was being verbally abused by a drunk fan in the crowd.
The crowd started abusing him after he failed to save a goal.
examples.zh: 他没有守住球门，丢了一分，观众开始对他破口大骂。

definitions.en: the use of something in a way that is harmful or morally wrong
definitions.zh: （尤指出于个人私利的）滥用;妄用

examples.en: abuse of power The investigation revealed abuses of power committed by the governor's office.
alcohol abuse The actress disclosed that she was seeking treatment for alcohol abuse.
drug abuse Chronic drug abuse can lead to gradual brain damage.
substance abuse He had struggled with substance abuse for much of his life.
It was an abuse of her kindness to keep asking Ms Lawson for favours.
The government say they are trying to do more to educate the public about the consequences of drug abuse.
After years of abuse from her husband, she eventually found the courage to leave him.
Judge Hawthorne was chosen to head the team investigating the allegations of abuse.
There are few crimes more truly shocking than the abuse of children.
The family said they had endured years of torment and abuse at the hands of the neighbours.

definitions.en: cruel, violent, or unfair treatment of someone

examples.en: child abuse She claimed to have been a victim of child abuse.
examples.zh: 她声称自己曾是虐待儿童罪行的受害者。

examples.en: physical abuse She endured physical abuse (= bad treatment) from her stepfather.
sexual abuse He was arrested for the sexual abuse of a minor.
The nasty way he treats his colleagues borders on abuse.

definitions.en: rude and offensive words said to another person

examples.en: verbal abuse He had apparently experienced a lot of verbal abuse from his co-workers.
examples.zh: 显然他经常遭到同事的辱骂。

examples.en: torrent of abuse They hurled/unleashed a torrent of abuse at the actors (= they said a lot of rude and offensive things to the actors).
term of abuse "Idiot!" is a term of abuse (= an insulting expression).
examples.zh: Idiot! 是一句带有侮辱性的话。

examples.en: I was astonished at the stream of abuse coming from his lips.

definitions.en: the pedal (= a part that you push with your foot) in a vehicle that makes it go faster

examples.en: Start-up accelerators have become increasingly popular elements of the regional growth infrastructure.
examples.zh: 创业加速器已成为区域增长基础设施中越来越受欢迎的元素。

examples.en: Accelerator programmes are a relatively new way of supporting start-ups.
examples.zh: 加速器计划是支持初创企业的一种相对较新的方式。

definitions.en: following or obeying a rule, law, wish, etc.

examples.en: In accordance with her wishes, she was buried in France.
examples.zh: 按照她的遗愿，人们将她安葬在法国。

definitions.en: an arrangement with a bank to keep your money there and to allow you to take it out when you need to

examples.en: close an account I withdrew my money and asked the bank to close my account.
open an account I've opened an account with a building society .
examples.zh: 我在房屋互助协会开了个账户。

examples.en: I paid the money into my account this morning.
examples.zh: 今天上午我把钱存入了我的账户。

examples.en: She paid the cheque into her account.
examples.zh: 她把支票存入了账户。

examples.en: She deposited the check in/into her account.
examples.zh: 她把支票存入了账户。

examples.en: I need to withdraw some money from my account.
examples.zh: 我得从我的账户上取出一些钱来。

examples.en: I was relieved to see from my statement that my account was in credit.
I closed that bank account when I came to London.
The account was in debit at the end of the month.
To open an account, you need to make a minimum deposit of $500.
Alison drew some money out of her account to pay for our trip.

definitions.en: a written or spoken description or record of an event

examples.en: give an account of She gave a thrilling account of her life in the jungle.
examples.zh: 她把自己在丛林中生活的经历描述得惊险刺激。

examples.en: keep an account of He kept a detailed account of the suspect's movements.
examples.zh: 他详细记录了犯罪嫌疑人的一举一动。

examples.en: Several eyewitnesses' accounts differed considerably from the official version of events.
examples.zh: 几个目击者对于事件的叙述和官方的说法有很大出入。

examples.en: By all accounts, San Francisco is a city that's easy to fall in love with.
examples.zh: 人人都说旧金山是个很容易令人倾心的城市。

examples.en: By his own account, he's quite wealthy.
examples.zh: 他自称很富有。

examples.en: She gave a clear, factual account of the attack.
The book is a work of fiction and not intended as a historical account.
The foregoing account was written 50 years after the incident.
She gave a hair-raising account of her escape through the desert.
She gave a clear and lucid account of her plans for the company's future.

definitions.en: because of something:
definitions.zh: 出于健康原因，他不喝酒。

examples.en: He doesn't drink alcohol on account of his poor health.
examples.zh: 出于健康原因，他不喝酒。

definitions.en: an agreement with a shop or business that allows you to buy things and pay for them later

examples.en: Could you put it on/charge it to my account (= can I pay for it later), please?
examples.zh: 把这笔钱记在我的（赊购）账上好吗？

examples.en: Do you have an account at this store/with us, madam?
examples.zh: 女士，你在我们商店有赊购账户吗？

examples.en: settle an account Could you please settle your account in full (= give us all the money you owe us)?
examples.zh: 您可以把赊的账全结清吗？

examples.en: If the advertising agency loses this account, it will make a big dent in their profits.
examples.zh: 如果该广告公司丢掉了这个客户，他们的利润就会锐减。

definitions.en: to not be important:
definitions.zh: 他来不来对我来说都无关紧要。

examples.en: It's of no account to me whether he comes or not.
examples.zh: 他来不来对我来说都无关紧要。

definitions.en: to think of someone or something in the stated way

examples.en: She was accounted a genius by all who knew her work.
examples.zh: 所有知道她作品的人都认为她是个天才。

definitions.en: Someone who is accountable is completely responsible for what they do and must be able to give a satisfactory reason for it.
definitions.zh: 应负责任的；应作解释的；应加以说明的

examples.en: accountable to She is accountable only to the managing director.
examples.zh: 她只对总裁负责。

examples.en: Politicians should be accountable to the public who elected them.
examples.zh: 政界人士应对他们的选民负责。

examples.en: accountable for The recent tax reforms have made government more accountable for its spending.
examples.zh: 最近的税务改革使政府的开支更加透明化。

examples.en: hold someone accountable for something You have to hold your employees accountable for their actions, or else they will just do whatever they please.

definitions.en: the fact of being exact or correct

examples.en: Patricia has checked the reports and can vouch for the accuracy of the information.
with accuracy Experience is needed to use the machine with accuracy.
pinpoint accuracy The computer will calculate your position with pinpoint accuracy.
great accuracy He always hits the ball with great accuracy.
high degree of accuracy Predictions can be made with a high degree of accuracy.
The latest missiles can be fired with incredible accuracy.
Her paintings are almost photographic in their detail and accuracy.
All high-tech weaponry demands frequent servicing to ensure accuracy.

definitions.en: correct, exact, and without any mistakes

examples.en: an accurate machine
examples.zh: 精密的机器

examples.en: an accurate description
examples.zh: 准确的描述

examples.en: The figures they have used are just not accurate.
examples.zh: 他们引用的数字太不准确。

examples.en: Her novel is an accurate reflection of life in Spain.
examples.zh: 她的小说是西班牙生活的真实写照。

examples.en: We hope to become more accurate in predicting earthquakes.
examples.zh: 我们希望能更准确地预报地震。

examples.en: We'll need accurate costings before we can agree to fund the scheme.
The number is accurate to three decimal places.
The film makes no attempt to be historically accurate.
Her predictions turned out to be uncannily accurate.
I hereby certify that the above information is true and accurate.

definitions.en: in a way that is correct, exact, and without any mistakes

examples.en: The plans should be drawn as accurately as possible.
examples.zh: 应尽可能准确地制定计划。

examples.en: For the experiment to be valid, it is essential to record the data accurately.
examples.zh: 为了使实验有效，必须准确地记录数据。

examples.en: Great precision is required to align the mirrors accurately.
It's still not possible to accurately predict the occurrence of earthquakes.
There is a trade-off between doing the job accurately and doing it quickly.
It was my brief to make sure that the facts were set down accurately.

definitions.en: to say that someone has done something morally wrong, illegal, or unkind

examples.en: "It wasn't my fault." "Don't worry, I'm not accusing you."
examples.zh: “不是我的错。”“别担心，我不是在指责你。”

examples.en: accuse someone of something He's been accused of robbery/murder.
examples.zh: 他被指控犯有抢劫／谋杀罪。

examples.en: Are you accusing me of lying?
examples.zh: 你是在指责我撒谎吗？

examples.en: The surgeon was accused of negligence.
examples.zh: 这名外科医生被控玩忽职守。

examples.en: The government stands accused of eroding freedom of speech.
examples.zh: 政府因侵犯言论自由受到谴责。

examples.en: She's been accused of acting dishonestly.
She accused her employer of sex discrimination .
He's been accused of robbery/murder.
Are you accusing me of lying?
"It wasn't my fault." "Don't worry, I'm not accusing you."

definitions.en: to make yourself familiar with new conditions

examples.en: It'll take time for me to accustom myself to the changes.
examples.zh: 我要一段时间后才能适应这些变化。

definitions.en: an abbreviation consisting of the first letters of each word in the name of something, pronounced as a word
definitions.zh: 首字母缩拼词，首字母组合词，首字母缩略词

examples.en: AIDS is an acronym for "Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome".
examples.zh: AIDS （艾滋病）是 Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome（获得性免疫缺损综合征）的首字母缩拼词。

definitions.en: If a bad situation is acute, it causes severe problems or damage.

examples.en: She felt acute embarrassment/anxiety/concern at his behaviour.
examples.zh: 她对他的行为感到极其尴尬／忧虑／担忧。

examples.en: The problem of poverty is particularly acute in rural areas.
examples.zh: 贫困问题在农村地区尤为严重。

examples.en: acute abdominal pains
examples.zh: 剧烈腹痛

examples.en: an acute attack of appendicitis
examples.zh: 阑尾炎急性发作

definitions.en: used to describe intelligence, senses, etc. that are very good, accurate, and able to notice very small differences
definitions.zh: （感官等）敏锐的，灵敏的;机敏的，有洞察力的

examples.en: acute eyesight/hearing
examples.zh: 敏锐的视觉／听觉

examples.en: an acute sense of smell
examples.zh: 灵敏的嗅觉

examples.en: a woman of acute intelligence/judgment
examples.zh: 机敏聪颖／有敏锐判断力的女性

definitions.en: An acute angle is less than 90 degrees.
a sign that is written above a letter in some languages, showing you how to pronounce the letter

examples.en: There's an acute accent on the e in "café".
examples.zh: Café这个词中字母e上面有个尖音符号。

definitions.en: enough or satisfactory for a particular purpose
definitions.zh: 足够的;合格的;合乎需要的

examples.en: Have we got adequate food for 20 guests?
examples.zh: 我们的食物够招待20位客人吗？

examples.en: I didn't have adequate time to prepare.
examples.zh: 我没有充足的时间来准备。

examples.en: It's not a big salary but it's adequate for our needs.
examples.zh: 工资虽不优厚，但足以满足我们的需要了。

examples.en: The council's provision for the elderly is barely adequate (= is not enough).
examples.zh: 市政会给老年人提供的保障不足。

examples.en: Will future oil supplies be adequate to meet world needs?
examples.zh: 未来的石油供应能满足世界的需要吗？

examples.en: Without the adequate provision of childcare, many women who wish to work are unable to do so.
New legislation still does not offer adequate protection for many endangered species.
They demanded adequate remuneration for their work.
Children need adequate amounts of nutritious foods to grow up strong and healthy.
Parents complained that the new school didn't have adequate resources to deal with the large amount of pupils.

definitions.en: in a way that is enough or satisfactory for a particular purpose
definitions.zh: 足够地;合格地;合乎需要地

examples.en: While some patients can be adequately cared for at home, others are best served by care in a hospital.
examples.zh: 虽然有些病人可以在家里得到充分的照顾，但其他病人最好在医院接受治疗看护。

examples.en: Manufacturers may lack the means to adequately test their product.
examples.zh: 制造商可能缺乏对产品进行充分测试的手段。

examples.en: Many countries don't adequately report missing passports to international authorities.
Have the risks been adequately studied?
There will never be a way to adequately thank them.

definitions.en: the fact of someone behaving exactly according to rules, beliefs, etc.

examples.en: He was noted for his strict adherence to the rules.
examples.zh: 他因为严守规则而闻名。

definitions.en: very near, next to, or touching

examples.en: They work in adjacent buildings.
examples.zh: 他们在相邻的两座楼里工作。

examples.en: They lived in a house adjacent to the railway.
examples.zh: 他们家住在铁路旁边。

examples.en: The spillover from the adjacent river flooded the lower fields.
She took a knife and fork from an adjacent table.
They took a boat trip and toured the southern coastline and adjacent islands.
They found a lovely hotel adjacent to the sea front.
The bus station is adjacent to the railway station.

definitions.en: to add something decorative to a person or thing

examples.en: The bride's hair was adorned with white flowers.
examples.zh: 新娘的头上戴着白色的花朵。

definitions.en: the fact of an event happening, an invention being made, or a person arriving
definitions.zh: （事件、发明或人物的）出现，来临，到来

examples.en: Life was transformed by the advent of the steam engine.
examples.zh: 蒸汽机的出现使人类生活发生了翻天覆地的变化。

definitions.en: the period of four weeks before Christmas
definitions.zh: 降临节（圣诞节前的四个星期）

definitions.en: having a negative or harmful effect on something

examples.en: adverse publicity They received a lot of adverse publicity about the changes.
adverse effect So far the drug is thought not to have any adverse effects.
examples.zh: 迄今为止，人们一直认为这种药物没有任何副作用。

examples.en: adverse reaction I had an adverse reaction to the injection. I had an adverse reaction to the injection.
adverse conditions The match has been cancelled because of adverse conditions.
adverse weather The forecasters are expecting adverse weather for the next few days.
He attracted a lot of adverse publicity with his speech about unmarried mothers.
Reactions to the proposal so far have been adverse, but we may convince people in time.
The world record attempt was postponed due to adverse conditions.
He knew there would be an adverse reaction from his friends, so he tried to keep their affair secret.
It is clear that these violent films have an adverse effect on children.

definitions.en: to make something known generally or in public, especially in order to sell it
definitions.zh: （尤指为了销售某物）登广告，做广告，宣传;公布;征聘

examples.en: We advertised our car in the local newspaper.
examples.zh: 我们在当地报纸上登了广告出售我们的轿车。

examples.en: He advertises his services on the company notice board.
examples.zh: 他在公司的布告牌上张贴广告来宣传其业务。

examples.en: I'm going to advertise for (= put a notice in the newspaper, local shop, etc., asking for) someone to clean my house.
examples.zh: 我打算登广告雇个人来做家庭保洁。

examples.en: They advertise clothes 'for the fuller figure'.
It is most expensive to advertise at peak viewing times.
The concert was well enough advertised but ticket sales were poor.
Teaching posts are advertised in Tuesday's edition of the paper.
The market for those dreadful compilation records you see advertised on TV is just another of life's great mysteries.

definitions.en: to publicly support or suggest an idea, development, or way of doing something

examples.en: She advocates taking a more long-term view.
examples.zh: 她主张把目光放得更长远一些。

examples.en: He advocates the return of capital punishment.
examples.zh: 他主张恢复死刑。

examples.en: We will continue to advocate for a regional, cooperative approach to the construction project.
He was part of an extreme political group that advocated the complete overthrow of the political system.
Heart disease specialists advocate a diet low in cholesterol.
The author denied that his new book advocated violence.
Trotsky advocated worldwide revolution.
After the death of their son, they devoted themselves to advocating for police reform.
Our mission is to advocate for better support and counselling for current and former soldiers.

definitions.en: a lawyer who speaks for or defends someone in a court of law

examples.en: She has been the advocate for plaintiffs in many successful libel cases.
Candidates for the post need to have at least 5 years experience as a solicitor or advocate.
Since regulators laid charges against him, his advocates have filed a flurry of papers.
Many autism advocates believe that diagnosis takes too long.
Consumer advocates say that the changes will disproportionately affect the poor.
Food safety advocates called for a change in the law.
The singer became a passionate advocate for breast screening after it led to early detection of her cancer.

definitions.en: a liking or sympathy for someone or something, especially because of shared characteristics
definitions.zh: （尤指因为有相同的特征而引起的）情投意合，喜爱，吸引

examples.en: She seems to have a natural affinity for/with water.
examples.zh: 她好像天生就喜欢水。

examples.en: There are several close affinities between the two paintings.
examples.zh: 这两幅画有多处非常相似的地方。

definitions.en: to state something as true

examples.en: The suspect affirmed (that) he had been at home all evening.
examples.zh: 犯罪嫌疑人声称他整个晚上都呆在家中。

examples.en: She affirmed her intention to apply for the post.
examples.zh: 她承认自己有意应聘这一职位。

examples.en: The government has affirmed its commitment to equal rights.
examples.zh: 政府声明了其致力于权利平等的承诺。

definitions.en: → before
mentioned earlier

examples.en: The aforementioned Mr Parkes then entered the cinema.
examples.zh: 前面提到的这位帕克斯先生接着进入了电影院。

definitions.en: the person or people mentioned earlier

examples.en: The aforementioned was/were seen waiting outside the building.
examples.zh: 有人看到前面提到的这个／这些人在楼外等候。

definitions.en: If you do something afresh, you deal with it again in a new way.

examples.en: start afresh She tore up the letter and started afresh.
examples.zh: 她撕掉信，又重新写。

examples.en: We agreed to look afresh at her original proposal.
examples.zh: 我们同意重新考虑她最初的建议。

definitions.en: showing anger and a willingness to attack other people
definitions.zh: 好斗的;富于攻击性的;挑衅的

examples.en: The stereotype is that men tend to be more aggressive than women.
If I criticize him, he gets aggressive and starts shouting.
examples.zh: 如果我批评他，他就会寻衅大吵大闹。

examples.en: Aggressive behaviour will not be tolerated in the classroom.
He is rebellious, aggressive and at times self-destructive.
He's slightly aggressive, which a lot of people find a bit off-putting when they first meet him.
The mood of the crowd suddenly turned aggressive.

definitions.en: determined to win or succeed and using forceful action to do this
definitions.zh: 积极进取的；有冲劲的；雄心勃勃的

examples.en: an aggressive election campaign
examples.zh: 声势浩大的竞选活动

examples.en: aggressive marketing tactics
examples.zh: 雄心勃勃的营销策略

examples.en: Both players won their first-round matches in aggressive style.
examples.zh: 两名选手都以敢打敢拼、志在必得的打法赢得了第一轮比赛。

examples.en: She carved out a reputation for herself as an aggressive negotiator.
We need our team to be more aggressive.

definitions.en: An aggressive condition is one that is serious and quickly spreads in the body or gets worse.

examples.en: The more aggressive the cancer, the further and faster it spreads.
examples.zh: 癌症越呈恶性，其发展就越广越快。

examples.en: Brain cancer requires aggressive treatment such as surgery.
examples.zh: 脑部肿瘤需要采用积极治疗手段，比如手术。

definitions.en: able to move your body quickly and easily

examples.en: Monkeys are very agile climbers.
examples.zh: 猴子动作敏捷，善于攀援。

examples.en: You need to have agile fingers to do this kind of work.
examples.zh: 从事这种工作，手指必须非常灵巧。

definitions.en: able to think quickly and clearly

examples.en: For a man of 80, he has a remarkably agile mind.
examples.zh: 对于一个80岁的老人来说，他才思惊人地敏捷。

definitions.en: used for describing ways of planning and doing work in which it is understood that making changes as they are needed is an important part of the job
definitions.zh: （工作中）见机行事的，反应快速的

definitions.en: the ability to move your body quickly and easily

examples.en: He has the agility of a mountain goat.
examples.zh: 他像山羊一样敏捷。

examples.en: The acrobats display jawdropping feats of strength, agility, and control.
examples.zh: 杂技演员技艺展示了令人瞠目结舌的力量、敏捷性和控制力。

examples.en: This job requires considerable mental agility.
examples.zh: 这项工作需要相当好的思维敏捷性。

examples.en: We underestimate the agility of a child's mind.
examples.zh: 我们低估了孩子思维的敏捷性。

examples.en: Business agility means a company is always in a position to take account of market changes.
examples.zh: 商业上的敏捷意味着一个公司总是能够考虑到市场的变化。

examples.en: Constant change is the new dynamic of the global economy, and makes agility even more necessary than ever.
examples.zh: 不断的改变是全球经济的新动力，这使得灵活性比以往任何时候都更加必要。

examples.en: These trainers are packed with features to boost speed and agility.
Each task is designed to increase your mental agility.
There needs to be a balance between the short-term need for agility and flexibility in business and the long-term need for stability.

definitions.en: someone who does not know, or believes that it is impossible to know, if a god exists
definitions.zh: 不可知论者（对神存在与否不能肯定或认为不可知）

examples.en: Although he was raised a Catholic, he was an agnostic for most of his adult life.
examples.zh: 他虽然从小接受的是天主教教育，但成年后基本上是个不可知论者。

examples.en: The group includes atheists and agnostics as well as religious believers.

definitions.en: having the belief that it is not possible to know whether god exists

examples.en: Whether we are religious, atheist, or agnostic, there are some moral values we all share.
He is agnostic on the existence of God.

definitions.en: relating to hardware or software that can be used with many different types of platform (= system)
definitions.zh: （硬件和软件）跨平台的，适用不同平台的

definitions.en: having some of the same qualities

examples.en: They speak a language akin to French.
examples.zh: 他们说的是一种类似法语的语言。

definitions.en: an expression of sadness or disappointment, especially when there is no hope that a situation will change

examples.en: "Alas!" cried the man.
I love football but, alas, I have no talent as a player.
examples.zh: 我喜欢足球。唉，可惜我没有足球运动员的天分。

examples.en: "Will you be able to come tomorrow?" "Alas, no."
examples.zh: “你明天能来吗？”“唉，不行。”

examples.en: Alas and alack, he will not help me.
Alack and alas, the food was spoiled.
Alas and alack, nobody at that time had the job of fact-checking the statements of state officials.
He tried to behave, but alas and lack, he ended up back in prison.
Alack and alas, the restaurant owner turned down my request.
There was, alas, no more money.
Alas, the show was not successful.

definitions.en: although

examples.en: The evening was very pleasant, albeit a little quiet.
examples.zh: 那个夜晚过得非常愉快，尽管有点儿冷清。

examples.en: He tried, albeit without success.
examples.zh: 虽然没有成功，可他努力了。

definitions.en: a part of mathematics in which signs and letters represent numbers
to make something bad such as pain or problems less severe

examples.en: The drugs did nothing to alleviate her pain/suffering.
examples.zh: 这些药物对减轻她的病痛／痛苦没有丝毫作用。

definitions.en: to give something to someone as their share of a total amount, to use in a particular way

examples.en: The government is allocating £10 million for health education.
examples.zh: 政府将拨出1000万英镑作为健康教育经费。

examples.en: As project leader, you will have to allocate people jobs/allocate jobs to people.
examples.zh: 作为项目负责人，你必须给大家分派工作。

examples.en: It is not the job of the investigating committee to allocate blame for the disaster/to allocate blame to individuals.
examples.zh: 调查委员会的任务不是确定谁该为这场灾难负责任／哪些人应该负责任。

examples.en: The local council has decided not to allocate funds for the project.
The president has agreed to allocate further funds to develop the new submarine.
Tickets will be allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.
There are ten marks allocated to every question.
Try to allocate yourself a set time each day to practise your exercises.

definitions.en: to give something, especially a share of something available, for a particular purpose
definitions.zh: （尤指将现有的东西）分配;分派

examples.en: They allotted everyone a separate desk.
examples.zh: 他们给每个人都配备了个人专用的办公桌。

examples.en: They allotted a separate desk to everyone.
examples.zh: 他们给每一个人都单独配发了桌子。

examples.en: The ministry of culture will be allotted about 15% less this year.
examples.zh: 今年文化部得到的拨款将减少10%。

examples.en: Three hours have been allotted to/for this task.
examples.zh: 这项任务限定在3小时内完成。

examples.en: The museum is planning to increase the amount of space allotted to modern art.
examples.zh: 博物馆正计划增加现代艺术展区的面积。

definitions.en: an important official who works in a foreign country representing his or her own country there, and who is officially accepted in this position by that country

examples.en: The UK's ambassador in Moscow has refused to comment.
examples.zh: 英国驻莫斯科大使已拒绝发表评论。

examples.en: She's a former ambassador to the United States.
examples.zh: 她是前驻美国大使。

examples.en: Late last night, the French ambassador was summoned to the Foreign Office to discuss the crisis.
examples.zh: 昨晚深夜法国大使被外交部召见，商讨这起危机事宜。

examples.en: At that time, Sir Jeremy Greenstock was the British UN ambassador (= represented the United Kingdom at the United Nations).
He accused the ambassador of diplomatic double-talk.
The ambassador denied that any missiles had been fired across the border.
The ambassador is giving a banquet for the visiting president.
The ambassador has offered to act as a go-between for the two countries involved in the conflict.
As an ambassador, she enjoys a very privileged status.

definitions.en: having or expressing more than one possible meaning, sometimes intentionally
definitions.zh: 含糊不清的;引起歧义的，模棱两可的;不明确的

examples.en: His reply to my question was somewhat ambiguous.
examples.zh: 他对我的提问回答得有些含糊不清。

examples.en: The wording of the agreement is ambiguous.
examples.zh: 该协议的措词模棱两可。

examples.en: The government has been ambiguous on this issue.
examples.zh: 在此问题上政府立场一直不明确。

examples.en: The contract is phrased in a rather ambiguous way.
His attitude to environmental issues was sometimes quite ambiguous.
Legal experts claim that the law on this matter is ambiguous, and that the way the company interpreted it was reasonable.
What do you think the title really means? It's rather ambiguous, isn't it?
If you don't use the correct grammar the meaning can be a bitambiguous and lead to confusion.

definitions.en: to change the words of a text, especially a law or a legal document
definitions.zh: 修订，修正，修改（法律文件等）

examples.en: MPs were urged to amend the law to prevent another oil tanker disaster.
examples.zh: 下院议员们被敦促修订该法律，以防止油轮泄漏灾难再次发生。

examples.en: In line 20, "men" should be amended (= changed) to "people".
examples.zh: 第20行中的 men 应改为 people。

examples.en: Until the constitution is amended, the power to appoint ministers will remain with the president.
examples.zh: 在宪法修正之前，总统仍然有权任命部长。

definitions.en: in the middle of or surrounded by

examples.en: On the floor, amid mounds of books, were two small envelopes.
examples.zh: 地板上的书堆里有两个小信封。

examples.en: The new perfume was launched amidst a fanfare of publicity.
examples.zh: 这种新香水在一阵大张旗鼓的广告宣传下推出市场。

examples.en: a brief moment of levity amid the solemn proceedings
Her office was an oasis of peace and sanity amid the surrounding chaos.
The town sits amid gentle hills and dense forest.
The actors made their bows amid great applause.
The president cancelled his visit amid speculation that his health was failing.

definitions.en: to reduce a debt or cost by paying small regular amounts

examples.en: They pay monthly loan payments based on a formula that amortizes the debt over 15 years, at 8 percent interest.
The economics of a show depend on the number of weeks over which the producer can amortize the start-up costs.
Economics dictate that schedule because it enables clinics to treat patients in shifts to amortize the cost of the equipment, he said.
I know how to make a profit, and I know how to amortize the debt.
That cost, he said, could be amortized over the life of the revenue stream.

definitions.en: the sign &, used for "and"
more than enough

examples.en: You'll have ample opportunity to ask questions after the talk.
examples.zh: 演讲结束后你们会有足够的时间提问。

examples.en: There's ample evidence that the lawyer knew exactly what she was doing.
examples.zh: 有充分的证据表明，该律师十分清楚她自己在做什么。

examples.en: They had ample warning of the factory closure.
examples.zh: 对于工厂的倒闭，他们早就得到了大量警告。

examples.en: "Will that be enough paper for you?" "Oh yes, that's ample."
There's ample room for another desk in the reception area.
"Will $500 be enough spending money?" "That will be ample."
They believe they've got ample evidence to convict her of the crime.
We should have ample time for a drink before the show begins.

definitions.en: If the shape of someone's body or a part of their body is ample, it is large.

examples.en: her ample bosom
examples.zh: 她丰满的胸部

examples.en: His ample girth (= his big stomach) was clear evidence of his passion for food.
examples.zh: 从他那大腹便便的样子一眼就能看出，他是个非常好吃的人。

definitions.en: Amplification makes music or other sounds louder.

examples.en: electronic amplification
examples.zh: 电子扩音

examples.en: The horror lies in the violence itself, which needs no amplification.
examples.zh: 暴力行为本身就令人恐惧，对此无需作任何阐释。

definitions.en: a large amount or wide range

examples.en: The sheer amplitude of the novel invites comparisons with Tolstoy.
examples.zh: 这部小说鸿篇巨制、气势恢宏，仅此就足以和托尔斯泰的作品相提并论。

definitions.en: the distance between the top and the bottom of a wave
having similar features to another thing and therefore able to be compared with it
definitions.zh: 相似的;类似的;可比拟的

examples.en: analogous to The experience of a mystic trance is in a sense analogous to sleep or drunkenness.
The emergency vehicle for the International Space Station is analogous to a lifeboat.
examples.zh: 国际空间站的应急车辆类似于救生艇。

examples.en: The two TV programmes - set in different cities - are roughly analogous big-city police dramas.
These are two analogous situations.
San Francisco's Golden Gate Park is spectacular and not in any way analogous to New York's Central Park.
The way a string vibrates is somewhat analogous to the way ripples spread out in water.
Analogous colours are harmonious colours which sit side by side on the colours wheel — related colours such as yellow, yellow-orange, orange, orange-red, and red.

definitions.en: An analogue recording is one that is made by changing the sound waves into electrical signals of the same type. An analogue signal is a signal of this type.

examples.en: I started recording the sounds on analogue reel-to-reel tape.
This is the standard method used to convert analogue signals to digital.
A vinyl record is analogue. A CD is digital, which means that the sound, which starts as analogue, has to be digitally coded.
The album was recorded in Paris on analogue equipment.
That year the vast majority of television being watched was still on analogue terrestrial channels.
The 2,000 households in the area which are still watching analogue have less than four weeks to convert to digital.
There's been a real transition from the old analogue security cameras.
We are upgrading the existing CCTV cameras from analogue to digital.

definitions.en: something that is similar to or can be used instead of something else
definitions.zh: 相似物，类似物;对等物;代用物

examples.en: He has been studying the European analogues of the British Parliament.
examples.zh: 他一直在研究相当于英国议会的欧洲政治机构。

examples.en: An analogue to penguins, the extinct plotopterids, appeared in the North Pacific about 37 million years ago.
This is the closest analogue we have on Earth to what might exist elsewhere in our solar system.
Modern-day Olympic events, such as running, jumping, and discus-throwing, have classical analogues.

definitions.en: a comparison between things that have similar features, often used to help explain a principle or idea

examples.en: He drew an analogy between the brain and a vast computer.
examples.zh: 他把大脑比作一台庞大的计算机。

examples.en: It is sometimes easier to illustrate an abstract concept by analogy with (= by comparing it with) something concrete.
examples.zh: 有时用具体事物作比拟来阐释抽象概念会更容易些。

definitions.en: providing support or help
definitions.zh: 辅助的，补充的；附属的，附加的

examples.en: ancillary staff/workers
examples.zh: 勤杂人员／工人

examples.en: an ancillary role
examples.zh: 配角

examples.en: Campaigning to change government policy is ancillary to the charity's direct relief work.
examples.zh: 除了直接采取救援行动外，慈善组织还大力开展活动，促成政府改变其政策。

definitions.en: again or one more time, especially in a different way

examples.en: The film tells anew the story of his rise to fame and power.
examples.zh: 这部电影重现了他成名掌权的发迹史。

definitions.en: the day on which an important event happened in a previous year

examples.en: We always celebrate our wedding anniversary with dinner in an expensive restaurant.
examples.zh: 我们总是去豪华饭店就餐来庆祝我们的结婚纪念日。

examples.en: Tomorrow is the 30th anniversary of the revolution.
examples.zh: 明天是革命30周年纪念日。

examples.en: We are planning a dinner to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the founding of the company.
Tomorrow's parade will mark the 50th anniversary of the battle.
We went for a slap-up meal on our wedding anniversary.
It was their 25th wedding anniversary last week.
On this anniversary of the tragedy we remember the living as well as the dead.

definitions.en: to add a short explanation or opinion to a text or image

examples.en: Annotated editions of Shakespeare's plays help readers to understand old words.
examples.zh: 莎士比亚剧作的注释本可以帮助读者理解剧中较古老的词语。

examples.en: an annotated bibliography/manuscript/edition
His great-granddaughter has painstakingly transcribed and annotated his wartime diaries.
The students annotate their photos, saying why they are relevant.
The book's annotated bibliography fills 45 pages.
You are allowed to bring annotated copies of the novel you have been studying into the exam.
Any attached documentation should be annotated with explanatory notes for clarification.
Students arrive at the lecture equipped with printed notes: all they have to do is to annotate these printouts.
He annotates and indexes a page in his notebook.
Typically I use this program to annotate a document with my own structured content.
Annotated data has facilitated recent advances in part of speech tagging, parsing, and other language processing issues.

definitions.en: to make someone angry

examples.en: Tina really annoyed me in the meeting this morning.
examples.zh: 在今天上午的会上，蒂娜真是把我气坏了。

examples.en: I'm sorry - is my cough annoying you?
examples.zh: 对不起，我咳嗽打扰你了吧？

examples.en: It annoys me that she just expects us to help.
examples.zh: 她只是眼巴巴地盼着我们去帮她，我真是气得慌。

examples.en: It really annoys me when people expect me to tip as well as pay a service charge in a restaurant.
examples.zh: 在饭店里，付了服务费后他们还想要小费，这很让我恼火。

examples.en: I'm sure he says these things deliberately to annoy you.
It really annoys me when people push in front of me like that.
They were afraid to complain about the noise in case they annoyed the neighbours.
The film portrayed the artist as a complete drunk, which has annoyed a lot of people.
After a while her coughing really began to annoy me.

definitions.en: different from what is usual, or not in agreement with something else and therefore not satisfactory
definitions.zh: 反常的;异常的;不规则的

examples.en: In a multicultural society is it not anomalous to have a blasphemy law that only protects one religious faith?
examples.zh: 在一个多元文化的社会里，制定只保护一种宗教信仰的亵渎法，难道不反常吗？

examples.en: The experiment yielded anomalous results.
examples.zh: 实验产生了异常的结果。

examples.en: You can't account in advance for anomalous events that could ruin your plans, like weather.
Events of the past few weeks are making our deal increasingly anomalous.
Temporary staff are in an anomalous position.

definitions.en: a person or thing that is different from what is usual, or not in agreement with something else and therefore not satisfactory
definitions.zh: 异常的人（或事物）；不规则；（同一种类中的）畸形

examples.en: Statistical anomalies can make it difficult to compare economic data from one year to the next.
examples.zh: 统计上的不规范会使年与年之间的经济数据难以互相比较。

examples.en: The anomaly of the social security system is that you sometimes have more money without a job.
examples.zh: 社会保障制度下的反常现象是，失业有时反而拿钱更多。

definitions.en: the situation in which someone's name is not given or known

examples.en: The police have reassured witnesses that they will be guaranteed anonymity.
examples.zh: 警方让目击证人放心，保证不公开他们的姓名。

definitions.en: made or done by someone whose name is not known or not made public
definitions.zh: 匿名的；不知姓名的；名字不公开的

examples.en: The money was donated by an anonymous benefactor.
examples.zh: 这笔钱来自一位不愿透露姓名的捐赠者。

examples.en: Police said an anonymous caller warned that a bomb was about to go off.
examples.zh: 警方说有人打来匿名电话，警告一枚炸弹即将爆炸。

examples.en: An attempt to implant an embryo using an egg from an anonymous woman donor was unsuccessful.
examples.zh: 用一位匿名女性捐献的卵子来植入胚胎的尝试未获成功。

examples.en: He received an anonymous letter threatening to disclose details of his affair if he didn't pay the money.
examples.zh: 他收到一封匿名信，威胁说如果他不付钱，就把他的风流韵事都抖搂出来。

examples.en: For reasons of personal safety, the informant wishes to remain anonymous.
examples.zh: 为了自身安全，举报人不希望透露姓名。

examples.en: He has a rather anonymous face.
examples.zh: 他相貌平平。

examples.en: An anonymous letter alerted police to the possibility of a terrorist attack at the airport.
An anonymous businesswoman donated one million dollars to the charity.
Thanks to a large gift from an anonymous donor, the charity was able to continue its work.
After years of research, scholars have finally ascribed this anonymous play to Christopher Marlowe.
An anonymous tipster has leaked confidential government information to the press.

definitions.en: without the name of someone who has done a particular thing being known or made public

examples.en: The donation was made anonymously.
examples.zh: 捐赠是匿名进行的。

examples.en: The information was posted anonymously online.
examples.zh: 该信息以匿名方式发布在网上。

examples.en: At the time he was adopted, all adoptions were done anonymously.
The book was anonymously written.
The school received an anonymously donated piano.

definitions.en: to imagine or expect that something will happen

examples.en: We don't anticipate any trouble.
examples.zh: 我们不希望出现任何问题。

examples.en: We had one or two difficulties along the way that we didn't anticipate.
examples.zh: 在进行的过程中，我们遇到一两个没有预料到的难题。

examples.en: Are you anticipating a lot of people at the party tonight?
examples.zh: 你预计今晚的聚会会有很多人来吗？

examples.en: They anticipate having several applicants for the job.
examples.zh: 他们预计会有几个人来应聘这份工作。

examples.en: They anticipate that they will have several applicants for the job
examples.zh: 他们预计会有几个人来应聘这份工作。

examples.en: At this stage we can't really anticipate what will happen.
examples.zh: 在这个阶段我们真的无法预料会发生什么。

examples.en: The anticipated inflation figure is lower than last month's.
examples.zh: 预计中的通货膨胀数字低于上月。

examples.en: We've tried to anticipate the most likely problems, but it's impossible to be prepared for every eventuality.
We don't anticipate any snags with the negotiations.
We thought the concert in the park would be popular but we didn't anticipate so many people turning up.
The holiday cost a lot more than we had anticipated.
Police are anticipating trouble at tonight's game.

definitions.en: to take action in preparation for something that you think will happen
definitions.zh: 采取行动来防止;先发制人

examples.en: It's always best to anticipate a problem before it arises.
examples.zh: 防患于未然永远都是最明智之举。

examples.en: The army anticipated (= took action in preparation for) the explosion by evacuating the town.
examples.zh: 这支军队预料会发生爆炸，已疏散了镇上居民。

definitions.en: the highest point or top of a shape or object

examples.en: apex of the apex of a triangle/pyramid
examples.zh: 三角形的顶点／金字塔的塔尖

definitions.en: the highest point or most successful part of something

examples.en: apex of He reached the apex of his career during that period.
examples.zh: 那个时期他的事业达到顶峰。

definitions.en: the pointed end of an organ

examples.en: A chest x-ray showed an abnormality in the left lung apex.
examples.zh: 胸部透视显示左肺尖部异常。

definitions.en: abbreviation for Advance Purchase Excursion: a system of cheap travel tickets that must be bought a particular number of days before travelling
definitions.zh: 预付款旅游票优惠（Advance Purchase Excursion的缩写）

examples.en: an Apex fare
examples.zh: 预付款旅游票优惠价

definitions.en: able to be seen or understood
definitions.zh: 清晰可见的；显而易见的；明白易懂的

examples.en: Her unhappiness was apparent to everyone.
examples.zh: 谁都看得出来她很不开心。

examples.en: It was becoming increasingly apparent that he could no longer take care of himself.
examples.zh: 他再也无法自理生活了，这一点日益明显。

examples.en: I was on the metro this morning when, for no apparent reason, the man opposite me suddenly screamed.
examples.zh: 今天上午在地铁上，我对面的一个男人突然莫名其妙地尖叫起来。

examples.en: There are one or two apparent discrepancies between the two reports.
examples.zh: 这两份报告看上去有一两处出入。

examples.en: She has this apparent innocence which, I suspect, she uses to her advantage.
examples.zh: 她貌似天真，但我认为，这只是对她有利的伪装。

examples.en: Various reasons are given for the apparent glass ceiling women hit in many professions.
Criminal gangs are terrorizing the city with apparent impunity.
At school she was a late developer, and it wasn't until she went to university that her talents became apparent.
In the middle of the meeting she suddenly laid into him for no apparent reason.
A small worry in the back of your mind can for no apparent reason take on massive proportions in the middle of the night.

definitions.en: a small tube-shaped part that is joined to the intestines on the right side of the body and has no use in humans

examples.en: She had her appendix out (= medically removed) last summer.
examples.zh: 去年夏天她做了阑尾切除手术。

definitions.en: a separate part at the end of a book or magazine that gives extra information

examples.en: There's an appendix at the end of the book with a list of dates.
examples.zh: 书后有一个附录，列有年代日期。

definitions.en: affecting or relating to a person or thing

examples.en: This part of the law is only applicable to companies employing more than five people.
examples.zh: 该法律这一部分条款仅适用于有5名以上雇员的公司。

examples.en: The new qualifications are applicable to all European countries.
examples.zh: 新的限定性条件适用于所有欧洲国家。

definitions.en: not completely accurate but close

examples.en: The train's approximate time of arrival is 10.30.
examples.zh: 这列火车大概在10点30分到站。

examples.en: The approximate cost will be about $600.
examples.zh: 费用大约为600美元。

examples.en: Can you give me an approximate idea of the numbers involved?
examples.zh: 你能粗略地告诉我参与的人数吗？

examples.en: The way that the trunk of a tree divides into smaller and smaller branches and twigs is an approximate fractal pattern.
You only need to submit a plan with approximate dimensions at this stage.
The recipe only gives an approximate cooking time.
The following costings for the project are only an approximate guide.
We can only give an approximate idea of cost at this stage.

definitions.en: to be almost the same as

examples.en: The newspaper reports of the discussion only roughly approximated to (= were not exactly the same as) what was actually said.
examples.zh: 报纸关于这次商讨的报道并不完全符合实际。

examples.en: Student numbers this year are expected to approximate 5,000 (= to be about 5,000).
examples.zh: 预计今年学生人数可达5000人左右。

definitions.en: close to a particular number or time although not exactly that number or time

examples.en: The job will take approximately three weeks, and cost approximately £1,000.
examples.zh: 这项工作大概需时3周， 费用为1000英镑左右。

examples.en: Due to a slight technical hitch the concert will be starting approximately half an hour late.
In 1992 the population of Cairo was approximately 6,500,000.
One pound is approximately equal to 454 grams.
The length of the bay is approximately 200 miles.
The earth takes approximately 365 days to go round the sun.

definitions.en: in a way that is suitable or right for a particular situation

examples.en: aptly named We spent a week at the aptly named Grand View Hotel.
examples.zh: 我们在丽景酒店住了一周，酒店的风景真是名副其实。

examples.en: "Perseverance" aptly describes his hard work to complete the project.
examples.zh: “毅力”这个名字很恰当地描述了他为完成这个项目所做的努力。

examples.en: It has been very aptly pointed out that laws are subject to change.
Calling the play a masterpiece aptly sums it up.
Her ambition is aptly illustrated by her success.

definitions.en: mysterious and known only by a few people
definitions.zh: 神秘的;秘密的;晦涩难解的

examples.en: He was the only person who understood all the arcane details of the agreement.
examples.zh: 他是唯一能看懂协议中所有那些晦涩难解的繁缛条文的人。

examples.en: This argument may seem arcane to those not closely involved in the world of finance.
examples.zh: 对于那些不谙金融界事务的人而言，这种观点如同天书。

definitions.en: of or belonging to an ancient period in history

examples.en: an archaic system of government
examples.zh: 古老的政体

examples.en: an archaic law/rule/language
examples.zh: 古代法律／法规／语言

examples.en: The Olympics united the fiercely independent city-states that characterized Greece in the archaic age.
They discovered hitherto unknown texts in archaic scripts.
The case study cites the problems in mapping archaic science terminology to appropriate current terms.
How did such bizarre and archaic ideas make their way into educational policy?
The budgetary systems were archaic and over-complicated.

definitions.en: a large, flat area surrounded by seats used for sports or entertainment
definitions.zh: （体育比赛或表演用的）场地，竞技场，剧场

examples.en: an Olympic/a sports arena
examples.zh: 奥运会／运动会比赛场地

definitions.en: an activity that involves argument and discussion
definitions.zh: 活动场所;斗争场所;竞争舞台

examples.en: After 30 years in the political arena, our local member of parliament is retiring next year.
examples.zh: 我们当地选出的这位下议院议员已经在政治舞台上活跃了30年，明年他就要退休了。

definitions.en: used when stating an opinion or belief that you think can be shown to be true

examples.en: He is arguably the world's best football player.
examples.zh: 他可能是世界上最好的足球运动员。

examples.en: Arguably, the drug should not have been made available.
examples.zh: 或许这种药根本就不该上市。

definitions.en: to plan, prepare for, or organize something

examples.en: I'm trying to arrange my work so that I can have a couple of days off next week.
examples.zh: 我正设法把工作安排好，以便下周能够休息两天。

examples.en: arrange something for something The meeting has been arranged for Wednesday.
examples.zh: 会议已经安排在了周三。

examples.en: arrange for She's arranged for her son to have swimming lessons.
examples.zh: 她已经安排她儿子去上游泳课了。

examples.en: arrange with I've already arranged with him to meet at the cinema.
examples.zh: 我已和他约好了在电影院见面。

examples.en: They arranged to have dinner the following month.
examples.zh: 他们约在下个月共进晚餐。

examples.en: I'd deliberately arranged that they should arrive at the same time.
examples.zh: 我特意安排好让他们同时到达。

examples.en: We haven't yet arranged when to meet.
examples.zh: 我们还没有约好什么时候会面。

examples.en: My secretary will phone you to arrange a meeting.
The company will arrange transport from the airport.
I've specially arranged my trip so that I'll be home on Friday evening.
We've arranged to stay overnight at my sister's house.
They tried to arrange a ceremony with as little fuss as possible.

definitions.en: to put a group of objects in a particular order
definitions.zh: 整理，使有条理；排列；布置

examples.en: She arranged her birthday cards along the shelf.
examples.zh: 她把生日贺卡在架子上摆成一排。

examples.en: Who arranged these flowers so beautifully?
examples.zh: 这花插得真漂亮，谁的手艺？

examples.en: His books are neatly arranged in alphabetical order.
examples.zh: 他的书都按照字母顺序排得整整齐齐。

examples.en: I'll have to do some thinking about how best to arrange the books.
The desks were arranged in rows of ten.
I've arranged the pictures of the animals alphabetically from aardvark to zebra.
I've arranged the books alphabetically so don't muddle them up.
We arranged the chairs in a semicircle.

definitions.en: to make changes to a piece of music so that it can be played in a different way, for example by a particular instrument

examples.en: Beethoven's fifth symphony has been arranged for the piano.
examples.zh: 贝多芬的第五交响乐被改编成了钢琴曲。

definitions.en: to move up or climb something

examples.en: They slowly ascended the steep path up the mountain.
examples.zh: 他们沿着陡峭的山路缓缓向上攀登。

examples.en: There's a long flight of steps ascending (= leading up) to the cathedral doors.
examples.zh: 要往上走很长一段台阶才能到达大教堂的门前。

definitions.en: to rise to a position of higher rank
definitions.zh: 登上高的职位;升级，晋级

examples.en: He eventually ascended to the position of chief executive.
examples.zh: 他最终登上了总裁的宝座。

definitions.en: to discover something

examples.en: The police have so far been unable to ascertain the cause of the explosion.
examples.zh: 警方到目前为止尚未查明爆炸原因。

examples.en: Have you ascertained whether she's coming or not?
examples.zh: 你问清楚了吗，她来还是不来？

examples.en: I ascertained that no one could overhear us before I told Otto the news.
examples.zh: 确信没有人能听到我们的谈话之后，我才把这个消息告诉了奥托。

definitions.en: to believe or say that something is caused by something else

examples.en: To what do you ascribe your phenomenal success?
examples.zh: 你认为你取得如此巨大成功的原因是什么？

definitions.en: to believe or claim that something was said, written, or created by a particular person
definitions.zh: 把…归属于；认为是…所说（或写、创作等）

examples.en: After years of research, scholars have finally ascribed this anonymous play to Christopher Marlowe.
examples.zh: 经过多年研究，学者们终于认定这部佚名剧作出自克里斯托弗•马洛的手笔。

definitions.en: to believe that a particular quality or feature belongs to or is typical of someone or something

examples.en: People like to ascribe human feelings to animals.
examples.zh: 人们愿意认为动物有像人一样的情感。

definitions.en: a belief or decision that a particular quality or feature belongs to or is typical of someone or something
definitions.zh: 赋予某人或某物特定的性质或特质，归属

examples.en: Most teachers would reject the ascription of particular traits to whole groups of children on the basis of social class.
examples.zh: 大多数教师会拒绝根据社会阶层将某些特性赋予整个儿童群体。

examples.en: Animism is the ascription of a spiritual life to all of creation.
Her tendency to sweeping generalizations and ascription of motivation is clear.
We've learned something that might be relevant to the legal ascription of responsibility.
the ascription of traditional domestic and non-domestic roles to the female and male characters in the book
Ethnic descriptions such as "Spaniard" and "mestizo" were definitions of social and fiscal status rather than of genetic ascription.

definitions.en: a belief or claim that something was said, written, or created by a particular person
definitions.zh: 对（作品、内容）归属权的认定

examples.en: The ascription of the text to Gerves du Bus is questionable.
examples.zh: 不能确认文稿是热尔维斯杜巴斯所写。

examples.en: The general likeness of this poem to Barbour's accepted work in verse-length, dialect and style made the ascription plausible.
The ascription to Sir William Coventry arose because the printer used a manuscript found among Coventry’s papers.
The author accepts the ascription of all these treatises to Khayyam.
The ascription of this argument to Alexander seems reasonable.

definitions.en: a belief or claim that something is caused by something else

examples.en: the ascription of our successes to our own merit
examples.zh: 将我们的成功归功于自身的优点

examples.en: the ascription of natural phenomena to a divine cause
In any scientific ascription of cause, we must carefully regard the facts.
the ascription of these acts to evil powers

definitions.en: on or to one side

examples.en: Stand aside, please, and let these people pass.
examples.zh: 请往边上站一站，让这些人过去。

examples.en: He pulled the curtain aside.
examples.zh: 他把窗帘拉开。

examples.en: I gave her a plate of food but she pushed it aside.
examples.zh: 我给她一盘吃的，可她把盘子推开了。

examples.en: I've forgotten my wallet, so could you put this book aside (= keep this book) for me and I'll come back later on.
examples.zh: 我忘了带钱包，麻烦你把这本书替我留一下，我稍后再来买。

examples.en: She took me aside (= took me away from the other people) to tell me the news.
examples.zh: 她把我拉到一边告诉了我这个消息。

examples.en: Every week I put aside some money for a new TV.
examples.zh: 我每星期省下一些钱，以后好买台新电视机。

examples.en: Let's leave that matter aside for now and talk about the more urgent problem facing us.
examples.zh: 眼下我们暂且把那件事放一放，先来讨论我们面临的更为紧迫的问题。

examples.en: The crowd watched as the referee drew the player aside and spoke to him.
They elbowed the onlookers aside.
Stand aside, please, so the doctor can get through.
In all seriousness now - joking aside - I do think there's a problem here that we've got to get sorted.
She laid aside her book and went to answer the phone.

definitions.en: except for:
definitions.zh: 缺钱依然是个问题，但是除此以外，我们一切都还顺利。

examples.en: Money continues to be a problem but aside from that we're all well.
examples.zh: 缺钱依然是个问题，但是除此以外，我们一切都还顺利。

definitions.en: a remark that someone makes in a quiet voice because they do not want everyone to hear it

examples.en: a whispered aside
examples.zh: 悄悄话

examples.en: The informative asides about rural life make this wine guide rather special.
examples.zh: 这本品酒指南不时插入一些有关乡村生活的描述，这让人感到耳目一新。

definitions.en: to have a strong wish or hope to do or have something

examples.en: Few people who aspire to fame ever achieve it.
examples.zh: 追求显达名声的人很少能如愿以偿。

examples.en: As a child, he aspired to be a great writer.
examples.zh: 孩提时他渴望成为一名伟大的作家。

examples.en: She has the kind of lifestyle most of us aspire to.
The majority of people don't aspire to greatness - they just want to be normal.
From a young age, he aspired to be an Olympic medal winner.
He quickly reached standards of musicianship that the rest of us can only aspire to.
She's just a young soap opera actress but she aspires to great Hollywood roles.

definitions.en: to judge or decide the amount, value, quality, or importance of something

examples.en: The insurers will need to assess the flood damage.
examples.zh: 保险公司需要对洪水造成的损失进行估定。

examples.en: They assessed the cost of the flood damage at £2,500.
examples.zh: 他们估定洪水造成的损失为2500英镑。

examples.en: Exams are not the only means of assessing a student's ability.
examples.zh: 考试并不是评定学生能力的唯一手段。

examples.en: It's too early to assess the long-term consequences of the two countries' union.
examples.zh: 现在判定两国联盟的长期影响还为时过早。

examples.en: We need to assess whether the project is worth doing.
examples.zh: 我们需要评估一下这个项目是否值得去做。

examples.en: Under the tutelage of Professor Roberts, the 900 delegates assessed and discussed the social market economy.
The rate of breakdown of muscle protein was assessed.
We expect the new scheme for assessing claims to come into operation early next year.
Authorities are assessing damage caused by last night's stormy weather.
They couldn't agree the best way to assess their students.

definitions.en: to help

examples.en: You will be expected to assist the editor with the selection of illustrations for the book.
examples.zh: 希望你能帮助编辑为本书挑选插图。

examples.en: assist in The army arrived to assist in the search.
examples.zh: 军队赶到协助搜寻。

examples.en: assist with They are looking for volunteers to assist with passing out meals.
I have cooperated fully with the police and am simply assisting them with their inquiries.
Migrating birds make use of airstreams to assist them on their long journey south.
Yasmin assisted in the preparation of this article.
Emergency crews, assisted by the army, are searching for survivors.
The doctor performed a small operation to assist the patient's breathing.
The photographers could face charges of manslaughter and failure to assist the victims of the crash.

definitions.en: in ball sports, an occasion when a player touches the ball and this leads to another player on the same team scoring a goal or point
definitions.zh: （篮球、足球等的）助攻;（棒球等的）助杀

examples.en: He has the most assists in the Premier League this season.
He set an NBA record for most assists by a rookie.

definitions.en: to accept something to be true without question or proof
definitions.zh: 假定，假设;臆断，想当然地认为

examples.en: We mustn't assume the suspects' guilt.
examples.zh: 我们千万不能臆断犯罪嫌疑人有罪。

examples.en: I assumed (that) you knew each other because you went to the same school.
examples.zh: 你们不是上的同一所学校嘛？我还以为你们两个认识呢。

examples.en: Let's assume that they're coming and make plans on that basis.
examples.zh: 我们还是假定他们会来，并以此来作出计划。

examples.en: We can't assume the suspects to be guilty simply because they've decided to remain silent.
examples.zh: 我们不能仅仅因为疑犯选择保持沉默就断定他们有罪。

examples.en: If you haven't heard by Friday, assume I'm not coming.
She was young and she was wearing student-type clothes so I assumed she was studying here.
There was a knock at the door. Now Jan knew her mother had promised to visit, so she assumed it was her.
They were chatting quite amiably on the phone last night so I assumed everything was okay.
I assumed things had gone well for him as he had a big grin on his face.

definitions.en: to pretend to have a different name or be someone you are not, or to express a feeling falsely

examples.en: Moving to a different town, he assumed a false name.
examples.zh: 搬家到了另外一个城镇后，他用了个假名字。

examples.en: He assumed a look of indifference but I knew how he felt.
examples.zh: 他装出一副无所谓的样子，但我明白他的内心感受。

examples.en: assume an identity During the investigation, two detectives assumed the identities of antiques dealers.
examples.zh: 在案件调查中，两名侦探假装成古董商。

definitions.en: to take or begin to have responsibility or control, sometimes without the right to do so, or to begin to have a characteristic
definitions.zh: 承担，担任；僭取，夺取；呈现，具有

examples.en: She is likely to assume the position of team captain next season.
I didn't want to assume the risk of trying to fix the car myself.
The new president assumes office at midnight tonight.
examples.zh: 新总统在今晚午夜就职。

examples.en: The issue has assumed considerable political proportions (= has become a big political problem).
examples.zh: 这已经成为一个很大的政治问题。

examples.en: assume control The terrorists assumed control of the plane and forced it to land in the desert.
examples.zh: 恐怖分子控制了飞机，并使其迫降在沙漠中。

examples.en: assume responsibility If you leave your belongings here, we cannot assume responsibility for what happens to them.

definitions.en: a promise

examples.en: She gave me her assurance (that) she would sign the contract immediately.
examples.zh: 她向我保证她会马上签署合同。

examples.en: Despite the government's repeated assurances to the contrary, taxation has risen over the past decade.
examples.zh: 尽管政府一再保证不会增税，可在过去10年中税收还是提高了。

examples.en: Despite repeated assurances that the product is safe, many people have stopped buying it.
The fact that the number of deaths from cancer in the area has doubled surely gives the lie to official assurances of the safety of nuclear power.
The management gave their assurances that there would be no more job losses.
Despite assurances that the vaccine was safe, many parents chose to ignore government advice.
The council has given assurances that the new building development will not encroach on public land.

definitions.en: confidence

examples.en: He spoke with calm assurance.
examples.zh: 他说话时冷静而自信。

definitions.en: a type of insurance against events that will certainly happen, such as death, not those that may happen, such as illness, fire, or having your property stolen
to tell someone confidently that something is true, especially so that they do not worry
definitions.zh: （尤指为消除其忧虑而）确定地对…说，向…保证，使确信;让…放心

examples.en: assure sb of sth The unions assured the new owners of the workers' loyalty to the company.
examples.zh: 工会向新企业主保证，工人们对公司是忠诚的。

examples.en: rest assured You can rest assured (= feel confident) that I shall be there as promised.
examples.zh: 你放心，我答应了就一定会到的。

examples.en: "Don't worry, your car will be ready tomorrow," the mechanic assured him.
examples.zh: “别担心，你的车明天就可以修好，”修理工向他保证。

examples.en: She assured him (that) the car would be ready the next day.
examples.zh: 她向他保证说他的车第二天就能修好。

examples.en: The prime minister assured the electorate taxes would not be increased after the election.
examples.zh: 首相向选民保证选举后不会增税。

examples.en: The police have assured the public that the escaped prisoners will not evade recapture for long.
The Foreign Secretary assured anti-federalist MPs that he would not agree to anything which limited British sovereignty.
She assured me that my fears were baseless.
"Rest assured, Mrs. Cooper" said the police officer. "We will find your son for you."
Breaking your leg isn't funny , I can assure you.

definitions.en: to cause something to be certain

examples.en: The play's popularity has been assured by the critics' rave reviews.
examples.zh: 评论家们的赞誉确保了这部剧的走红。

examples.en: Journalists may enter the danger zone but unfortunately we cannot assure their safety.
A last-minute deal assured the company's future.
A second goal moments from the end assured us of a place in the final.
Excellent exam results meant that she was assured of a place at university.
An anonymous donation assured the museum's long-term future.

definitions.en: (of an organization) to promise to pay an amount of money to a person or their family if that person becomes ill, gets injured, or dies, in return for small regular payments
in a way that is very surprising

examples.en: She did astonishingly well in her exams.
examples.zh: 她考试考得出奇地好。

examples.en: Astonishingly, I've never visited the British Museum in all the years I've lived here.
examples.zh: 令人惊讶的是，我在这里生活了这些年，却从来没有参观过大英博物馆。

examples.en: The cello sonata was astonishingly beautiful.
It’s been, astonishingly, five years since my last visit.
She has an astonishingly vivid memory.
Even as a child he was astonishingly inventive.

definitions.en: away from the correct path or correct way of doing something
definitions.zh: 偏离正轨地；迷路地；离开正道地；误入歧途地

examples.en: The letter must have gone astray in the post.
examples.zh: 这封信一定是在邮递过程中丢失了。

examples.en: I was led astray by an out-of-date map.
examples.zh: 我被一张旧地图引入歧途。

examples.en: Her parents worried that she might be led astray (= encouraged to behave badly) by her unsuitable friends.
examples.zh: 她父母担心她会因为交友不当而走上歪路。

definitions.en: relating to atoms

examples.en: atomic structure/nuclei
examples.zh: 原子结构／核

examples.en: atomic energy/power
examples.zh: 原子能

examples.en: At the nuclear reprocessing plant they extract plutonium from spent atomic fuel rods.
Electrons and protons are atomic particles.
Many servicemen suffered radiation sickness after the early atomic tests.
He read about the atomic bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki.
They researched cancer-mortality rates for survivors of atomic blasts.

definitions.en: on or at the top of

examples.en: She sat atop a two-metre wall.
examples.zh: 她坐在一堵两米高的墙上。

definitions.en: to reach or succeed in getting something

examples.en: He has attained the highest grade in his music exams.
examples.zh: 他在音乐考试中得了最高分。

examples.en: We need to identify the best ways of attaining our objectives/goals.
examples.zh: 我们需要确定达到目标的最佳办法。

examples.en: India attained independence in 1947, after decades of struggle.
examples.zh: 经过几十年的斗争，印度于1947年获得独立。

definitions.en: to show, say, or prove that something exists or is true

examples.en: Thousands of people came out onto the streets to attest their support for the democratic opposition party.
examples.zh: 数以千计的人走上街头声援主张民主的反对党。

examples.en: attest to The number of old German cars still on the road attests to the excellence of their manufacture.
examples.zh: 在马路上仍可以看到相当数量的老款德国车，这表明其制造工艺十分精良。

examples.en: As his career attests, he is a world-class tennis player.
examples.zh: 他是世界一流的网球选手，其职业生涯便是最好例证。

examples.en: The thing is — and my wife will attest to this — I am so insensitive I probably didn't notice.
As any serious gamer will attest, online experiences can have real-life consequences.
The exporter's signature may need to be attested by a witness.
Any deletion or alteration made before the affidavit is sworn shall be attested by the commissioner.

definitions.en: a formal statement that you make and officially say is true
to make an official examination of the accounts of a business and produce a report
to go to a class or educational course for pleasure or interest, without being tested or receiving a grade at the end

examples.en: As a senior citizen, he is allowed to audit university classes.
examples.zh: 作为一个老年人，他得到准许可以旁听大学的课程。

definitions.en: an official examination of the accounts of a business

examples.en: The company has an audit at the end of each financial year.
examples.zh: 这家公司在每个财政年度的年末都要进行一次审计。

definitions.en: to increase the size or value of something by adding something to it

examples.en: He would have to find work to augment his income.
examples.zh: 他可能不得不找活儿干来增加收入。

definitions.en: showing that you are confident, in control, and expect to be respected and obeyed
definitions.zh: 专断的；命令式的；权威性的

examples.en: She has an authoritative manner that at times is almost arrogant.
examples.zh: 她爱发号施令，有时几乎到了盛气凌人的地步。

examples.en: The book is an authoritative account of the Second World War.
examples.zh: 这本书对第二次世界大战作了非常权威的记述。

definitions.en: giving help or support, especially to a more important person or thing

examples.en: auxiliary staff/nurses
examples.zh: 辅助人员／助理护士

definitions.en: a person whose job is to give help or support to other workers

examples.en: semi-skilled auxiliaries
examples.zh: 半熟练的辅助工

definitions.en: a verb that gives grammatical information not given by the main verb of a sentence

examples.en: In the sentence "she has finished her book", "has" is an auxiliary.
examples.zh: 在句子she has finished her book中，has是助动词。

definitions.en: a large amount of ice, snow, and rock falling quickly down the side of a mountain

examples.en: We were swamped by an avalanche of letters/phone calls/complaints.
examples.zh: 大批的来信／电话／投诉让我们疲于应付。

definitions.en: (a person or thing that causes) a feeling of strong dislike or of not wishing to do something
definitions.zh: 厌恶，反感;讨厌的人（或事物）

examples.en: I felt an instant aversion to his parents.
examples.zh: 我立刻对他的父母产生了反感。

examples.en: She has a deep aversion to getting up in the morning.
examples.zh: 早上她很不愿意起床。

examples.en: Greed is my pet aversion (= the thing I dislike most of all).
examples.zh: 我最痛恨贪得无厌。

definitions.en: difficult to use, do, or deal with
definitions.zh: 难用的；棘手的；难处理的，难对付的

examples.en: It's an awkward corner, so take it slowly.
examples.zh: 这个转弯不好过，开慢点儿。

examples.en: Some of the questions were rather awkward.
examples.zh: 有些问题很难回答。

examples.en: It was an awkward ascent, but we reached the top eventually.
examples.zh: 那段上坡路很难走，但最后我们还是爬到了山顶。

examples.en: My car's quite awkward to drive.
examples.zh: 我的车很难开。

examples.en: He's an awkward customer (= a difficult person to deal with).
examples.zh: 他是个很难应付的顾客。

examples.en: If he persists in asking awkward questions, then send him to the boss.
When two of your best friends argue it puts you in a very awkward position.
He fielded some awkward questions very skilfully.
If they ask any awkward questions, just let me do the talking.
It's going to be awkward, to say the least.

definitions.en: causing problems, worry, or embarrassment
definitions.zh: 令人尴尬的；使人难堪的

examples.en: an awkward position/situation
examples.zh: 令人尴尬的位置／局面

examples.en: There followed an awkward silence while we all tried to think of something to say.
examples.zh: 然后是一段令人尴尬的沉默，我们都绞尽脑汁地想说点儿什么。

examples.en: They'd chosen an awkward time to call as I'd just got into the bath.
examples.zh: 他们偏偏在我刚开始洗澡的时候来电话，真不是时候。

examples.en: The police asked some awkward questions about where the money had come from.
examples.zh: 警方围绕钱的来源问了一些让人很难作答的问题。

examples.en: I always feel awkward when I'm with Chris - he's so difficult to talk to.
examples.zh: 我跟克里斯在一起总感到别扭——与他这人交谈太费劲儿。

examples.en: He seemed a little awkward when I first met him.
examples.zh: 我第一次见他的时候，他有点儿放不开。

examples.en: He's very deft at handling awkward situations.
Somehow she managed to fend off the awkward questions.
It's always awkward when you meet someone you know, but you can't remember their name.
Her remarks produced an awkward silence.
After a long, awkward pause someone asked a question.

definitions.en: intentionally not helpful
definitions.zh: 难相处的；不合作的；故意为难的

examples.en: Just stop being so awkward and help me push the car, will you!
examples.zh: 别再跟我闹别扭了，快帮我推推车，好不好！

definitions.en: moving in a way that is not natural, relaxed, or attractive
definitions.zh: 笨拙的;别扭的;不灵活的;难看的

examples.en: His movements were slow and awkward.
examples.zh: 他的动作迟缓笨拙。

definitions.en: the line of bones down the centre of the back that provides support for the body

examples.en: She stood with her backbone rigid.
examples.zh: 她直挺挺地站着。

definitions.en: the most important part of something, providing support for everything else:
definitions.zh: 农业是这个国家的经济支柱。

examples.en: Farming is the backbone of the country's economy.
examples.zh: 农业是这个国家的经济支柱。

examples.en: Will he have the backbone to tell them what he thinks?
examples.zh: 他有胆量把自己的想法告诉他们吗？

definitions.en: someone who gives financial support to something

examples.en: We need financial backers for the project.
examples.zh: 我们需要有人资助这项工程。

definitions.en: a large number of things that you should have done before and must do now

examples.en: I've got a huge backlog of work to do.
examples.zh: 我有大量积压的工作要做。

definitions.en: a measurement of the amount of information that can be sent between computers, through a phone line, etc.

examples.en: The system will handle signals that need high bandwidth, for instance those that encode TV pictures.
examples.zh: 这个系统可以处理需要高带宽的信号，比如编码压缩过的电视图像信号。

examples.en: high-bandwidth services/applications
examples.zh: 高带宽服务／应用

examples.en: I don't have the bandwidth to read stuff I know isn't going to lead to productive discussion.
examples.zh: 我没有精力去看那种没什么正面意义的东西。

examples.en: If people misreport or misrepresent my arguments, I am using more of my bandwidth to correct the misreporting.
examples.zh: 如果我的论点被误传或歪曲了，那我会花多一点精力去加以纠正。

examples.en: Tasks like simultaneously streaming several different video signals over a wireless connection require more bandwidth.
Once software begins to use the extra bandwidth, you'll likely be glad you have it.
There's enough bandwidth to let you surf, stream and down- load all at the same time.

definitions.en: the range of frequencies used to send information over a distance using phone wires
a place where drinks, especially alcoholic drinks, are sold and drunk, or the area in such a place where the person serving the drinks stands

examples.en: They noticed him going into the hotel bar.
examples.zh: 他们看见他进了宾馆酒吧间。

examples.en: at the bar There weren't any free tables, so I sat at the bar.
examples.zh: 没有空桌子了，我只好坐在吧台旁。

examples.en: behind the bar Why don't you ask the guy behind the bar (= serving drinks there)?
examples.zh: 你怎么不问一问吧台里的那个服务员？

examples.en: As a general rule, we don't allow children in the bar.
You'll find her in the bar almost every evening about six o'clock.
The hotel bar is open to non-residents.
She roamed around America for a year, working in bars and restaurants.
She fell into a conversation with a man at the bar.

definitions.en: a long, thin, straight piece of metal or wood

examples.en: The gorilla rattled the bars of its cage.
examples.zh: 大猩猩把笼子的铁栅栏弄得吱嘎吱嘎响。

examples.en: a bar of soap
examples.zh: 一块肥皂

examples.en: a chocolate bar
examples.zh: 一条巧克力棒

examples.en: He was chomping away on a bar of chocolate.
Some maniac was running down the street waving a massive metal bar.
a candy bar
"Would you get me a bar of chocolate from the kitchen?" "Another one?!"
Loop the rope over the bar.

definitions.en: one of the small equal parts into which a piece of music is divided, containing a fixed number of beats

examples.en: Normally the emphasis falls on the first beat of the bar.
beats in a bar Waltzes have three beats in a bar.
examples.zh: 华尔兹每小节有3拍。

definitions.en: a stripe
something that prevents a particular event or development from happening
definitions.zh: 阻碍某事发生或发展的东西

examples.en: a bar to A lack of formal education is no bar to becoming rich.
examples.zh: 没有接受正规教育并不妨碍致富。

definitions.en: to prevent something or someone from doing something or going somewhere, or to not allow something

examples.en: The rule bars the use of evidence secured through an illegal search.
bar someone from something The incident led to him being barred from the country.
bar something to someone Most of the network of trails are barred to private vehicles.
bar someone's way I tried to push past her but she barred my way (= stood in front of me and prevented me from getting past).
examples.zh: 我试图从她旁边挤过去，但她还是挡住了我的去路。
