definitions.en: to put bars across something, especially to keep it closed

examples.en: We barred the door to stop anyone getting into the room.
examples.zh: 我们闩上门以防有人进入房间。

definitions.en: except

examples.en: Everyone is leaving the village, bar the very old and ill.
examples.zh: 除了年老体弱者，其他人都要离开村庄。

definitions.en: lawyers who are allowed to argue a case in a higher court

examples.en: She studied law and was called to the Bar in 2013.
After being admitted to the bar in 1998, he represented other people who had got into trouble with the regulators.

definitions.en: a long pole, fence, wall, or natural feature, such as a mountain or sea, that stops people from going somewhere

examples.en: Barriers have been erected all along the route the Pope will take.
examples.zh: 在教皇将要经过的路上，沿途都设置了路障。

examples.en: The mountains acted as a natural barrier to the spread of the disease.
examples.zh: 群山成为了阻止该疾病扩散的天然屏障。

examples.en: Passengers are requested to show their tickets at the barrier.
examples.zh: 旅客需要在检票口出示车票。

examples.en: The land drops (away) sharply behind the barrier.
At the given signal, the group rushed forward to the barrier.
The barrier absorbed the main impact of the crash.
They've put barriers up to stop people (from) getting through.
The talks were meant to break down barriers between the two groups.

definitions.en: anything that prevents people from being together or understanding each other

examples.en: Despite the language barrier (= not speaking the same language), they soon became good friends.
examples.zh: 尽管语言上存在障碍，他们还是很快就成为了好朋友。

examples.en: Shyness is one of the biggest barriers to making friends (= something that makes this difficult).
examples.zh: 害羞是交友的最大障碍之一。

definitions.en: a game in which prizes can be won by matching numbers on a card with those chosen by chance
definitions.zh: 宾戈游戏（一种赌博游戏）

definitions.en: an expression of surprise and, usually, pleasure, for example when something happens successfully

examples.en: I was just about to borrow some money when bingo! - the cheque arrived.
examples.zh: 我正打算去借钱时，嘿——支票到了。

examples.en: I was just about to give up waiting when bingo! - he turned up.
examples.zh: 我正要放弃，不想再等的时候，嘿，他来了。

definitions.en: not able to see or not noticing what is around you

examples.en: The room was completely dark and I fumbled blindly for the door.
examples.zh: 房间里一片漆黑，我摸索着找门。

definitions.en: not thinking about or understanding what you are doing

examples.en: They just blindly followed orders.
examples.zh: 他们只是盲目地服从命令。

definitions.en: to swell up, or to make someone or something swollen

examples.en: If I eat it, my stomach bloats up.
examples.zh: 我要是吃了它就会胀肚。

definitions.en: something that stops something else passing through, or the act of stopping something passing through

examples.en: His death was caused by a blockage in one of his arteries.
examples.zh: 他死于动脉阻塞。

definitions.en: used to express anger or to emphasize what you are saying in a slightly rude way

examples.en: I've had a bloody awful week.
examples.zh: 我这个星期该死的糟透了。

examples.en: It's a bloody disgrace that some war widows don't get a decent pension.
examples.zh: 有些因战争而守寡的妇女没有得到数额合理的抚恤金，这种事真他妈丢人。

examples.en: Don't be a bloody idiot!
examples.zh: 别他妈像个傻瓜似的！

examples.en: This computer's bloody useless! It's always going wrong.
examples.zh: 这台电脑他妈什么用也没有！总是出问题。

examples.en: Don't you tell me what to do! I'll do what I bloody well like in my own house.
examples.zh: 不要告诉我该干什么！在我自己家里我他妈想干什么就干什么。

examples.en: I had a bloody good time last night.
examples.zh: 我昨晚玩得真他妈痛快。

examples.en: Life would be bloody boring if nothing ever went wrong.
examples.zh: 如果什么都是顺顺当当的，那生活也太他妈沉闷了。

examples.en: That sod Wilkins, excuse my French, has taken my bloody parking space.
You stupid bloody fool!
You trod on my bloody foot!
That car's been nothing but bloody trouble since I got it.
How did Pearson make the team? He's bloody useless.

definitions.en: covered with or full of blood

examples.en: a bloody nose
examples.zh: 流血的鼻子

examples.en: It was a long and bloody battle and many men were killed.
examples.zh: 那是一场漫长而残酷的战斗，死了许多人。

examples.en: There is a danger that America's metaphoric 'war on drugs' may turn into a bloody reality.
A bloody civil war followed the proclamation of an independent state.
He told the doctor he had been passing bloody stools.
This is the latest act of terrorism in a long-standing and bloody campaign of violence.
The songs describe the bloody battles between the clans.

definitions.en: to make something bloody

examples.en: The first punch bloodied his nose.
examples.zh: 第一拳就把他的鼻子打出了血。

definitions.en: A blunt pencil, knife, etc. is not sharp and therefore not able to write, cut, etc. well.
saying what you think without trying to be polite or considering other people's feelings
definitions.zh: 直率的;生硬的;直截了当的

examples.en: I'll be blunt - that last piece of work you did was terrible.
examples.zh: 我就直说了——你创作的最后一件作品糟糕透了。

definitions.en: to make something less sharp

examples.en: My recent bad experience has blunted my enthusiasm for travel.
examples.zh: 最近的倒霉经历已经使我对旅游的热情减弱了不少。

definitions.en: a large cigarette or cigar containing the drug cannabis

examples.en: Kids are coming across blunts, pills, other illegal drugs and booze in their classrooms, lockers, bathrooms and playfields.
Smoking blunts is becoming a common practice with teenagers.
They would just sit for hours at a time, smoke as many blunts as their system could handle.
Officers observed two partially smoked marijuana blunts in the room.
"I tell students that joints, cigars and blunts have seven times more tar than cigarettes," he said.

definitions.en: false, not real, or not legal
definitions.zh: 假的;假冒的，伪造的;非法的

examples.en: On investigation, his claim was found to be bogus.
examples.zh: 调查发现，他的说法并不属实。

examples.en: She produced some bogus documents to support her application.
examples.zh: 她制作了一些伪造的文件来支持她的申请。

definitions.en: silly or stupid

examples.en: She must be bonkers to do that.
examples.zh: 她那么做真是蠢透了。

definitions.en: an extra amount of money that is given to you as a present or reward for good work as well as the money you were expecting

examples.en: a productivity bonus
examples.zh: 生产效益奖金

examples.en: a Christmas bonus
examples.zh: 圣诞节奖金

examples.en: The company used to give discretionary bonus payments.
examples.zh: 公司过去常常会酌情发放一些奖金。

examples.en: I love the job, and it's an added bonus that it's so close to home.
examples.zh: 我喜欢这份工作，它还有个优点是离家很近。

examples.en: The salary was set at £12,000, plus a bonus if the company had a good year.
Good weather is an added bonus but the real appeal of the area is the fantastic variety of landscape.
Profit sharing is when the profits of a company are given to staff in the form of an additional bonus payment.
The DVD version of the film features a bonus disc containing extra footage and interviews with the actors.
The new contract gives workers less salary, but bonuses for hitting efficiency and sales targets.

definitions.en: to (cause to) move up or away after hitting a surface

examples.en: The ball bounced off the goalpost and into the net.
examples.zh: 球击中门柱弹进了球网。

examples.en: She bounced the ball quickly.
examples.zh: 她快速地拍球。

examples.en: Her bag bounced (= moved up and down) against her side as she walked.
examples.zh: 她走起路来身侧的挎包一颠一颠的。

examples.en: The children had broken the bed by bouncing (= jumping up and down) on it.
examples.zh: 孩子们老在床上蹦，把床蹦坏了。

examples.en: He bounced the baby (= lifted it up and down) on his knee.
examples.zh: 他把婴儿放在膝上颠着玩。

examples.en: Television pictures from all over the world are bounced off satellites (= are sent to and returned from them).
examples.zh: 世界各地的电视画面都通过卫星转播。

examples.en: Tom bounced in, smiling broadly.
examples.zh: 汤姆蹦蹦跳跳地进来了，脸上笑开了花。

examples.en: He wasn't even trying to score - the ball just bounced off the jammy beggar's head into the goal.
He thought the ball had bounced on the line, but the umpire said it was out.
Martha threw back the covers and bounced out of bed.
He bounced into the office, full of the joys of spring.
One of the boys stood there, bouncing a basketball.

definitions.en: to (cause a cheque to) not be paid or accepted by a bank because there is no money in the account
definitions.zh: （因账上存款不敷）（使支票）拒付，退回

examples.en: I had to pay a penalty fee when my cheque bounced.
examples.zh: 由于支票被银行拒付，我不得不付一笔罚金。

examples.en: To my horror the bank bounced the cheque.
examples.zh: 令我震惊的是，银行拒付了支票。

definitions.en: If an email that you send bounces or is bounced, it comes back to you because the address is wrong or there is a computer problem.
definitions.zh: （电子邮件）被退回，发送（电子邮件）失败

definitions.en: the act of bouncing, or the quality that makes something able to bounce

examples.en: In tennis you have to hit the ball before its second bounce.
examples.zh: 打网球时必须在球第二次落地之前出手击打。

examples.en: This shampoo will give your hair bounce (= make it look attractively thick) and shine.
examples.zh: 这种洗发精会使头发富有弹性和光泽。

definitions.en: an improvement or change to a higher level, for example in how popular a politician or party is

examples.en: His speech to the convention may have given him a bounce.
examples.zh: 他在大会做的演讲可能已让他一战成名。

examples.en: The city has seen a bounce in homicide.
examples.zh: 这座城市杀人凶案数反弹了。

examples.en: "I think we are getting close to a bounce in the market," Paulsen added.
Typically, the economy gets a bit of a bounce coming out of a recession.
In early December, polls showed a bounce for the candidate.

definitions.en: either of two symbols put around a word, phrase, or sentence in a piece of writing to show that what is between them should be considered as separate from the main part

examples.en: Biographical information is included in brackets.
examples.zh: 括号里给出了作者的个人信息。

examples.en: You should include the date of publication in round brackets after the title.
examples.zh: 你应该在题目后面的圆括号里注明出版日期。

examples.en: The country's initials are given in brackets after the figure.
She'd put brackets around the clause.
What does the figure in brackets refer to?

definitions.en: a group with fixed upper and lower limits

examples.en: They were both surgeons in a high income bracket.
examples.zh: 他俩都属于高收入阶层的外科医生。

examples.en: Most of our students are in the 18–22 age bracket.
examples.zh: 我们大多数的学生都属于18至22岁这个年龄段。

examples.en: Her pay rise brought her into a new tax bracket.
examples.zh: 她薪水的增加使她进入了新的纳税等级。

definitions.en: a piece of metal, wood, or plastic, usually L-shaped, that is fastened to a wall and used to support something such as a shelf
definitions.zh: （通常呈L形、固定在墙上的）托架，支架，小壁架

definitions.en: a diagram (= drawing) showing a series of games played in a tournament (= sports competition)

examples.en: In some tournaments, the full bracket is determined before the first game.
examples.zh: 有些锦标赛的总赛程表在第一场比赛前就已经确定好了。

definitions.en: to put brackets around words, phrases, numbers, etc.

examples.en: I've bracketed the parts of the text that could be omitted.
examples.zh: 我已把文章可省略的部分用括号括起来了。

definitions.en: If you bracket two or more things or people, you consider them to be similar or connected to each other.
definitions.zh: 将…相提并论；把…归入同一类

examples.en: He's often bracketed with the romantic poets of this period although this does not reflect the range of his work.
examples.zh: 人们常常把他与这个时期的浪漫诗人相提并论，尽管这并不能反映出他的作品的涉猎范围。

definitions.en: an act of breaking a law, promise, agreement, or relationship

examples.en: They felt that our discussions with other companies constituted a breach of/in our agreement.
examples.zh: 他们认为我们与别的公司谈判违反了我们之间的协定。

examples.en: He was sued for breach of contract.
examples.zh: 他因为违反合同而被起诉。

examples.en: There have been serious security breaches (= breaks in our security system).
examples.zh: 安全体系出了大问题。

examples.en: Ten percent of this group were in persistent trouble for minor breaches of the peace.
The theatre was in breach of the safety laws for having no fire doors.
examples.zh: 由于没有消防门，这家剧院违反了安全法规。

definitions.en: a hole that is made in a wall or in another structure being used for protection during an attack
definitions.zh: （攻击墙或防御工事时打出的）缺口

examples.en: A cannon ball had made a breach in their castle walls.
examples.zh: 一颗炮弹在他们城堡的墙上打出了一个缺口。

definitions.en: to break a law, promise, agreement, or relationship

examples.en: They breached the agreement they had made with their employer.
examples.zh: 他们违反了曾与雇主达成的协议。

definitions.en: to make an opening in a wall or fence, especially in order to attack someone or something behind it
definitions.zh: （尤指为从背后突袭）在…上打开缺口

examples.en: Their defences were easily breached.
examples.zh: 他们的防线轻而易举就被突破了。

definitions.en: a violent escape, especially by a group, from prison

examples.en: There has been a mass breakout from one of Germany's top security jails.
examples.zh: 德国一所戒备森严的监狱发生了集体越狱事件。

definitions.en: used in connection with smaller groups that separate from a meeting to discuss a particular issue, before returning to the main meeting

examples.en: a breakout session/meeting

definitions.en: using only a few words or lasting only a short time

examples.en: His essays are models of clarity and brevity.
examples.zh: 他的散文简洁、清晰，堪称典范。

examples.en: Brevity is, in almost everything, a virtue.
examples.zh: 几乎在任何情况下，简洁都是一种美德。

definitions.en: a rectangular block of hard material used for building walls and houses

examples.en: The chimney was made of bricks.
examples.zh: 烟囱是用砖砌的。

examples.en: red-brick houses
examples.zh: 红砖房

examples.en: He was so embarrassed - his face went brick-red (= a dark red).
examples.zh: 他尴尬极了，脸涨得紫红紫红的。

examples.en: The house has a small backyard, surrounded by a high brick wall.
The boundary of the car park is delineated by a low brick wall.
The plaster on the walls has been removed to expose the original bricks underneath.
We saw rioters pelting police with bricks and bottles.
A group of tiny brick houses is tucked away behind the factory.

definitions.en: an electronic device, such as a smartphone, that cannot be used at all
definitions.zh: 砖头（指无法使用的电子设备，如智能手机）

examples.en: Without a fast connection, your tablet turns into a brick.
examples.zh: 没有快速的连接，你的平板电脑就会变成一块砖头。

definitions.en: a very helpful and kind person who can be trusted

examples.en: Thanks for bringing all that food along to the party, Tony. You're a brick!
examples.zh: 托尼，谢谢你带那些食物来参加聚会，你真是个好心人！

definitions.en: for a short time

examples.en: We chatted briefly about the weather.
examples.zh: 我们聊了几句天气。

examples.en: She appears briefly in the new Bond film.
He was briefly famous in his twenties but then sank into obscurity.
He appears briefly towards the end of the film in a cameo role as a priest.
She worked briefly as a journalist, but attracted attention as a writer with her stories of village life.
He made his living briefly as a newspaper reporter before getting into politics.

definitions.en: using few words or without giving a lot of details

examples.en: Briefly, the company needs to cut its expenditure.
examples.zh: 简言之，公司需要缩减开支。

definitions.en: full of light, shining

examples.en: bright sunshine
examples.zh: 明媚的阳光

examples.en: The rooms were bright and airy.
examples.zh: 这些房间都明亮通风。

examples.en: The lights are too bright in here - they're hurting my eyes.
examples.zh: 这里的灯太亮，把我的眼睛刺痛了。

examples.en: A bright star was shining in the East.
examples.zh: 东方闪烁着一颗明亮的星。

examples.en: When she looked up her eyes were bright with tears.
examples.zh: 她抬头望时眼睛里闪烁着泪光。

examples.en: In 2009 I moved to New York, attracted by the bright lights (= the promise of excitement) of the city.
examples.zh: 我为纽约多姿多彩的城市生活所吸引，于2009年移居到此。

examples.en: The light was so bright that I had to cover my eyes.
The office is quite bright and airy - it's a pleasant working environment.
It was a bright moonlit night.
The lake sparkled in the bright sunlight.
He screwed his eyes tight shut against the bright light.

definitions.en: strong in colour

examples.en: Leslie always wears bright colours.
examples.zh: 莱斯莉总是穿颜色鲜艳的衣服。

examples.en: He said hello and I felt my face turn bright red.
examples.zh: 他跟我打招呼，我感到自己的脸变得通红。

examples.en: a bright shade of green
examples.zh: 鲜艳的绿色

examples.en: She painted her nails a bright red.
You can't mistake their house - it's got a bright yellow front door.
She looks best in bright, vibrant colours, like red and pink.
Jazz the dress up with some bright accessories.
His eyes were bright blue with a roguish twinkle in them.

definitions.en: intelligent and quick to learn

examples.en: They were bright children, always asking questions.
examples.zh: 这些孩子很聪明，总爱问问题。

examples.en: She was enthusiastic and full of bright ideas and suggestions.
examples.zh: 她很有热情，满脑子好主意和建议。

examples.en: Da Silva plays a bright, lonely student from New York, adrift in small-town Arizona.
She's fresh out of/from university and very bright.
He's very good-looking but not terribly bright.
She wants a man who is bright, attractive and funny as well, which is asking the impossible in my opinion.
He's a bright enough student - he just lacks motivation.

definitions.en: full of hope for success or happiness

examples.en: You're very bright and cheerful this morning.
examples.zh: 你今天早上心情不错。

examples.en: Things are starting to look brighter for British businesses.
examples.zh: 英国工商业界情况开始有了好转。

examples.en: She's an excellent student with a bright future.
examples.zh: 她是个非常优秀的学生，前途一片光明。

examples.en: The receptionist had a bright set smile on his face, but I could tell that he was bored.
The experts are painting a brighter picture of the state of the economy.
With no job or qualifications, the future didn't look bright for James.
The customers loved her bright and cheerful manner.
Look on the bright side - no one was badly hurt.

definitions.en: A car's brights are its headlights (= the powerful lights at the front) when they are brightest and aimed straight ahead:

examples.en: You can't just go around driving with your brights on.

definitions.en: delicate and easily broken

examples.en: As you get older your bones become increasingly brittle.
examples.zh: 随着年龄增长，人的骨头变得越来越脆。

examples.en: The pond was covered in a brittle layer of ice.
examples.zh: 池塘上结了薄薄的一层冰。

definitions.en: A brittle voice or laugh sounds sharp and slightly nervous.

examples.en: She gave a brittle laugh and turned away.
examples.zh: 她冷笑了一声，转身走了。

examples.en: His voice was so brittle it seemed about to crack completely.

definitions.en: a person who buys and sells foreign money, shares in companies, etc., for other people

examples.en: a commodity/insurance/mortgage broker
examples.zh: 商品／保险／抵押经纪人

examples.en: I called my broker for advice about investing in the stock market.
examples.zh: 我给我的经纪人打电话咨询股市投资的问题。

examples.en: During the war Wallas became a power broker in governmental circles.
examples.zh: 战争期间华莱士成了政界的权力经纪人。

definitions.en: to arrange something such as a deal, agreement, etc. between two or more groups or countries
definitions.zh: 作为经纪人（或中间人）安排（交易、协议等）

examples.en: The diplomats have failed in their attempts to broker a ceasefire.
examples.zh: 外交官们促成停火的尝试已告失败。

definitions.en: cruel, violent, and completely without feelings

examples.en: a brutal dictator
examples.zh: 残暴的独裁者

examples.en: He had presided over a brutal regime in which thousands of people had "disappeared".
examples.zh: 在他残暴的统治下，成千上万的人“失踪”了。

examples.en: He was imprisoned in 1945 for the brutal murder of a twelve-year-old girl.
examples.zh: 1945年，他因残忍杀害了一名12岁女孩而入狱。

examples.en: She spoke with brutal honesty - I was too old for the job.
examples.zh: 她毫不留情地直接告诉我，我年纪太大，不适合这份工作。

examples.en: This was a particularly brutal and cowardly attack.
The rebel army behaved in a brutal fashion.
The psychologist's attitude seemed far too casual, even brutal.
She wrote a thriller about a brutal serial killer.
The assault was premeditated and particularly brutal.

definitions.en: a rough and sometimes violent man

examples.en: Take your hands off me, you brute!
examples.zh: 把手松开，你这畜牲！

examples.en: Your dog's an ugly brute, isn't it?
examples.zh: 你那条狗是个丑陋的家伙，对吧？

examples.en: The oldest elephant was lame, poor brute.
examples.zh: 那头最老的大象腿跛了，可怜的家伙。

definitions.en: great physical force or strength:
definitions.zh: 最后她使出吃奶的力气将他推了出去。

examples.en: In the end she used brute force to push him out.
examples.zh: 最后她使出吃奶的力气将他推了出去。

definitions.en: a plan to show how much money a person or organization will earn and how much they will need or be able to spend

examples.en: The firm has drawn up a budget for the coming financial year.
examples.zh: 公司草拟了下一财政年度的预算方案。

examples.en: Libraries are finding it increasingly difficult to remain within (their) budget.
examples.zh: 图书馆感到想把支出维持在预算之内越来越难。

examples.en: an annual budget of £40 million
examples.zh: 每年4000万英镑的预算

examples.en: The school budget is going to be cut again this year.
The project went over budget because of a miscalculation at the planning stage.
She managed to complete her last film well within budget.
I propose that we wait until the budget has been announced before committing ourselves to any expenditure.
People on limited budgets should avoid travelling during the holiday season if they can.

definitions.en: to plan how much money you will spend on something

examples.en: An extra $20 million has been budgeted for schools this year.
examples.zh: 今年学校预算增加了2000万英镑。

examples.en: They simply said that they had not budgeted for pay increases this year.
More people are using home computers to help them plan and budget efficiently.
The financial director says he's budgeting for a full computer upgrade in the New Year.
Make sure you've budgeted carefully and know that you can actually afford the expense.
This price rise means that the bill is going to be much higher than we have budgeted for.
He is responsible for ensuring that project schedules are realistic and that time and money are properly budgeted.

definitions.en: very cheap

examples.en: a budget holiday/hotel/price
examples.zh: 廉价旅行度假／廉价宾馆／低廉的价格

definitions.en: in the UK, the official statement that the government makes about how much it will collect in taxes and spend on public services in the future
a small car, usually with no roof
definitions.zh: （为在崎岖路面行驶而设计的无顶）小汽车

examples.en: He continued to play golf, but had to move around the course in a buggy.
a golf/dune buggy
examples.zh: 高尔夫球车／沙丘汽车

examples.en: You have to try to steer a four-wheel-drive buggy with large tires through an obstacle course of dirt hills, small boulders, and mud.
Your bags are brought to the hotel from the gates in a buggy.

definitions.en: a light carriage (= a road vehicle pulled by horses that was used in the past) pulled by one horse

examples.en: Her brother drove her to school by horse and buggy every day.
The family had to cross the creek in their buggy.
He born in the era of the horse and buggy and lived long enough to see jet planes.

definitions.en: Computer programs that are buggy contain mistakes and do not work correctly.
definitions.zh: （计算机程序）因为有太多错误而无法正常工作的

examples.en: A hasty, buggy fix can be worse that the problem it is supposed to correct.
buggy code
examples.zh: 错误很多的代码

definitions.en: full of annoying bugs (= insects)

examples.en: It's too buggy to eat outside this time of year.
examples.zh: 每年这个时候蚊虫太多，不适合在室外吃饭。

definitions.en: something or someone that is very large

examples.en: She eased her large bulk out of the chair.
examples.zh: 她缓缓地将她那庞大的身躯从椅子上挪开。

examples.en: It was a document of surprising bulk.
examples.zh: 这是一份篇幅惊人的文件。

examples.en: The office buys paper in bulk to keep down costs.
examples.zh: 办公室批量买进纸张以减少成本。

examples.en: In fact, the bulk of the book is taken up with criticizing other works.
examples.zh: 事实上，这本书大部分篇幅都是在批评其他作品。

definitions.en: A bulky object takes up a lot of space, or is difficult to move or carry.

examples.en: Early mobile phones were bulky and expensive.
bulky equipment
examples.zh: 庞大的设备

examples.en: I hate carrying cash around in a bulky wallet.
He struggled to fit the bulky load into the compartment.
The box is light but bulky.
Security guards searched anyone wearing bulky clothes.
This winter's hot trend is bulky knits.

definitions.en: a small, metal object that is shot from a gun

examples.en: A bullet had lodged in the boy's leg.
examples.zh: 一颗子弹嵌进了男孩的腿里。

examples.en: The bullet went clean through his shoulder.
She cried out in pain as the bullet grazed her shoulder.
The bullet entered the body at chest height.
The houses in the village were pockmarked with bullet holes.
He was hit by a ricochet from a stray bullet.

definitions.en: a heavy load that you carry

examples.en: The little donkey struggled under its heavy burden.
examples.zh: 小毛驴驮着重物艰难前行。

examples.en: the burden of responsibility
examples.zh: 责任的重担

examples.en: My elderly mother worries that she's a burden to me.
examples.zh: 我年迈的母亲担心她成为我的拖累。

examples.en: Buying a house often places a large financial burden on young couples.
examples.zh: 买房子往往会让年轻夫妇背上沉重的经济负担。

examples.en: The war had put an insupportable financial burden on the country.
The irony of it is that the new tax system will burden those it was intended to help.
A huge burden was lifted from my shoulders when I told my parents about my problem.
Such a high increase will impose an undue burden on the local tax payer.
Not until this turbulent region can shuffle off the burdens of the past will it be able to settle peacefully into the community of nations.

definitions.en: to trouble someone with something difficult or unpleasant

examples.en: I don't want to burden you with my problems.
examples.zh: 我不想拿我的困难烦扰你。

definitions.en: to put a dead body into the ground

examples.en: His father is buried in the cemetery on the hill.
examples.zh: 他的父亲葬在山上的墓地里。

examples.en: The dog trotted off to bury its bone.
examples.zh: 那只狗颠儿颠儿地跑到一边去埋它的骨头。

examples.en: buried treasure
examples.zh: 埋藏的财宝

examples.en: If an avalanche strikes, skiers can be buried alive by snow.
examples.zh: 如果发生雪崩，滑雪者可能会被雪活埋。

examples.en: She buried both her parents last year.
examples.zh: 去年她的父母都过世了。

examples.en: No-one should have to bury their child.
A ceasefire has been called to allow the survivors to bury their dead.
He made a short speech at the graveside, then the body was finally buried.
Robin Hood asked to be buried where his arrow landed.
X marks the spot where the treasure is buried.
In AD 79 the city of Pompei was buried under a layer of ash seven metres deep.

definitions.en: to put something in a place where it is difficult or impossible to find or see

examples.en: I found the article buried away in the business section of the newspaper.
examples.zh: 我在报纸的商务版找到了这篇文章。

examples.en: She buried her face in her hands and began to sob.
examples.zh: 她双手掩面开始啜泣。

examples.en: He'd had to bury his pain over the years.
examples.zh: 多年来他不得不将痛苦埋在心底。

examples.en: Many of us have been hurt in relationships, and a frequent form of defence is to bury your feelings.
I found some old letters buried in a drawer.
This is a story of deceit and complicity by government and industry to bury the truth about the dangers of uranium mining.
Everyone knows that Friday is when you release news you want to bury, because Friday news gets reported in the little-read Saturday papers.
Many soldiers bury the feelings and memories of war when they get home.
Could she really entirely bury her anger about the media circus she had to endure?
Her constructive criticism and wise counselling helped me uncover buried feelings and emotions which I had long denied.

definitions.en: a set of wires, covered by plastic, that carries electricity, phone signals, etc.

examples.en: a length of cable
examples.zh: 一根电缆

examples.en: The road has been dug up in order to lay cables.
examples.zh: 道路已被挖开，以便铺设电缆。

examples.en: overhead power cables
examples.zh: 高架电缆

examples.en: This cable attaches the printer to the computer.
I tripped over a cable.
a length of cable
overhead power cables
Underground cables carry electricity to all parts of the city.

definitions.en: → cable television
definitions.zh: （电视节目或电话信号的）有线传输系统（同 cable televisioin）

examples.en: The office has gone over to cable.
examples.zh: 办公室改用有线系统了。

examples.en: This channel is only available on cable.
examples.zh: 这个频道只有通过有线电视才能收到。

examples.en: Now that we have cable, we get a much better picture.
Nearly one home in ten across the country is wired up to receive TV via cable.
a cable channel
He works for a cable television company.
They broadcast on cable.

definitions.en: to put cables under the ground in an area so that television or phone signals can be sent along them

examples.en: Many rural areas have not yet been cabled.
examples.zh: 许多乡村地区还没有铺设电缆。

examples.en: You will have to buy a satellite dish or get cabled up.
examples.zh: 你得买一个卫星天线或是铺设电缆。

definitions.en: the regular rise and fall of the voice

examples.en: Cadence is usually the hardest aspect of a foreign language to adopt.
It can be difficult to find the words and cadences to deliver your argument effectively.

definitions.en: a set of chords (= different notes played together) at the end of a piece of music

examples.en: She incorporated cadences from gospel in her singing.
Music theorists of the later eighteenth century paid new attention to prosody and rhythmic cadence.

definitions.en: related to or according to a rule, principle, or law, especially in the Christian Church

examples.en: a canonical rule
examples.zh: 一条基督教教规

examples.en: The Pope indicated that he would not consider any applications for canonical pardon.
examples.zh: 教皇表示，他不会考虑任何请求赦免的申请。

examples.en: The canonical excommunication of the bishops for their illegal ordinations was considered.
The infallibility of the canonical scriptures rested on the authority of councils and church tradition.
The canonical implications are not clear.
In Roman Catholicism, is there any canonic difference between a delegate and a commissary?

definitions.en: considered to be among the best and most important, and worth studying

examples.en: The space will allow canonical works by these artists to be always on view.
the canonical writers of American literature
His reworked version of the score became the canonic version.
examples.zh: 他修改后的乐谱成为了经典版本。

examples.en: Some ideas in fandom deviate a long way from the canonical material.
Canonical works are those that are considered authoritative by the fan community.
examples.zh: 经典作品是粉丝群体认为具有权威性的作品。

examples.en: I think Hemingway is easily the most overrated canonical writer in history.
This is the canonical text on film criticism.
Goethe is thought of as a canonical figure of Western culture.
They study canonical films of all genres.
Notre Dame du Haut is one of Le Corbusier's most canonical works.
He combines research into highly obscure aspects of opera with reflections on such canonic works as Die Zauberflöte.

definitions.en: the number of elements (= separate items) in a mathematical set

examples.en: subsets with a small cardinality
examples.zh: 基数很小的子集

definitions.en: the symbol ^ , found on a keyboard and used in marking text
a small waterfall, often one of a group
definitions.zh: 小瀑布（常指瀑布群的分支）

examples.en: A cascade of golden hair fell down his back.
examples.zh: 金发如瀑布般披散在他的肩头。

definitions.en: to fall quickly and in large amounts
definitions.zh: 如瀑布般下落；大量倾泻

examples.en: Coins cascaded from/out of the fruit machine.
examples.zh: 大量硬币从吃角子老虎机中倾泻而出。

definitions.en: to pass on information by giving it to just a few people, who then give it to more people; to be passed on in this way

examples.en: Guest information is cascaded through employee shift briefings.
examples.zh: 客人信息是在员工轮班简报时传达的。

examples.en: Communication cascades down the organization until the information has been communicated through each level, to the front lines.
examples.zh: 整个组织逐级沟通，直到信息通过各阶层传达到一线员工。

definitions.en: Casual clothes are not formal or not suitable for special occasions.

examples.en: He was dressed in casual clothes--sneakers, blue jeans, and a t-shirt.
His casual behaviour was wholly inappropriate for such a formal occasion.
His clothes were artfully arranged to look stylishly casual.
Everyone else was in jeans and casual gear and I had my office clothes on - I stuck out like a sore thumb.

definitions.en: not taking or not seeming to take much interest
definitions.zh: 不在意的;不放在心上的;漫不经心的

examples.en: The psychologist's attitude seemed far too casual, even brutal.
examples.zh: 那个心理学家的态度似乎太过冷淡了，甚至有些冷酷。

examples.en: Security around the conference hotel seemed almost casual.
examples.zh: 举行会议的酒店周围的安全措施似乎不太严格。

examples.en: Although close to tears, she tried to make her voice sound casual.
examples.zh: 尽管都快掉眼泪了，可她还是尽力使自己的声音听上去满不在乎。

definitions.en: not regular or fixed
definitions.zh: 不固定的;不定期的;临时的

examples.en: casual sex They were having casual sex but it never became a serious relationship.
casual labour/labourers/workers
examples.zh: 零工／临时工

definitions.en: not serious or considered, or done by chance
definitions.zh: 不经意的，随意的；碰巧的

examples.en: casual observer To a casual observer, everything might appear normal.
examples.zh: 对于一个漫不经心的人来说，也许看上去一切正常。

examples.en: It was just a casual comment - I didn't mean it to be taken so seriously.
examples.zh: 我只是随便说说而已，不是当真的。

definitions.en: a book with a list of all the goods that you can buy from a shop

examples.en: a mail-order catalogue
examples.zh: 邮购目录

examples.en: Do you often buy goods from mail-order catalogues?
Our autumn catalogue is now available from our usual stockists.
Granddad was browsing through a seed and plant catalogue.
I've sent off for a catalogue, as I can't get in to their Cambridge shop nowadays.
If you order goods from the catalogue, they usually arrive within the week.

definitions.en: A catalogue of unwanted events is a series of them.

examples.en: The whole holiday was a catalogue of disasters.
examples.zh: 整个假期祸事接二连三。

examples.en: a catalogue of errors/crimes/complaints
examples.zh: 一连串的错误／罪行／抱怨

definitions.en: to record something, especially in a list

examples.en: Many plants become extinct before they have even been catalogued.
examples.zh: 许多植物甚至还没来得及编入目录就已经灭绝了。

definitions.en: a sudden event that causes very great trouble or destruction

examples.en: They were warned of the ecological catastrophe to come.
examples.zh: 他们收到警告，生态灾难即将来临。

examples.en: The emigration of scientists is a catastrophe for the country.
examples.zh: 大量科学家移居国外对这个国家大为不利。

definitions.en: general and intended to include everything
definitions.zh: 宽泛的；包罗万象的；面面俱到的

examples.en: "South London" is a catch-all phrase/term for anywhere south of the river.
examples.zh: “伦敦南部”是个很宽泛的名称，适用于河以南的任何地方。

definitions.en: a very general description
a warning to consider something before taking any more action, or a statement that limits a more general statement
definitions.zh: （进一步行动前的）警告，告诫;限制条款

examples.en: He agreed to the interview, with the caveat that he could approve the final article.
examples.zh: 他答应接受访问，条件是访问稿刊登前须经他同意。

definitions.en: the action of preventing part or the whole of a book, film, work of art, document, or other kind of communication from being seen or made available to the public, because it is considered to be offensive or harmful, or because it contains information that someone wishes to keep secret, often for political reasons

examples.en: censorship of the press
examples.zh: 新闻审查制度

examples.en: She called TV news censorship of violent news "hypocritical".
Some websites are subject to government censorship.
We do not advocate the censorship of artistic expression.
There has always been movie censorship since the first films were created to be seen by the masses.
They started a petition asking the government to abandon plans for internet censorship.

definitions.en: in, at, from, or near the centre or most important part of something
definitions.zh: 中心的，中央的；中部的；靠近中心的；核心的

examples.en: central Europe/London
examples.zh: 中欧／伦敦中部

examples.en: Of course, you pay more for premises with a central location (= in or near the centre of a town).
examples.zh: 当然，位于市中心的房屋就要贵一些。

examples.en: Some late news has just come in - a bomb has exploded in central London.
In many churches the side aisles are separated from the central aisle by a row of arches.
The bridge's central support gave way, tipping the coach into the river.
The large central diamond is surrounded by eight smaller stones.
I love the wide open spaces of central Australia.

definitions.en: main or important

examples.en: a central role
examples.zh: 主要角色

examples.en: Community involvement is central to our plan.
examples.zh: 社区的参与对我们的计划很重要。

examples.en: I failed to grasp the film's central concept.
Eugenics was the central, and most controversial, part of his social philosophy.
The central character of the play is a flaky neurotic.
The central argument of the book is that some of the plays were not written by Shakespeare.
The central character is a malevolent witch out for revenge.

definitions.en: controlled or organized in one main place

examples.en: central authorities
examples.zh: 中央当局

examples.en: the US central bank
examples.zh: 美国中央银行

examples.en: central planning
examples.zh: 中心规划

definitions.en: an area of central Scotland that was one of nine local government regions until 1996
definitions.zh: （苏格兰）中央区（原为九个地方行政区之一，1996年撤销）

examples.en: At that time she worked for Central Regional Council.
examples.zh: 那时候她在中央区市政会工作。

examples.en: Central was one of the 12 regions into which Scotland was divided from 1975 to 1996.
Central was formed in 1975 by bringing together the traditional county areas of Stirlingshire and Clackmannanshire, plus parts of the west side of the traditional county of West Lothian.
As a region, Central was the upper of two tiers of local government.
Listen to our Traffic Scotland Radio broadcast for Central, Tayside and Fife.

definitions.en: the middle point or part

examples.en: There was a large table in the centre of the room.
examples.zh: 屋子中央有张大桌子。

examples.en: the town centre
examples.zh: 市中心

examples.en: She's the centre of attention everywhere she goes.
examples.zh: 她不论走到哪里都是众人瞩目的焦点。

examples.en: Mark was at the centre of the argument.
examples.zh: 马克处于争论的风口浪尖上。

examples.en: A social worker was at the centre of the scandal.
examples.zh: 一位社会工作者处于这起丑闻的漩涡之中。

examples.en: They've got a fabulous apartment in the centre of Paris.
Make a fold across the centre of the card.
I love those chocolates with the caramel centres.
Is that cake cooked properly in the centre?
Try to hit the centre of the target.

definitions.en: a place or building, especially one where a particular activity happens

examples.en: a sports/leisure/health centre
examples.zh: 体育／休闲／保健中心

examples.en: a garden/shopping centre
examples.zh: 园艺／购物中心

examples.en: Grants will be given to establish centres of excellence (= places where a particular activity is done extremely well) in this field of research.
examples.zh: 将会拨款在这一领域建立出色的研究中心。

examples.en: The centre provides expert advice for people with financial problems.
He's gone down to the health centre to collect his prescriptions.
He works in a sports centre instructing people in the use of the gym equipment.
In the refugee centres we saw many children whose stomachs were distended because of lack of food.
Have you seen the designs for the new shopping centre?

definitions.en: in politics, the people in a group who hold opinions that are not extreme but are between two opposites

examples.en: His political views are known to be left of/right of centre.
examples.zh: 人们知道他的政治观点属于中间偏左／右。

examples.en: a centre left party
examples.zh: 中左派（政党）

definitions.en: to put something in the middle of an area

examples.en: Centre (= put at equal distances from the left and right sides of the page) all the headings in this document.
examples.zh: 把这份文件中的所有标题居中。

definitions.en: (a set of) formal acts, often fixed and traditional, performed on important social or religious occasions

examples.en: a wedding/graduation ceremony
examples.zh: 结婚／毕业典礼

examples.en: We weren't married in church, but we had a civil ceremony in a registry office.
The Olympics climaxed in a spectacular closing ceremony.
The glittering ceremony conjured up images of Russia's imperial past.
Several foreign dignitaries attended the ceremony.
The queen, in full ceremonial dress, presided over the ceremony.

definitions.en: very formal and polite behaviour

examples.en: She arrived at the airport without the pomp and ceremony that usually accompanies important politicians.
examples.zh: 她抵达了机场，没有通常与重要政界人士相伴的盛况和礼仪。

examples.en: I handed her my letter of resignation without ceremony (= in an informal way).
examples.zh: 我毫不客气地向她递交了辞职书。

definitions.en: having no doubt or knowing exactly that something is true, or known to be true, correct, exact, or effective
definitions.zh: 肯定的，无疑的；有把握的；确定的

examples.en: Are you absolutely certain (that) you gave them the right number?
examples.zh: 你敢肯定你给他们的号码没错吗？

examples.en: I feel certain (that) you're doing the right thing.
examples.zh: 我确信你做得对。

examples.en: You should make certain (that) everyone understands the instructions.
examples.zh: 你应该确保大家都能听懂指令。

examples.en: The police seem certain (that) they will find the people responsible for the attack.
examples.zh: 警方似乎确信他们会查出这起袭击事件的责任人。

examples.en: I'm not certain how much it will cost.
examples.zh: 我不能确定这要花费多少钱。

examples.en: He was quite certain about/of his attacker's identity.
examples.zh: 他对袭击他的人的身份很肯定。

examples.en: One thing is certain - she won't resign willingly.
examples.zh: 有一点是肯定的——她不会心甘情愿地辞职。

examples.en: I don't know for certain if she's coming.
examples.zh: 我不能肯定她是否会来。

examples.en: I'm absolutely certain I left my purse on the desk.
I feel certain I'm right.
He's not certain whether to invest in the property market.
Make certain that we have enough drink for the party.
It is by no means certain that we'll finish the project by June.

definitions.en: impossible to avoid or extremely likely
definitions.zh: 不可避免地，必然地；极可能地

examples.en: The population explosion is certain to cause widespread famine.
examples.zh: 人口激增必然会引起大范围的饥荒。

examples.en: Oil prices are certain to rise following the agreement to limit production.
examples.zh: 限制石油生产的协议签订后油价必然会上涨。

examples.en: After all his hard work, he's certain to pass his exams.
examples.zh: 凭他这么用功，一定会通过考试的。

examples.en: The team looks almost certain to win the match.
examples.zh: 这支球队看上去几乎准能赢得比赛。

examples.en: It is virtually certain (that) she will win the gold medal.
examples.zh: 几乎可以肯定她会赢得金牌。

examples.en: Even if a ceasefire can be agreed, how can they make certain (that) neither side breaks it?
examples.zh: 即便双方都同意停火，又怎么能确保他们不会出尔反尔呢？

examples.en: Cancer sufferers no longer face certain death as they once did.
examples.zh: 癌症患者不再像从前那样面临着必死的命运了。

examples.en: This scandal will mean certain defeat for the party in the election.
examples.zh: 这一丑闻将意味着该党在选举中必败无疑。

examples.en: His execution became certain when the state board refused his request for commutation.
With Steve Lewis at the helm, we are certain of success.
Reducing the number of staff is certain to inflame the already angry medical profession.
It is by no means certain that we'll finish the project by June.
She is riding the fastest horse and is certain to romp home.

definitions.en: named but neither famous nor known well

examples.en: I had lunch today with a certain George Michael - not the George Michael, I should explain.
examples.zh: 我今天和一位叫乔治‧迈克尔的人共进了午餐——我得说明一下，不是那个乔治‧迈克尔。

definitions.en: limited

examples.en: I like modern art to a certain extent/degree, but I don't like the really experimental stuff.
examples.zh: 在一定程度上我还是喜欢现代艺术的，可是不喜欢太实验性的东西。

definitions.en: particular but not named or described
definitions.zh: （没有指明或描述的）某，某个，某些

examples.en: We have certain reasons for our decision, which have to remain confidential.
examples.zh: 我们作出这样的决定是有一定原因的，这些原因仍得保密。

examples.en: Do you think war is justifiable in certain circumstances?
examples.zh: 你是否认为在某些情况下战争是正当的？

examples.en: Certain members of the audience may disagree with what I'm about to say.
examples.zh: 听众中某些人可能会对我要说的话有异议。

examples.en: The song has a certain appeal, but I'm not sure what it is.
examples.zh: 这首歌有某种感染力，可是我说不出到底是什么。

examples.en: I felt a certain sense of joy that his candidacy had failed.
I must admit that she has a certain glamour about her.
For all his fine qualities, he lacked a certain self-restraint.
It's an entertaining movie, but there's a certain predictability about it.
The disease seems to be specific to certain types of plant.
Some people find that certain foods trigger their headaches.
It's against the law to leave children under a certain age alone in the house.
Farmers are eligible for government support if they let a certain amount of land lie fallow.
I'm allergic to certain foods.

definitions.en: some

examples.en: Certain of the candidates were well below the usual standard, but others were very good indeed.
examples.zh: 有些候选人离通常的标准差得很远，但其他一些确实很出色。

definitions.en: used to reply completely or to emphasize something and show that there is no doubt about it
definitions.zh: （用于肯定的答复或强调）当然，毫无疑问，确实，肯定

examples.en: She certainly had a friend called Mark, but I don't know whether he was her boyfriend.
examples.zh: 她确实有个朋友叫马克，不过我不知道他是不是她男友。

examples.en: "This is rather a difficult question." "Yes, it's certainly not easy."
examples.zh: “这实在是个难题。”“是呀，确实不容易。”

examples.en: "Do you think more money should be given to education?" "Certainly!"
examples.zh: “你认为应当给教育拨更多经费吗?”“当然啦！”

examples.en: "Had you forgotten about our anniversary?" "Certainly not! I've reserved a table at Michel's restaurant for this evening."
examples.zh: “你是不是把我们的结婚纪念日给忘了?”“当然没有！我已经在米歇尔餐厅预订了今晚的桌位。”

examples.en: "Could you lend me £10?" "Certainly."
examples.zh: “你能借给我10英镑吗？”“当然可以。”

examples.en: "Did you take any money out of my purse?" "Certainly not!"
examples.zh: “你是不是从我钱包里拿钱了?”“绝对没有！”

examples.en: We certainly don't lead a life of luxury but we're not poor either.
The design certainly looks good on paper.
The campaign has certainly succeeded in raising public awareness of the issue.
I suppose (that) you think that's funny. Well, I certainly don't.
His time in the army certainly toughened him up.

definitions.en: very likely to happen
definitions.zh: 必然地;很有可能发生地

examples.en: She will certainly win the election if the opinion polls are accurate.
examples.zh: 如果民意调查准确无误，她定会赢得这场选举。

definitions.en: to ask an amount of money for something, especially a service or activity
definitions.zh: （尤指对某一服务或活动）收费，要价，开价

examples.en: How much/What do you charge for a haircut and blow-dry?
examples.zh: 剪发和吹风要收多少钱？

examples.en: The bank charged commission to change my traveller's cheques.
examples.zh: 银行兑现我的旅行支票收取了手续费。

examples.en: They charge you $20 just to get in the nightclub.
examples.zh: 进夜总会的门他们就收你20美元。

examples.en: The local museum doesn't charge for admission.
examples.zh: 当地博物馆不收门票费。

examples.en: Charge the bill to my account, please.
examples.zh: 请把账单记在我账上。

examples.en: How much do you charge for hiring out a bicycle for a week?
He charges anywhere from $20 to $50 for a haircut and blow-dry.
The restaurant charges shockingly high prices for its food.
My plumber charges £20 an hour.
The policy of charging air travellers for vegetarian meals proved unpopular.

definitions.en: (of the police) to make a formal statement saying that someone is accused of a crime

examples.en: She's been charged with murder.
examples.zh: 她被控谋杀。

examples.en: She is charged with murdering her husband.
examples.zh: 她被指控谋害了自己的丈夫。

examples.en: The paper charged her with using the company's money for her own purposes.
examples.zh: 报纸指责她把公司的钱挪作己用。

examples.en: Two sisters have been charged with murder.
He tried to run away from the police and was charged with resisting arrest.
He was charged with driving 75 mph in a 55 mph zone.
Several England supporters were arrested and charged with disturbing the peace after the match.
Six rugby internationals were charged with taking drugs to improve their performance.

definitions.en: to move forward quickly and violently, especially towards something that has caused difficulty or anger

examples.en: The bull lowered its horns and charged.
examples.zh: 公牛俯下牛角发动进攻。

examples.en: The violence began when the police charged (at) a crowd of demonstrators.
examples.zh: 当警察冲向一群示威者时，暴力冲突开始了。

examples.en: I've been charging about/around all day and I'm exhausted.
examples.zh: 我一整天跑来跑去，累坏了。

examples.en: He came charging up the stairs to tell me the good news.
examples.zh: 他匆匆跑上楼来告诉我这个好消息。

examples.en: The children charged in, demanding ice cream.
As the protesters drew nearer, the soldiers charged.
Stop charging around - you'll wear yourself out!
The herd of rhinos charged towards us.
Crowds of demonstrators charged police lines.

definitions.en: to put enough explosive into a gun to fire it once
to order someone to do something

examples.en: He was charged with taking care of the premises.
examples.zh: 他被吩咐去看管那些房屋。

definitions.en: to put electricity into an electrical device such as a battery

examples.en: She drove the car round the block to charge (up) its batteries.
examples.zh: 为了给电池充电她开车围着街区转。

examples.en: It's not working - I don't think the battery is charging.
examples.zh: 不行呀——我觉得电池没有在充电。

definitions.en: the amount of money that you have to pay for something, especially for an activity or service
definitions.zh: （尤指某一活动或服务的）收费，费用，价格

examples.en: Is there a charge for children or do they go free?
examples.zh: 小孩要收费还是免费？

examples.en: There's an admission charge of £5.
examples.zh: 要收5英镑的入场费。

examples.en: They fixed my watch free of charge.
examples.zh: 他们免费修好了我的手表。

examples.en: Adults pay an admission charge but children get in free.
Although long-distance phone calls are going up, the charge for local calls will not alter.
Interest charges on an overdraft are usually quite high.
Prescription charges are rising in June.
I just want a basic bank account with low charges.

definitions.en: a formal police statement saying that someone is accused of a crime

examples.en: The 19-year-old will be appearing in court on Thursday where she will face criminal charges.
examples.zh: 这位19岁的少女将于周四出庭，她将面临刑事指控。

examples.en: He has been arrested on a charge of murder.
examples.zh: 他因被控谋杀而遭逮捕。

examples.en: The police brought a charge of theft against him.
examples.zh: 警方指控他犯了盗窃罪。

examples.en: The police have had to drop (= stop) charges against her because they couldn't find any evidence.
examples.zh: 警方因为找不到任何证据不得不撤销了对她的指控。

examples.en: He claimed he had been arrested on a trumped-up (= false) charge.
examples.zh: 他声称自己是受诬告而被捕的。

examples.en: The president responded angrily to the charge that she had lost touch with her country's people.
examples.zh: 有指责说总统和自己的国民失去了联系，对此她做出了愤怒的回应。

examples.en: Her refusal to condemn the violence laid/left her open to the charge of positive support for the campaign (= allowed people to say that she supported it).
examples.zh: 她拒绝谴责暴力，这很容易给人留下她支持那场运动的口实。

examples.en: The company pleaded guilty to the charge of manslaughter.
The charge of murder was altered to the lesser charge of manslaughter.
Both men vehemently denied the charges against them.
Criminal charges will be brought against the driver of the lorry.
Simson, aged 19, was convicted on two charges of burglary.

definitions.en: being the person who has control of or is responsible for someone or something:
definitions.zh: 索菲离开后，谁来管理这个部门？

examples.en: Who will be in charge of the department when Sophie leaves?
examples.zh: 索菲离开后，谁来管理这个部门？

examples.en: Her ex-husband has charge of the children during the week and she has them at the weekend.
examples.zh: 她的前夫在工作日照看孩子，她则在周末照顾孩子。

examples.en: His boss asked him to take charge of the office for a few days while she was away.
examples.zh: 老板让他在她离开的这段时间负责办公室工作。

definitions.en: the amount of explosive to be fired at one time, or the bullet or other explosive object shot from a gun
an attack in which people or animals suddenly run forward

examples.en: a charge of buffalo/elephants
examples.zh: 野牛／大象的猛冲

examples.en: a police charge
examples.zh: 警方的突然袭击

definitions.en: an order to do something
the amount or type of electricity that an electrical device stores or that a substance carries

examples.en: My phone had run out of charge.
The electron has a negative charge and the proton has a positive charge.
Is it all right to leave/put the battery on charge overnight?
examples.zh: 让电池充一夜电可以吗？

definitions.en: to hurry after someone or something in order to catch him, her, or it

examples.en: The police car was going so fast, it must have been chasing someone.
examples.zh: 警车开得这么快，一定是在追捕什么人。

examples.en: She was chasing (after) a man who had snatched her bag.
examples.zh: 她在追赶一个抢走她包的男子。

examples.en: It was plucky of you to chase after the burglar.
There are too many people chasing too few jobs.
The thieves easily outran the policewoman who was chasing them.
He drove through the red lights in an attempt to shake off the police car that was chasing him.
Dad chased him into the garden.

definitions.en: to hurry or run in various directions

examples.en: She couldn't study with the children chasing around the house.
examples.zh: 孩子们在房子里跑来跑去，她没法学习。

definitions.en: to try to get something that is difficult to get or achieve

examples.en: It's depressing how many people there are chasing so few jobs.
examples.zh: 这么多人在争夺这么少的几份工作，真是让人沮丧。

examples.en: After years of chasing her dreams, she finally got a part in a film.
examples.zh: 追逐自己的梦想这么多年，她终于在一部电影中得到了一个角色。

examples.en: She's always chasing (after) men.
examples.zh: 她总是追求男人。

definitions.en: to run after a person or an animal in a threatening way in order to make him, her, or it leave

examples.en: He used to chase the children away from his apple trees.
examples.zh: 他过去常把孩子们从他的苹果树旁赶走。

examples.en: She's always chasing cats out of the garden to protect her precious birds.
examples.zh: 她总是把猫赶出花园，以保护她那些珍贵的小鸟。

definitions.en: the act of going after someone or something very quickly in order to catch him, her, or it

examples.en: a tedious movie with endless car/police chases
examples.zh: 充斥着没完没了追车／警察追捕场面的乏味电影

definitions.en: Chewy food needs to be chewed a lot before it is swallowed.
definitions.zh: （食物）难嚼的，耐嚼的，嚼不烂的

examples.en: The meat was tasteless and chewy.
examples.zh: 这肉淡而无味而且嚼不烂。

definitions.en: a job or piece of work that is often boring or unpleasant but needs to be done regularly
definitions.zh: 日常琐事;杂务;乏味的例行工作

examples.en: I'll go shopping when I've done my chores (= done the jobs in or around the house).
examples.zh: 我把家务琐事干完后要去购物。

examples.en: I find writing reports a real chore (= very boring).
examples.zh: 我觉得写报告真是件烦人的事。

definitions.en: in a way that follows the order in which a series of events happened
definitions.zh: 按事件发生的年代顺序排列地

examples.en: The dates are arranged chronologically.
examples.zh: 日期是按时间顺序排列的。

examples.en: The essays are organized thematically rather than chronologically.
examples.zh: 这些文章是按主题而不是按时间排列的。

examples.en: The five hundred chronologically organized pages provide a factual, historical narrative.
Posts are shown chronologically on the web page
The story unfolds chronologically.

definitions.en: a system of writing that prevents most people from understanding the message

examples.en: The message was written in cipher.
examples.zh: 电文是用密码写的。

definitions.en: a person or group of people without power, but used by others for their own purposes, or someone who is not important
definitions.zh: 为人所利用的小人物；无名小卒；无足轻重的人

examples.en: The interim government is a mere cipher for military rule.
examples.zh: 过渡政府不过是军事统治的一枚棋子。

definitions.en: a zero

examples.en: If you have no children, enter a cipher in the space on the form.
examples.zh: 如果你没有孩子，在表上的空格里填零。

definitions.en: a regular pattern of visits or the places visited

examples.en: They first met each other on the tennis circuit (= while at different tennis competitions).
examples.zh: 他们在网球巡回赛上首次交锋。

examples.en: He was a familiar figure on the lecture circuit.
examples.zh: 他是巡回讲学中的座上常客。

examples.en: The judge had served for many years on the northeastern Circuit.
examples.zh: 这位法官多年来供职于东北部巡回法庭。

examples.en: a circuit judge
examples.zh: 巡回法官

examples.en: He always takes part in the polo circuit.
We see each other on the dinner party circuit.

definitions.en: something shaped approximately like a circle, especially a route, path, or sports track that starts and ends in the same place
definitions.zh: 环行物;（尤指）环形道路（或路线、赛道）

examples.en: They test the car tyres on a motor racing circuit.
examples.zh: 他们在环形赛车道上测试轮胎。

examples.en: We made a leisurely circuit of the city walls.
examples.zh: 我们悠闲地围着城墙转悠。

definitions.en: a closed system of wires or pipes through which electricity or liquid can flow

examples.en: A defect was found in the water-cooling/electrical circuit.
examples.zh: 在水冷回路／电路中发现了一点毛病。

definitions.en: shaped like a circle

examples.en: a circular flowerbed/tablecloth
examples.zh: 圆形花坛／桌布

definitions.en: a letter or notice sent to a large number of people
definitions.zh: （同时发给很多人的）通知，通报，传单

examples.en: Circulars and other junk mail go straight in the bin.
examples.zh: 传单和其他的垃圾邮件直接扔进垃圾桶。

definitions.en: a fact or event that makes a situation the way it is

examples.en: I think she coped very well under the circumstances.
examples.zh: 我觉得在那种情况下她处理得很不错。

examples.en: Obviously we can't deal with the problem until we know all the circumstances.
examples.zh: 显然，我们只有了解全部情况后才能处理这问题。

examples.en: She died in suspicious circumstances.
examples.zh: 她死得很蹊跷。

examples.en: We oppose capital punishment in/under any circumstances.
examples.zh: 无论在什么情况下，我们都不赞成死刑。

examples.en: Under no circumstances should you (= you should not) approach the man.
examples.zh: 你无论如何都不应该联系那个男人。

examples.en: The meeting has been cancelled due to circumstances beyond our control.
examples.zh: 会议因不可控情况的出现而被取消了。

examples.en: They were victims of circumstance.
examples.zh: 他们是客观环境的牺牲品。

examples.en: We were obliged to go by force of circumstance.
examples.zh: 形势所迫，我们不得不走。

examples.en: Grants are available depending on your circumstances.
examples.zh: 视经济状况，符合条件可获得助学金。

examples.en: The new circumstances compelled a change in policy.
An unfortunate conjunction of circumstances led to his downfall.
I'd planned a romantic evening together, but circumstances conspired against it - friends arrived unexpectedly and then Dave was called out to an emergency.
The circumstances of her death have been hotly disputed.
His tact was exemplary, especially considering the circumstances.

definitions.en: to avoid something, especially cleverly or illegally
definitions.zh: （尤指巧妙或非法地）逃避，规避，绕过

examples.en: Ships were registered abroad to circumvent employment and safety regulations.
examples.zh: 船只在外国注册以规避雇用及安全规定。

definitions.en: a word or piece of writing taken from a written work

examples.en: All citations are taken from the 2007 edition of the text.
examples.zh: 全部引文均出自该文本2007年的版本。

definitions.en: an official request for someone to appear in a law court

examples.en: The court issued a contempt citation against city council members who refused to comply with a court order.
examples.zh: 法庭对拒不遵守法庭命令的的市议会成员们发出了藐视法庭的传票。

definitions.en: official praise for a person in the armed forces for brave actions

examples.en: The four soldiers are to receive citations from the president for their brave actions.
examples.zh: 这4名士兵将因其英勇行为将受到总统的嘉奖。

definitions.en: the quality of being clear and easy to understand

examples.en: There has been a call for greater clarity in this area of the law.
examples.zh: 人们呼吁该法规的这个方面应该更加明确一些。

definitions.en: the quality of being easy to see or hear

examples.en: She was phoning from Australia, but I was amazed at the clarity of her voice.
examples.zh: 她是从澳大利亚打来的电话，可是声音那么清晰，让我觉得惊讶。

definitions.en: the ability to think clearly and not be confused

examples.en: He has shown great clarity of mind.
examples.zh: 可以看出他的思维极其清晰。

examples.en: mental clarity
examples.zh: 头脑清醒

definitions.en: Classified information is officially stated to be secret.

examples.en: These documents contain classified material.
examples.zh: 这些文件中有机密材料。

definitions.en: having or showing the ability to learn and understand things quickly and easily

examples.en: Judy has never been very clever, but she tries hard.
examples.zh: 朱迪向来不是很聪明，可是她很用功。

examples.en: Fiona is very clever at physics.
examples.zh: 菲奥纳很擅长物理。

examples.en: Charlie has a clever idea/plan for getting us out of our present difficulties.
examples.zh: 查利有个妙计可以使我们摆脱现在的困境。

examples.en: My mother is very clever with her hands.
examples.zh: 我妈妈手很巧。

examples.en: I've got a clever little gadget for opening jars.
examples.zh: 我有个设计巧妙的开罐器。

examples.en: That's enough clever talk, young lady.
examples.zh: 别跟我油嘴滑舌了，小姐。

examples.en: Did you paint this yourself? You clever girl!
He's always telling everybody how clever his children are.
Edith may seem a bit crazy, but she's quite clever really.
How clever of you to buy chocolate chip cookies - they're my favourites.
The building relies on clever design rather than on ornament for its impressive effect.

definitions.en: to hit someone or something hard and repeatedly

examples.en: If you do that again, I'll clobber you (one).
examples.zh: 你再干那事，我会狠揍你一顿。

examples.en: The government is proposing new measures to clobber tax dodgers.
examples.zh: 政府正在拟议新的措施来严惩逃税者。

examples.en: The new supermarket is really going to clobber the small local shops.
examples.zh: 新超市确实会使当地的小店铺蒙受巨大的经济损失。

definitions.en: to defeat completely

examples.en: The government clobbered the opposition's proposals.
examples.zh: 政府彻底否决了反对党的提议。

definitions.en: possessions, especially those that you carry around with you, or clothes
definitions.zh: 财物;（尤指）随身物品;衣物

examples.en: I've got far too much clobber in my handbag.
examples.zh: 我提包里的东西太多了。

examples.en: Did you bring all your tennis clobber?
examples.zh: 打网球用的东西你都带好了吗？

definitions.en: in the direction in which the hands (= thin parts that point) of a clock move

examples.en: Turn the knob clockwise/in a clockwise direction.
examples.zh: 沿顺时针方向转动旋钮。

definitions.en: a test consisting of a piece of text with words missing that you have to fill in
a sign or some information that helps you to find the answer to a problem, question, or mystery

examples.en: Police are still looking for clues in their search for the missing girl.
examples.zh: 警方仍在寻找失踪女孩的线索。

examples.en: I'm never going to guess the answer if you don't give me a clue.
examples.zh: 要是你不给我一点提示，我永远也猜不到答案。

examples.en: The police found a vital clue to the girl's disappearance in a wooded area near her home.
Forensic scientists are going over the victim's flat in a search for clues about the murderer.
The police are collecting clues in order to piece together the details of the day she died.
He pored over the letter searching for clues about the writer.
The police tried to reconstruct the crime using the statements of witnesses and clues that they had found.

definitions.en: behaviour that does not show any skill or thought

examples.en: With her usual clumsiness, she had opened her mouth and upset everyone.
examples.zh: 她以自己一贯的鲁莽开口直言，结果惹恼了所有人。

examples.en: The minister displayed a political clumsiness that his opponents exploited.
examples.zh: 这位部长表现出了政治上的不成熟，使他的对手利用这一点趁机获益。

examples.en: "I'll see you about, later," he said, hating the clumsiness of the words.
Their lives were marked by a series of small personal failures due to their social clumsiness.
Some of this is reaction to perceived clumsiness of the administration.

definitions.en: movement or behaviour that is not careful and controlled, and makes you likely to have accidents
definitions.zh: 笨拙、不小心、不谨慎的动作或行为;鲁莽

examples.en: To compound my clumsiness, I accidentally knocked over my bottle of water.
examples.zh: 我不小心打翻了自己的水瓶，让我更显得笨手笨脚。

examples.en: Todd collided with a woman who berated him for his clumsiness.
examples.zh: 托德与一个女人相撞，女人因而骂他傻头傻脑。

examples.en: His symptoms included stiffness, slowness and clumsiness.
Grinning at his own clumsiness, he replaced the lamp on the table.
He danced with the gusto and clumsiness of a child.

definitions.en: heavy and solid in an ugly way

examples.en: The clunky handsets looked old-fashioned as soon as they were launched.
examples.zh: 这些笨重的手机刚投放市场就显得过时了。

examples.en: He wore a clunky gold ring.
examples.zh: 他戴着一枚硕大的金戒指。

definitions.en: awkward or badly done

examples.en: The writing is clunky in places and full of clichés.
examples.zh: 这篇文章多处显得突兀，充满了陈词滥调。

definitions.en: old-fashioned or slow

examples.en: It was the slow, clunky technology it used that was responsible for the firm's failure.
examples.zh: 正是公司采用的缓慢老式的技术导致了破产。

definitions.en: (a lot of objects in) a state of being untidy

examples.en: Sorry about the clutter in the kitchen.
examples.zh: 对不起，厨房里杂乱不堪。

examples.en: My desk is covered in/full of clutter.
examples.zh: 我书桌上堆得乱七八糟。

definitions.en: to fill something in an untidy or badly organized way

examples.en: The kids always clutter the hall (up) with school bags and coats and stuff.
examples.zh: 孩子们总是在厅里乱放书包、外套和乱七八糟的东西。

examples.en: Every shelf is cluttered with ornaments.
examples.zh: 每层架子上都塞满了装饰品。

examples.en: I try not to clutter (up) my mind with useless information.
examples.zh: 我尽量不往脑子里装那些无用的东西。

definitions.en: someone whose job is to teach people to improve at a sport, skill, or school subject
definitions.zh: 教练;辅导老师;私人教师;家庭教师

examples.en: a tennis/maths coach
examples.zh: 网球教练／数学辅导老师

examples.en: Gus Poyet was appointed as head coach.
examples.zh: 加斯·波耶特被任命为总教练。

examples.en: The coach issued a diktat that all team members must attend early-morning practice.
Our football coach has worked hard to inculcate a team spirit into the players.
The world's number one tennis player and his coach parted company earlier this month.
She's an excellent coach who knows how to get results.
We got a rollicking from the coach at half time.

definitions.en: a long motor vehicle with comfortable seats, used to take groups of people on journeys

examples.en: We're going to the airport by coach.
examples.zh: 我们要坐大巴去机场。

examples.en: a coach trip
examples.zh: 乘长途汽车旅行

examples.en: The teachers counted the students as they got on to the coach.
The coach has air conditioning and reclining seats.
The coach stopped for us to eat lunch but within half an hour we were on our way again.
The pick-up point for the long-distance coaches is now in the new bus station.
The coach, taking children on a school trip, careered down a slope and collided with a bank.

definitions.en: to give special classes in sports or a school subject, especially privately, to one person or a small group
definitions.zh: 训练;培训;指导;（尤指私人）辅导

examples.en: She coaches students in French, usually for exams.
examples.zh: 她辅导学生法语，通常是准备考试的学生。

examples.en: He coached the Blue Devils during their last championship season.
examples.zh: 上一赛季他在蓝魔队执教。

examples.en: He coached the England team for years.
She coaches rich kids to pass their exams.
She was coaching the basketball team.
I've been invited to coach the under-eleven rugby team.
His opponents accused him of coaching witnesses on their testimony.
Presidential candidates are coached by armies of advisers.

definitions.en: the cheapest type of seats on a plane or train

examples.en: Even the company's director flies coach most of the time.
examples.zh: 即便是公司的主管大多数时候也都坐经济舱。

definitions.en: If two or more things coalesce, they come or grow together to form one thing or system.
rough and not smooth or soft, or not in very small pieces
definitions.zh: 粗糙的；不光滑的；不柔软的；粗大的

examples.en: coarse sand/breadcrumbs
examples.zh: 粗沙／面包屑

examples.en: Linen is a coarse-grained fabric.
examples.zh: 亚麻布是一种质地粗糙的布料。

definitions.en: rude and offensive

examples.en: a coarse joke
examples.zh: 粗俗的笑话

examples.en: coarse language
examples.zh: 粗话

definitions.en: to arrange something, such as laws or rules, into a system
definitions.zh: 把…编成法典；编纂（法典、条例等）

definitions.en: the use of force to persuade someone to do something that they are unwilling to do

examples.en: He claimed the police had used coercion, threats, and promises to obtain the statement illegally.
examples.zh: 他声称警方曾用威逼、恐吓和许诺的方法来非法获取供词。

definitions.en: A cogent argument, reason, etc. is clearly expressed and persuades people to believe it.
definitions.zh: （论据或理由）令人信服的，有说服力的，强有力的

definitions.en: connected with thinking or conscious mental processes
definitions.zh: 感知的;认知的;认识力的

examples.en: Some of her cognitive functions have been impaired.
examples.zh: 她的部分认知能力受到了损害。

examples.en: cognitive behaviour/development
examples.zh: 认知行为／认知能力的发展

examples.en: cognitive psychology
examples.zh: 认知心理学

definitions.en: to happen at or near the same time

examples.en: I timed my holiday to coincide with the children's.
examples.zh: 我把我的假期安排在孩子们学校放假的时候。

examples.en: If the heavy rain had coincided with an extreme high tide, serious flooding would have resulted.
examples.zh: 如果暴雨同时伴有汹涌的潮汛，就会导致严重的洪灾。

examples.en: Our views coincide on a range of subjects.
examples.zh: 我们在一系列问题上的观点不谋而合。

examples.en: If our schedules coincide, we'll go to Spain together.
examples.zh: 如果我们的日程安排一致，就能一起去西班牙。

examples.en: We timed our trip to coincide with my cousin's wedding.
The publication of the book was planned to coincide with the screening of the television series.
When I went to visit her in hospital, my visit coincided with Angela's.
I have a feeling that coincides with our departmental meeting - can we make it Tuesday instead?
We only get one day a week off, and our days off rarely coincide.

definitions.en: to fall down suddenly because of pressure or having no strength or support
definitions.zh: （由于压力、无力或缺乏支持而）倒塌，坍塌;崩溃，垮掉

examples.en: Thousands of buildings collapsed in the earthquake.
examples.zh: 数以千计的建筑物在地震中倒塌了。

examples.en: The chair collapsed under her weight.
examples.zh: 椅子承受不了她的重量散架了。

examples.en: He thought his whole world had collapsed when his wife died.
examples.zh: 妻子去世后他觉得自己的整个世界都坍塌了。

examples.en: He collapsed and died of a heart attack.
examples.zh: 他心脏病发作，倒地而亡。

examples.en: All chairs collapse for easy storage.
examples.zh: 所有的椅子折叠起来以方便存放。

examples.en: It looks as if the whole political edifice of the country is about to collapse.
I should think you're about ready to collapse after all that walking.
The building's foundations are rather shaky, and it could collapse at any time.
At the end of the race his legs gave out and he collapsed on the ground.
The woman staggered and collapsed in a heap.

definitions.en: (of people and business) to suddenly be unable to continue or work correctly

examples.en: Lots of people lost their jobs when the property market collapsed.
examples.zh: 房地产市场崩溃后，许多人丢了工作。

examples.en: Talks between management and unions have collapsed.
examples.zh: 劳资谈判彻底破裂。

examples.en: Share prices collapsed (= became lower suddenly) after news of poor trading.
examples.zh: 贸易不景气的消息传出后股价暴跌。

examples.en: The company is staggering under a $15 million debt and will almost certainly collapse by the end of the year.
Things have gone badly for him since his business collapsed.
All charges against them were withdrawn after the prosecution's case collapsed.
The talks have collapsed and both sides have resorted to brinkmanship.
She'd invested extensively in stocks and got her fingers burned when the market collapsed.

definitions.en: the sudden failure of a system, organization, business, etc.
definitions.zh: （系统、组织、企业等的）崩溃，瓦解，失败

examples.en: I don't know what caused the collapse of her marriage.
examples.zh: 我不知道她婚姻失败的原因是什么。

examples.en: A poor economy has caused the collapse of thousands of small businesses.
examples.zh: 经济萧条导致数以千计的小型企业破产。

examples.en: Negotiations between the two countries are on the brink/verge of collapse (= very soon going to fail).
examples.zh: 两国的谈判濒临破裂。

examples.en: He suffered a mental/nervous collapse after ten years' teaching.
examples.zh: 执教10年后，他的精神／神经崩溃了。

examples.en: The collapse of the company was described as the greatest financial debacle in US history.
The collapse of Communism changed East-West relations for ever.
The company is on the edge of collapse.
The collapse of its rivals brought fortuitous gains to the company.
The collapse of the bank is an ominous reminder of the fragility of the world's banking system.

definitions.en: the sudden falling movement of a person or structure that has become too weak to stand
definitions.zh: 昏倒，昏迷;（建筑物的）倒塌，坍塌

examples.en: He was taken to hospital after his collapse on the pitch.
examples.zh: 他昏倒在赛场后被送往医院。

examples.en: the collapse of a building during the earthquake
examples.zh: 地震中一座大楼的倒塌

definitions.en: (especially of moving objects) to hit something violently

examples.en: The two vans collided at the crossroads.
examples.zh: 两辆货车在十字路口相撞。

examples.en: It was predicted that a comet would collide with one of the planets.
examples.zh: 预计一颗彗星将与某行星相撞。

definitions.en: an accident that happens when two vehicles hit each other with force

examples.en: There has been a collision on the southbound stretch of the motorway.
examples.zh: 在高速公路南向路段上发生了一起撞车事故。

examples.en: Two drivers were killed in a head-on (= direct) collision between a car and a taxi last night.
examples.zh: 昨晚，一辆轿车与一辆出租车迎面相撞，两名司机身亡。

examples.en: The cyclist was in a collision with a bus.
examples.zh: 那个骑自行车的人与一辆公共汽车相撞。

definitions.en: a strong disagreement

examples.en: There was a collision of interests/opinions.
examples.zh: 存在利益冲突／意见不和。

definitions.en: informally and in a way that is more suitable for use in speech than in writing
definitions.zh: （词语或表达法）口语地，通俗语地，非正式地

examples.en: Colloquially referred to as a "brain attack", a stroke occurs when the flow of blood to the brain is interrupted.
examples.zh: 俗称“脑梗”，当流向大脑的血液被中断时就会发生中风。

examples.en: Jardine writes clearly and colloquially for the non-academic reader.
examples.zh: 雅尔丁为非学术界的读者写得很清楚、很口语化。

examples.en: Some people accused the Vice President of speaking too colloquially.
His actors talk colloquially and quickly, sometimes over one another.
Sepsis, colloquially known as blood poisoning, can occur in response to almost any bacterial infection.

definitions.en: the lower and bigger half of the bowels in which water is removed from solid waste
the symbol : used in writing, especially to introduce a list of things or a sentence or phrase taken from somewhere else
the standard unit of money used in Costa Rica and El Salvador
definitions.zh: （哥斯达黎加和萨尔瓦多的标准货币单位）科朗

definitions.en: a fight, especially during a war

examples.en: There was fierce combat between the two sides.
examples.zh: 双方展开了激烈的战斗。

examples.en: No one knew how many troops had died in combat.
examples.zh: 没有人知道在战斗中死了多少士兵。

examples.en: The soldiers were engaged in hand-to-hand combat.
examples.zh: 士兵们展开了肉搏战。

examples.en: armed combat (= fighting with weapons)
examples.zh: 武装斗争

examples.en: unarmed combat (= fighting without weapons)
examples.zh: 非武装斗争

examples.en: The film explores the combat between good and evil.
examples.zh: 影片探讨了善恶之间的较量。

definitions.en: to try to stop something unpleasant or harmful from happening or increasing
definitions.zh: 与…战斗；与…作斗争；打击；制止

examples.en: to combat crime/terrorism/inflation/disease
examples.zh: 打击犯罪／打击恐怖主义／抑制通货膨胀／与疾病作斗争

examples.en: The government is spending millions of dollars in its attempt to combat drug abuse.
examples.zh: 政府正斥资数百万美元打击滥用毒品的行为。

examples.en: I have to combat this constant desire to eat chocolate.
examples.zh: 我不得不克制自己老想吃巧克力的欲望。

definitions.en: informal for comfortable

examples.en: a comfy chair
examples.zh: 舒适的椅子

definitions.en: funny and making you want to laugh

examples.en: a comic actor/performance
examples.zh: 滑稽演员／表演

examples.en: "Modern Times" is a classic comedy starring Charlie Chaplin, the grandaddy of comic film actors.
The speech had several comic touches.
The BBC is moving into virgin territory by producing comic programmes of this type.

definitions.en: a magazine or book that contains a set of stories told in pictures with a small amount of writing
definitions.zh: （尤指面向儿童的）连环画杂志，漫画杂志

definitions.en: someone who entertains people by telling jokes
definitions.zh: 逗笑的人；喜剧演员，滑稽演员

definitions.en: the section of a newspaper where comic strips appear:
definitions.zh: 父亲读报纸头版，孩子们看连环漫画。

examples.en: Dad read the front page while the kids read the comics.
examples.zh: 父亲读报纸头版，孩子们看连环漫画。

definitions.en: related to making money by buying and selling things

examples.en: a commercial organization/venture/success
examples.zh: 商业组织／企业／成功

examples.en: commercial law
examples.zh: 商法

examples.en: The commercial future of the company looks very promising.
examples.zh: 这家公司的商业前景看起来非常光明。

examples.en: Some robotic research prototypes have generated commercial products.
This is a commercial product, not free software.
The actor tends to alternate between indie art films and more commercial projects.
Now it's time for a commercial break.
Don't forget this is a commercial enterprise - we're here to make money.
The city council wants to put the bus service on a commercial footing.
The protection of the Antarctic from commercial exploitation is an important goal of environmentalists.
The drug is still being tested and will not go into commercial production for at least two years.
A product as good as that is a guarantee of commercial success.
The estate agent has many commercial properties, such as restaurants, on its books.
Some residents are worried about the prospect of commercial development in the area.

definitions.en: an advertisement that is broadcast on television or radio

examples.en: He went to make a drink during the commercials.
She was in a commercial for breakfast cereal.
a car/fast food commercial

definitions.en: happening often or often seen or experienced and so not considered to be special
definitions.zh: 平常的；常见的；普通的；普遍的

examples.en: Electric cars are increasingly commonplace.
examples.zh: 电动汽车越来越普及了。

definitions.en: a boring remark that is used very often and does not have much meaning
definitions.zh: 陈词滥调；寒暄客套；老生常谈

examples.en: We exchanged commonplaces about the weather over cups of tea.
examples.zh: 我们喝了几杯茶，就天气方面的话题寒暄了几句。

definitions.en: short form of communications: used to refer to methods of sending messages, especially long-distance methods such as radio, telephone, and internet, and to the messages themselves
definitions.zh: 尤指电台、电话和网络等长途通讯方式或传递的信息（communications的缩短形式）

examples.en: We use a variety of comms, from mobile phones to ISDN, and even standard phone lines.
examples.zh: 我们使用各种通信方式，从移动电话到ISDN，甚至是标准电话线。

examples.en: If all your comms are encrypted, it's harder to check for someone who is hiding something.
examples.zh: 如果所有通讯都是加密的，那么就更难检查出有人在隐藏什么。

examples.en: All the comms channels were cut.
examples.zh: 所有的通讯频道都被切断。

examples.en: We had to get these families out of the desert with no radio comms.
What's happening with national comms and broadband is very exciting.
Radio is down at the moment but they estimate they’ll have comms back in about an hour.
She was applying for a civilian job using the skills she'd acquired in military comms.
The proposed DSL standard had the backing of major players in the PC and comms industries.

definitions.en: consisting of parts that are positioned together closely or in a tidy way, using very little space

examples.en: compact soil/sand
examples.zh: 密实的土壤／沙子

examples.en: a compact camera/bag
examples.zh: 小巧的相机／提包

examples.en: What a compact office! How did you fit so much into so little space?
examples.zh: 多么小的办公室！你们是如何把这么多东西塞进这么小的空间的？

definitions.en: a small, flat case that contains women's face powder
definitions.zh: （装女性扑面用香粉的）带镜粉盒

examples.en: a powder compact
examples.zh: 粉盒

definitions.en: a small car
a formal agreement between two or more people, organizations, or countries

examples.en: They made a compact not to reveal any details.
examples.zh: 他们互相约定不透露任何细节。
