definitions.en: to press something together in a tight and solid way

examples.en: Cars had compacted the snow until it was like ice.
examples.zh: 汽车把雪碾得越来越实，直到变得像冰一样。

definitions.en: as compared to something else

examples.en: We couldn't afford it and yet we're comparatively well off (= we are richer than most people).
examples.zh: 我们买不起，不过我们还算比较富裕的。

examples.en: Comparatively speaking, this machine is easy to use.
examples.zh: 比较而言，这台机器用起来很方便。

definitions.en: the act of comparing two or more people or things

examples.en: They made a comparison of different countries' eating habits.
examples.zh: 他们对不同国家的饮食习惯作了一番比较。

examples.en: By/In comparison with the French, the British eat far less fish.
examples.zh: 与法国人相比，英国人吃鱼要少得多。

examples.en: You can't really draw a comparison between the two cases - they're entirely different.
I thought I was badly treated but my experiences pale in comparison with yours.
It was a tame film in comparison to some that she's made.
The family emigrated to New Zealand in 1949, which seemed a wonderland in comparison with post-war England.

definitions.en: the fact of considering something similar or of equal quality to something else

examples.en: She drew a comparison between life in the army and life in prison.
examples.zh: 她把军队生活比作坐牢。

examples.en: To my mind there's no comparison between the two restaurants (= one is much better than the other).
examples.zh: 在我心目中，这两家饭店根本不能相比。

examples.en: He's a good writer but he doesn't bear/stand comparison with Shakespeare (= he is not nearly as good as Shakespeare).
examples.zh: 他是位好作家，但无法与莎士比亚相提并论。

definitions.en: able to exist, live, or work successfully with something or someone else
definitions.zh: 可共存的；协调的；兼容的

examples.en: It was when we started living together that we found we just weren't compatible.
examples.zh: 等到开始生活在一起时，才发现我们根本合不来。

examples.en: Such policies are not compatible with democratic government.
examples.zh: 此类政策与民主政体格格不入。

examples.en: Are their two blood groups compatible (= can blood from one person be given to the other person)?
examples.zh: 他俩的血型相容吗？

examples.en: An MP's job is just not compatible with family life, especially when the children are very young.
Do you think that idea is compatible with your Christian faith?
Each department in the company has its own mode of operation and none of them are compatible.
This software allows you to write files from compatible CD-RW drives.

definitions.en: able to do something well
definitions.zh: 有能力的；能干的；称职的

examples.en: a competent secretary/rider/cook
examples.zh: 能干的秘书／骑手／厨师

examples.en: I wouldn't say he was brilliant but he is competent at his job.
examples.zh: 我虽然不会说他是才华横溢，可他还是能胜任工作的。

examples.en: She has shown herself to be a highly competent manager.
She's extremely competent and industrious - an asset to the department.
It's a competent enough piece of writing but it has no flair.
Charlie can cope here without you - he's perfectly competent.
They are a very competent group of people, but they lack flexibility and originality.

definitions.en: to say that something is wrong or not satisfactory

examples.en: Lots of people have complained about the noise.
examples.zh: 许多人抱怨过这噪声。

examples.en: You're always complaining!
examples.zh: 你总是牢骚满腹！

examples.en: He complained that his boss was useless and he had too much work.
examples.zh: 他抱怨说老板无能，而且他干的活太多。

examples.en: If the service was so bad why didn't you complain to the manager?
examples.zh: 如果服务质量这么差的话，你为什么不向经理投诉呢？

examples.en: Over three hundred listeners phoned in to complain about the programme.
Teachers complain that more of their time is taken up with administration than with teaching.
They were afraid to complain about the noise in case they annoyed the neighbours.
She was complaining about all the sex and violence on television.
After her fall she complained that she couldn't bend her leg properly.

definitions.en: willing to do what other people want you to do

examples.en: a compliant child
examples.zh: 听话的孩子

examples.en: The company expects to be reclassified as soon as its factories are fully compliant with the Federal Clean Air Act.
examples.zh: 一旦属下工厂完全符合《联邦清洁空气法案》定下的规则，这家公司预计将会尽快被重新定类。

definitions.en: to make something more difficult to deal with, do, or understand
definitions.zh: 使复杂化；使更难懂；使更麻烦

examples.en: It will only complicate the situation if we invite his old girlfriend as well.
examples.zh: 如果我们同时也邀请他以前的女友，只会使事情更加复杂。

examples.en: The rescue operation has been complicated by bad weather.
examples.zh: 恶劣的天气使救援工作变得更加艰难。

examples.en: These new rules have complicated the tax system even further.
examples.zh: 这些新规定使税收制度更加复杂。

examples.en: The breathing problem has now been complicated by a chest infection.
examples.zh: 因为胸腔感染，他的呼吸问题变得更加严重了。

definitions.en: involving a lot of different parts, in a way that is difficult to understand

examples.en: complicated instructions
examples.zh: 复杂的指令

examples.en: I had to fill in this really complicated form.
examples.zh: 我必须填写这张非常复杂的表格。

examples.en: The rules are rather complicated to follow.
examples.zh: 这些规定遵守起来挺复杂的。

examples.en: The relationship is a bit complicated. He's my mother's cousin's daughter's child.
examples.zh: 关系有点儿复杂，他是我母亲的表姐的女儿的孩子。

examples.en: His lecture was complicated and difficult to follow.
Our lawyer guided us through the more complicated questions on the form.
A complicated system of sewers runs under the city.
The road system was so complicated that we had to stop several times to find our bearings.
Your conclusion is good, but the final sentence is too long and complicated.

definitions.en: to act according to an order, set of rules, or request

examples.en: He's been ordered to have the dog destroyed because it's dangerous, but he refuses to comply.
examples.zh: 那只狗很危险，因此他接到了把它人道毁灭的命令，但他拒绝服从。

examples.en: There are serious penalties for failure to comply with the regulations.
examples.zh: 不遵守规定者将受到严惩。

examples.en: The court issued a contempt citation against city council members who refused to comply with a court order.
He was happy to comply without any further urging from me.
He was jailed for refusing to comply with the court order.
The builders have not complied with the architect's instructions.
She had very set views about the funeral, and everyone was happy to comply with her wishes.

definitions.en: something that is made of various different parts
definitions.zh: （由不同部分组成的）混合物，合成物，综合体

examples.en: The main character in her latest novel is a composite of several public figures of that era.
examples.zh: 她那新小说中的主人公是集那个时期几位公众人物形象于一身的综合形象。

examples.en: Scientists have put together a composite picture of what the earth's crust is like.
examples.zh: 科学家们拼出了一幅地壳形状复合图。

examples.en: The bodies of these racing cars are moulded from the same composite that is used for making aircraft.
examples.zh: 这些赛车的车体所用的是一种与制造飞机机身相同的复合材料。

definitions.en: a chemical that combines two or more elements

examples.en: Salt is a compound of sodium and chlorine.
examples.zh: 盐是钠和氯的化合物。

examples.en: Many fertilizers contain nitrogen compounds.
examples.zh: 许多化学肥料都含有氮化合物。

examples.en: Then there was his manner, a curious compound of humour and severity.
examples.zh: 还有他的态度，那是一种幽默与严厉的奇异混合。

examples.en: 'Bodyguard' and 'floppy disk' are two examples of compounds.
examples.zh: bodyguard 和 floppy disk 是两个复合词。

definitions.en: an area surrounded by fences or walls that contains a group of buildings

examples.en: The gates opened and the troops marched into their compound.
examples.zh: 大门开了，部队进入他们的大院。

examples.en: The embassy compound has been closed to the public because of a bomb threat.
examples.zh: 因受到炸弹威胁，使馆区停止对公众开放。

definitions.en: to make a problem or difficult situation worse

examples.en: Her terror was compounded by the feeling that she was being watched.
examples.zh: 她有种被人监视的感觉，这加剧了她的恐惧感。

examples.en: His financial problems were compounded when he unexpectedly lost his job.
examples.zh: 他意外地失去了工作，这使他手头更紧了。

examples.en: Severe drought has compounded food shortages in the region.
examples.zh: 严重的干旱加剧了该地区的食品短缺问题。

definitions.en: to mix two things together

examples.en: Most tyres are made of rubber compounded with other chemicals and materials.
examples.zh: 大多数轮胎都是用橡胶和其他化学制品、材料混合制成的。

definitions.en: consisting of two or more parts

examples.en: compound interest
examples.zh: 复利

examples.en: The investment fund has achieved annual compound returns of 18.2 percent.
examples.zh: 投资得到了18.2%的复利年回报率。

definitions.en: to understand something completely

examples.en: I fail to comprehend their attitude.
examples.zh: 我无法理解他们的态度。

examples.en: He doesn't seem to comprehend the scale of the problem
examples.zh: 他似乎没意识到问题的严重性。

examples.en: I'll never comprehend why she did what she did.
examples.zh: 我永远也不会明白她为什么那么做。

examples.en: I don't think he fully comprehends that she won't be here to help him.
examples.zh: 我看他还没有充分意识到她是不会来这里帮他的。

definitions.en: complete and including everything that is necessary
definitions.zh: 全面的；综合的；包罗万象的；详尽的

examples.en: We offer you a comprehensive training in all aspects of the business.
examples.zh: 我们向你提供这个行业各方面的综合培训。

examples.en: Is this list comprehensive or are there some names missing?
examples.zh: 这份名单是完整的还是遗漏了一些名字？

examples.en: He has written a fully comprehensive guide to Rome.
examples.zh: 他写了一本内容详尽的罗马旅游指南。

examples.en: The two governments have agreed to engage in a comprehensive dialogue to resolve the problem.
If you are a young, inexperienced driver, it is worth having comprehensive insurance.
The book gives a comprehensive account of the English Civil War.
This is a comprehensive guide to the birds of the British Isles.
We gave the house a comprehensive spring clean.

definitions.en: able to be pressed into a smaller space

examples.en: compressible gas
examples.zh: 可压缩气体

examples.en: Because plastic bags are light and compressible, they constitute only 2 percent of landfill.
examples.zh: 塑料袋因为很轻并且可以压缩，只占填埋垃圾的百分之二。

examples.en: Files are compressible in some applications, which saves memory.
examples.zh: 文件在某些​​应用程序中是可压缩的，从而节省内存。

examples.en: The degree to which the data is compressible depends on how many repeating patterns the data has.
examples.zh: 数据的可压缩程度取决于其中包含多少重复模式。

examples.en: Water is far less compressible than air.
Hot dogs are the biggest choking culprits because they are cylindrical, compressible, and about the size of a young child's airway.
The polygon can be encoded in a digital, compact, compressible format
Built in compression ensures that highly compressible files are stored efficiently.

definitions.en: an agreement in an argument in which the people involved reduce their demands or change their opinion in order to agree

examples.en: It is hoped that a compromise will be reached in today's talks.
examples.zh: 希望今天的会谈能达成妥协。

examples.en: In a compromise between management and unions, a four percent pay rise was agreed in return for an increase in productivity.
examples.zh: 资方与工会达成和解，同意以加薪4%来作为提高生产力的回报。

examples.en: The government has said that there will be no compromise with terrorists.
examples.zh: 政府声称不会对恐怖分子让步。

examples.en: The talks are aiming at a compromise.
The leader's aggressive stance seems to have foreclosed any chance of diplomatic compromise.
I'm hopeful that we can reach a compromise.
Negotiations are a two-way thing - both sides have to come to a compromise.
A compromise has to be reached between all the powerful vested interests before any restoration work in the city can take place.

definitions.en: to accept that you will reduce your demands or change your opinion in order to reach an agreement with someone

examples.en: Party unity is threatened when members will not compromise.
examples.zh: 成员们不愿妥协，党内团结受到了威胁。

examples.en: Well, you want $400 and I say $300, so let's compromise at/on $350.
examples.zh: 好吧，你要价400美元，我说300美元，那我们就来个折中价350美元。

examples.en: Somebody will have to compromise if we are to break the deadlock between the two warring factions.
The reluctance of either side to compromise means that the talks are doomed to fail.
The antagonists in this dispute are quite unwilling to compromise.
The dispute had reached an impasse, as neither side would compromise.
They have become irreconcilable, with both sides refusing to compromise any further.

definitions.en: to allow your principles to be less strong or your standards or morals to be lower

examples.en: Don't compromise your beliefs/principles for the sake of being accepted.
examples.zh: 不要为了得到别人的认同就放弃了你自己的信仰／原则。

examples.en: If we back down on this issue, our reputation will be compromised.
examples.zh: 如果我们在这个问题上打退堂鼓的话，会有损我们的声誉。

examples.en: His political career ended when he compromised himself by accepting bribes.
examples.zh: 他的政治生涯因他收受贿赂而宣告结束。

definitions.en: to risk having a harmful effect on something

examples.en: We would never compromise the safety of our passengers.
examples.zh: 我们永远不会做有损乘客安全的事。

definitions.en: to put things together as a connected series
definitions.zh: 使连接,使连锁，把…连成一串

examples.en: The filename is a series of concatenated words with no spaces.
examples.zh: 文件名是一串单词连接在一起，之间没有空格。

definitions.en: to prevent something from being seen or known about

examples.en: The listening device was concealed in a pen.
examples.zh: 窃听装置藏在了笔里。

examples.en: I tried to conceal my surprise when she told me her age.
examples.zh: 她告诉我她的年龄时，我尽力掩饰自己的惊讶。

examples.en: It was said that the police concealed vital evidence.
examples.zh: 据说警方隐瞒了关键的证据。

examples.en: Is there something you're concealing from me?
examples.zh: 你是不是有事瞒着我？

examples.en: To conceal his identity, the man's voice has been dubbed over .
Her brave smile concealed a deep hurt.
Her saintly manner concealed a devious mind.
He had a microphone concealed in his pocket.
The sewage tank was concealed behind a line of bushes.

definitions.en: to imagine something

examples.en: I think my uncle still conceives of me as a four-year-old.
examples.zh: 我觉得我叔叔仍然把我看成是4岁的小孩。

examples.en: He couldn't conceive of a time when he would have no job.
examples.zh: 他不能想象有一天他会没有工作。

examples.en: I can't conceive (= it is too shocking to imagine) how anyone could behave so cruelly.
examples.zh: 我不能想象有人竟然能做出如此残酷的事情。

examples.en: I find it hard to conceive (= it is too shocking to imagine) that people are still treated so badly.
examples.zh: 我觉得难以想象人们竟然还受着那样的虐待。

definitions.en: to invent a plan or an idea
definitions.zh: 构想，想出，设想出（计划或主意）

examples.en: He conceived the plot for this film while he was still a student.
examples.zh: 他还是个学生的时候就已经构想出了这部影片的情节。

examples.en: The exhibition was conceived by the museum's director.
examples.zh: 这次展览是由博物馆馆长构思设计的。

definitions.en: to become pregnant, or to cause a baby to begin to form

examples.en: Do you know exactly when you conceived?
examples.zh: 你知道自己怀孕的确切时间吗？

examples.en: The baby was conceived in March, so will be born in December.
examples.zh: 这孩子是3月份怀上的，所以会在12月出生。

definitions.en: to direct your attention or your efforts towards a particular activity, subject, or problem
definitions.zh: 专注，专心；全神贯注；集中（注意力）

examples.en: Come on, concentrate! We don't have all day to do this.
examples.zh: 快点儿，集中注意力！我们可没有一整天时间来干这事。

examples.en: I can't concentrate on my work with all that noise.
examples.zh: 那么吵，我没法集中精力。

examples.en: I find running concentrates the mind (= helps me to think).
examples.zh: 我发现跑步能使我集中心思。

examples.en: I'm going to concentrate on my writing for a while.
examples.zh: 我要专心写作一会儿。

examples.en: The company is concentrating (its resources) on developing new products.
examples.zh: 公司正在集中力量开发新产品。

examples.en: I couldn't concentrate on my work - my mind was on other things.
She was concentrating on her book and his voice startled her.
Just try to concentrate on hitting the ball.
I can't concentrate because of all the noise the builders are making.
It's a good film, but the plot is very complicated and you really have to concentrate.

definitions.en: to bring or come together in a large number or amount in one particular area

examples.en: Most of the country's population is concentrated in the north.
examples.zh: 该国的大多数人口都集中在北部。

examples.en: In the dry season, the animals tend to concentrate in the areas where there is water.
examples.zh: 在干旱的季节，动物常常聚集在有水的地区。

definitions.en: to make a liquid or substance stronger and reduce its size by removing water from it
a liquid from which some of the water has been removed

examples.en: fruit-juice concentrate
examples.zh: 浓缩果汁

examples.en: a mineral concentrate
examples.zh: 精矿

definitions.en: short and clear, expressing what needs to be said without unnecessary words
definitions.zh: 简洁的;简明的;简练的;简明扼要的

examples.en: Make your answers clear and concise.
examples.zh: 回答要言简意赅。

definitions.en: to end a speech, meeting, or piece of writing

examples.en: She concluded the speech by reminding us of our responsibility.
examples.zh: 她在演讲结束时提醒我们所担负的责任。

examples.en: Before I conclude, I'd like to thank you all for coming.
examples.zh: 在结束发言前，我想感谢诸位的光临。

examples.en: The concert concluded with a rousing chorus.
examples.zh: 音乐会以激动人心的大合唱结束。

examples.en: It's difficult to guess what they will conclude from the evidence.
The report concluded that there was no evidence of collusive behaviour between the banks.
Judge Keenan concluded that the surveillance had been lawful.
The meeting concluded with a prayer.
The book concludes with a plea for a change in the law.

definitions.en: to judge or decide something after thinking carefully about it
definitions.zh: 断定;决定;推断出;作出（结论）

examples.en: The jury concluded from the evidence that the defendant was innocent.
examples.zh: 陪审团依据证据判裁决告无罪。

examples.en: The discussions continued late into the night, but nothing was concluded.
examples.zh: 一直谈到深夜，可是没有作出任何决定。

definitions.en: a very hard building material made by mixing together cement, sand, small stones, and water

examples.en: reinforced concrete
examples.zh: 钢筋混凝土

examples.en: a concrete floor/path
examples.zh: 混凝土地面／道路

examples.en: a grey concrete building
examples.zh: 灰色的混凝土建筑物

examples.en: The bomb shelter has concrete walls that are three metres thick.
The foundation of the house is built from rubble overlaid with concrete.
A row of reinforced concrete pillars supports the bridge.
Weeds were pushing up through the cracks in the concrete.
Don't walk on the concrete until it has set.

definitions.en: to cover something in concrete

examples.en: Why did you concrete over that nice garden?
examples.zh: 你为什么把那个漂亮的花园铺上混凝土？

definitions.en: clear and certain, or real and existing in a form that can be seen or felt
definitions.zh: 确定的，确实的；具体的，有形的，实在的

examples.en: They think she killed her husband, but they have no concrete evidence.
examples.zh: 他们认为她杀死了自己的丈夫，但是没有确凿的证据。

examples.en: We have a general idea of what we want, but nothing concrete at the moment.
examples.zh: 我们对需要什么有个大致的想法，可是具体的东西目前还没有想好。

examples.en: It is sometimes easier to illustrate an abstract concept by analogy with something concrete.
He drew attention to the absence of concrete evidence against the defendant.
I have a suspicion that he's having an affair, though I don't have any concrete proof.

definitions.en: to criticize something or someone strongly, usually for moral reasons
definitions.zh: （通常指出于道义而）谴责，指责

examples.en: The terrorist action has been condemned as an act of barbarism and cowardice.
examples.zh: 人们谴责这一恐怖行径是野蛮和懦弱的行为。

examples.en: The film was condemned for its sexism.
examples.zh: 这部影片被指责带有性别歧视。

examples.en: She was the only politician to condemn the proposed law on moral grounds .
The president took the unusual step of altering his prepared speech in order to condemn the terrorist attack.
Her refusal to condemn the violence left her open to the charge of positive support for the campaign .
Prince Charles condemned the assertively modernist style of architecture.
The Opposition has condemned the government's proposed tax increases.

definitions.en: to admit that you have done something wrong or something that you feel guilty or bad about
definitions.zh: 坦白;供认，招认;承认（错误或罪行）

examples.en: She confessed to her husband that she had sold her wedding ring.
examples.zh: 她向丈夫坦白她卖掉了结婚戒指。

examples.en: He confessed to sleeping/having slept through most of the movie.
examples.zh: 他承认看这部电影时大部分时间都在睡觉。

examples.en: He has confessed to the murder.
examples.zh: 他供认了谋杀罪行。

examples.en: I have to confess (that) when I first met Reece I didn't think he was very bright.
examples.zh: 我得承认，我第一次见到瑞斯的时候，觉得他不是很聪明。

examples.en: It was all very confusing, I must confess.
examples.zh: 我得承认，我觉得这一切太令人费解了。

examples.en: to confess your sins
examples.zh: 忏悔你的罪过

examples.en: I have to confess I'm a bit of a sucker for musicals.
I must confess that I got my sums wrong - the house extension is costing a lot more than I expected.
The killer confessed that he often drugged his victims before he killed them.
He was granted immunity from prosecution because he confessed the names of the other spies.
He confessed that he had sinful thoughts.

definitions.en: being certain of your abilities or having trust in people, plans, or the future
definitions.zh: 自信的；有信心的；确信的；有把握的；信任的

examples.en: Be a bit more confident in yourself!
examples.zh: 你应该再自信一点！

examples.en: They don't sound confident about the future of the industry.
examples.zh: 他们听起来对该产业的未来信心不足。

examples.en: I'm confident of his skills as a manager.
examples.zh: 我对他作经纪人很有信心。

examples.en: Are you confident that enough people will attend the event?
examples.zh: 你确信会有足够的人参加这项赛事吗？

examples.en: It was a confident performance.
examples.zh: 这是一场充满自信的表演。

examples.en: She is a confident and practised speaker who always impresses her audience.
Wales are supremely confident of winning the match.
The police are confident that this new line of inquiry will lead them to the murderer.
The most important rule in life is always to appear confident.
His confident leadership inspired his followers.

definitions.en: secret or private, often in a formal, business, or military situation

examples.en: All information will be treated as strictly confidential.
examples.zh: 所有的信息都将被视作绝密。

examples.en: A person's medical records are confidential.
We have certain reasons for our decision, which have to remain confidential.
He locked the confidential documents in his filing cabinet.
The letter was marked 'Personal. Strictly confidential.'
Someone has leaked confidential government information to the press.

definitions.en: to limit an activity, person, or problem in some way

examples.en: Let's confine our discussion to the matter in question, please!
examples.zh: 请把讨论集中在正题上！

examples.en: Please confine your use of the phone to business calls.
examples.zh: 请注意电话仅限于办公用途。

examples.en: By closing the infected farms we're hoping to confine the disease to the north of the region (= stop it from spreading to other areas).
examples.zh: 通过关闭受感染的农场，我们希望把疾病限制在该地区的北部。

examples.en: The hostages had been confined for so long that they couldn't cope with the outside world.
examples.zh: 人质被关押了那么久，以致于他们对外面的世界一时适应不了。

examples.en: We know that the illness is not confined to any one group in society.
examples.zh: 我们知道这种疾病不只局限于社会的某一个群体中。

examples.en: Men have always played an active part in leading worship while women have been confined to more passive roles.
The campaign aims to dispel the prejudice that AIDS is confined to the homosexual community.
Let's confine our remarks to the facts, shall we?
We confined our research to families with only one autistic child.
It was terrible for our research to be confined by lack of money.

definitions.en: the situation in which a person or animal is kept somewhere, usually by force

examples.en: She spent most of those years under house arrest or close confinement.
examples.zh: 那些年她大部分时间都处于软禁或严密监禁之中。

definitions.en: to behave according to the usual standards of behaviour that are expected by a group or society
definitions.zh: 顺从;遵从;随大流，顺应习俗

examples.en: At our school, you were required to conform, and there was no place for originality.
examples.zh: 我们学校要求每个人循规蹈矩，容不得创新求变。

definitions.en: a situation in which a place is too blocked or crowded, causing difficulties

examples.en: These facilities are designed to ease the congestion of emergency rooms.
examples.zh: 这些设施旨在缓解急诊室的拥挤状况。

examples.en: Cases are being repeatedly delayed because of congestion in the courts.
examples.zh: 由于庭审密集，案件一再拖延。

examples.en: This spray helps to ease nasal congestion.
examples.zh: 这种喷雾剂能帮助缓解鼻腔阻塞。

examples.en: Symptoms include sneezing, congestion, and runny nose.
examples.zh: 症状包括打喷嚏、鼻腔阻塞和流鼻涕。

examples.en: He took to bed for weeks each winter with chest congestion.
examples.zh: 每年冬天，他都会因胸腔充血而卧床数周。

examples.en: Congestion in the lungs or other vital organs of the body tends to destroy the tissues of the affected parts.
examples.zh: 肺部或身体其他重要器官的充血往往会破坏受影响部位的组织。

definitions.en: a word such as 'and', 'but', 'while', or 'although' that connects words, phrases, and clauses in a sentence

examples.en: The conjunction "although" joins the two clauses in the sentence "He left, although I begged him not to."
When you write a series of nouns or adjectives, such as 'purple, green and blue', you use a conjunction before the last one, instead of a comma.

definitions.en: the situation in which events or conditions combine or happen together

examples.en: An unfortunate conjunction of circumstances led to his downfall.
examples.zh: 一连串不幸事件同时发生，导致了他的下台。

examples.en: There is a team of writers working in conjunction (with each other) on the book.
examples.zh: 有一个作家小组在合作撰写这部书。

examples.en: Mars and Venus will be in exact conjunction on 1 September.
examples.zh: 火星和金星在9月1日会正好相合。

definitions.en: a feeling or idea that is suggested by a particular word although it need not be a part of the word's meaning, or something suggested by an object or situation
definitions.zh: 内涵意义;隐含意义;联想意义

examples.en: The word "lady" has connotations of refinement and excessive femininity that some women find offensive.
examples.zh: lady 一词有优雅和过分娇柔的涵义，因此某些女性觉得该词具有冒犯意。

definitions.en: Consecutive events, numbers, etc. follow one after another without an interruption.
definitions.zh: 连续的，连贯的，不间断的

examples.en: This is the fifth consecutive weekend that I've spent working.
examples.zh: 这是我连续第五个周末加班。

examples.en: Unemployment has risen again for the third consecutive month.
Coventry City have proved untouchable this season - they've just won their sixth consecutive game.
It was United's sixth consecutive win this season.
We have visited your mother for five consecutive Christmases.
He did 300 consecutive press-ups.

definitions.en: a generally accepted opinion or decision among a group of people

examples.en: The general consensus in the office is that he can't do his job.
examples.zh: 办公室成员一致认为他无法胜任工作。

examples.en: Could we reach a consensus on this matter? Let's take a vote.
examples.zh: 我们能在这件事上达成共识吗？我们来表决吧。

examples.en: We were unable to reach a consensus about membership fees.
There is little consensus about the issue of smacking children.
We managed to get a consensus about not smoking in the office.

definitions.en: permission or agreement

examples.en: They can't publish your name without your consent.
examples.zh: 未经你的同意他们不能公布你的名字。

examples.en: Patients must be informed of the risks of the surgery and give their informed consent.
You can only come on the trip if you have your parents' written consent.
Her latest novel, by common consent, is her best yet.
examples.zh: 她最新一本小说被普遍认为是她迄今最好的作品。

examples.en: The child's parents or guardians must give their consent before she has the operation.
The agreement was terminated by mutual consent.
The airline requires children travelling alone to have a notarized letter of consent from one or both parents.
Her father's blessing represented a bestowal of consent upon her marriage.
She gave her consent to the sale of the painting.
The contract will contain provisions for the parties to obtain necessary consents from third parties.

definitions.en: to agree to do something, or to allow someone to do something

examples.en: Very reluctantly, I've consented to lend her my car.
examples.zh: 我很不情愿地答应把车借给她。

examples.en: My aunt never married because her father wouldn't consent to her marriage.
examples.zh: 我姑妈终生未嫁，因为她父亲不同意她的婚事。

definitions.en: a result of a particular action or situation, often one that is bad or not convenient
definitions.zh: （常指不好或不利的）结果，后果

examples.en: Not making a will can have serious consequences for your children and other family members.
examples.zh: 不立遗嘱可能会给你的子女或者家庭其他成员带来严重后果。

examples.en: Scientists think it is unlikely that any species will actually become extinct as a consequence of the oil spill.
examples.zh: 科学家们认为实际上不太可能会有什么物种因为石油泄漏而灭绝。

examples.en: I told the hairdresser to do what she wanted to my hair, and look at the consequences!
examples.zh: 我告诉理发师就按她的想法来做我的头发好了，可瞧瞧结果！

examples.en: Well, if you insist on eating so much, you'll have to suffer/take (= accept and deal with) the consequences!
examples.zh: 好吧，要是你坚持吃这么多，你会自食其果的！

examples.en: The money was of little consequence to Tony.
examples.zh: 这钱对于托尼来说无足轻重。

examples.en: The accident was the inevitable consequence of carelessness.
He left as a direct consequence of what she said.
The drought has had devastating consequences.
The government is trying to do more to educate the public about the consequences of drug abuse.
He always makes snap decisions and never thinks about their consequences.

definitions.en: a result of a particular action or situation, often one that is bad or not convenient
definitions.zh: （常指不好或不利的）结果，后果

examples.en: Not making a will can have serious consequences for your children and other family members.
examples.zh: 不立遗嘱可能会给你的子女或者家庭其他成员带来严重后果。

examples.en: Scientists think it is unlikely that any species will actually become extinct as a consequence of the oil spill.
examples.zh: 科学家们认为实际上不太可能会有什么物种因为石油泄漏而灭绝。

examples.en: I told the hairdresser to do what she wanted to my hair, and look at the consequences!
examples.zh: 我告诉理发师就按她的想法来做我的头发好了，可瞧瞧结果！

examples.en: Well, if you insist on eating so much, you'll have to suffer/take (= accept and deal with) the consequences!
examples.zh: 好吧，要是你坚持吃这么多，你会自食其果的！

examples.en: The money was of little consequence to Tony.
examples.zh: 这钱对于托尼来说无足轻重。

examples.en: The accident was the inevitable consequence of carelessness.
He left as a direct consequence of what she said.
The drought has had devastating consequences.
The government is trying to do more to educate the public about the consequences of drug abuse.
He always makes snap decisions and never thinks about their consequences.

definitions.en: not usually liking or trusting change, especially sudden change

examples.en: a conservative society/outlook
examples.zh: 保守的社会／观念

examples.en: Older people tend to be more conservative and a bit suspicious of anything new.
examples.zh: 上年纪的人往往很保守，对任何新的东西都有些怀疑。

examples.en: He's a very conservative dresser - he always looks like he's wearing his father's clothes!
examples.zh: 他穿着十分保守——看上去总像是穿着他父亲的衣服！

examples.en: He has a very conservative approach to management.
She's very conservative in her eating habits.
When it comes to music, my tastes are quite conservative.

definitions.en: A conservative guess or calculation is likely to be less than the real amount.

examples.en: If I said there were three million unemployed, that would be a conservative estimate.
examples.zh: 我要是说失业人数有300万的话，那是保守估计。

definitions.en: belonging to or supporting the British political party that traditionally supports business and opposes high taxes and government involvement in industry

examples.en: the Conservative Party
examples.zh: 保守党

examples.en: Conservative policies
examples.zh: 保守党奉行的政策

examples.en: a Conservative MP/government
examples.zh: 保守党议员／政府

examples.en: Did you vote Conservative at the last election?
examples.zh: 上次选举你投的是保守党的票吗？

examples.en: He was a Conservative minister in the Thatcher government.
She always votes Conservative.

definitions.en: to keep and protect something from damage, change, or waste
definitions.zh: 保护；保藏；保存；保留；节约

examples.en: To conserve electricity, we are cutting down on our heating.
examples.zh: 为了省电，我们在减少中央供热。

examples.en: The nationalists are very eager to conserve their customs and language.
examples.zh: 民族主义者非常热衷于保留他们的习俗和语言。

examples.en: I'm not being lazy - I'm just conserving my energy/strength for later.
examples.zh: 我不是在偷懒——我只是在为以后保存精力／体力。

definitions.en: a type of jam in which the fruit is whole or in large pieces

examples.en: apricot/strawberry conserve
examples.zh: 杏脯／草莓蜜饯

definitions.en: large or of noticeable importance
definitions.zh: 相当大的；相当多的；非常可观的；相当重要的

examples.en: The fire caused considerable damage to the church.
examples.zh: 那场火灾给教堂造成了重大损失。

examples.en: She's a woman of considerable abilities.
Drug trafficking is a matter of considerable concern for the entire international community .
Gymnastics is a sport that requires a considerable level of coordination.
The army is reported to be encountering considerable resistance.
A considerable amount of time and effort has gone into this exhibition.

definitions.en: to become, or cause something to become, stronger, and more certain

examples.en: The success of their major product consolidated the firm's position in the market.
examples.zh: 公司主打产品取得的成功巩固了该公司在市场上的地位。

examples.en: She hoped that marriage would consolidate their relationship.
examples.zh: 她希望婚姻能巩固他们的关系。

examples.en: The party consolidated its hold on power during its term of office.
examples.zh: 该党在执政期间加强了对政权的控制。

examples.en: The company has been expanding rapidly, and I feel it's now time to consolidate (= stop growing and make our present position stronger).
examples.zh: 公司一直在快速扩展，我觉得该是巩固的时候了。

examples.en: The two firms consolidated to form a single company.
examples.zh: 两家小公司合并成了一家公司。

definitions.en: the process of becoming or being made stronger and more certain

examples.en: The company is entering a period of consolidation.
examples.zh: 公司正进入整合期。

examples.en: Putin announced one of the most sweeping consolidations of presidential power since the fall of communism.
examples.zh: 普京宣布整合总统权力，这是自共产主义垮台以来最全面的整合措施之一。

definitions.en: the process in which businesses join together to make a single organization

examples.en: We have seen a similar consolidation of booksellers and distributors.
examples.zh: 我们也看到了书商与发行商的合并。

examples.en: Many employees have been impacted by mergers and consolidations.
examples.zh: 许多雇员的职位受到了兼并与联合的影响。

examples.en: There is no objection in principle to the consolidation of bank accounts in order to obtain better terms of business from the bank.
examples.zh: 原则上不反对合并银行账户，以从银行获得更好的商务条款。

examples.en: credit counseling firms that promise relief through the magic of debt consolidation
examples.zh: 承诺通过债务合并妙招而减免负债的信贷咨询公司

definitions.en: very noticeable or attracting attention, often in a way that is not wanted

examples.en: In China, her blonde hair was conspicuous.
examples.zh: 在中国，她的金发十分惹眼。

examples.en: He tried not to look conspicuous and moved slowly along the back of the room.
examples.zh: 他尽量不使自己显眼，沿着房间后部慢慢移动。

definitions.en: happening a lot or all the time
definitions.zh: 经常发生的;连续发生的;连续不断的

examples.en: He's in constant trouble with the police.
examples.zh: 他与警方麻烦不断。

examples.en: machines that are in constant use
examples.zh: 经常使用的机器

examples.en: I can't concentrate with Ann's constant chatter.
I have to combat this constant desire to eat chocolate.
I seem to have a constant compulsion to eat.
The constant noise drove me crazy .
The journalists were frustrated by her constant deflection of their questions.

definitions.en: staying the same, or not getting less or more

examples.en: We ran at a fairly constant speed.
examples.zh: 我们保持了比较平稳的跑动速度。

examples.en: The fridge keeps food at a constant temperature.
examples.zh: 冰箱在恒温下保存食物。

definitions.en: A constant companion or friend who is loyal to you.
definitions.zh: （伴侣或朋友）忠实的，忠贞不渝的，始终如一的

definitions.en: a particular number or amount that never changes
one of the parts that a substance or combination is made of

examples.en: What are the basic constituents of the mixture?
examples.zh: 这种混合物的基本成分是什么？

definitions.en: a voter (= person who can vote) in a particular area of the country

examples.en: The MP worked hard, always talking to his constituents and listening to their problems.
examples.zh: 这位参议员工作努力，总是跟本区选民交谈，听取他们的难处。

definitions.en: to control and limit something

examples.en: The country's progress was constrained by a leader who refused to look forward.
examples.zh: 国家领导人缺乏发展眼光限制了该国的进步。

definitions.en: If advice, criticism, or actions are constructive, they are useful and intended to help or improve something.
definitions.zh: 建设性的；有益的；有用的；积极的

examples.en: She criticized my writing, but in a way that was very constructive.
examples.zh: 她批评了我的作品，但是以一种非常建设性的方式——我受益匪浅。

examples.en: If you don't have anything constructive to say, it's better to say nothing.
examples.zh: 要是你没什么有用的话要说，最好保持安静。

definitions.en: to get information or advice from a person, book, etc. with special knowledge on a particular subject

examples.en: If the symptoms get worse, consult your doctor.
examples.zh: 如果症状恶化就要去就医。

examples.en: I'm not quite sure how to get there - I'd better consult a map.
examples.zh: 我不是很清楚怎么去那儿——我最好查查地图。

examples.en: Why didn't you consult me about this?
examples.zh: 你怎么没跟我商量这件事？

examples.en: This afternoon the president was consulting with his advisers.
examples.zh: 今天下午总统在征求顾问们的意见。

examples.en: If you have any problems, consult the FAQs on our website.
If any of these symptoms occur while you are taking the medicine, consult your doctor immediately.
If the pain persists, consult a doctor.
They consulted the village elders.
I'll have to consult my colleagues about the proposed changes.

definitions.en: someone who advises people on a particular subject

examples.en: a management/financial/computer consultant
examples.zh: 管理／财务／计算机顾问

examples.en: a firm of public relations consultants
examples.zh: 公关顾问公司

examples.en: We ought to hire a public relations consultant to help improve our image.
The doctor gave him a referral to the consultant.
Eventually they found a consultant they felt they could do business with .
She set up in business as a management consultant.
She is a feng shui consultant.

definitions.en: the amount used or eaten

examples.en: As a nation, our consumption of junk food is horrifying.
examples.zh: 我们整个国家对垃圾食品的消费量十分惊人。

examples.en: We need to cut down on our fuel consumption by having fewer cars on the road.
examples.zh: 我们需要通过减少路上行驶的车辆以降低燃料消耗。

examples.en: The meat was clearly unfit for human consumption (= not safe for people to eat).
examples.zh: 这肉显然不适合给人食用。

examples.en: These products are not for national consumption, but for export.
examples.zh: 这些产品只供出口不供国内消费。

examples.en: This memo is for internal consumption only.
examples.zh: 这份备忘录仅供内部使用。

examples.en: The movie was not intended for public consumption.
examples.zh: 这部电影不打算公映。

examples.en: The commonest cause of cirrhosis is alcohol consumption.
In this period, there were 974 outbreaks of communicable disease attributed to the consumption of raw milk.
The new exhaust system, it is claimed, will lower fuel consumption.
She said that industrialized countries should reduce their gluttonous consumption of oil.
The inspector declared the meat fit for human consumption .

definitions.en: old-fashioned for tuberculosis (= a serious disease of the lungs)
existing or happening now, and therefore seeming modern

examples.en: contemporary music/literature/art/fashion
examples.zh: 当代音乐／文学／艺术／时尚

examples.en: Although the play was written hundreds of years ago, it still has a contemporary feel to it.
examples.zh: 这个剧本尽管写于几百年前，但读来仍具现代感。

examples.en: I wanted to update my kitchen and put in something more contemporary.
The contemporary dance scene is flourishing in Britain at the moment.
The band's sound is very contemporary.
I thought the contemporary dialogue sounded a little out of place.
She gave a series of lectures on contemporary British writers.
Contemporary psychology tells us that a person's mental health is supported by having good social networks.

definitions.en: belonging to the same period, or to a stated period in the past

examples.en: Almost all of the contemporary accounts of the event have been lost.
examples.zh: 几乎所有关于这一事件的同时期的记载都遗失了。

examples.en: Most of the writers he was contemporary with were interested in the same subjects.
examples.zh: 和他同时代的大多数作家都对同样的题材感兴趣。

examples.en: They are very similar artists, but they are not contemporary.
Contemporary readers would have understood these political references very clearly.

definitions.en: someone living during the same period as someone else

examples.en: Was he a contemporary of Shakespeare's?
examples.zh: 他和莎士比亚是同时代的人吗？

examples.en: She didn't mix with her contemporaries, preferring the company of older people.
examples.zh: 她不和同龄人在一起，而是喜欢和年长的人相处。

definitions.en: a legal document that states and explains a formal agreement between two different people or groups, or the agreement itself

examples.en: a contract of employment
examples.zh: 雇用合同

examples.en: a temporary/building contract
examples.zh: 临时／建筑合同

examples.en: They could take legal action against you if you break (the terms of) the contract.
examples.zh: 如果你违反合同（条款），他们可以起诉你。

examples.en: My solicitor is drawing up (= writing) a contract.
examples.zh: 我的律师正在起草一份合同。

examples.en: Don't sign/enter into any contract before examining its conditions carefully.
examples.zh: 仔细核查合同条款之前，不要签定任何合同。

examples.en: They're the firm of architects who won the contract to design the Museum of Fine Art extension.
examples.zh: 他们就是赢得国家美术馆扩建工程设计合同的那家建筑公司。

examples.en: They used independent miners who worked under contract with the mining companies.
The contract between the two companies will expire at the end of the year.
They will only agree to sign the contract if certain conditions are met.
I'm on a temporary contract and have little financial security .
Under the terms of their contract, employees must give three months' notice if they leave.
Her firm have just won a cleaning contract worth £3 million.

definitions.en: to make or become shorter or narrower or generally smaller in size

examples.en: In spoken English, "do not" often contracts to "don't".
examples.zh: 在英语口语中，do not 常常缩略成 don't。

examples.en: As it cooled, the metal contracted.
examples.zh: 金属冷却后收缩了。

examples.en: A recession is a period when the economy is contracting.
examples.zh: 经济衰退期是指经济萎缩的一个时期。

examples.en: Agricultural output has contracted by 2.3 percent.
examples.zh: 农业产量下降了百分之二点三。

definitions.en: to catch or become ill with a disease

examples.en: He contracted malaria while he was travelling.
examples.zh: 他旅行时染上了疟疾。

definitions.en: to make a legal agreement with someone to do work or to have work done for you

examples.en: Our company was contracted to build shelters for the homeless.
examples.zh: 我们公司刚刚签订合同，为无家可归的人建造收容所。

definitions.en: (of people) to say the opposite of what someone else has said, or (of one fact or statement) to be so different from another fact or statement that one of them must be wrong
definitions.zh: 反驳，否定;（事实或声明）（与…）相矛盾，（与…）有抵触

examples.en: If you're both going to lie, at least stick to the same story and don't contradict each other!
examples.zh: 如果你们俩都要撒谎，至少也要口径一致，不要互相矛盾！

examples.en: He kept contradicting himself when we were arguing - I think he was a bit confused.
examples.zh: 我们争论时他一直自相矛盾——我想他有点糊涂了。

examples.en: How dare you contradict (me)!
examples.zh: 你怎么敢顶撞我！

examples.en: Recent evidence has contradicted established theories on this subject.
examples.zh: 关于这一问题，最近发现的证据与已有理论相抵触。

definitions.en: to arrange a situation or event, or arrange for something to happen, using clever planning

examples.en: Couldn't you contrive a meeting between them? I think they'd really like each other.
examples.zh: 你不能为他们安排一次会面吗？我觉得他们真的互有好感。

examples.en: Somehow she contrived to get tickets for the concert.
examples.zh: 她不知通过什么方式搞到了音乐会的门票。

examples.en: Do you think you could contrive something for hanging my clothes on until I can get a wardrobe?
examples.zh: 你能设计个东西让我在买到衣橱前挂衣服吗？

definitions.en: causing disagreement or discussion

examples.en: a controversial issue/decision/speech/figure
examples.zh: 有争议的问题／决议／讲话／数字

examples.en: The book was very controversial.
examples.zh: 这本书很有争议。

examples.en: They hold widely divergent opinions on controversial issues like abortion.
Eugenics was the central, and most controversial, part of his social philosophy.
The official censors have excised the controversial sections of the report.
Her controversial speech was punctuated with noisy interjections from the audience.
She's been in the limelight recently, following the release of her controversial new film.

definitions.en: If lines, roads, or paths converge, they move towards the same point where they join or meet.
definitions.zh: （几条线或道路）会合，交会，汇合

examples.en: The paths all converge at the main gate of the park.
examples.zh: 这几条小路都在公园正门处交会。

examples.en: Due to roadworks, three lanes of traffic have to converge into two.
examples.zh: 由于修路，3条车道不得不合并成了两条。

examples.en: Ambulances, police cars, and fire engines all converged on the scene.
examples.zh: 救护车、警车和消防车都聚集到现场。

examples.en: 100,000 people are expected to converge on the town for the festival.
examples.zh: 预计有10万人聚集镇上庆祝节日。

definitions.en: the fact that two or more things, ideas, etc. become similar or come together
definitions.zh: （观点等）趋于一致，趋同;会合，聚集

examples.en: a convergence of interests/opinions/ideas
examples.zh: 利益／观点／想法趋同

examples.en: The convergence of pop-cultural trends and technological progress gave us camera phones and the "selfie".
examples.zh: 流行文化趋势和技术进步的结合让我们有了照相手机和“自拍”。

definitions.en: the opposite

examples.en: In the US, you drive on the right-hand side of the road, but in the UK the converse applies.
examples.zh: 在美国汽车靠右行驶，但在英国却正好相反。

examples.en: However, the converse of this theory may also be true.
examples.zh: 可是，该原理的逆命题也有可能成立。

definitions.en: opposite

examples.en: a converse effect/opinion/argument
examples.zh: 相反的效果／观点／论点

definitions.en: to have a conversation with someone

examples.en: She's so shy that conversing with her can be pretty hard.
examples.zh: 她那么害羞，跟她交谈很难。

definitions.en: in an opposite way

examples.en: Poor health is accepted as an attribute of normal ageing. Conversely, youth is depicted as a time of vitality and good health.
examples.zh: 身体不好被认为是正常衰老的一个属性。 相反，青春被形容为充满活力和健康的时期。

examples.en: The staff of any organisation are its best ambassadors or conversely may be its worst.
examples.zh: 任何组织的员工都是其最好的大使，反之也可能是最差的。

examples.en: Part of the projected image can fill the whole of the frame or conversely the image can be reduced to fill only part of it.
Are there any parts of your story that you're particularly proud of, or conversely, any where you think it really didn't work?

People who are driven to climb corporate ladders have parents who believe ladder climbing is important. Conversely, people who search in their careers for stability and structure acquire these values in their childhood.

definitions.en: curved or swelling out

examples.en: a convex lens/mirror
examples.zh: 凸透镜／凸面镜

definitions.en: to express a thought, feeling, or idea so that it is understood by other people
definitions.zh: 表达，传达（思想、感情或想法）

examples.en: His poetry conveys a great sense of religious devotion.
examples.zh: 他的诗歌表达了献身宗教的强烈感情。

examples.en: Please convey our condolences to the family.
examples.zh: 请向家人转达我们的哀思。

examples.en: I tried to convey in my speech how grateful we all were for his help.
examples.zh: 在发言中，我竭力地表达我们大家对他所给予帮助的感激之情。

examples.en: You don't want to convey the impression that we're not interested.
examples.zh: 你不想给人留下我们不感兴趣的印象吧。

examples.en: Few journalists have managed to convey the full horror of the situation.
What the book does convey very successfully is the horror of war.
No report can convey the unspeakable suffering that this war has caused.
Words alone cannot convey the untold misery endured by people in these refugee camps.
With a single wag of her finger she managed to convey her total disapproval.

definitions.en: to take or carry someone or something to a particular place

examples.en: The goods are usually conveyed by rail.
examples.zh: 这种货物通常通过铁路运输。

examples.en: Could you convey a message to Mr Merrick for me, please?
examples.zh: 你能替我给梅里克先生传个口信吗？

definitions.en: to persuade someone or make someone certain

examples.en: He managed to convince the jury of his innocence.
examples.zh: 他使陪审团相信他是无辜的。

examples.en: It's useless trying to convince her (that) she doesn't need to lose any weight.
examples.zh: 要让她相信她无需减肥是徒劳的。

examples.en: I hope this will convince you to change your mind.
examples.zh: 我希望这会说服你改变主意。

examples.en: Agricultural companies have failed to convince consumers that GM foods are safe.
His doctor tried to convince him that he wasn't really ill and that it was all in the mind.
Autosuggestion is the power of mind over matter - if you convince yourself that you are cured, you will be.
It took a lot of persuasion to convince the committee of the advantages of the new scheme.
Sometimes my imagination runs away with me and I convince myself that they are having an affair.

definitions.en: a group of vehicles or ships that travel together, especially for protection

examples.en: A convoy of trucks containing supplies was sent to the famine area.
examples.zh: 一支满载补给品的车队被派往饥荒严重的地区。

examples.en: Shall we all drive to the party in convoy so we don't get lost?
examples.zh: 我们结队开车去参加聚会，免得迷路，好吗？

definitions.en: to travel with a vehicle or group of people to make certain that they arrive safely

examples.en: Two tanks convoyed the trucks across the border.
examples.zh: 两辆坦克护送卡车穿越边境。

definitions.en: to make many different things work effectively as a whole

examples.en: We need someone to coordinate the whole campaign.
examples.zh: 我们需要有个人来协调整个活动。

examples.en: A number of charities are coordinating their efforts to distribute food to the region.
examples.zh: 几家慈善机构通力协作为该地区分发食品。

examples.en: Right now I'm having problems co-ordinating thought and speech.
They co-ordinated diaries and made the event happen.

definitions.en: to match or look attractive together

examples.en: The bed linen coordinates with the bedroom curtains.
examples.zh: 床单和卧室的窗帘很相配。

examples.en: a coordinating jacket and skirt
examples.zh: 搭配得体的上衣和裙子

examples.en: One approach is to decide on a colour theme, perhaps to co-ordinate with the room in which the pictures will hang.
To co-ordinate with the modern kitchen, the cooker is finished in dove grey.
Take a lightweight jacket and co-ordinating trousers or skirt.

definitions.en: one of a pair of numbers and/or letters that show the exact position of a point on a map or graph

examples.en: Put in the GPS coordinates and zoom in on the map.
The pilot had been given the wrong co-ordinates.
Students learn the fundamentals of graphs and how X and Y coordinates work.
Users can download the coordinates and find out how far they are from the nearest supply.
They use GPS to lock in the helicopter's co-ordinates while flying over a field so they can easily return to exactly the same spot.
I took a note of the co-ordinates for the rendezvous.

definitions.en: clothes, especially for women, that are made in matching colours or styles so that they can be worn together:

examples.en: Her usual style is sophisticated coordinates for day wear.

definitions.en: a line of police, soldiers, vehicles, etc. positioned around a particular area in order to prevent people from entering it

examples.en: There was a police cordon around the building.
examples.zh: 大楼周围设了警戒线。

definitions.en: a stone in a corner of a building, especially one with the date when the building was made or other writing on it

examples.en: In most countries, the family unit is still the cornerstone of society.
examples.zh: 在大多数国家，家庭仍然是社会的基础单位。

definitions.en: something that results from something else

examples.en: Unfortunately, violence is the inevitable corollary of such a revolutionary change in society.
examples.zh: 不幸的是，这种社会大变革必然会导致暴力事件。

definitions.en: relating to a large company

examples.en: corporate finance
examples.zh: 公司财务

examples.en: a corporate merger
examples.zh: 公司合并

examples.en: corporate executives
examples.zh: 公司主管

examples.en: All adults take corporate responsibility for the upbringing of the tribe's children.
examples.zh: 部落里所有成年人集体承担抚养孩子的责任。

examples.en: The trend towards downshifting, where employees swap the stress of corporate life for more quality time, tempted 6% of the UK workforce last year.
The company's guests at Ascot are entertained in the corporate hospitality area.
At about this time the new ideas about 'corporate management' had begun to infiltrate into local government.
Once he started at Paramount in 1967, he moved rapidly up the corporate ladder.
Protesters have called the building a monument to corporate greed.

definitions.en: a collection of written or spoken material stored on a computer and used to find out how language is used

examples.en: All the dictionary examples are taken from a corpus of billions of words.
examples.zh: 字典中的所有例子都取自一个收录了数十亿词汇的语料库。

definitions.en: a collection of a single writer's work, or of writing about a particular subject
a body or the main part of an organ
intended to improve a situation

examples.en: corrective measures/action
examples.zh: 纠正措施／行为

examples.en: corrective surgery
examples.zh: 矫正手术

definitions.en: something that improves a situation
definitions.zh: 纠正物；矫正物，起矫正作用的东西

examples.en: This European report on the internet provides a corrective to the usual US-based views.
examples.zh: 网上的这篇欧洲报道矫正了一般以美国为基础的观点。

definitions.en: dishonestly using your position or power to get an advantage, especially for money
definitions.zh: 贪赃舞弊的;以权谋私的;腐败的

examples.en: Both companies are under investigation for corrupt practices.
examples.zh: 两家公司都在接受腐败行为调查。

examples.en: The whole system was corrupt - every official she approached wanted money before helping her.
examples.zh: 整个体系都腐败了——她接触的所有官员在帮她之前都索要钱财。

examples.en: a corrupt society
examples.zh: 道德败坏的社会

examples.en: He called for checks to discover whether corrupt officials are being bribed to connive in shoddy construction.
A powerful and corrupt elite has bled this country dry.
One election cannot erase 65 years of a corrupt one-party political process.
Their accusations of corruption are hypocritical - they have been just as corrupt themselves.
The old corrupt, totalitarian regime was overthrown.

definitions.en: When information on a computer becomes corrupt, it cannot be used because it has changed and become wrong.
definitions.zh: （计算机上的信息）被破坏的，有错误的

examples.en: corrupt data
examples.zh: 损坏的数据

examples.en: a corrupt file
examples.zh: 被破坏的文件

definitions.en: to make someone or something become dishonest or immoral
definitions.zh: 腐蚀…;使腐化堕落;使道德败坏

examples.en: The study claimed that violence on television corrupts the minds of children.
examples.zh: 研究称电视上的暴力镜头腐蚀了孩子们的思想。

definitions.en: to change information on a computer so that it is wrong and cannot be used

examples.en: Most of the data on the hard drive was corrupted when the power went out.
examples.zh: 硬盘上的大多数数据在停电时损坏了。

definitions.en: illegal, bad, or dishonest behaviour, especially by people in positions of power
definitions.zh: （尤指当权者的）贪污，腐败，堕落

examples.en: The film is about a young police officer and his struggle to expose corruption in the force.
examples.zh: 影片讲述的是一个年轻警察的故事，表现了他是如何克服困难揭露警方内部腐败的。

examples.en: Political corruption is widespread throughout the country.
examples.zh: 政治腐败在全国蔓延。

examples.en: The investigation revealed political chicanery and corruption at the highest levels.
It's a clear case of corruption.
He caught the public imagination as a crusader against corruption.
Recent discoveries about corruption have done serious damage to the company's reputation.
He resigned from the committee in disgust at the corruption.

definitions.en: in language, a word whose original form has been changed

examples.en: The swear word "bloody" is wrongly thought by some to be a corruption of the words "by our Lady".
examples.zh: 有些人错误地认为咒骂用语 bloody 是 by our Lady 的变体。

definitions.en: the fact of information on a computer being changed so that it is wrong and cannot be used

examples.en: data corruption
examples.zh: 数据毁损

definitions.en: to reduce or remove the effect of something unwanted by producing an opposite effect

examples.en: Drinking a lot of water counteracts the dehydrating effects of hot weather.
examples.zh: 大量饮水可缓解炎热天气引起的脱水。

definitions.en: Something that is counterintuitive does not happen in the way you would expect it to.
definitions.zh: 反直觉的;与预期相反的

examples.en: It may seem counterintuitive to open a shop in the middle of a recession.
Steering a sailboat is counter-intuitive - you push the tiller the opposite way to the way you want to go.
examples.zh: 控制帆船方向与通常的习惯是不一样的——推拉舵柄的方向与要去的航向正好相反。

examples.en: His careful and counterintuitive analysis of the market brought him great success.
It seems like a counterintuitive way to run a business.
He steered the company in a surprising direction and made some counterintuitive choices.
This decision might sound counter-intuitive now, but it worked.

definitions.en: a person or thing that has the same purpose as another one in a different place or organization
definitions.zh: （与不同地方或组织的人或物）作用相同者，相对应者

examples.en: The prime minister is to meet his European counterparts to discuss the war against drugs.
examples.zh: 首相将会见欧洲各国首脑就打击毒品犯罪进行商讨。

definitions.en: to force a lot of things into a small space

examples.en: Eight children were crammed into the back of the car.
examples.zh: 8个孩子被塞进了汽车的后座。

examples.en: The room was packed and we were crammed against the door.
examples.zh: 房间里挤得水泄不通，我们被挤靠在门边。

examples.en: I had to cram three countries into a week's business trip.
examples.zh: 我出差一周跑了3个国家。

definitions.en: to try to learn a lot very quickly before an exam

examples.en: She's cramming for her history exam.
examples.zh: 为应付历史考试她拼命突击。

definitions.en: a rude word meaning unpleasant or of very bad quality
definitions.zh: 讨厌的，没价值的；蹩脚的，劣质的

examples.en: He had a series of crappy jobs.
examples.zh: 他做了一连串没意思的工作。

definitions.en: a standard by which you judge, decide about, or deal with something
definitions.zh: （作出评判、决定或处理的）标准，准则

examples.en: The Health Service should not be judged by financial criteria alone.
examples.zh: 公共医疗卫生服务不应仅仅以财务状况的好坏为标准来评判。

examples.en: Patients can now be compulsorily detained in hospital only under tightly drawn criteria.
The Health Service should not be judged by financial criteria alone.
We're looking for a very specific sort of person and this woman seems to fulfil all of our criteria.
More specific and less subjective criteria should be used in selecting people for promotion within the company.

definitions.en: a standard by which you judge, decide about, or deal with something
definitions.zh: （作出评判、决定或处理的）标准，准则

examples.en: The Health Service should not be judged by financial criteria alone.
examples.zh: 公共医疗卫生服务不应仅仅以财务状况的好坏为标准来评判。

examples.en: Patients can now be compulsorily detained in hospital only under tightly drawn criteria.
The Health Service should not be judged by financial criteria alone.
We're looking for a very specific sort of person and this woman seems to fulfil all of our criteria.
More specific and less subjective criteria should be used in selecting people for promotion within the company.

definitions.en: the act of saying that something or someone is bad

examples.en: The designs for the new mosque have attracted widespread criticism.
examples.zh: 人们普遍对新清真寺的设计不满意。

examples.en: I have a few criticisms to make about your speech.
examples.zh: 对于你的讲话我有几点不能苟同。

examples.en: literary criticism
examples.zh: 文学评论

examples.en: If you have any constructive (= helpful) criticism of the project, I'd be glad to hear it.
examples.zh: 如果你对这个方案有什么建设性的意见，我很乐意听一听。

examples.en: Her lack of interest in the project has provoked sharp criticism from colleagues.
You must learn not to overreact to criticism.
The government is currently facing severe criticism.
I think he felt discouraged because of all the criticism he'd received.
His report contained serious criticisms of the finance director and of the entire board of management.

definitions.en: a report of something such as a political situation or system, or a person's work or ideas, that examines it and provides a judgment, especially a negative one
definitions.zh: 评论文章，评论（常指否定的评论）

examples.en: a Marxist critique of neo-liberal policy
examples.zh: 以马克思主义观点评论新自由政策的文章

definitions.en: to give an opinion or judgment about a piece or work, book, film, etc.

examples.en: Students take turns critiquing each other's work.
examples.zh: 学生们轮流评论彼此的作品。

definitions.en: extremely important or necessary
definitions.zh: 至关重要的；关键的；决定性的

examples.en: a crucial decision/question
examples.zh: 至关重要的决定／问题

examples.en: Her work has been crucial to the project's success.
examples.zh: 她的工作对于这个项目的成功至关重要。

examples.en: It is crucial that the problem is tackled immediately.
examples.zh: 关键是要马上着手处理这一问题。

examples.en: Price will be a crucial factor in the success of this new product.
The motif of betrayal and loss is crucial in all these stories.
The turning point in her political career came when she was chosen to fight a crucial by-election.
During the trial, the prosecution was accused of withholding crucial evidence from the defence.
The Panama Canal provides a crucial shipping link between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans.

definitions.en: If something is crunchy, it is firm and makes a short loud noise when it is eaten, pressed, etc.
definitions.zh: （食物）脆生生的，松脆的，鲜脆的

examples.en: The toast was delicious, hot, and crunchy.
The chips have a crunchy coating.
She heard footsteps in the crunchy snow.
The pie filling was sweet and the crust deliciously crunchy.
The cereal stays crunchy even with milk poured over it.
The seeds add a lovely crunchy texture to the salad.
The bread has a crunchy outside and a soft, chewy inside.
It sounded like he was eating something crunchy.
The dry leaves were crunchy underfoot.
The tracks feature drums that sound as if they were recorded in a tin shed, crunchy guitars and grinding bass lines.
Distortion pedals produce a crunchier and grittier tone.

definitions.en: the most important or serious part of a matter, problem, or argument

examples.en: The crux of the country's economic problems is its foreign debt.
examples.zh: 外债是该国经济问题的症结所在。

examples.en: The issue of an arms embargo will be at the crux of the negotiations in Geneva.
examples.zh: 武器禁运将是日内瓦谈判的核心议题。

definitions.en: If a liquid crystallizes, it turns into crystals.

examples.en: The event helped to crystallize my thoughts.
examples.zh: 这一事件使我理清了思绪。

definitions.en: someone who has done something wrong

examples.en: Police hope the public will help them to find the culprits.
examples.zh: 警方希望公众能协助他们抓到罪犯。

examples.en: Children in this country are getting much too fat, and sugar and sweets are the main culprits.
examples.zh: 该国儿童过于肥胖，糖和甜食是罪魁祸首。

definitions.en: awkward because of being large, heavy, or not effective

examples.en: cumbersome equipment
examples.zh: 笨重的设备

examples.en: cumbersome bureaucracy
examples.zh: 繁琐的官僚体制

definitions.en: increasing by one addition after another

examples.en: The cumulative effect of using so many chemicals on the land could be disastrous.
examples.zh: 使用大量化学物质经年累月会对土地造成灾难性的后果。

definitions.en: interested in learning about people or things around you

examples.en: I was curious to know what would happen next.
examples.zh: 我很好奇想知道接下来会发生什么事。

examples.en: Babies are curious about everything around them.
examples.zh: 婴儿对周围的一切都很好奇。

examples.en: "Why did you ask?" "I was just curious."
examples.zh: “你为什么要问?”“好奇而已。”

examples.en: A crowd of curious onlookers soon gathered to see what was happening.
The visitors were surrounded on all sides by curious children.
Shall I show you what I bought her? I can see you're curious.
Tell me, I'm curious, what exactly was going on in his office just then?
"Why do you keeping asking about my neighbours?" "Oh, I'm just curious."

definitions.en: strange and unusual
definitions.zh: 稀奇的，古怪的，不寻常的

examples.en: There was a curious-looking man standing outside.
examples.zh: 外面站着个长相古怪的男人。

examples.en: A curious thing happened to me yesterday.
examples.zh: 昨天我碰到件怪事。

examples.en: It's curious (that) Billy hasn't phoned when he promised he would.
examples.zh: 比利说他会打电话来，可是还没打过来，真蹊跷。

definitions.en: a person with responsibility for protecting or taking care of something or keeping something in good condition
definitions.zh: 监护人；监管人；看护人；保管人

examples.en: the custodian of a museum/castle
examples.zh: 博物馆／城堡的看护人

examples.en: She sees herself as a custodian of the public's morals.
examples.zh: 她视自己为公共道德的捍卫者。

definitions.en: the legal right or duty to care for someone or something, especially a child after its parents have separated or died
definitions.zh: 照管，监护;（尤指对父母离异或去世的孩子的）监护权

examples.en: The court awarded/granted/gave custody of the child to the father.
examples.zh: 法庭将孩子的监护权判给了父亲。

examples.en: The mother got/received custody of the child.
examples.zh: 母亲得到了孩子的监护权。

examples.en: The parents were given joint custody.
examples.zh: 孩子的父母获取了共同监护权。

definitions.en: the state of being kept in prison, especially while waiting to go to court for trial
definitions.zh: （尤指等候法庭审判期间的）监禁，拘留

examples.en: The suspect is now in custody.
examples.zh: 嫌疑犯正被看押候审。

examples.en: You will be remanded in custody until your trial.
examples.zh: 你会一直被看押到受审的那一天。

definitions.en: usual

examples.en: She's not her customary cheerful self today.
examples.zh: 她今天不像往常那样乐呵呵的。

definitions.en: traditional

examples.en: In my village, it is customary for a girl to take her mother's name.
examples.zh: 在我们村，女孩按习俗随母姓。

definitions.en: to hang loosely, or to hold something so that it hangs loosely
definitions.zh: （使）垂下；（使）悬荡；悬挂；提着来回摆动

examples.en: Loose electric wires were dangling from the wall.
examples.zh: 松散的电线从墙上垂了下来。

examples.en: He dangled the puppet in front of the children.
examples.zh: 他在孩子们面前耍木偶。

examples.en: I've tried dangling all kinds of offers in front of him to get him to work harder at school, but nothing works.
examples.zh: 为了让他学习用心点儿，我曾试过种种许诺，可是根本不起任何作用。

definitions.en: socially acceptable or good
definitions.zh: 正派的；体面的，正经的；像样的，相当不错的

examples.en: Everyone should be entitled to a decent wage/standard of living.
examples.zh: 人人都应该有权获得像样的工资／过上体面的生活。

examples.en: I thought he was a decent person.
examples.zh: 我以为他是个正派人物。

examples.en: It was very decent (= kind) of you to help.
examples.zh: 你能帮忙，真是个大好人啊。

examples.en: It made quite a decent-sized (= large) hole.
examples.zh: 它弄出了一个很大的洞。

examples.en: After the recent scandal, the priest is expected to do the decent thing and resign from his position.
examples.zh: 近来的丑闻过后，估计那个牧师会做该做的事，引咎辞职。

examples.en: Are you decent yet?
examples.zh: 你穿好衣服了吗？

examples.en: You can come in now, I'm decent.
examples.zh: 我穿好衣服了，你可以进来了。

examples.en: All I need is a roof over my head and a decent meal.
He seemed like a decent sort of a fellow.
The law obliges companies to pay decent wages to their employees.
I haven't had a decent night's sleep in years.
The makers of the film will want to see a decent return on their investment.

definitions.en: to separate from someone or something else; to separate something from something else that it was joined to or part of

examples.en: Tractors that haul Mexican-made goods to American consumers must decouple from their trailers on the Mexican side.
examples.zh: 向美国消费者运送墨西哥制造商品的拖拉机必须与墨西哥方面的拖车脱钩。

examples.en: The optimists thought the rest of the world would be able to decouple from the problems of the American housing market.
examples.zh: 乐观主义者认为，世界其他地区将能够与美国房地产市场的问题脱钩。

examples.en: Some people hope to decouple migration policy from party politics.
examples.zh: 一些人希望将移民政策与政党政治分离开来。

examples.en: There used to be a theory of decoupling, where the emerging economies of developing nations would decouple from the United States' economy.
The numbers again raise the question of whether Japan is managing to decouple its economy from dependence on the US.

definitions.en: something or someone used to trick or confuse people, especially something or someone that is not what they appear to be
definitions.zh: 诱饵，圈套;用作诱饵的人

examples.en: Air Force One flew ahead as a decoy, with the President in another plane behind.
These attacks could be a decoy, a manoeuvre to throw everyone off guard.
When a driver sees a patrol car, they don't know if it's a real police officer or if it's a decoy.
They used a girl hitch-hiker as the decoy to get him to stop.
The officer arrested a bike thief after planting "decoy" bikes around the city.
The spinning decoys try to mimic the flashing of a bird's wings, luring waterfowl toward the decoy.

definitions.en: the process of reaching a decision or answer by thinking about the known facts, or the decision that is reached
definitions.zh: （根据已知的事实所作出的）推断，推论;推论所促成的决定

examples.en: Through a process of deduction, the detectives discovered the identity of the killer.
examples.zh: 经过一番仔细推断，警探们搞清了杀手的身份。

examples.en: All we can do is make deductions from the available facts.
examples.zh: 我们只能根据目前掌握的情况作出各种推论。

definitions.en: the calculation of taking an amount or a part of something away from a total or the amount that is taken away

examples.en: The interest I receive on my savings account is paid after the deduction of tax.
examples.zh: 付给我的存款利息已经扣了税。

examples.en: After deductions, his taxable income is $30,000.
examples.zh: 除掉免税项目后，他的应税收入为3万美元。

definitions.en: a state of not having, or not having enough, of something that is needed

examples.en: Pregnant women often suffer from iron deficiency.
examples.zh: 孕妇常常缺铁。

examples.en: Deficiencies in the education system have been much in the news.
examples.zh: 新闻媒体对教育体制的种种不足进行了大量的报道。

examples.en: Severe iron deficiency can cause developmental delay and growth retardation.
The teacher even implied that he was suffering from some kind of mental deficiency.
The blood tests revealed a deficiency in certain key minerals and vitamins.
There were clear deficiencies in the standard of service provided.
Despite his deficiencies as a husband, he was a good father to his children.

definitions.en: not able to be changed or improved
definitions.zh: 最终的，决定性的;不可更改的

examples.en: a definitive judgment/ruling
examples.zh: 最终的判决

examples.en: There are no definitive answers/solutions to this problem.
examples.zh: 这个问题没有决定性的答案/彻底的解决办法。

examples.en: The police have no definitive proof of her guilt.
examples.zh: 警察并未掌握她犯罪的确凿证据。

examples.en: He's written the definitive guide to Thailand.
examples.zh: 他写出了最完整可靠的泰国旅游指南。

definitions.en: to make a computer organize its files and free space so that it can operate more quickly

examples.en: Defragment your computer overnight.
examples.zh: 让你的电脑在夜间整理磁盘碎片。

definitions.en: in a skilful, clever, or quick way

examples.en: He deftly caught the ball.
examples.zh: 他很熟练地抓住了那个球。

examples.en: She deftly avoided answering his question.
examples.zh: 她熟练地回避了这个问题。

examples.en: All of the actors handle the dialogue deftly.
They must act deftly if they are to regain control over the region.
She deftly managed not to step on the cat.

definitions.en: no longer existing, living, or working correctly
definitions.zh: 不复存在的；死亡的；失灵的

examples.en: members of a now defunct communist organization
examples.zh: 目前已解散的共产主义组织的成员

examples.en: I think this TV is defunct!
examples.zh: 我看这台电视已经不中用了！

definitions.en: to cause people to feel that they or other people have no value and do not have the respect or good opinion of others
definitions.zh: 贬低;降低…的身份;侮辱…的人格

examples.en: Pornography degrades women.
examples.zh: 色情作品是对女性的侮辱。

definitions.en: to spoil or destroy the beauty or quality of something

examples.en: Every day the environment is further degraded by toxic wastes.
examples.zh: 环境日益受到有毒废物的危害。

definitions.en: (of a substance) to change into a more simple chemical structure

examples.en: These chemicals quickly degrade into harmless compounds.
examples.zh: 这些化学制品能很快降解为无害化合物。

definitions.en: (often of something bad) intentional or planned
definitions.zh: （常指坏事）有意的，故意的，蓄意的

examples.en: a deliberate attack/insult/lie
examples.zh: 蓄意攻击／侮辱／说谎

examples.en: We made a deliberate decision to live apart for a while.
examples.zh: 经过慎重考虑，我们决定分开住一段时间。

examples.en: From her slow, deliberate speech I guessed she must be drunk.
examples.zh: 她说话慢吞吞的，我猜她肯定喝醉了。

examples.en: Some women make a deliberate choice to rear a child alone.
Can you pick out the three deliberate mistakes in this paragraph?
I don't think his name was left off the list by accident - I think it was deliberate.
This was a deliberate attempt to wreck the peace talks.
I didn't take a deliberate decision to lose weight. It just happened.

definitions.en: to think or talk seriously and carefully about something

examples.en: The jury took five days to deliberate on the case.
examples.zh: 陪审团花了5天时间对此案进行了慎重讨论。

examples.en: The committee has deliberated the question at great length.
examples.zh: 委员会已经仔细讨论过这个问题了。

examples.en: He's deliberating whether or not to accept the new job that he's been offered.
examples.zh: 他正在慎重考虑是否接受提供给他的这份新工作。

definitions.en: intentionally

examples.en: I'm sure he says these things deliberately to annoy me.
examples.zh: 我敢肯定他是故意说这些来气我的。

examples.en: Calmly and deliberately, she cut up his suits one by one.
examples.zh: 她平静从容地把他的西服一件一件剪成碎片。

examples.en: It is believed that the building was set on fire deliberately.
I deliberately didn't have lunch so that I would be hungry tonight.
He was sent off for deliberately tripping Robson when he was about to score a goal.
I didn't do it deliberately - it was an accident!
The paintwork has been deliberately scratched.

definitions.en: (something or someone that gives) great pleasure, satisfaction, or happiness

examples.en: My sister's little boy is a real delight.
examples.zh: 我姐姐的小男孩很招人喜欢。

examples.en: I read your letter with great delight.
examples.zh: 我非常愉快地读了你的来信。

examples.en: The children squealed in delight when they saw all the presents under the Christmas tree.
examples.zh: 孩子们看到圣诞树下的各种礼物时兴奋地叫了起来。

examples.en: He seems to take great delight in (= enjoys) teasing his sister.
examples.zh: 他似乎觉得逗他妹妹玩很有趣。

examples.en: We're just discovering the delights of being retired.
examples.zh: 我们刚刚开始体会到退休后的种种乐趣。

examples.en: He guffawed with delight when he heard the news.
My initial surprise was soon replaced by delight.
The little dog's tail wagged in delight.
Her eyes shone with delight.
When his wife bore him a child he could not hide his delight.

definitions.en: to give someone great pleasure or satisfaction

examples.en: Peter's academic success delighted his family.
examples.zh: 彼得在学校里的良好表现让全家人感到高兴。

definitions.en: very pleasant, attractive, or enjoyable
definitions.zh: 令人高兴的，使人愉快的，给人快乐的

examples.en: Our new neighbours are delightful.
examples.zh: 我们的新邻居很和气。

examples.en: Thank you for a delightful evening.
examples.zh: 谢谢你让我度过了一个非常愉快的夜晚。

definitions.en: to mark or describe the limits of something

examples.en: Police powers are delimited by law.
examples.zh: 警察的权限是由法律来划定的。

definitions.en: to make something easier to understand

examples.en: What I need is a book that will demystify the workings of a car engine for me.
examples.zh: 我需要的是一本能通俗易懂地讲解汽车发动机工作原理的书。

definitions.en: a statement that something is not true or does not exist

examples.en: The CEO issued a denial of the report that she is about to resign.
examples.zh: 首席执行官发表声明，对她将辞职的报道予以否认。

examples.en: Officials did not believe the runner's denial that he had taken drugs.
examples.zh: 那位赛跑选手否认服药，但官员们并不相信。

examples.en: His denial of responsibility for the accident was unconvincing.
examples.zh: 他否认自己对这场事故负有任何责任，但他的说法并不令人信服。

definitions.en: the fact of not allowing someone to do or have something

examples.en: a gross denial of justice
examples.zh: 对正义的粗暴践踏

definitions.en: an unwillingness to accept that something unpleasant is true

examples.en: He's still in denial about the break-up of his relationship.
examples.zh: 他仍然无法接受他们分手这一事实。

definitions.en: the situation in which someone behaves in a way that goes against their beliefs and what they think is right

examples.en: If I did what you ask, it would be a denial of everything I stand for (= believe in).
examples.zh: 如果我照你说的做了，那就等于背叛了我的信念。

definitions.en: to represent something

examples.en: The colour red is used to denote passion or danger.
examples.zh: 红色用于表示激情或危险。

definitions.en: with a lot of things close together

examples.en: England was once a densely wooded country (= a lot of trees grew close together there).
examples.zh: 英格兰曾经林木茂密。

examples.en: Mexico City is one of the most densely populated cities in the world (= a lot of people live close together there).
examples.zh: 墨西哥城是世界上人口密度最大的城市之一。

examples.en: His books tend to be densely written (= contain a lot of information and ideas and are difficult to understand).
examples.zh: 他的书往往内容庞杂、晦涩难懂。

examples.en: The garden is very densely planted, leaving little opportunity for weeds to get established.
The foot soldiers stood in densely packed rows.
This was the most densely occupied area of the country during that period.
The fur grows very densely, keeping the animal's skin dry and largely insect-free.
From the plane we could see the plains below, densely covered with zebra, wildebeest and antelope.

definitions.en: the number of people or things in a place when compared with the size of the place

examples.en: The area has a high/low population density.
examples.zh: 该地区人口密度大／小。

examples.en: We were unable to move because of the density of the crowd.
examples.zh: 人太多，我们根本就动不了。

definitions.en: the relationship between the mass of a substance and its size

examples.en: Lead has a high density.
examples.zh: 铅的密度大。

examples.en: Aluminium is low in density.
examples.zh: 铝的密度小。

definitions.en: to represent or show something in a picture or story

examples.en: Her paintings depict the lives of ordinary people in the last century.
examples.zh: 她的画反映了上个世纪普通人的生活。

examples.en: In the book, he depicts his father as a tyrant.
examples.zh: 在那本书里，他把自己的父亲描写成了一个暴君。

examples.en: People were shocked by the advertisement which depicted a woman beating her husband.
examples.zh: 人们对那个女人殴打丈夫的广告感到非常震惊。

examples.en: In the film, he is able to depict the sense of otherness and alienation that many teenagers feel.
The rock drawings depict a variety of stylized human, bird and mythological figures and patterns.
Cupid is usually depicted as a winged boy with a bow and arrow.
John Bull is traditionally depicted as a short fat man wearing a waistcoat with the British flag on it.
In the film she's depicted as a very cold and calculating character.

definitions.en: to reduce something in size or amount, especially supplies of energy, money, etc.
definitions.zh: 消耗；耗费（资源、金钱、精力等）

examples.en: If we continue to deplete the earth's natural resources, we will cause serious damage to the environment.
examples.zh: 如果我们继续消耗地球上的自然资源，将对环境造成严重的破坏。

examples.en: The illness depletes the body of important vitamins.
examples.zh: 这种疾病会消耗人体中重要的维生素。

examples.en: That last holiday seriously depleted my bank account!
examples.zh: 上次度假花掉了我银行账户里的一大笔钱！

definitions.en: to leave something somewhere

examples.en: The flood waters fell, depositing mud over the whole area.
examples.zh: 洪水退去后留下的淤泥覆盖了整个区域。

examples.en: The bus deposited me miles from anywhere.
examples.zh: 公共汽车把我扔在一个前不着村后不着店的地方。

examples.en: The cuckoo deposits her eggs in other birds' nests.
examples.zh: 布谷鸟把自己的蛋下在其他鸟类的窝里。

examples.en: I deposited my luggage in a locker at the station.
examples.zh: 我把行李存放在车站的储物柜里了。
