We inject the rhinoceros with a powerful sedative, so that we can then examine it closely.
He squatted down and examined the front wheel of his bike.
Don't sign any contract before examining its conditions carefully.
We started our research into local history by examining the town's origins.

definitions.en: to test someone's knowledge or skill in a particular subject

examples.en: We were examined on European history.
examples.zh: 我们参加了欧洲史考试。

examples.en: You'll be examined in three main areas; speaking, listening, and reading comprehension.
examples.zh: 将从3个主要方面对你们进行测试，即口语、听力和阅读理解。

definitions.en: too much

examples.en: Excessive exercise can sometimes cause health problems.
examples.zh: 体育锻炼过度有时也会导致健康问题。

examples.en: Any more pudding would simply be excessive.
examples.zh: 一口布丁也不能吃了，再吃就吃多了。

examples.en: The film was criticized for its excessive violence and nudity.
The message of the campaign is that excessive drinking is a health risk.
They complained about excessive government intrusion into their legitimate activities.
For years, the organization was constrained by excessive bureaucracy.
The word 'lady' has connotations of refinement and excessive femininity that some women find offensive.

definitions.en: limited to only one person or group of people
definitions.zh: 专用的，专有的；独有的，独占的

examples.en: This room is for the exclusive use of guests.
examples.zh: 这个房间是专供客人使用的。

examples.en: an exclusive interview
examples.zh: 独家采访

examples.en: an exclusive private club
examples.zh: 豪华私人俱乐部

examples.en: an exclusive part of town
examples.zh: 城镇的高档住宅区

examples.en: From our website you can watch exclusive video clips and audio clips.
Being rich and being a Socialist are not mutually exclusive.
For the programme, we were allowed exclusive access to the judges' private world.
The two channels are fighting for exclusive rights to cover the championship.
There is a danger of being exclusive if you're not willing to be flexible about different styles of worship.

definitions.en: not including something:
definitions.zh: 总金额是不是不包括服务费？

examples.en: Is the total exclusive of service charges?
examples.zh: 总金额是不是不包括服务费？

definitions.en: a story that is published in one magazine, website, etc., and no others
definitions.zh: 独家新闻;独家报道;独家专访

examples.en: The newspaper published an exclusive about the escape.
examples.zh: 这家报纸刊登了有关这次逃跑事件的独家报道。

definitions.en: only

examples.en: This offer is available exclusively to our established customers.
examples.zh: 只有本店老客户才能享受这一优惠价。

examples.en: an exclusively female audience
examples.zh: 仅限女性观众

definitions.en: a typical or good example of something

examples.en: It is an exemplar of a house of the period.
examples.zh: 这是那个时期房屋建筑的典范。

definitions.en: complete and including everything

examples.en: an exhaustive study/report
examples.zh: 全面的研究／详尽的报告

definitions.en: in a way that is complete and includes everything

examples.en: The survey was exhaustively documented.
examples.zh: 调查记录详尽。

examples.en: an exhaustively researched article
examples.zh: 一篇详尽研究的文章

examples.en: The environmental impact of the proposed road was studied exhaustively.
The story was exhaustively researched by a reporter whose reputation for honest reporting is unquestioned.
It wasn't possible to exhaustively test how effective this technology is.

definitions.en: to show something publicly

examples.en: He frequently exhibits at the art gallery.
examples.zh: 他经常在艺术馆展出作品。

examples.en: In the summer the academy will exhibit several prints that are rarely seen.
examples.zh: 该学会将于今年夏天对外展出几幅罕见的版画作品。

examples.en: He exhibited great self-control considering her rudeness.
examples.zh: 她那么无礼，他表现出如此的克制力已很不错了。

examples.en: Most zoos try to exhibit animals in naturalistic settings.
The crown, decorated with diamonds and other precious stones, was exhibited in a special case.
The new wing of the museum had to be large enough to exhibit several huge dinosaur skeletons.
We have exhibited many of his works at our gallery in recent years.
One member of our pottery class is now exhibiting and selling her ceramics very successfully.

definitions.en: an object that is shown to the public in a museum, etc.

examples.en: The museum has a fascinating collection of exhibits ranging from Iron Age pottery to Inuit clothing.
examples.zh: 这个博物馆收藏的展品，从铁器时代的陶器，到因纽特人的衣物，丰富多彩，令人称奇。

examples.en: Let's go see the new dinosaur exhibit.
examples.zh: 我们去看这个有关恐龙的新展览吧。

examples.en: Is exhibit C the weapon that you say was used?
examples.zh: 物证C是不是你说的所使用的武器？

definitions.en: the increase of something in size, number, or importance
definitions.zh: （尺寸、数量或重要性的）扩大，增加，扩展

examples.en: the rapid expansion of the software industry
examples.zh: 软件业的迅速发展

examples.en: Expansion into new areas of research is possible.
examples.zh: 拓展新的研究领域是可能的。

examples.en: an expansion of industry
examples.zh: 工业的发展

examples.en: We need a dynamic expansion of trade with other countries.
The president said NATO expansion will finally erase the boundary line in Europe artificially created by the Cold War.
Lack of space is the main restraint on the firm's plans for expansion.
Any further industrial expansion in the area will face fierce opposition from environmentalists.
Given the necessary investment, this region is perfect for economic expansion.

definitions.en: to make something happen more quickly

examples.en: Something needs to be done to expedite the process.
examples.zh: 需要采取措施以加快进程。

definitions.en: the use of money, time, or effort

examples.en: Buying a bigger car has proved to be well worth the expense.
examples.zh: 事实证明，买辆大点儿的车还是很值得的。

examples.en: We've just had a new garage built at great expense.
examples.zh: 我们刚花了很多钱建了一座新车库。

examples.en: We went on holiday at my father's expense (= he paid for it).
examples.zh: 我们去度了假，一切费用由我父亲支付。

examples.en: It's silly to go to the expense of (= spend money on) buying new clothes when you don't really need them.
examples.zh: 花钱买你并不真正需要的新衣服是很傻的。

examples.en: Our biggest expense this year was our summer holiday.
examples.zh: 我们今年最大的开销就是暑期度假。

examples.en: We need to cut down on our expenses.
examples.zh: 我们必须削减开支。

examples.en: I need to get my expenses approved.
examples.zh: 我得去办报销核准手续。

examples.en: He claimed that the restaurant bill was a legitimate business expense.
Just buy it - never mind the expense!
Owning a car involves a lot of expense - it's unavoidable.
She rationalized the expense by saying that the costly carpet she had bought would last longer than a cheaper one.
All the time and expense involved in keeping up to date with the changes has been worthwhile.

definitions.en: clear and exact
definitions.zh: 清楚明白的；明确的；不含糊的

examples.en: I gave her very explicit directions how to get here.
examples.zh: 我明确地告诉她来这儿怎么走。

examples.en: She was very explicit about (= said very clearly and exactly) what she thought was wrong with the plans.
examples.zh: 她明确地指出了计划中她认为有误的地方。

examples.en: I wasn't aware that I would be paying - you certainly didn't make it explicit (= state it clearly).
examples.zh: 我不知道我得付款——毫无疑问是你没说清楚。

examples.en: a sexually explicit movie
examples.zh: 有露骨性爱场面的电影

definitions.en: in a way that is clear and exact

examples.en: I told you quite explicitly to be home by midnight.
examples.zh: 我非常明确地告诉过你要在午夜前回到家。

examples.en: It should be explicitly stated exactly what the grant covers.
examples.zh: 应清楚说明拨款的确切适用范围。

examples.en: The charter explicitly forbids war.
This brief mini-lesson explicitly demonstrates what a writer does while composing a text.
The company didn’t explicitly promise to supply universal service.

definitions.en: to break up into pieces violently, or to cause something to do this

examples.en: A bomb exploded at one of the capital's busiest railway stations this morning.
examples.zh: 今天上午一枚炸弹在首都最繁忙的一个火车站爆炸。

examples.en: He was driving so fast that his car tyre exploded.
examples.zh: 他车开得太快导致爆胎。

examples.en: If you pull the pin out of a hand-grenade, it'll explode.
The plane dived towards the ground and exploded in a ball of flame.
Witnesses reported seeing a huge orange fireball as the oil refinery exploded.
The terrorists exploded the device just as a convoy was passing.
She could hear the distant sound of fireworks exploding.

definitions.en: to react suddenly with a strong expression of emotion
definitions.zh: （情感）突然迸发，爆发，突然发作

examples.en: "What on earth do you think you're doing?" she exploded (= said angrily).
examples.zh: “你到底知不知道你在干什么？”她大发雷霆。

examples.en: The children exploded into giggles (= suddenly started laughing uncontrollably).
examples.zh: 孩子们突然咯咯地笑起来。

definitions.en: to increase very quickly

examples.en: The population has exploded in the last ten years.
examples.zh: 在过去10年中，人口激增。

definitions.en: to show something to be wrong

examples.en: This book finally explodes some of the myths about the origin of the universe.
examples.zh: 这本书最终推翻了一些关于宇宙起源的毫无根据的说法。

definitions.en: to use something in a way that helps you

examples.en: We need to make sure that we exploit our resources as fully as possible.
examples.zh: 我们需要确保尽可能充分利用资源。

examples.en: The increased globalization of the commodity trading business is something we must exploit.
Exporters can currently exploit the favourable exchange rates.
When a great opportunity like that comes along, you'd be a fool not to exploit it.
We have a lot of knowledge and experience in this department that we are not currently exploiting fully.
Finding myself in a dream situation, I exploited it to the full.

definitions.en: to use someone or something unfairly for your own advantage

examples.en: Laws exist to stop companies exploiting their employees.
examples.zh: 法律规定禁止公司剥削其员工。

examples.en: This planet on which we all live should be cherished and not exploited.
He was portrayed as a ruthless opportunist who exploited the publicity at every opportunity.
high street banks have been accused of exploiting small firms.
The minister was sacked for exploiting his position for his own personal gain.
She frequently exploits her authority by getting other people to do things for her.

definitions.en: something unusual, brave, or funny that someone has done
definitions.zh: 不寻常的行为；英勇的行为；奇遇

examples.en: She was telling me about her exploits while travelling around Africa.
examples.zh: 她跟我讲述了她在非洲各地旅游时的奇遇。

definitions.en: the use of something in order to get an advantage from it

examples.en: Britain's exploitation of its natural gas reserves began after the Second World War.
examples.zh: 英国对其天然气资源的开发利用始于二战后。

examples.en: The protection of the Antarctic from commercial exploitation is an important goal of environmentalists.
The ruthless exploitation of the world's resources is rapidly destroying our planet.
Exploitation by colonial powers in the past is often blamed for the continuing problems in these countries.
The company is accused of child exploitation, which is a very serious charge.
Fair trade measures are essential to stop the exploitation of small Third-World coffee farmers.

definitions.en: the act of using someone unfairly for your own advantage

examples.en: Marx wrote about the exploitation of the workers.
examples.zh: 马克思著书论述了工人受剥削的情况。

definitions.en: in a way that becomes quicker and quicker as something that increases becomes larger

examples.en: Malthus wrote about the risks involved in the world's population increasing exponentially.
examples.zh: 马尔萨斯论述了世界人口激增带来的风险。

examples.en: We are growing exponentially in resources, people, and financing.
examples.zh: 我们在资源、人力和资金上都得到了飞速发展。

examples.en: Solar capacity is growing not just steadily, but exponentially.
Demand for his services has grown exponentially.
The cell divides rapidly, increasing the numbers of cells exponentially.

definitions.en: the fact of experiencing something or being affected by it because of being in a particular situation or place

examples.en: You should always limit your exposure to the sun.
examples.zh: 无论什么时候，都不要长时间晒太阳。

examples.en: Even a brief exposure to radiation is very dangerous.
examples.zh: 即便只是短暂受辐射也是非常危险的。

examples.en: According to received wisdom, exposure to low level radioactivity is harmless.
We know that prolonged exposure to vibration can weaken aircraft components.
She gradually became deaf after lengthy exposure to noise in the workplace.
Researchers concluded that low level exposure to the chemical was unlikely to cause harm.
Even at low levels of exposure, lead can impair a child's learning ability and cause behavioural problems.

definitions.en: the fact of something bad that someone has done being made public]

examples.en: The exposure of the politician's love affair forced him to resign.
examples.zh: 他在风流韵事曝光后，被迫辞职。

definitions.en: the fact of an event or information being often discussed in newspapers and on the television, etc.
definitions.zh: （在报纸或电视上的）报道，宣传，曝光

examples.en: His last movie got so much exposure in the press.
examples.zh: 他最新拍摄的电影受到了新闻媒体的极大关注。

definitions.en: a serious medical condition that is caused by being outside in very cold weather
definitions.zh: （暴露于严寒而导致的）冻伤（病），受寒

examples.en: All the members of the expedition to the South Pole died of exposure.
examples.zh: 南极探险队的所有队员都死于冻伤。

definitions.en: a single photograph on a piece of film, or the amount of time a piece of film is open to the light when making a photograph
definitions.zh: （底片的）一张;曝光时间，曝光量

examples.en: There are 24 exposures on this film.
examples.zh: 这个胶卷有24张底片。

definitions.en: the direction in which something faces

examples.en: Our dining room has a northern exposure (= faces north), so it's rather cold.
examples.zh: 我们的饭厅是朝北的，所以很冷。

definitions.en: covering a large area; having a great range
definitions.zh: 广阔的，大面积的；覆盖范围广的

examples.en: a school with extensive grounds
examples.zh: 操场很大的学校

examples.en: extensive repairs to the motorway
examples.zh: 对高速公路的大规模维修

examples.en: Her knowledge of music is extensive (= she knows a lot about music).
examples.zh: 她的音乐知识非常广博。

examples.en: The wedding received extensive coverage in the newspapers (= it was written about a lot).
examples.zh: 报纸对这次婚礼进行了全面报道。

examples.en: The body was found after an extensive search by police with tracker dogs.
There is an extensive repertoire of music written for the flute.
Several of the crash victims had to have extensive plastic surgery.
Her knowledge of English grammar is very extensive.
The house needed extensive alterations when we moved in.

definitions.en: in a way that covers a large area or has a great range

examples.en: The house was extensively rebuilt after the fire.
examples.zh: 火灾发生后，这间房屋进行了大规模重建。

examples.en: The side effects of the new drug are being extensively researched.
examples.zh: 目前正在对新药的副作用进行广泛研究。

examples.en: He draws extensively on scholarly research for his essey.
She said she expects to travel extensively after retirement.
Researchers have extensively studied the effects of class size on student achievement.

definitions.en: in a way that relates to the outside of something, or that is on or comes from the outside

examples.en: Externally the house is in need of repair.
examples.zh: 这所房屋的外部需要修缮。

examples.en: Externally she appeared calm, but inside she was furious.
examples.zh: 她表面上看起来很平静，但内心却很愤怒。

examples.en: If the relevant data already exists externally, you can import it.
Airline telephones, on the other hand, transmit radio signals to and from antennas mounted externally on the aircraft.
Externally administered assessments tied to standards shift the focus of teaching to test-related information.

definitions.en: not directly connected with or related to something

examples.en: extraneous information
examples.zh: 无关信息

examples.en: These questions are extraneous to the issue being discussed.
examples.zh: 这些问题和正在讨论的议题无关。

definitions.en: very unusual, special, unexpected, or strange
definitions.zh: 非凡的；特别的；意想不到的；令人惊奇的

examples.en: He told the extraordinary story of his escape.
examples.zh: 他讲述了他离奇的逃亡经历。

examples.en: Her voice had an extraordinary hypnotic quality.
examples.zh: 她的嗓音有一种奇特的催眠效果。

examples.en: an extraordinary coincidence
examples.zh: 令人吃惊的巧合

examples.en: He presided over an extraordinary meeting of the country's leading clergy.
We witnessed the extraordinary sight of an old lady climbing a tree to rescue her cat.
The rain forest is home to many extraordinary creatures.
She has an extraordinary memory and can remember details and names that I've long forgotten.
Whatever is that extraordinary object in the sky?
Eastern Europe was completely transformed by the extraordinary events of 1989 and 1990.

definitions.en: to guess or think about what might happen using information that is already known

examples.en: You can't really extrapolate a trend from such a small sample.
examples.zh: 你不能仅凭这样一件小小的样品就推断出潮流真正的发展趋势。

definitions.en: to guess or think about what might happen using information that is already known

examples.en: You can't really extrapolate a trend from such a small sample.
examples.zh: 你不能仅凭这样一件小小的样品就推断出潮流真正的发展趋势。

definitions.en: cloth or material for making clothes, covering furniture, etc.

examples.en: dress fabric
examples.zh: 裙装衣料

examples.en: seats upholstered in red fabric
examples.zh: 罩有红色布料的座位

examples.en: cotton fabrics
examples.zh: 棉织物

examples.en: Linen is a coarse-grained fabric.
The fibres are woven into fabric.
This fabric is similar to wool, only cheaper.
The designs are printed onto the fabric by hand.
She designs her own fabrics using woodblocks and stencils to create patterns on the material.

definitions.en: the structure or parts of something:

examples.en: the fabric of society
examples.zh: 社会结构

definitions.en: to make something possible or easier

examples.en: The new ramp will facilitate the entry of wheelchairs.
examples.zh: 新坡道将方便轮椅进入。

examples.en: The current structure does not facilitate efficient work flow.
examples.zh: 当前的体系不利于实施高效的工作流程。

definitions.en: extremely good

examples.en: You look fantastic in that dress.
examples.zh: 你穿着那件连衣裙看起来棒极了。

examples.en: We had a fantastic time.
examples.zh: 我们玩得很痛快。

examples.en: They won the tournament? How fantastic!
examples.zh: 他们赢了锦标赛？太棒了！

examples.en: The camera work in some of these animal documentaries is fantastic.
Don't miss out on the fantastic bargains in our summer sale.
She's a fantastic all-round sportswoman.
That was a fantastic goal.
We had a fantastic meal at that new restaurant.

definitions.en: strange and imaginary, or not reasonable
definitions.zh: 幻想的；怪诞的，荒诞不经的

examples.en: He drew fantastic animals with two heads and large wings.
examples.zh: 他画了一些长着两个头和巨大翅膀的怪兽。

examples.en: It seemed fantastic that they still remembered her 50 years later.
examples.zh: 50年过去了，他们依然记得她，这似乎令人难以置信。

definitions.en: A fantastic amount is very large.

examples.en: She must be earning a fantastic amount of money.
examples.zh: 她一定是在赚大钱。

definitions.en: a style that is popular at a particular time, especially in clothes, hair, make-up, etc.
definitions.zh: （尤指服装、发式、妆容的）流行款式，时兴式样

examples.en: Long hair is back in fashion for men.
examples.zh: 男士又时兴留长发了。

examples.en: Fur coats have gone out of fashion.
examples.zh: 皮草大衣已经过时了。

examples.en: a programme with features on sport and fashion
examples.zh: 以运动和时尚为主题的节目

examples.en: She always wears the latest fashions.
examples.zh: 她总是穿着最新款式的服装。

examples.en: Many adults follow fashion in clothes and social opinions as much as their children do.
Emma spends money like it's going out of fashion.
examples.zh: 埃玛花钱如流水。

examples.en: We did a fashion shoot on the beach, with the girls modelling swimwear.
Whatever the latest fashion trend, you can be sure Nicki will be wearing it.
There's a double-page spread on the latest fashions.
Our fashion editor gives you the lowdown on winter coats for this season.
They made their money in the fashion business.

definitions.en: a way of doing things

examples.en: The rebel army behaved in a brutal fashion.
examples.zh: 叛军行事残忍。

examples.en: I can cook, after a fashion.
examples.zh: 我会做饭，但做得不太好。

examples.en: During the bomb scare, the customers were asked to proceed in an orderly fashion out of the shop.
He gets on with things in his own peculiar fashion.
Charles picked at his food in a bored fashion.
The developers dealt with the problem in the time-honoured fashion, burying the industrial waste in landfill sites.
Paul, in typically rude fashion, told him he was talking rubbish.

definitions.en: to make something using your hands

examples.en: He fashioned a hat for himself from/out of newspaper.
examples.zh: 他用报纸给自己做了一顶帽子。

definitions.en: US spelling of favoured
with no fear

examples.en: They fought fearlessly against the invading armies.
examples.zh: 他们无畏地与入侵的军队作战。

examples.en: He deserves credit for fearlessly addressing the issues facing the company.
examples.zh: 他大胆解决公司面临的问题，值得称赞。

examples.en: When he looked up at her, Jenna met his eyes fearlessly.
We will fight fearlessly to protect your interests.
The little bird observed her fearlessly.

definitions.en: able to be made, done, or achieved
definitions.zh: 可行的;行得通的;做得到的

examples.en: With the extra resources, the project now seems feasible.
examples.zh: 有了额外的资源，这个计划现在看来是可行的。

examples.en: It may be feasible to clone human beings, but is it ethical?
examples.zh: 克隆人类也许可以做到，但是这合乎道德吗？

definitions.en: to act dishonestly in order to get something for yourself, or to change something dishonestly, especially to your advantage
definitions.zh: （尤指为个人利益而）用诡计获得;篡改，伪造

examples.en: She managed to fiddle a free trip to America.
examples.zh: 她想办法骗取了一趟去美国的免费旅行。

examples.en: He had been fiddling the accounts/books/finances for years.
examples.zh: 多年来他一直在伪造账目／账簿／财务状况。

definitions.en: to move things about or touch things with no particular purpose

examples.en: Put your papers down and stop fiddling with them!
examples.zh: 把文件放下，不要乱摆弄！

definitions.en: to play the violin
a violin

examples.en: to play the fiddle
examples.zh: 拉小提琴

definitions.en: something difficult to do, especially because the things involved are small or need careful use of the fingers

examples.en: I find threading a needle a terrible fiddle.
examples.zh: 我觉得穿针是件难事。

examples.en: It's a real fiddle to assemble because of all the small parts.
examples.zh: 这些零件太小，装配起来确实是件难事。

definitions.en: something dishonest that someone does in order to get money or other advantages
definitions.zh: （为获得金钱或其他利益而做的）不诚实行为

examples.en: a tax fiddle
examples.zh: 偷税行为

examples.en: Everyone suspected they were on the fiddle (= cheating).
examples.zh: 大家都怀疑他们在搞欺诈。

definitions.en: honest or lasting support, or loyalty, especially to a sexual partner
definitions.zh: （尤指对性伴侣的）忠诚，忠实，忠贞

examples.en: Somerset Maugham's comedy of marital fidelity, "The Constant Wife"
examples.zh: 萨默塞特‧毛姆关于婚姻忠诚的喜剧《忠实的妻子》

examples.en: How important do you think sexual fidelity is in a marriage?
examples.zh: 你认为婚姻中肉体上的忠诚有多重要？

definitions.en: the degree to which the detail and quality of an original, such as a picture, sound, or story, is copied exactly

examples.en: The best ink-jet printers can reproduce photographs with amazing fidelity.
examples.zh: 最好的喷墨打印机输出的照片效果会非常逼真。

definitions.en: to make a final and certain decision about a plan, date, etc.
definitions.zh: 把（计划、日期等）最后定下

examples.en: We'll finalize the details later.
examples.zh: 细节我们稍后再定。

definitions.en: good or good enough; healthy and well
definitions.zh: 好的;足够好的;健康的，身体好的

examples.en: I felt terrible last night but I feel fine this morning.
examples.zh: 我昨晚感觉很不舒服，但今天早上就好了。

examples.en: The apartments are very small, which is fine for one person.
examples.zh: 这些公寓很小，一个人住正合适。

examples.en: "Are you all right?" "Everything's just fine, thanks."
examples.zh: “你没事吧？”“我没什么事，谢谢。”

examples.en: "I'll come to your place at eight." "Fine. See you then."
examples.zh: “我8点到你家来。”“好的，到时见。”

examples.en: To all outward appearances everything was fine, but under the surface the marriage was very shaky.
"Don't you worry about a thing. Everything's going to be just fine," smiled Robin reassuringly.
My diet would be fine if only I didn't have this weakness for sweet things.
I don't understand how he can say that everything's fine when it's so obvious that it's not.
Stop fiddling about with your hair - it looks fine.

definitions.en: excellent or much better than average

examples.en: purveyors of fine wines and gourmet food
examples.zh: 美酒佳肴的供应商

examples.en: The world's finest collection of Impressionist paintings is housed in the Musée d'Orsay in Paris.
examples.zh: 巴黎奥塞美术馆拥有世界上最棒的印象派画作馆藏。

examples.en: This building is the finest example of its type.
examples.zh: 这栋建筑是同类建筑中最杰出的代表。

examples.en: The painting is regarded as an exceptionally fine portrait.
The museum has a fine collection of valuable glass.
Jones made sure of his place in the side with three fine goals.
Some of the finest examples of woodcarving in Europe can be found in medieval churches.
She has a liking for fine wines.

definitions.en: very thin or in very small pieces or drops

examples.en: The baby's head was covered in fine blond hair.
examples.zh: 婴儿满头的金色细发。

examples.en: The eruption had covered the town with a fine layer of ash.
examples.zh: 火山爆发给整个镇子覆盖了一层细细的火山灰。

examples.en: Apply a fine line of highlighter along the middle of your top lip.
examples.zh: 用唇线笔沿着上嘴唇的中线细细地画上一道。

examples.en: She has inherited her mother's fine (= delicate and beautiful) features.
examples.zh: 她继承了母亲清秀的面容。

definitions.en: very exact and delicate, or needing to be done, treated, or considered very carefully

examples.en: I understood in general what she was talking about, but some of the finer details/points were beyond me.
examples.zh: 我大致上理解她在说些什么，但是有一些具体的细节／问题没有弄明白。

definitions.en: sunny and dry

examples.en: The forecast said it would be fine and dry today.
examples.zh: 天气预报说今天天气晴朗干燥。

definitions.en: bad or not convenient

examples.en: That's a fine (= very unpleasant) thing to say about your father after all he's done for you!
examples.zh: 你的父亲为你做了这一切之后，你居然说出这种混账话来！

examples.en: He picked a fine time to leave us.
examples.zh: 在那个时候扔下我们走了，他可真是会挑时间。

definitions.en: an amount of money that has to be paid as a punishment for not obeying a rule or law

examples.en: The maximum penalty for the offence is a $1,000 fine.
examples.zh: 违者最高可罚1000美元。

examples.en: If found guilty, he faces six months in jail and a heavy (= severe) fine.
examples.zh: 如果被判有罪，等待他的将是6个月的监禁和巨额罚款。

examples.en: I've just had to pay £10 for a parking fine.
He did not have to pay his speeding fine because he pleaded diplomatic immunity.
They're appealing to the High Court to reduce the prison sentence to a fine.
The judge said that the fine would serve as a warning to other motorists who drove without due care.
The librarian slapped a fine on him for returning the books late.

definitions.en: to charge someone an amount of money as a punishment for not obeying a rule or law

examples.en: Drivers who exceed the speed limit can expect to be fined heavily.
examples.zh: 超速驾车的司机会被罚以重金。

examples.en: They fined him £100 for using threatening behaviour.
examples.zh: 他因为有威胁恐吓行为而被罚100英镑。

examples.en: He was fined for defacing library books.
She was fined for speeding last month.
The council has begun to fine drivers who park in the bus lanes.
The judge decided to fine him rather than impose a prison sentence.
He was fined for impersonating a police officer.

definitions.en: in a satisfactory way

examples.en: "Will a loan of $500 be sufficient?" "That will suit me fine."
examples.zh: “借你500美元够吗？”“足够了。”

examples.en: It was working fine yesterday.
examples.zh: 它昨天还好好的。

examples.en: Work is going fine.
The TV seems to be working fine now.
Part-time work suits me fine.
A three-bedroom house would suit us fine.
A three-day week would suit me fine.

definitions.en: having a limit or end
definitions.zh: 有限的;有限制的;有尽的

examples.en: The funds available for the health service are finite and we cannot afford to waste money.
examples.zh: 用于公共医疗卫生事业的资金是有限的，我们浪费不起。

examples.en: We only have a finite amount of time to complete this task - we can't continue indefinitely.
examples.zh: 我们完成这项任务的时间是有限的，不能无限期地做下去。

definitions.en: in a form that shows the tense and subject of a verb, rather than the infinitive form or a participle

examples.en: In the following sentence "go" is finite: "I often go to the cinema."
examples.zh: 在句子 I often go to the cinema 中，go 是限定动词。

definitions.en: coming off easily in small, flat, thin pieces
definitions.zh: 易碎成小薄片的；易脱落的

examples.en: dry, flaky skin
examples.zh: 干燥易脱皮的皮肤

examples.en: a flaky scalp
examples.zh: 易脱皮屑的头皮

definitions.en: behaving in a way that is strange, not responsible or not expected
definitions.zh: 疯疯癫癫的;不靠谱的;古怪的

examples.en: The central character of the play is a flaky neurotic.
examples.zh: 该剧的中心人物是一个疯疯癫癫的神经质的人。

examples.en: The truth is actors are often flaky, unreliable and unstable people.
Jane Fonda plays the stern grandmother of a flaky So-Cal teenager.
The image some people have of a chemistry teacher is that of a flaky looking fellow in a white coat writing an incomprehensible math formula on a black board.
She is typecast as a flaky California single mom who cooks vegan and talks about auras and stuff.

definitions.en: a stream of hot, burning gas from something on fire

examples.en: The flames grew larger as the fire spread.
examples.zh: 火势蔓延，火焰越来越高。

examples.en: The car flipped over and burst into flames (= started burning immediately).
examples.zh: 汽车翻了，顿时起火燃烧起来。

examples.en: When the fire engine arrived the house was already in flames (= burning).
examples.zh: 消防车赶到时，房屋已是一片火海。

examples.en: The plane dived towards the ground and exploded in a ball of flame.
A sheet of flame shot up into the air immediately after the explosion.
It took the firefighters several hours to extinguish the flames.
There was a series of explosions and the van burst into flames.
The car blew up when flames reached its fuel tank.

definitions.en: a powerful feeling

examples.en: Flames of passion swept through both of them.
examples.zh: 他们俩一时间激情澎湃。

definitions.en: an angry or offensive email
definitions.zh: 愤怒（或无礼）的电子邮件

examples.en: flame wars
examples.zh: 电子邮件辱骂大战

definitions.en: to burn (more) brightly

examples.en: The fire flamed cosily in the hearth.
examples.zh: 壁炉里的火暖烘烘地燃烧着。

examples.en: The fire suddenly flamed (up).
examples.zh: 火突然烧起来了。

definitions.en: If an emotion flames, you feel it suddenly and strongly.

examples.en: Seeing the damage made hatred flame within her.
examples.zh: 看到损毁的情况，她心头燃起了仇恨之火。

examples.en: His face flamed (red) with anger.
examples.zh: 他气得脸通红。

definitions.en: to send an angry or insulting email
definitions.zh: 发送愤怒（或无礼）的电子邮件

examples.en: Please don't flame me if you disagree with this message.
examples.zh: 如果您不同意此郵件的内容，也请不要给我发送恶意回信。

definitions.en: to become level or cause something to become level

examples.en: Several trees were flattened (= knocked down) by the storm.
examples.zh: 暴风雨把几棵大树刮倒了。

examples.en: The path flattens (out) (= does not go up so much) as it reaches the top of the hill.
examples.zh: 到达山顶时，这条小路变得平坦了。

examples.en: He was immediately flattened by a single punch to the head.
The whole area was flattened in the bombing raid - not a building was left standing.
After a few hundred yards, you come to an area where the ground flattens out.
That tent is not secure, you know. The first gust of wind will flatten it.
A fight broke out and we had to grab Kevin to stop him flattening the other guy.

definitions.en: to become level and thinner or to cause something to become level and thinner

examples.en: Flatten the pastry into a thin disc with your hands.
examples.zh: 用手把油酥面团压成薄圆片。

examples.en: The biscuits will flatten out as they cook.
examples.zh: 烘烤时饼干会变得扁平。

definitions.en: a fault, mistake, or weakness, especially one that happens while something is being planned or made, or that causes something not to be perfect

examples.en: I returned the material because it had a flaw in it.
examples.zh: 我退掉了那块布料，因为有瑕疵。

examples.en: There's a fatal flaw in your reasoning.
examples.zh: 你的推理有一处致命的缺陷。

examples.en: This report is full of flaws.
examples.zh: 这个报告充满错谬。

examples.en: a character flaw
examples.zh: 性格上的缺陷

definitions.en: to cause something to be not perfect

examples.en: A tiny mark flawed the otherwise perfect silk shirt.
examples.zh: 一个小小的污点使本来完美的丝绸衬衫有了瑕疵。

definitions.en: a young bird that has grown feathers and is learning to fly
new and without experience
definitions.zh: 新的，刚开始的;缺乏经验的

examples.en: The current economic climate is particularly difficult for fledgling businesses.
examples.zh: 目前的经济气候对刚起步的公司来说特别艰难。

definitions.en: If you flip something, you turn it over quickly one or more times, and if something flips, it turns over quickly.

examples.en: I flipped the book (over) to look at the back cover.
examples.zh: 我很快地把书翻过去看了看封底。

examples.en: I lost my place in my book when the pages flipped over in the wind.
examples.zh: 风把书页吹乱了，我找不到正在看的那个地方了。

examples.en: You turn the machine on by flipping (= operating) the switch on the side.
examples.zh: 把边上的开关按一下，机器就启动了。

examples.en: The captains flipped a coin into the air (= made it turn over in the air to see which side it landed on) to decide which side would bat first.
examples.zh: 两队队长向空中抛出一枚硬币，决定哪一方先击球。

examples.en: I don't want to spend the rest of my life flipping burgers.
examples.zh: 我可不想一辈子都翻烤汉堡。

definitions.en: to buy something, such as a house, that is not in very good condition, improve it, then sell it quickly for more money
definitions.zh: 迅速转售（稍作装修后的房子）以获取利润

examples.en: I am going to take three weeks' vacation and flip this house.
examples.zh: 我要休假三个星期，稍微装修一下这个房子然后卖掉。

examples.en: She makes money flipping old and tired furniture pieces.

definitions.en: an occasion when something turns over quickly or repeatedly

examples.en: a flip of a coin
examples.zh: 抛硬币

examples.en: The acrobats were doing somersaults and flips (= jumping and turning their bodies over in the air).
examples.zh: 杂技演员们正在翻筋斗和空翻。

definitions.en: informal for flippant
used when you are slightly annoyed

examples.en: Oh, flip, I've missed the bus.
examples.zh: 哦不！我错过公交车了。

definitions.en: soft and not able to keep a firm shape or position

examples.en: a floppy hat
examples.zh: 软帽

examples.en: a dog with big floppy ears
examples.zh: 耷拉着大耳朵的狗

examples.en: He's got floppy blond hair that's always falling in his eyes.
examples.zh: 他有着柔软的金发，总是垂到眼睛里。

definitions.en: a diskette (= a flat, circular device, usually inside a square container, that has a magnetic covering and is used for storing computer information)

examples.en: The data is stored on a hard disk and backed up on a floppy disk.
The company supplies things like printer ink, computer paper and floppy disks direct to your door.

definitions.en: to change, especially continuously and between one level or thing and another

examples.en: Vegetable prices fluctuate according to the season.
examples.zh: 蔬菜价格随着季节变化而涨落。

examples.en: Her wages fluctuate between £150 and £200 a week.
examples.zh: 她的周薪在150英镑到200英镑之间浮动。

examples.en: Her weight fluctuates wildly.
examples.zh: 她的体重起伏很大。

examples.en: fluctuating prices
examples.zh: 波动的价格

definitions.en: a change, or the process of changing, especially continuously between one level or thing and another

examples.en: fluctuations in share prices/the exchange rate/temperature
examples.zh: 股价/汇率/温度的波动

examples.en: weight fluctuations
examples.zh: 体重波动

examples.en: There are many issues that are likely to cause fluctuation in fish stocks.
Some fluctuations in mood are perfectly normal.
Scientists have concluded that temperature fluctuations may increase the spread of infectious diseases.
These mood swings could be caused by hormonal fluctuations.
His weight fluctuations caused problems for his trainers.

definitions.en: (of a plan or machine) so simple and easy to understand that it is unable to go wrong or be used wrongly
definitions.zh: （计划或机器）连傻子都懂的，操作简便的，万无一失的

examples.en: I don't believe there's any such thing as a foolproof system for making money.
examples.zh: 我认为不存在任何万无一失的赚钱方法。

examples.en: This new recipe is supposed to be foolproof.

definitions.en: the mark made by a person's or animal's foot
the amount of space on a surface that something needs
definitions.zh: （某物所占的）平面面积;（尤指计算机所占的）台面

examples.en: The new computer has a smaller footprint.
examples.zh: 这台新计算机占地要小一些。

examples.en: The footprint of the new Treasury building
examples.zh: 新财政部大楼的占地面积

examples.en: We took the decision to invest in new countries and grow our global footprint.
examples.zh: 我们决定到新的国家投资，扩大我们在全球所及之处。

definitions.en: to not have or do something enjoyable
definitions.zh: 放弃，摒绝（令人愉悦之物）

examples.en: I shall have to forgo the pleasure of seeing you this week.
examples.zh: 这周我只好放弃与您相见的那份荣幸了。

definitions.en: to develop all the details of a plan for doing something

examples.en: to formulate a new plan
examples.zh: 制订一个新计划

examples.en: to formulate legislation
examples.zh: 制订法律

definitions.en: to take care of a child, usually for a limited time, without being the child's legal parent
definitions.zh: 收养，代养，抚育（他人子女，通常指有限的一段时间）

examples.en: Would you consider fostering (a child)?
examples.zh: 你会考虑代养一个孩子吗？

definitions.en: to encourage the development or growth of ideas or feelings

examples.en: I'm trying to foster an interest in classical music in my children.
examples.zh: 我正努力培养我的孩子们对古典音乐的兴趣。

examples.en: They were discussing the best way to foster democracy and prosperity in the former communist countries.
examples.zh: 他们正在讨论促进前共产主义国家民主和繁荣的最佳办法。

definitions.en: used to refer to someone or something connected with the care of children, usually for a limited time, by someone who is not the child's legal parent
definitions.zh: （在有限的一段时间内）（所）收养的，（所）代养的

examples.en: a foster care/home/child/mother
examples.zh: 非亲代养／寄养家庭／代养的孩子／养母

examples.en: The neglected child was removed from her home and placed with a foster family.
examples.zh: 这名未受妥善照顾的女孩子被送到了一户寄养家庭中。

examples.en: As a child, he had lived with a series of foster parents.
examples.zh: 他小时候和好几任养父母一起生活过。

examples.en: her foster father/mother
examples.zh: 她的养父/养母

definitions.en: a number that results from dividing one whole number by another

examples.en: ¼ and 0.25 are different ways of representing the same fraction.
examples.zh: 1/4和0.25是同一分数的不同表达方式。

examples.en: Although sexual and violent crimes have increased by 13 percent, they remain only a tiny/small fraction of the total number of crimes committed each year.
examples.zh: 尽管性犯罪和暴力犯罪案件增长了13%，但它们在每年的罪案总数中所占比例仍然很小。

examples.en: They can produce it at a fraction of the cost of (= much more cheaply than) traditional methods.
examples.zh: 他们能够以大大低于传统方法的成本生产该物。

examples.en: Could you just move a fraction to the right, so I can get by?
examples.zh: 你能向右移动一点点，让我过去吗？

examples.en: Reputable charities spend most of the donated money on aid and a tiny fraction on administration.
For his maths homework, he's got to convert various fractions to decimals and decimals to fractions.
How would you express 25% as a fraction, children?

definitions.en: extremely small

examples.en: The fall in the value of the yen might result in a fractional increase in interest rates of perhaps a quarter of one percent.
examples.zh: 日元贬值可能会引起0.25%的小幅加息。

examples.en: fractional ownership
examples.zh: 部分拥有权

definitions.en: A fragile object is easily damaged or broken.
definitions.zh: 易损坏的；易碎的；脆弱的

examples.en: Be careful with that vase - it's very fragile.
examples.zh: 拿那只花瓶要小心——它很容易碎。

examples.en: Some of the more fragile items are kept in a locked case.
All that was left of the bones were a few fragile fragments.
Digital copies are used because the original documents are too fragile.
Our vaccine supply system is fragile.
This is one of the world's most fragile ecosystems.
He suddenly looked old and fragile.
She was in a fragile condition and needed a lot of emotional support.
A cup of tea is just the thing when you're feeling fragile.

definitions.en: honest, sincere, and telling the truth, even when this might be awkward or make other people uncomfortable
definitions.zh: 坦率的，坦诚的;直言不讳的

examples.en: a full and frank discussion
examples.zh: 全面而坦诚的讨论

examples.en: There followed a frank exchange of views.
examples.zh: 接下来大家坦诚地交换了意见。

examples.en: The magazine, which gives frank advice about sex and romance, is aimed at the teenage market.
examples.zh: 这本杂志面向青少年市场，对性和恋爱问题作出坦率的忠告。

examples.en: To be perfectly frank with you, I don't think she's the person for the job.
examples.zh: 十分坦诚地告诉你，我认为她不适合做那份工作。

definitions.en: to print a mark on a stamp so that the stamp cannot be used again, or to print a mark on an envelope to show that the cost of sending it has been paid
definitions.zh: 在（邮票）上盖戳;在（信封）上盖戳表示邮资已付

definitions.en: to make someone feel annoyed or less confident because they cannot achieve what they want

examples.en: It frustrates me that I'm not able to put any of my ideas into practice.
examples.zh: 我的任何想法都无法付诸实践，这让我很灰心。

definitions.en: to prevent the plans or efforts of someone or something from being achieved

examples.en: The continuing civil war is frustrating the efforts of relief agencies.
examples.zh: 持续的内战使救济机构的努力受挫。

definitions.en: to do something that is expected, hoped for, or promised, or to cause it to happen

examples.en: A school fails if it does not fulfil the needs/requirements of its pupils.
examples.zh: 学校如果不能满足学生的需求／要求，那它就是失败的。

examples.en: At the age of 45, she finally fulfilled her ambition to run a marathon.
examples.zh: 45岁时，她终于实现了跑马拉松的夙愿。

examples.en: Zoos fulfil an important function in the protection of rare species.
examples.zh: 动物园起着保护珍稀动物的重要功能。

examples.en: He has failed to fulfil his duties as a father.
examples.zh: 他没有尽到做父亲的责任。

examples.en: We're looking for a very specific sort of person and this woman seems to fulfil all of our criteria.
examples.zh: 我们正在寻找某种特定的人，而这个女人似乎符合我们的所有标准。

examples.en: So did the course fulfil all your expectations?
examples.zh: 那么这门课程符合你各方面的期望吗？

examples.en: We're suing our suppliers for failing to fulfil their contract.
examples.zh: 我们正在和供应商打官司，因为他们没有履行合同。

examples.en: She doubts whether she'll ever be able to fulfil her ambition.
The captain was demoted to sergeant for failing to fulfil his duties.
As a child I was quite a good dancer, but I didn't fulfil my early promise.
Traditional ways of life fulfilled both economic and spiritual needs.
As a partner he fulfils all my main criteria.

definitions.en: to satisfy someone or to make someone feel happy

examples.en: I don't feel that my present way of life really fulfils me.
examples.zh: 我觉得现在的生活无法真正地令我满意。

examples.en: I've finally found a job in which I can fulfil myself (= completely develop my abilities and interests).
examples.zh: 我终于找到了一份能发挥自己才干的工作。

examples.en: Motherhood fulfilled me.
The job pays well but it doesn't fulfil me.
I'm looking for work that will fulfil me.
Spiritually, my way of life didn't fulfil me.
Fame and fortune was all well and good but it didn't fulfil me.

definitions.en: easy to exchange or trade for something else of the same type and value
definitions.zh: 易于（与同类或同价物品）交换（或交易）的

examples.en: fungible goods/commodities
examples.zh: 易于交易的货物／商品

examples.en: fungible assets/bonds
examples.zh: 易于交易的资产／债券

definitions.en: an object that has a wide round opening at the top, sloping sides, and a narrow tube at the bottom, used for pouring liquids or powders into containers with narrow necks

examples.en: After you grind the coffee, use a funnel to pour it into the jar.
examples.zh: 磨好咖啡后，用漏斗将其倒入罐中。

definitions.en: a vertical metal pipe on the top of a ship or steam train through which smoke comes out
to put something, or to travel, through a funnel or something that acts like a funnel
definitions.zh: （使）流经漏斗;（使）经过漏斗形口子

examples.en: The wind funnels down these narrow streets.
examples.zh: 风吹过狭窄的街道。

examples.en: The children funnelled along the corridor into the school hall.
examples.zh: 孩子们沿走廊涌进学校礼堂。

examples.en: If you funnel the oil into the engine, you're less likely to spill it.
examples.zh: 如果你用漏斗往发动机里注油，就不太可能洒出来。

examples.en: No one knows who has been funnelling weapons to the terrorists.
examples.zh: 没人知道是谁一直在向恐怖分子输送武器。

definitions.en: a show of anger, worry, or excitement that is unnecessary or greater than the situation deserves
definitions.zh: 紧张不安；大惊小怪；过分激动

examples.en: She made such a fuss when Richard spilled a drop of wine on her blouse!
examples.zh: 理查德将一滴酒溅在她的衬衫上，她好一阵大惊小怪。

examples.en: It's all a fuss about nothing.
examples.zh: 这完全是无谓的大惊小怪。

examples.en: I don't see what the fuss is about - he seems like a fairly ordinary-looking guy to me.
examples.zh: 我看不出来为什么那么激动——在我看来他不过是一个长相普通的家伙。

examples.en: We tried to arrange a ceremony with as little fuss as possible.
examples.zh: 我们试图安排一个尽可能低调的仪式。

examples.en: She doesn't see her grandchildren very often so she makes a real fuss of them when she does.
examples.zh: 她不怎么经常见到孙子们，所以见到时往往对他们非常宠爱。

examples.en: I felt so ashamed of myself for making such a fuss.
He kicked up a tremendous fuss about having to wait.
I can't think what's got into him. He doesn't usually make such a fuss.
God, he's such a drama queen! I've never seen such a fuss.
I grant that it must have been upsetting but even so I think she made a bit of a fuss.

definitions.en: attention given to small matters that are not important

examples.en: The article was entitled "Making up with the minimum of fuss: a five-minute beauty routine that every busy woman should know".
examples.zh: 这篇文章题目为“费心最少的化妆——每位忙碌女性都应该知道的5分钟日常美容程序”。

definitions.en: to give too much attention to small matters that are not important, usually in a way that shows that you are worried and not relaxed
definitions.zh: （为小事）过于烦恼，过度焦虑;过于关注（细节）

examples.en: Please, stop fussing - the food's cooking and there's nothing more to do until the guests arrive.
examples.zh: 别瞎忙活了——饭正做着，客人们来之前没什么要做的了。

examples.en: It irritates me the way she's always fussing with her hair!
examples.zh: 她老是摆弄自己的头发，真让人受不了。

definitions.en: a small device or machine with a particular purpose
definitions.zh: 小器具;小装置;小玩意儿

examples.en: kitchen gadgets
examples.zh: 厨房器具

examples.en: Have you seen this handy little gadget - it's for separating egg yolks from whites.
examples.zh: 你见过这种很有用处的小装置吗——它是用来把蛋黄和蛋清分开的。

definitions.en: to calculate an amount, especially by using a measuring device
definitions.zh: （尤指用测量仪器）测量，计量，测算

examples.en: Use a thermometer to gauge the temperature.
examples.zh: 用温度计测量一下温度。

examples.en: I tried to gauge (= guess) the weight of the box.
examples.zh: 我试着估量箱子有多重。

definitions.en: to make a judgment about something, usually people's feelings
definitions.zh: 判定，判断（通常指人的感受）

examples.en: A poll was conducted to gauge consumers' attitudes.
examples.zh: 为了摸清消费者的看法，进行了一次民意调查。

examples.en: It's difficult to gauge how they'll react.
examples.zh: 很难判断他们会作何反应。

definitions.en: a device for measuring the amount or size of something

examples.en: a fuel/rain/temperature gauge
examples.zh: 燃料表／雨量计／温度计

examples.en: a tyre gauge
examples.zh: 胎压计

examples.en: a narrow-gauge/standard-gauge railway
examples.zh: 窄轨距／标准轨距铁轨

examples.en: a narrow gauge
examples.zh: 窄口径

examples.en: a gauge of 16 millimetres
examples.zh: 16毫米的口径

definitions.en: a way of judging or showing something, especially how successful or popular something is
definitions.zh: （尤指对是否成功或受欢迎的）评判方法，判断方法

examples.en: The fact that the play is being performed on Broadway is a gauge of its success.
examples.zh: 该剧在百老汇演出，从这一点就可以判定它是很成功的。

definitions.en: used in adjectives to express the width of the space inside a cylinder, especially the inside of a gun barrel (= part shaped like a tube)

examples.en: a twelve-gauge shotgun
examples.zh: 口径为12毫米的猎枪

definitions.en: a device, often consisting of connecting sets of wheels with teeth (= points) around the edge, that controls how much power from an engine goes to the moving parts of a machine

examples.en: Does your car have five or six gears?
examples.zh: 你的车是4挡的还是5挡的？

examples.en: I couldn't find reverse gear.
examples.zh: 我找不着倒挡了。

examples.en: The car should be in gear (= with its gears in position, allowing the vehicle to move).
examples.zh: 车挡应该挂着。

examples.en: When you start a car you need to be in first (US also low) gear.
examples.zh: 汽车启动时应该挂第一挡。

examples.en: After a slow start, the leadership campaign suddenly shifted into top gear (= started to advance very quickly).
examples.zh: 争夺领导权的竞选活动启动缓慢，但是后来却突然开足了马力紧锣密鼓地进行起来。

examples.en: Push the clutch in, put the car into gear, rev the engine and then gently let the clutch out.
To go backwards, you must put the car into reverse gear.
The car's gears aren't meshing properly.
In cars that are automatics, you don't have to bother with shifting gears.
It's no good - these gears are shot.

definitions.en: the equipment, clothes, etc. that you use to do a particular activity
definitions.zh: （从事某活动的）装备，用具，衣服

examples.en: fishing/camping gear
examples.zh: 钓鱼／野营用具

examples.en: Police in riot gear (= protective clothing) arrived to control the protesters.
examples.zh: 身着防暴衣的警察赶来制服抗议者。

examples.en: She wears all the latest gear.
examples.zh: 她穿的衣服都是最新潮的。

examples.en: Police in riot gear were called in to quell the unrest.
Everyone else was in jeans and casual gear and I had my office clothes on - I stuck out like a sore thumb.
We got ourselves togged up in walking gear for the hike.
A shop selling bondage gear has opened on the high street.
Police in riot gear lined up at the end of the street.

definitions.en: drugs

examples.en: The traffickers knew that there would always be someone willing to move the gear.
examples.zh: 非法贩卖者们知道总有人愿意转移毒品。

definitions.en: If something is genuine, it is real and exactly what it appears to be.
definitions.zh: 真正的；非伪造的；名副其实的

examples.en: genuine leather
examples.zh: 真皮

examples.en: If it is a genuine Michelangelo drawing, it will sell for millions.
examples.zh: 如果这幅画是米开朗琪罗的真迹，它的售价将会达数百万。

examples.en: He's a very genuine person.
examples.zh: 他为人很真诚。

examples.en: Machiko looked at me with genuine surprise - "Are you really going?" she said.
examples.zh: 町子真的很惊讶，她看着我问道：“你果真要走吗？”

examples.en: He has demonstrated a genuine interest in the project.
A number of forged works of art have been sold as genuine.
In an indiscreet moment, the president let his genuine opinions be known.
The police are trying to sift out the genuine warnings from all the hoax calls they have received.
I'm not surprised the offer wasn't genuine, it sounded too good to be true.

definitions.en: a small problem or fault that prevents something from being successful or working as well as it should

examples.en: We'd expected a few glitches, but everything's gone remarkably smoothly.
examples.zh: 我们本来预计会出现一些小的差错，但是一切都进展得非常顺利。

examples.en: The system has been plagued with glitches ever since its launch.
examples.zh: 系统自启动以来小故障不断。

examples.en: The computer failure was due to a glitch caused by lightning.
examples.zh: 这次计算机故障时由于雷电造成的脉冲干扰。

definitions.en: an alphabetical list, with meanings, of the words or phrases in a text that are difficult to understand

examples.en: a glossary of technical terms
examples.zh: 术语汇编

definitions.en: involving violence and blood
definitions.zh: 血腥暴力的;残暴的;血淋淋的

examples.en: a very gory film
examples.zh: 血腥暴力的电影

examples.en: a gory description of the operation
examples.zh: 对手术过程的血淋淋的描述

definitions.en: any of the four books of the Bible that contain details of the life of Jesus Christ
definitions.zh: 福音书（《圣经》中记载耶稣生平的四福音书之一）

examples.en: St Mark's Gospel/the Gospel according to St Mark
examples.zh: 《马可福音》

examples.en: to preach/spread the gospel
examples.zh: 宣讲／传布福音

definitions.en: the complete truth
definitions.zh: （用于强调某事的真实性）绝对真实

examples.en: If Mary tells you something, you can take it as gospel.
examples.zh: 玛丽告诉你的事，你可以绝对相信。

definitions.en: a style of religious music originally performed by black Americans
definitions.zh: （起源于美国黑人中的）福音音乐

definitions.en: a set of principles or ideas that someone believes in

examples.en: the gospel of hard work
examples.zh: 勤奋工作的信条

definitions.en: conversation or reports about other people's private lives that might be unkind, disapproving, or not true
definitions.zh: （有关别人隐私的）流言蜚语，闲言碎语，闲聊

examples.en: Her letter was full of gossip.
examples.zh: 她信里讲的都是些闲言碎语。

examples.en: I don't like all this idle gossip.
examples.zh: 我讨厌这些无聊的流言蜚语。

examples.en: I've got some juicy gossip for you.
examples.zh: 我有些特别有趣的小道消息告诉你。

examples.en: Have you heard the (latest) gossip?
examples.zh: 你听到（最近流传的）那些闲言碎语了吗？

examples.en: Jane and Lyn sat in the kitchen having a good gossip about their friends.
examples.zh: 简和琳恩坐在厨房里大聊特聊自己的那帮朋友。

examples.en: She's a terrible gossip.
examples.zh: 她是个很要命的长舌妇。

examples.en: I've just been talking to the girls downstairs and I've got some delicious gossip.
Some magazines contain nothing but scandal and gossip.
She always keeps me up to date with the latest gossip.
There's a lot of gossip about me going around. What have you been saying?
Let's go for a coffee - I need to catch up on all the gossip.

definitions.en: to talk about other people's private lives
definitions.zh: （对别人的隐私）说长道短，传播流言蜚语

examples.en: Stop gossiping and do your work.
examples.zh: 别再闲扯了，继续干点活去。

examples.en: People have started to gossip about us.
examples.zh: 人们已经开始说我们的闲话了。

examples.en: She's been gossiping and hasn't done a stroke of work all morning.
Janet spends hours gossiping on the phone.
I wouldn't tell her your secrets - you know how she likes to gossip.
It's not only the women around here who like to gossip, you know!
The old folk sit gossiping in the village square for most of the morning.

definitions.en: short form of have got to

examples.en: I gotta go now.
examples.zh: 我得走了。

examples.en: He's gotta be kidding.
examples.zh: 他一定是在开玩笑。

definitions.en: happening or changing slowly over a long period of time or distance

examples.en: There has been a gradual improvement in our sales figures over the last two years.
examples.zh: 在过去的两年里，我们的销售额一直在逐步上升。

examples.en: As you go further south, you will notice a gradual change of climate.
examples.zh: 当你一路往南走下去的时候，你就会注意到气候的细微变化。

examples.en: Her conversion to Buddhism/Islam was a very gradual process.
There has been a gradual degeneration of the judicial system in the last few years.
He suffered a gradual loss of memory.
There is a gradual build-up of suspense throughout the film, until it comes to an unexpected ending.
He lamented the gradual disintegration of family values.

definitions.en: made of, or seeming like, granules
definitions.zh: 由颗粒构成的;颗粒状的

examples.en: a granular texture
examples.zh: 粒状结构

definitions.en: including small details

examples.en: The analysis needs to be more granular.
examples.zh: 分析需要更为细致。

definitions.en: the quality of including a lot of small details
definitions.zh: 由颗粒构成；颗粒状；含颗粒

examples.en: The marketing analysis offers a high level of granularity.
examples.zh: 营销分析提供了细颗粒度的数据。

definitions.en: to quickly take something in your hand(s) and hold it firmly

examples.en: Rosie suddenly grasped my hand.
examples.zh: 罗茜突然抓住了我的手。

examples.en: We must grasp every opportunity to strengthen economic ties with other countries.
examples.zh: 我们必须把握住一切机会加强与其他国家的经济联系。

examples.en: She suddenly grasped my arm and looked at me.
I grasped the gun with both hands and took aim.
The baby stretched out a tiny hand and grasped my finger tightly.
Feeling herself falling, she grasped the rail with both hands.
The eagle swooped down and grasped the rabbit in its sharp talons.

definitions.en: to understand something, especially something difficult
definitions.zh: 理解，领悟，明白（尤指费解之事）

examples.en: I think I managed to grasp the main points of the lecture.
examples.zh: 我觉得我还是理解了演讲的要点。

examples.en: The government has acknowledged that homelessness is a problem but it has failed to grasp the scale of the problem.
examples.zh: 政府已经承认很多人无家可归是个问题，但还没有意识到问题的严重性。

definitions.en: the act of holding onto someone or something

examples.en: He shook my hand with a very firm grasp.
examples.zh: 他非常有力地和我握了握手。

examples.en: The presidency at last looked within her grasp (= it looked possible that she might become president).
examples.zh: 终于，总裁的职位对她来说似乎伸手可及。

examples.en: Why is success always beyond my grasp (= impossible to get)?
examples.zh: 为什么我总是功败垂成？

examples.en: The gold medal slipped from his grasp (= he was unable to get it) in the last moments of the race.
examples.zh: 在比赛最后一刻他与金牌失之交臂。

examples.en: I sometimes think that he's losing his grasp on reality (= his ability to judge what is real and what is not).
examples.zh: 有时我觉得他认不清现实。

definitions.en: understanding

examples.en: He has a good grasp of the issues.
examples.zh: 他对这些问题非常了解。

definitions.en: showing or expressing thanks, especially to another person
definitions.zh: （尤指对其他人）感激的，表示感谢的

examples.en: I'm so grateful (to you) for all that you've done.
examples.zh: 你为我做了这一切，我（对你）真是感激不尽。

examples.en: If you could get that report finished by Thursday, I'd be very grateful.
examples.zh: 如果你能在周四之前完成那份报告，我将感激不尽。

examples.en: After the earthquake we felt grateful to be alive.
examples.zh: 地震后，我们大难不死，真是应该感谢上苍。

examples.en: I'm just grateful that I'm not still working for him.
examples.zh: 真是谢天谢地，我现在不再为他工作了。

examples.en: I would be most grateful if you would send me the book immediately.
examples.zh: 如果你能马上把书寄给我，我将感激不尽。

examples.en: It was a long journey and I was grateful for his company.
I tried to convey in my speech how grateful we all were for his help.
I'd be eternally grateful if you could arrange it.
We are all profoundly grateful for your help and encouragement.
The authors are grateful for the unceasing support of the editors in London and New York.

definitions.en: wanting a lot more food, money, etc. than you need

examples.en: greedy, selfish people
examples.zh: 贪婪自私的人

examples.en: He's greedy for power/success.
examples.zh: 他贪恋权力／极度渴望成功。

examples.en: You greedy pig! You're not having another chocolate biscuit!
It would be greedy to have another slice of cake.
He was portrayed as a greedy, grasping man.
Don't be greedy!
Is it just greedy to want more money?

definitions.en: the surface in a cooker that can be heated to very high temperatures and under which you put food to be cooked
to cook food over fire or hot coals, usually on a metal frame

examples.en: Dad was grilling chicken in the back yard.
examples.zh: 爸爸在后院烤鸡。

examples.en: I'll grill the bacon rather than fry it.
examples.zh: 我会炙烤培根，而不是油煎。

definitions.en: to ask someone a lot of questions for a long time

examples.en: After being grilled by the police for two days, Johnson signed a confession.
examples.zh: 被警方审问了两天后，约翰逊在供状上签字认罪。

examples.en: Her parents would grill her about where she'd been.
examples.zh: 她父母会盘问她到哪儿去了。

definitions.en: to hold very tightly

examples.en: The baby gripped my finger with her tiny hand.
examples.zh: 婴儿的小手紧紧握住我的手指。

examples.en: Old tyres won't grip (= stay on the surface of the road) in the rain very well.
examples.zh: 在雨天，旧轮胎在路面上防滑性能不是太好。

examples.en: Grip the top firmly and then twist it anti-clockwise.
I was terrified when he suddenly gripped my throat in his hands.
These two pieces of metal grip the wood, so you can saw through it.
His hands were all greasy and he couldn't grip the wheel properly.
Gripping the sides of the ladder tightly, she nervously climbed one step higher.

definitions.en: to keep someone's attention completely

examples.en: This trial has gripped the whole nation.
examples.zh: 这场审判引起了全国的关注。

examples.en: I was gripped throughout the entire two hours of the film.
examples.zh: 整整两个小时我都沉浸在这部电影里。

definitions.en: When an emotion such as fear grips you, you feel it strongly.
definitions.zh: （感情等）强烈地控制；对…产生强烈影响

examples.en: Then he turned towards me, and I was suddenly gripped by fear.
examples.zh: 然后，他转过身对着我，我突然惊恐万状。

definitions.en: control over something or someone

examples.en: Rebels have tightened their grip on the city.
examples.zh: 叛军已经加强了他们对这座城市的控制。

examples.en: He will do anything to keep his grip on power.
examples.zh: 为了保住权力他什么都会干。

examples.en: The Mafia has relaxed its grip on local businesses.
No one seems to have a firm grip on the company at the moment.
The demonstrations suggest that the president's grip on the country is loosening.
The army has tightened its grip on the region.
For several years the area has been in the grip of the rebel warlords.

definitions.en: a tight hold on something or someone

examples.en: She tightened her grip on my arm.
examples.zh: 她越来越用力地抓住我的胳膊。

examples.en: She would not loosen her grip on my arm.
examples.zh: 她紧抓住我的胳膊不放。

definitions.en: a bag for travelling that is smaller than a suitcase
If something is groundbreaking, it is very new and a big change from other things of its type.
definitions.zh: 开辟新天地的，开创性的，创新的

examples.en: His latest movie is interesting, but not groundbreaking.
examples.zh: 他最近拍的电影很有趣，但没有什么创新。

definitions.en: a religious leader or teacher in the Hindu or Sikh religion
definitions.zh: 古鲁（印度教或锡克教的宗教导师或领袖）

examples.en: a management guru
examples.zh: 管理界泰斗

examples.en: a lifestyle guru
examples.zh: 生活专家

definitions.en: an experience in which you see, hear, feel, or smell something that does not exist, usually because you are ill or have taken a drug

examples.en: A high temperature can cause hallucinations.
examples.zh: 高温可使人产生幻觉。

examples.en: auditory/olfactory hallucinations
examples.zh: 幻听／幻嗅

definitions.en: to (cause to) stop moving or doing something or happening

examples.en: "Halt!" called the guard. "You can't go any further without a permit."
examples.zh: “站住！”警卫喊道，“没有通行证不能入内。”

examples.en: Production has halted at all of the company's factories because of the pay dispute.
examples.zh: 由于工资纠纷，这家公司所有工厂都停产了。

examples.en: Security forces halted the demonstrators by blocking the road.
examples.zh: 警卫部队用封路的办法来制止示威者前进。

examples.en: The government has had to take measures to halt the population explosion.
The government must take measures, he said, to halt the country's slide into recession.
The ever-increasing demand for private cars could be halted by more investment in public transport.
Filming was halted after the lead actor became ill.
Building at the site was halted after human remains were unearthed earlier this month.

definitions.en: an occasion when something stops moving or happening

examples.en: the recent halt in production
examples.zh: 最近的停产

examples.en: Severe flooding has brought trains to a halt (= prevented them from moving) on several lines in Scotland.
examples.zh: 凶猛的洪水使苏格兰几条铁路线上的火车停驶。

examples.en: The bus came to a halt (= stopped) just in time to avoid hitting the wall.
examples.zh: 公共汽车正好及时停下，没有撞到墙上。

examples.en: If traffic increases beyond a certain level, the city grinds to a halt (= stops completely).
examples.zh: 如果交通流量的增长超过一定限度，城市就会陷于瘫痪状态。

examples.en: The car screeched to a halt (= stopped suddenly and noisily) just as the lights turned red.
examples.zh: 这辆车就在信号灯变红时嘎吱一声刹住了车。

examples.en: How many more people will have to die before they call a halt to the fighting?
examples.zh: 还要死多少人他们才会停战？

definitions.en: to reduce something by half or divide something into two equal pieces

examples.en: In the past eight years, the elephant population in Africa has been halved.
examples.zh: 在过去的8年中，非洲大象的数量减少了一半。

examples.en: The potatoes will cook more quickly if you halve them before you put them in the oven.
examples.zh: 如果把土豆切成两半再放进烤箱，它们会熟得更快。

examples.en: Their profits have halved in the last six months.
examples.zh: 在过去的6个月里，他们的利润减少了一半。

definitions.en: in American football, a pass in which the ball is put directly into the hands of another player
definitions.zh: （美式橄榄球中的）手递手传球，递传

examples.en: Flacco faked a handoff and looked to pass.
examples.zh: 弗拉克虚晃一枪，假意要将球递传给队友，实则寻求其他传球机会。

examples.en: A botched handoff that was meant for him never made it to his hands.
examples.zh: 递传失误，本来是要传给他，却一直没有递到他手上。

examples.en: The relay team failed to finish the final because of a botched baton handoff from Jones.
examples.zh: 由于琼斯的交接棒失误，接力队未能完成决赛。

examples.en: He swatted Smith away with a powerful handoff and galloped over the line to complete the scoring.
examples.zh: 他一手夹球，一手猛力推开史密斯，然后飞奔过线进球得分。

examples.en: Watch the defender closely to see if the handoff is an option.
examples.zh: 紧盯防守队员，看看是否有机会带球推人。

examples.en: Drug handoffs are easy to spot.
examples.zh: 手传手毒品交易很容易被发现。

examples.en: Failure to synchronize actions during hand-offs is visible in medical settings.
examples.zh: 在医疗环境下，交接期间未能实现同步是可能发生的现象。

examples.en: the handoff of power to an interim government
examples.zh: 把权力交给临时政府

definitions.en: to become or make hard

examples.en: The mixture hardens as it cools.
examples.zh: 混合物冷却时变硬了。

examples.en: It is thought that high cholesterol levels in the blood can harden the arteries (= make them thicker and stiffer, causing disease).
examples.zh: 据信血液中的胆固醇含量高会导致动脉硬化。

definitions.en: to become more severe, determined, or unpleasant
definitions.zh: （使）变严厉，（使）变坚强，（使）变冷酷

examples.en: Living in the desert hardened the recruits (= made them more strong and determined).
examples.zh: 沙漠的艰苦生活把新兵锻炼得更加坚强。

examples.en: As the war progressed, attitudes on both sides hardened (= became more severe and determined).
examples.zh: 随着战争的持续，双方的态度都变得更加强硬。

definitions.en: a piece of equipment with straps and belts, used to control or hold in place a person, animal, or object

examples.en: a safety harness
examples.zh: 安全带

examples.en: a baby harness
examples.zh: 婴儿学步用的系带

examples.en: a parachute harness
examples.zh: 降落伞吊带

definitions.en: to put a harness on a horse, or to connect a horse to a vehicle using a harness

examples.en: There is a great deal of interest in harnessing wind and waves as new sources of power.
examples.zh: 人们对开发利用风和波浪这些新能源非常感兴趣。

definitions.en: (a situation causing) difficulty or trouble

examples.en: I can't face the hassle of moving again.
examples.zh: 我受不了再搬一次家这个麻烦。

examples.en: My boss has been giving me a lot of hassle this week.
examples.zh: 这星期老板给我找了很多麻烦事。

examples.en: It's one of the few bars that women can go to and not get any hassle from men.
examples.zh: 那是女性可以去的为数不多的几个酒吧之一，在那里女性不会受到男人的任何烦扰。

examples.en: It was such a hassle trying to get my bank account changed that I nearly gave up.
examples.zh: 更换银行账号真是麻烦，我差点都想放弃了。

examples.en: I should have taken it back to the shop but I just didn't think it was worth (all) the hassle.
examples.zh: 我应该把它退回商店，但我认为不值得这么麻烦。

definitions.en: to annoy someone, especially by repeatedly asking them something
definitions.zh: 烦扰（尤指通过反复提问）

examples.en: I'll do it in my own time - just stop hassling me!
examples.zh: 我会自己安排时间做的——别烦我了！

examples.en: The children keep hassling me to take them to Disneyland.
examples.zh: 孩子们不断地烦我，要我带他们去迪斯尼乐园。

definitions.en: in a way that is not clearly remembered or noticed

examples.en: She only hazily remembered her previous visit.
examples.zh: 她只是模糊地记得她之前的访问。

examples.en: He was hazily aware of a dull ache in the small of his back.
examples.zh: 他隐隐约约地感觉到后背隐隐作痛。

examples.en: The sun rose hazily behind the palm trees.
examples.zh: 太阳朦胧地从棕榈树后升起。

examples.en: The streets and monuments of Athens stretched hazily away to the mountains.
examples.zh: 雅典的街道和纪念碑朦胧地延伸到群山之中。

examples.en: What was happening to her, she wondered hazily.
He walked along the hazily familiar terrain.
Brownish clouds drifted hazily over the landscape.
Meg saw him hazily through the steam from the pan.

definitions.en: a large, powerful light at the front of a vehicle, usually one of two

examples.en: You could see the beams of the car headlamps.
examples.zh: 你可以看到汽车前灯发出的光束。

examples.en: I had to change a headlamp bulb on my car.
examples.zh: 我得给车的前灯换个灯泡。

examples.en: He remembers mowing the lawn by the light of car headlamps.
Park the vehicle with the headlamps off.
I got into the car and turned off the headlamps.

definitions.en: a light worn on the head, for example when going under the ground

examples.en: Men wearing headlamps worked in tunnels as dark as tombs.
examples.zh: 人们戴着头灯在黑得像坟墓一样的地道里工作。

examples.en: The divers wore waterproof suits, helmets and headlamps.
examples.zh: 潜水员穿着防水服，戴着头盔和头灯。

examples.en: Workers' headlamps cut through the darkness.
The surgeon wears a headlamp to control the light she needs.
Wearing headlamps and 20-pound backpacks, they set out to jog an 85-mile loop around Glacier Peak.

definitions.en: the amount of space below a roof, ceiling, or bridge, or below the top of something

examples.en: It's a small car but there's lots of headroom.
examples.zh: 这是辆小型汽车，但它的内部空间很大。

examples.en: The low headroom on the staircase could be a problem.
For rear passengers there is plenty of width but not much headroom.
With this type of loft conversion there will be less headroom, as the slope of the roof is retained in the conversion.
The builders of the M6 in the 1960s did not leave enough headroom for boats to go under the motorway.
The space inside the tent is good, with decent headroom.
Pack the cucumbers into the canning jars, then cover with hot brine leaving 1/2 inch of headroom.

definitions.en: strongly felt and sincere

examples.en: heartfelt relief
examples.zh: 由衷的宽慰

examples.en: Please accept my heartfelt apologies/thanks.
examples.zh: 请接受我诚挚的道歉／感谢。

definitions.en: an extremely unpleasant or difficult place, situation, or experience
definitions.zh: 悲惨的境况；苦难的经历

examples.en: Work is sheer hell at the moment.
examples.zh: 目前的工作如同在地狱一般。

examples.en: The last few months have been absolute hell.
examples.zh: 最后几个月完全就是在受罪。

examples.en: I'll go to Hell for this.
examples.zh: 因为这件事我会下地狱的。

examples.en: I worked for her for two years and she made my life hell.
examples.zh: 我给她打了两年工，她让我的日子很不好过。

examples.en: Living with her has been pure hell recently.
I'd see her in hell before I'd agree to an arrangement like that.
It was a living hell for the soldiers out there on the front line.
" Didn't you enjoy teaching, then?" "No, it was sheer hell - I'd never be a teacher."
She had to suffer the hell of giving birth to her dead baby.

definitions.en: used to express anger or to add emphasis

examples.en: Oh hell, I've forgotten my key!
examples.zh: 该死，我忘带钥匙了！

examples.en: What the hell was that noise?
examples.zh: 那到底是什么声音？

examples.en: We don't have a hope in hell (= we have no hope) of meeting such a tight deadline.
examples.zh: 我们决不可能在这么紧的期限内完成。

definitions.en: that is the reason or explanation for

examples.en: His mother was Italian, hence his name - Luca.
examples.zh: 他母亲是意大利人，所以他的名字叫——卢卡。

examples.en: Peter's leaving at the end of this week - hence his anxiety to get his work finished.
The prime minister was attending the conference, hence all the extra security.
He's just got a pay rise, hence the new car.
She's just found out she failed her exams, hence her bad mood.
The firm is owned by Mark Atkins, hence the name - MA Advertising.

definitions.en: from this time

examples.en: The project will be completed at the end of the decade, two years hence.
examples.zh: 本项目将于这个10年期结束时完成，从现在算起还有两年时间。

definitions.en: with these words or with this action

examples.en: I hereby pronounce you man and wife.
examples.zh: 我特此宣布你们结为夫妻。

definitions.en: to pause before you do or say something, often because you are uncertain or nervous about it

examples.en: She hesitated slightly before answering the detective's question.
examples.zh: 在回答警探的问题之前，她稍稍犹豫了一下。

examples.en: "Do you love me?" she asked. He hesitated and then said, "I'm not sure."
examples.zh: “你爱我吗？”她问道。他犹豫了一下，然后说：“我不确定。”

examples.en: If you need anything, don't hesitate to call me.
examples.zh: 如果你需要什么，马上给我打电话。

examples.en: Should you ever need anything, please don't hesitate to contact me.
He hesitated before ringing her, fearful of what she might say.
He hesitated before opening the door and walking into the room.
She hesitated in the middle of the sentence.
He sounded nervous, and kept hesitating as he spoke.

definitions.en: consisting of parts or things that are very different from each other

examples.en: Switzerland is a heterogeneous confederation of 26 self-governing cantons.
examples.zh: 瑞士是一个由26个不同的自治州组成的联邦。

definitions.en: (of a method of teaching) allowing students to learn by discovering things themselves and learning from their own experiences rather than by telling them things
(of some animals) to spend the winter sleeping

examples.en: The turtle hibernates in a shallow burrow for six months of the year.
examples.zh: 这种龟每年在浅地洞里冬眠6个月。

definitions.en: the state of being asleep for the winter

examples.en: Bears go into hibernation in the autumn.
examples.zh: 熊在秋天进入冬眠状态。

examples.en: It was the time of year when rattlesnakes emerge from winter hibernation.
examples.zh: 这是每年响尾蛇从冬眠中醒来的时节。

examples.en: Bears are thinner following hibernation.
Bats are now beginning their long winter hibernation.
Research showed that some members of the mosquito species have evolved so that hibernation is triggered by a shorter day length.

definitions.en: to take control of an aircraft or other vehicle during a journey, especially using violence

examples.en: Two men hijacked a jet travelling to Paris and demanded $125,000.
examples.zh: 两名男子劫持了一架飞往巴黎的飞机并勒索125 000美元。

examples.en: He resents the way his ideas have been hijacked by others in the department.
examples.zh: 他对部门里其他人剽窃他想法的做法十分不满。

definitions.en: an occasion when someone uses force to take control of an aircraft or other vehicle

examples.en: The hijack ended with the release of all the plane's passengers unharmed.
examples.zh: 劫机事件以所有机上乘客平安获释而告终。

examples.en: He’s a leading suspect in the hijacking of the jetliner.
examples.zh: 他是民航机被劫持事件中的首要嫌疑人。

definitions.en: to limit the ability of someone to do something, or to limit the development of something

examples.en: High winds have hindered firefighters in their efforts to put out the blaze.
examples.zh: 大风妨碍了消防队员扑灭大火。

examples.en: Her progress certainly hasn't been hindered by her lack of experience.
examples.zh: 经验不足当然没有妨碍她的进步。

definitions.en: consisting of parts or people that are similar to each other or are of the same type
definitions.zh: 由同类事物（或人）组成的；同类的；相似的

examples.en: a homogeneous group/society
examples.zh: 由同种族人组成的群体／社会

examples.en: The population of the village has remained remarkably homogeneous.
examples.zh: 这个村庄的人口保持着显着的同族特征。

definitions.en: having a similar position, structure, value, or purpose
definitions.zh: （位置、结构、价值或目的等）相似的，相应的

examples.en: These activities are meant to be homologous to modern work conditions.
A model represents, or is homologous with, a specific aspect of perceived reality.
The wing of a bat and the arm of a man are homologous structures.
examples.zh: 蝙蝠的翅膀和人类的手臂是相应的同源结构。

examples.en: A female's paraurethral glands are homologous to the male prostate.
Feathers are traditionally considered homologous with reptilian scales.

definitions.en: in a way that is honest

examples.en: They have always dealt honestly and fairly with their customers.
examples.zh: 他们一直诚实公道地对待顾客。

examples.en: I can't honestly say what time I'll be home.
examples.zh: 我说不准什么时候能回家。

examples.en: I'll do it tomorrow, honestly (= I promise that I will do it).
examples.zh: 我明天会做，我发誓。

examples.en: I can honestly say that I have no interest whatsoever in the royal family.
He answered openly and honestly without any hesitation.
"You played brilliantly in the concert." "Oh thanks, do you honestly think so?"
Quite honestly, the thought of standing up and performing live terrified me.
I don't mind criticism as long as it's honestly given and doesn't get personal.

definitions.en: used to emphasize disapproval

examples.en: Honestly, you'd think she'd have asked you first!
examples.zh: 真够呛，你肯定以为她会先问问你的！

definitions.en: US spelling of honour
a quality that combines respect, being proud, and honesty

examples.en: a man of honour
examples.zh: 品德高尚的人

examples.en: We fought for the honour of our country.
examples.zh: 我们为我们国家的荣誉而战。

examples.en: a banquet in honour of the president
examples.zh: 为总统举行的宴会

examples.en: I felt honour bound to tell him the truth.
examples.zh: 我觉得应该告诉他事实真相。

examples.en: Would you do me the honour of accompanying me to the New Year Ball?
examples.zh: 你愿不愿意赏光陪我去参加新年舞会？

examples.en: Yes, Your Honour.
examples.zh: 是的，阁下。

examples.en: The case was dealt with by Her Honour Judge Kirkham.
examples.zh: 这个案子是由柯克汉姆法官阁下处理的。

examples.en: It's an honour to have you here today, Mr President.
Great honour attaches to winning this award.
The party was held in honour of Vivienne Westwood, that doyenne of British fashion.
It's an honour to meet you Sir.
He felt that his honour was at stake.

definitions.en: a reward, prize, or title that publicly expresses admiration or respect

examples.en: She received an honour for her services to the community.
examples.zh: 她因为社区提供服务而获得了表彰。

examples.en: The honours were the personal gift of the Queen.

definitions.en: If you complete a school or university qualification with honours, you achieve a high standard.

examples.en: She graduated with honours in June 1970.

definitions.en: to show great respect for someone or something, especially in public

examples.en: be honoured for He was honoured for his bravery.
examples.zh: 他因表现英勇而受到了表彰。

examples.en: We are honoured (= proud and happy) to have you here tonight.
examples.zh: 今晚您能光临我们深感荣幸。

examples.en: I would be honoured to meet him.
examples.zh: 要是能见到他，我会感到无比荣幸。

examples.en: They decided not to honour an existing order for aircraft.
examples.zh: 他们决定不兑现已有的飞机订单。

examples.en: Children need to be taught to honour other people's opinions.
The treaty honours the rights of both nations.
The press nowadays seems incapable of honouring anyone's privacy.
There actually is a rule about not blocking the front entrance, but no one honours it.
The fund was set up to honour the memory of Joe's grandmother, a tireless social campaigner.

definitions.en: to give someone public praise or a reward

examples.en: be honoured with He was honoured with a knighthood.
examples.zh: 他被封为爵士。

definitions.en: very unpleasant or bad

examples.en: He's got a horrible cold.
examples.zh: 他得了重感冒。

examples.en: What's that horrible smell?
examples.zh: 是什么气味这么难闻？

examples.en: That was a horrible thing to say!
examples.zh: 说那种话真是可恶！

examples.en: a horrible crime
examples.zh: 骇人的罪行

examples.en: What earthly reason can she have for being so horrible to you?
The sight was so horrible that I had to turn away.
Why should anyone want to eat something so horrible?
She's got a horrible little dog that yaps around your ankles.
I refuse to live under the same roof as that horrible man.

definitions.en: unfriendly and not liking something

examples.en: a hostile crowd
examples.zh: 不友好的人群

examples.en: The president had a hostile reception in Ohio this morning.
examples.zh: 总统今天早上在俄亥俄州受到了不友好的接待。

examples.en: There has been a hostile reaction to the government's proposed tax increases.
The security forces exercised great restraint by not responding to hostile attacks and threats.
The company has managed to fight off a hostile takeover bid .
Every day they had to run the gauntlet of hostile journalists on their way to school.
He's famous for conducting hostile interviews with politicians.

definitions.en: not agreeing with something

examples.en: I'm not hostile to (= against) the idea of change as such.
examples.zh: 我并不反对作这样的改变。

definitions.en: difficult or not suitable for living or growing

examples.en: hostile weather conditions
examples.zh: 恶劣的天气条件

examples.en: a hostile climate/environment
examples.zh: 恶劣的气候／环境

definitions.en: connected with the enemy in a war

examples.en: hostile aircraft/forces
examples.zh: 敌机／敌军

definitions.en: relating to situations in which one company wants to buy another company whose owners do not want to sell it

examples.en: The company convinced investors to reject a hostile bid from Enterprise Oil Plc worth £1.5 billion.
examples.zh: 这家公司说服了投资者拒绝由企业原油公司提出的价值15亿美元的敌意收购方案。

examples.en: a hostile merger
examples.zh: 敌意（并购）
