definitions.en: not proud or not believing that you are important

examples.en: He's very humble about his success.
examples.zh: 他对自己的成功十分谦虚。

examples.en: Please accept our humble apologies for the error.
examples.zh: 出现这样的差错，请接受我们真诚的道歉。

examples.en: Even when she became rich and famous, she never forgot her humble background.
examples.zh: 即使她发了财也出了名，她也永远没忘记自己卑微的出身。

examples.en: At that time she was just a humble mechanic.
examples.zh: 那时她只是个不起眼的技工。

examples.en: Welcome to our humble abode (= our home).
examples.zh: 欢迎来到寒舍。

definitions.en: to make someone understand that they are not as important or special as they thought

examples.en: He was humbled by the child's generosity.
examples.zh: 那个孩子的宽宏大量使他自惭形秽。

examples.en: The world champion was humbled (= unexpectedly defeated) by an unknown outsider in last night's race.
examples.zh: 昨晚比赛中那位世界冠军意外地被一位无名小卒击败。

definitions.en: a frame or fence for jumping over in a race

examples.en: He fell at the last hurdle.
examples.zh: 他在最后一个栏架处摔倒了。

examples.en: She cleared (= jumped over) all the hurdles easily and raced to the finishing line.
examples.zh: 她轻松地跨过所有栏架到达了终点。

examples.en: the 400-metre hurdles
examples.zh: 400米跨栏比赛

examples.en: There are still a few technical hurdles to be surmounted before the product can be put on sale to the public.
Getting planning permission for the building was only the first hurdle.
She overcame many hurdles to get to where she is today.
He knew that many hurdles stood between him and success.
We've overcome all the major hurdles.

definitions.en: a problem that you have to deal with before you can make progress

examples.en: Getting a work permit was the first hurdle to overcome.
examples.zh: 取得工作许可证是要解决的第一道难题。

examples.en: The cost of this exercise is proving to be a major hurdle.
examples.zh: 这种锻炼的费用将是一个很大的难题。

definitions.en: to run in a race in which there are hurdles to be jumped over, or to jump over something while running

examples.en: He hurdled the gate and scrambled up the hill.
examples.zh: 他翻越大门爬上了山坡。

definitions.en: the degree to which people keep themselves or their environment clean, especially to prevent disease

examples.en: Poor standards of hygiene mean that the disease spreads fast.
examples.zh: 糟糕的卫生状况意味着疾病会快速传播。

examples.en: health and hygiene regulations
examples.zh: 健康和卫生规定

examples.en: dental/personal hygiene
examples.zh: 口腔／个人卫生

examples.en: She is obsessed with personal hygiene and washes constantly.
The dentist stressed the need for good oral hygiene.
To prevent the spread of harmful bacteria, everyone should understand the basics of good food hygiene.
When those boys go to camp, I hate to think what their standards of hygiene are like!
The last two centuries have seen enormous improvements in health and hygiene generally.

definitions.en: a situation in which something is advertised and discussed in newspapers, on television, etc. a lot in order to attract everyone's interest

examples.en: media hype
examples.zh: 媒体炒作

examples.en: There's been a lot of hype around/surrounding his latest film.
examples.zh: 围绕他的最新影片进行了大规模的宣传。

examples.en: I've been put off reading the book by all the hype.
examples.zh: 所有的宣传炒作让我对这本书产生了反感。

definitions.en: to repeatedly advertise and discuss something in newspapers, on television, etc. in order to attract everyone's interest
definitions.zh: （通过新闻媒体）大肆宣传，炒作

examples.en: It's being hyped as the musical event of the year.
examples.zh: 它正被炒作成年度音乐盛事。

definitions.en: the symbol -, used to join two words together, or to show that a word has been divided into two parts at the end of one line and the beginning of the next

examples.en: There are hyphens in "well-to-do".
examples.zh: “well-to-do”中有连字符。

examples.en: Where should I put the hyphen to break the word 'authority'?
There is a hyphen between the two names.

definitions.en: an idea or explanation for something that is based on known facts but has not yet been proved

examples.en: Several hypotheses for global warming have been suggested.
examples.zh: 已经提出了好几种全球变暖的假说。

definitions.en: imagined or suggested but not necessarily real or true

examples.en: a hypothetical example/situation
examples.zh: 假设的例子／情况

examples.en: This is all very hypothetical but supposing Jackie got the job, how would that affect you?
examples.zh: 这当然全是假设，不过假若杰基得到了那份工作，对你又会有什么影响呢？

definitions.en: to think of an idea or ideas

examples.en: It is a great forum, where you can discuss, debate, ideate, and develop fresh new perspectives.
examples.zh: 这是一个特别棒的论坛，在这里你可以讨论、辩论、构思并发展新的观点。

examples.en: They were asked to ideate a new system.
examples.zh: 他们收到指示，要构建一个新的系统。

definitions.en: An idempotent element of a set does not change in value when multiplied by itself.
exactly the same, or very similar
definitions.zh: 完全相同的；极为相似的

examples.en: I've got three identical blue suits.
examples.zh: 我有3套完全相同的蓝色西装。

examples.en: The two rooms were virtually identical.
examples.zh: 这两个房间几乎一模一样。

examples.en: The interests of both parties may not be identical, but they do overlap considerably.
examples.zh: 双方的利益可能并不完全相同，但的确也有很多重合之处。

examples.en: The tests are identical to those carried out last year.
examples.zh: 这些测试和去年进行的完全相同。

examples.en: Hospitals always seem such impersonal places - rows of identical beds in dull grey rooms.
The measurements of both rooms were identical.
We flew over the city with its serried ranks of identical grey houses.
Their twins are virtually identical.
She had shut out two of her first four Wimbledon opponents by identical 6-0, 6-0 scores.

definitions.en: a group of words in a fixed order that has a particular meaning that is different from the meanings of each word on its own

examples.en: To "bite off more than you can chew" is an idiom that means you have tried to do something which is too difficult for you.
Both operas are very much in the modern idiom.
examples.zh: 两部歌剧都很有现代风格。

examples.en: He used a very obscure idiom which I can't quite bring to mind.
I wasn't familiar with the idiom so I had to guess what he meant.

definitions.en: containing or consisting of an idiom
definitions.zh: 习语的;成语的;合乎语言习惯的

examples.en: "Bite the bullet" is an idiomatic expression that means to accept something unpleasant without complaining.
examples.zh: bite the bullet 是个惯用表达法，意思是没有怨言地接受令人不快的事物。

examples.en: She was born in Italy but her English is fluent and idiomatic.
examples.zh: 她出生在意大利，却讲一口流利地道的英语。

definitions.en: having strange or unusual habits, ways of behaving, or features
definitions.zh: 有怪癖的，有不寻常特征的

examples.en: The film, three hours long, is directed in his usual idiosyncratic style.
examples.zh: 这部电影长达三个小时，是他惯常的独特执导风格。

examples.en: He follows a very idiosyncratic schedule.
examples.zh: 他的日程安排很特别。

examples.en: The new model of the car is just as idiosyncratic as the original.
He's been working on an idiosyncratic score to an equally idiosyncratic film.
An idiosyncratic approach to life and learning is a virtue throughout the college.

definitions.en: the electrical system in an engine that causes the fuel to burn or explode in order to start the engine

examples.en: Switch/Turn the ignition on.
examples.zh: 打开点火开关。

examples.en: an ignition key
examples.zh: 点火开关

definitions.en: not having enough knowledge, understanding, or information about something

examples.en: Many teenagers are surprisingly ignorant about current politics.
examples.zh: 许多青少年对时事政治表现出惊人的无知。

examples.en: We remained blissfully ignorant of the troubles that lay ahead.
examples.zh: 我们对将要出现的麻烦一无所知，这或许是一种幸运。

examples.en: Ignorant lout!
examples.zh: 没礼貌的混蛋！

definitions.en: to draw pictures for a book, magazine, etc.

examples.en: a beautifully illustrated book/old manuscript
examples.zh: 配有精美插图的书／旧手稿

definitions.en: to show the meaning or truth of something more clearly, especially by giving examples

examples.en: The lecturer illustrated his point with a diagram on the blackboard.
examples.zh: 讲师在黑板上用图表来说明自己的观点。

examples.en: This latest conflict further illustrates the weakness of the UN.
examples.zh: 最近的这次冲突进一步说明了联合国的软弱。

examples.en: The exhibition will illustrate how life evolved from water.
examples.zh: 这次展览将展示生命是如何从水中演化而来的。

examples.en: It is sometimes easier to illustrate an abstract concept by analogy with something concrete.
The incident graphically illustrates just how dangerous the situation in the war zone has become.
She illustrated her point by carrying out a simple experiment.
The incident illustrates the need for tighter security.
I need a volunteer to help me illustrate this next concept.

definitions.en: to behave in a similar way to someone or something else, or to copy the speech or behaviour, etc. of someone or something

examples.en: Some of the younger pop bands try to imitate their musical heroes from the past.
examples.zh: 一些较年轻的流行乐队极力模仿心目中昔日的音乐巨星。

examples.en: They produce artificial chemicals which exactly imitate particular natural ones.
examples.zh: 他们生产酷似某些天然物质的人造化学品。

definitions.en: extremely

examples.en: He was immensely popular in his day.
examples.zh: 在他那个时代，他非常受欢迎。

examples.en: She's an immensely talented young athlete.
examples.zh: 她是个才华横溢的年轻运动员。

definitions.en: protected against a particular disease by particular substances in the blood

examples.en: Most people who've had chickenpox once are immune to it for the rest of their lives.
examples.zh: 大多数出过水痘的人终生都对此病具有免疫力。

examples.en: He seems to be immune to colds - he just never gets them.
examples.zh: 他好像对感冒具有免疫力——他从不感冒。

examples.en: The press had criticized her so often that in the end she had become immune (to it).
examples.zh: 新闻界如此频繁地批评她，以至于到最后她都（对此）变得无动于衷了。

examples.en: Journalists, he insisted, must be immune (= protected) from prosecution.
examples.zh: 他坚持新闻记者必须免受起诉。

examples.en: The president claimed he was immune from prosecution.
She was immune to my charms.
The mice seemed to be immune to the effects of the drug.
She's an experienced politician, but she's not immune to criticism.
I'm not immune to suggestions, but they must be reasonable.

definitions.en: easily annoyed by someone's mistakes or because you have to wait

examples.en: He's a good teacher, but inclined to be a bit impatient with slow learners.
examples.zh: 他是个好老师，但对学得慢的学生却总有些不耐烦。

examples.en: You'd be hopeless taking care of children - you're far too impatient!
examples.zh: 照看孩子你是不行的——你太没有耐心了！

examples.en: He's got a lot of exciting ideas and he's impatient to get started.
examples.zh: 他有许多好主意，迫不及待地想动手做。

examples.en: People are increasingly impatient for change in this country.
examples.zh: 该国人民日益期盼变革。

examples.en: Ron's trouble is that he's too impatient.
He's very impatient and always interrupts me mid-sentence.
He was trying to explain but she became impatient and sent him away .
She used to be very impatient, but she's mellowed over time.
She can be a bit impatient with the slow learners.

definitions.en: extremely important or urgent
definitions.zh: 极重要的；紧急的；迫切的

examples.en: The president said it was imperative that the release of all hostages be secured.
examples.zh: 总统说当前要务是确保所有人质都获释。

examples.en: It's imperative to act now before the problem gets really serious.
examples.zh: 必须现在就采取行动，而不是等到问题变得难以收拾。

definitions.en: used to describe the form of a verb that is usually used for giving orders

examples.en: In the phrase "Leave him alone!", the verb "leave" is in the imperative form.
examples.zh: 在句子 Leave him alone! 中，动词 leave 用的是祈使语气。

definitions.en: the form of a verb that is usually used for giving orders

examples.en: In the phrase "Leave him alone!", the verb "leave" is an imperative/is in the imperative.
examples.zh: 在句子 Leave him alone! 中，动词 leave 是祈使语气动词。

examples.en: A sentence that is a command, such as 'Stand over there', contains an imperative.
The next lesson teaches you how to use the imperative tense of Spanish verbs correctly.

definitions.en: something that is extremely important or urgent

examples.en: Getting the unemployed back to work, said the mayor, is a moral imperative.
examples.zh: 市长说，让失业人员重返工作岗位是一件道义上必须做的事。

definitions.en: to intentionally copy another person's characteristics, such as their behaviour, speech, appearance, or expressions, especially to make people laugh

examples.en: She's the woman who impersonates all the celebrities on TV.
examples.zh: 她是在电视中扮演各种名人的那个人。

examples.en: He was fined for impersonating a police officer.
examples.zh: 他因假冒警察而被罚款。

definitions.en: to officially force a rule, tax, punishment, etc. to be obeyed or received

examples.en: Very high taxes have recently been imposed on cigarettes.
examples.zh: 最近对香烟开征很高的税。

examples.en: Judges are imposing increasingly heavy fines for minor driving offences.
examples.zh: 法官们对轻微的违章驾驶开始处以越来越重的罚款。

examples.en: The council has imposed a ban on alcohol in the city parks.
examples.zh: 市政会禁止在城市公园中饮酒。

examples.en: I don't want them to impose their religious beliefs on my children.
examples.zh: 我不希望他们把自己的宗教信仰强加给我的孩子们。

examples.en: We must impose some kind of order on the way this office is run.
examples.zh: 我们得给这个办公室的管理定出规章来。

examples.en: He wants the government to impose strict controls on dog ownership.
In view of the quantity of drugs involved, 16 years was the most lenient sentence the judge could impose.
The government has failed to impose its will upon regional communities.
Central government has imposed a cap on local tax increases.
Some people like the sense of structure that a military lifestyle imposes.

definitions.en: to expect someone to do something for you or spend time with you when they do not want to or when it is not convenient for them

examples.en: Are you sure it's all right for me to come tonight? I don't want to impose.
examples.zh: 你肯定我今天晚上来没有问题吗？我不想太麻烦你。

examples.en: She's always imposing on people - asking favours and getting everyone to do things for her.
examples.zh: 她总是强人所难——要人帮忙做这做那。

definitions.en: a person who pretends to be someone else in order to deceive others

examples.en: He felt like an impostor among all those intelligent people, as if he had no right to be there.
examples.zh: 他感觉自己在所有那些聪明人中间像个冒牌货——好像自己无权在那儿。

definitions.en: Impractical people are not naturally good at doing useful jobs such as making or repairing things.
definitions.zh: （人）无动手能力的，手不灵巧的

examples.en: It's impractical to have so many people all trying to use this equipment at the same time.
examples.zh: 要让这么多人同时使用这个设备是不切实际的。

examples.en: I love high heels but they're rather impractical.
examples.zh: 我喜欢高跟鞋，但它很不实用。

definitions.en: If an object or achievement is impressive, you admire or respect it, usually because it is special, important, or very large.
definitions.zh: （因特殊、重要或巨大而）给人留下深刻印象的

examples.en: That was an impressive performance from such a young tennis player.
examples.zh: 这么年轻的网球选手居然打得那么好。

examples.en: an impressive collection of modern paintings
examples.zh: 令人赞叹的现代绘画收藏品

examples.en: There are some very impressive buildings in the town.
examples.zh: 镇上有一些非常雄伟的建筑物。

examples.en: She's a very impressive public speaker.
examples.zh: 她是个不同凡响的演说家。

examples.en: She has an impressive command of the English language.
The front of the museum is very impressive.
Her loyalty to the cause is impressive.
The Giants winning streak remained unbroken for an impressive 19 games.
The building relies on clever design rather than on ornament for its impressive effect.

definitions.en: not good enough or too low in quality

examples.en: This work is woefully (= extremely) inadequate - you'll have to do it again.
examples.zh: 这活儿做得非常糟糕——你必须返工。

examples.en: She rejected the $2 million offer as totally inadequate.
examples.zh: 她认为200万美元的出价完全不够，因而拒绝了。

examples.en: Maddie's a real expert on art, so I feel completely inadequate whenever I talk to her about it.
examples.zh: 麦迪在艺术方面是个真正的专家，因此每次和她谈论艺术我都觉得自己不够格。

definitions.en: in a way that is not intentional

examples.en: He inadvertently deleted the file.
examples.zh: 他无意中删除了文件。

examples.en: I inadvertently ended up on the wrong street.
examples.zh: 我不经意间走错了街道。

examples.en: The text was edited in a way that inadvertently changed its meaning.
I'm worried that we might be inadvertently watering down the quality of the data.
We inadvertently threw away some of the receipts.

definitions.en: a particular life, in religions that believe that we have many lives

examples.en: He believes that he was a Roman warrior in a previous incarnation.
examples.zh: 他认为自己前世是一名罗马勇士。

examples.en: This film is the latest incarnation of a fairy tale that dates back to the Middle Ages.
examples.zh: 这部影片是对中世纪一则神话故事的最新演绎。

examples.en: He was the incarnation of evil (= was extremely evil).
examples.zh: 他是邪恶的化身。

definitions.en: something that encourages a person to do something

examples.en: Tax incentives have been very effective in encouraging people to save and invest more of their income.
examples.zh: 税收激励措施在鼓励人们将收入多储蓄和多投资方面很有效。

examples.en: There is little incentive for people to leave their cars at home when public transport remains so expensive.
examples.zh: 公共交通的费用居高不下，无法激励人们停开私家车。

examples.en: Bonus payments provide an incentive to work harder.
examples.zh: 支付奖金能刺激人们更努力工作。

examples.en: Indexation of pay rises to productivity will give people an incentive to work harder.
The incentive to sell more is large - and it plainly works.
The management have introduced a productivity incentive scheme for members of staff.
Tax incentives have been offered to attract industry to the region.
The new law provides incentives for businesses to invest in recycling equipment.

definitions.en: the beginning of an organization or official activity

examples.en: Since its inception in 1968, the company has been at the forefront of computer development.
examples.zh: 这家公司自从1968年成立以来一直处于计算机开发的最前沿。

definitions.en: used before saying something that is not as important as the main subject of conversation, but is connected to it in some way

examples.en: We had a marvellous meal at that restaurant you recommended - incidentally, I must give you the number of a similar one I know.
examples.zh: 我们在你推荐的那家餐馆美餐了一顿——顺便，我一定要把我知道的类似的一家餐馆的电话号码给你。

examples.en: Incidentally, I wanted to have a word with you about your travel expenses.
examples.zh: 顺便说一句，我打算和你谈一下你的开支报销问题。

definitions.en: the fact of containing some ideas, statements, arguments, etc. that do not agree with others, or something such as an idea, statement, or argument that has this quality
definitions.zh: （思想、说法或论点的）不一致;不一致的思想、说法或论点

examples.en: There are a few inconsistencies in what you've written.
examples.zh: 您写的内容有一些不一致的地方。

examples.en: There is some inconsistency between the theory and the practice.
examples.zh: 理论与实践之间存在一定的矛盾。

examples.en: Despite these inconsistencies, the film is very enjoyable.
The report contained conflicting evidence and many inconsistencies.
These accusations contain a glaring inconsistency.
The inconsistency of his evidence left serious room for doubt.

definitions.en: the fact that someone or something cannot be trusted to stay the same in behaviour or quality in all situations

examples.en: Logan showed his inconsistency in missing half his kicks.
examples.zh: 洛根的不稳定发挥体现在一半的球他没有击中

examples.en: The team's record shows frustrating inconsistency.
There was too much inconsistency in the performance.
The real problem is inconsistency of medical care across the country.

definitions.en: to include something as part of something larger

examples.en: Suggestions from the survey have been incorporated into/in the final design.
examples.zh: 调查中取得的建议已被纳入了最终的设计之中。

examples.en: This aircraft incorporates several new safety features.
examples.zh: 这种飞机具有几种新的安全特征。

examples.en: He has become more venturesome this season with dress designs that incorporate a variety of ethnic influences.
The film incorporates elements of fantasy and science fiction.
The program incorporates a powerful graphics tool.
The act incorporates the European Convention on Human Rights into English law.
His later work incorporates elements of popular culture.

definitions.en: impossible, or very difficult, to believe

examples.en: an incredible story
examples.zh: 令人难以置信的故事

examples.en: The latest missiles can be fired with incredible accuracy.
examples.zh: 最新型导弹的准确率高得令人难以置信。

examples.en: It seems incredible that no one foresaw the crisis.
examples.zh: 令人难以置信的是没有人预见到危机会发生。

examples.en: The conceit of that man is incredible!
She appears to actually like the man, which I find incredible.
"That's incredible!" he said, shaking his head in disbelief.
Johnson scored an incredible goal in the match.
The story is about theft, fraud and deceit on an incredible scale.

definitions.en: extremely good

examples.en: Yeah, it was an incredible performance.
examples.zh: 对，这是一场非常出色的表演。

examples.en: What an incredible motorbike!
examples.zh: 多棒的摩托车啊！

definitions.en: used for saying that something is very difficult to believe

examples.en: Incredibly, no one was hurt in the accident.
examples.zh: 事故中居然没有人受伤，这真是令人难以置信。

examples.en: We missed our flight but, incredibly, got there on time.
His car turned over and caught on fire and yet, incredibly, he escaped unscathed.

definitions.en: extremely

examples.en: He was incredibly rich/angry/quick.
examples.zh: 他极其富有／生气／迅速。

examples.en: An incredibly loud bang followed the flash.
examples.zh: 闪电之后跟着一声震耳欲聋的巨响。

examples.en: We thought that Egypt was an incredibly romantic country.
This coffee is incredibly smooth and rich.
My aunt is incredibly vague - she can never remember where she's left things.
He's overweight and bald, and yet somehow, he's incredibly attractive.
Their shows are always incredibly camp and flamboyant.

definitions.en: to experience something, usually something unpleasant, as a result of actions you have taken

examples.en: to incur debts/fines/bills
examples.zh: 招致债务／遭到罚款／要付账

examples.en: The play has incurred the wrath/anger of both audiences and critics.
examples.zh: 这部剧引起了观众和评论家的愤怒。

examples.en: Please detail any costs/expenses incurred by you in attending the interview.
examples.zh: 请详细列出你参加这次面试所产生的一切费用。

definitions.en: really or certainly, often used to emphasize something

examples.en: Indeed, it could be the worst environmental disaster in Europe this century.
examples.zh: 这确实可能是本世纪西欧最严重的环境灾难。

examples.en: Evidence suggests that errors may indeed be occurring.
examples.zh: 证据表明可能确实出错了。

examples.en: We live in strange times indeed.
examples.zh: 我们确实生活在不可思议的时代。

examples.en: Many people are very poor indeed.
examples.zh: 许多人确实很穷。

examples.en: "Is this your dog?" "It is indeed."/"Indeed it is."
examples.zh: “这是你的狗吗？”“当然是。”

examples.en: Yes, I did indeed say that.
examples.zh: 是的，我确实那样说过。

examples.en: For such creatures, speed is not important - indeed it is counterproductive.
examples.zh: 对这些生物而言，速度并不重要，速度太高甚至会适得其反。

examples.en: I am happy, indeed proud, to be associated with this project.
examples.zh: 能参与这个项目我很高兴，甚至感到骄傲。

examples.en: "Have I met him?" "You have indeed - and I can remember what you said about him."
The tests showed that your son does indeed have a hearing problem.
"Can you really play the trumpet?" "I can indeed."
If you are indeed a poet, let's hear one of your poems.
"That's very strange, isn't it?" "Yes, indeed - I can't explain it."

definitions.en: used to express surprise, anger, or lack of belief or interest
definitions.zh: （表示惊讶、恼怒、不相信、不感兴趣）

examples.en: "She said she won't come back until Monday." "Won't she, indeed?"
examples.zh: “她说她星期一以前不会回来。”“哦，是吗？”

examples.en: "When will we get a pay rise?" "When indeed?"
examples.zh: “我们什么时候能够加薪？”“是呀，什么时候加呢？”

definitions.en: for a period of time with no fixed end
definitions.zh: 不确定地;不清楚地;无限期地

examples.en: The negotiations have been put off/postponed indefinitely.
examples.zh: 谈判被无限期地推迟了。

definitions.en: impossible to judge as being different when compared to another similar thing
definitions.zh: 难以区别的，难以分辨的

examples.en: These forgeries are so good that they are more or less indistinguishable from the originals.
examples.zh: 这些赝品很逼真，几乎没法将它们和真品区分开来。

definitions.en: not able to be separated from something else or into different parts

examples.en: He regards e-commerce as an indivisible part of modern retail.
examples.zh: 他认为电子商务是现代零售业不可分割的一部分。

examples.en: A country's language is indivisible from its culture.
examples.zh: 一个国家的语言与其文化是不可分的。

definitions.en: certain to happen and unable to be avoided or prevented
definitions.zh: 不可避免的;必然发生的

examples.en: The accident was the inevitable consequence/result/outcome of carelessness.
examples.zh: 这一事故是粗心导致的必然结果。

examples.en: Eventually the inevitable happened and he had a heart attack.
examples.zh: 最终不可避免的情况还是发生了，他心脏病发作。

examples.en: Unfortunately, violence is the inevitable corollary of such a revolutionary change in society.
The eclipse of the ruling political party was inevitable.
The factory closure is seemingly inevitable.
The endless public appearances are an inevitable part of an election campaign.
When you're working such long hours, it's inevitable that your marriage will start to suffer.

definitions.en: in a way that cannot be avoided

examples.en: Their arguments inevitably end in tears.
examples.zh: 他们的争论不可避免地以眼泪告终。

examples.en: Such a large investment inevitably entails some risk.
The investigation into the air crash would inevitably apportion blame to certain members of the crew.
Political leaders almost inevitably pander to big business.
A war would inevitably set back the process of reform.
As the political emphasis shifts, Bulgaria will inevitably become more westernized.

definitions.en: the time when someone is a baby or a very young child

examples.en: Her youngest child died in infancy.
examples.zh: 她最小的孩子在幼年时夭折了。

examples.en: The system is still in its infancy.
examples.zh: 这个体系尚处于初期发展阶段。

definitions.en: → unfeasible
to secretly become part of a group in order to get information or to influence the way that group thinks or behaves

examples.en: A journalist managed to infiltrate the powerful drug cartel.
examples.zh: 一位记者设法打入了势力强大的贩毒集团内部。

examples.en: At about this time the new ideas about "corporate management" had begun to infiltrate (into) local government.
examples.zh: 大约此时，有关“共同管理”的一些新思想逐步开始为当地政府所接受。

definitions.en: to eat or drink something

examples.en: The chemicals can be poisonous if ingested.
examples.zh: 这些化学制品如果摄入体内可能会引起中毒。

definitions.en: a food that is used with other foods in the preparation of a particular dish

examples.en: The list of ingredients included 250 g of almonds.
examples.zh: 清单上列出的原料包括250克的杏仁。

examples.en: Trust is a vital ingredient in a successful marriage.
examples.zh: 信任是婚姻幸福的重要因素。

examples.en: When cooking the sauce, don't forget that all-important ingredient, fresh basil.
You mix all the dry ingredients together before you add the milk.
He created a wonderful meal from very few ingredients.
This tiny capsule contains two types of effective painkilling ingredients.
The exact ingredients of Coca Cola are a trade secret.

definitions.en: existing as a natural or basic part of something
definitions.zh: 内在的；固有的，生来就有的

examples.en: There are dangers/risks inherent in almost every sport.
examples.zh: 几乎每种运动本身都带有危险／风险。

examples.en: I have an inherent distrust of lawyers.
examples.zh: 我生来对律师就有种不信任感。

definitions.en: to prevent someone from doing something by making them feel nervous or embarrassed

examples.en: Some workers were inhibited (from speaking) by the presence of their managers.
examples.zh: 一些工人在他们的经理面前不敢讲话。

examples.en: This drug inhibits the growth of tumours.
examples.zh: 这种药可以抑制肿瘤的生长。

definitions.en: a feeling of embarrassment or worry that prevents you from saying or doing what you want

examples.en: After a couple of drinks he lost his inhibition and started talking and laughing loudly.
examples.zh: 几杯酒下肚后，他肆无忌惮地大声说笑起来。

examples.en: She was determined to shed her inhibitions and have a good time.
examples.zh: 她决心打消顾虑玩个痛快。

definitions.en: the act of stopping or slowing down a process, or the fact of this happening

examples.en: The therapy provides inhibition of inflammation.
examples.zh: 这种治疗抑制了发炎。

definitions.en: a new plan or process to achieve something or solve a problem

examples.en: The peace initiative was welcomed by both sides.
examples.zh: 和平倡议受到了双方的欢迎。

examples.en: The German leadership lauded the Russian initiative.
The UN called on all parties in the conflict to take a positive stance towards the new peace initiative.
Joe Walker will be spearheading our new marketing initiative.
The initiative has across-the-board support.
He began his lecture by talking down the initiatives of a rival company.

definitions.en: the ability to use your judgment to make decisions and do things without needing to be told what to do
definitions.zh: 主动性;自主决断行事的能力

examples.en: Although she was quite young, she showed a lot of initiative and was promoted to manager after a year.
examples.zh: 她虽然很年轻，但工作积极主动，一年后就被提拔为经理。

examples.en: I shouldn't always have to tell you what to do, use your initiative (= use your own judgment to decide what to do)!
examples.zh: 不能总让我告诉你该做什么，你应该有自己的判断！

examples.en: Some states acted under threat of litigation, and others on their own initiative.

definitions.en: the power or opportunity to win an advantage

examples.en: to seize/take/lose the initiative
examples.zh: 掌握／采取／失去主动权

examples.en: The ideal candidate for this position will take the initiative to proactively find solutions to problems.
Legislators have seized the initiative to codify the right into law.
Rebels seized the initiative and pressed deeper into occupied territory.
I'm waiting for our team to take the initiative and start scoring, instead of constantly playing defence.

definitions.en: extremely and unreasonably

examples.en: She gets insanely jealous if he so much as looks at another woman.
examples.zh: 哪怕他只是看一眼别的女人，她都会妒嫉得发疯。

definitions.en: showing a clear and usually original understanding of a complicated problem or situation
definitions.zh: 有洞察力的;见解深刻的

examples.en: She has written an insightful account of the modern art world.
examples.zh: 她对现代艺术世界做了深刻的描述。

examples.en: He was, of course, very funny and insightful.
examples.zh: 当然，他非常有趣，也很有见地。

examples.en: This is a very thoughtful, insightful analysis.
She said something I thought was very insightful.
This may be the most insightful comment the president has ever made.

definitions.en: small or not noticeable, and therefore not considered important

examples.en: Why bother arguing about such an insignificant amount of money?
examples.zh: 为什么为那点小钱争吵？

examples.en: The difference between the two results was insignificant.
examples.zh: 两个结果之间的差别无足轻重。

definitions.en: to say firmly or demand forcefully, especially when others disagree with or oppose what you say

examples.en: Greg still insists (that) he did nothing wrong.
examples.zh: 格雷格仍坚称自己没有做错任何事。

examples.en: Please go first - I insist!
examples.zh: 请先走——我坚持这样！

examples.en: She insisted on seeing her lawyer.
examples.zh: 她坚持要见她的律师。

examples.en: She's 85 now, but she will insist on doing all her own housework.
The men at City Hall insist that big spending cuts are just not practical.
The police insist that they are not relaxing their campaign against drugs.
They insisted that all swear words be deleted from the article.
She insisted on telling me every single detail of what they did to her in hospital.

definitions.en: immediately

examples.en: Both drivers were killed instantly.
examples.zh: 两名司机当即丧生。

examples.en: It's some comfort to his wife to know that he died instantly and didn't suffer.
We can now communicate instantly with people on the other side of the world.
She was knocked down by a car and killed instantly.
She jumps on her children instantly if they're disobedient.
The Eiffel Tower in Paris is an instantly recognizable landmark.

definitions.en: necessary and important as a part of a whole

examples.en: He's an integral part of the team and we can't do without him.
examples.zh: 他是球队不可缺少的一员，没有他我们可不行。

examples.en: Bars and terrace cafés are integral to the social life of the city.
examples.zh: 酒吧和露天咖啡座是这个城市社交生活不可缺少的一部分。

examples.en: All rooms have a flat-screen TV with integral DVD-player.
examples.zh: 所有房间都装有内置DVD播放机的平板电视。

examples.en: The integral garage had been converted to make another bedroom.
examples.zh: 内置车库被改造成了另一个卧室。

definitions.en: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles that you refuse to change

examples.en: No one doubted that the president was a man of the highest integrity.
examples.zh: 没有人怀疑总裁是个光明磊落的人。

examples.en: Keen to preserve his artistic integrity, he refused several lucrative Hollywood offers.
examples.zh: 他十分重视保持自己的艺德，几次拒绝了好莱坞报酬丰厚的演出邀请。

examples.en: Lord Fletcher, said the Bishop, was a man of unimpeachable integrity and character.
His comments cast a slur on the integrity of his employees.
Her probity and integrity are beyond question.
I had credited them with more integrity than they showed.
He was regarded as a man of absolute integrity.

definitions.en: the quality of being whole and complete

examples.en: A modern extension on the old building would ruin its architectural integrity.
examples.zh: 在古老建筑物上附加扩建的现代建筑会破坏建筑风格的完整统一。

definitions.en: involving a lot of effort or activity in a short period of time
definitions.zh: 密集的，集中的;加强的;深入的

examples.en: two weeks of intensive training
examples.zh: 两周的强化培训

examples.en: an intensive course in English
examples.zh: 英语强化课程

examples.en: Intensive bombing had reduced the city to rubble.
examples.zh: 密集轰炸把城市炸成了一片瓦砾。

examples.en: The course compresses two year's training into six intensive months.
The soil has been so heavily leached through intensive farming that it is no longer fertile.
The allies let loose an intensive artillery bombardment over the border.
As agriculture became more capital intensive, many farm labourers moved to the towns and cities to look for work.
In some areas, modern intensive farming is giving way to the re-introduction of traditional methods.

definitions.en: in a planned or intended way

examples.en: I didn't ignore her intentionally - I just didn't recognize her.
examples.zh: 我没有故意无视她——我只是没认出她。

examples.en: She was being intentionally unpleasant.
examples.zh: 她是故意耍态度的。

examples.en: I've never hurt anybody intentionally in my life.
It is a crime to intentionally hold back evidence.
We don't know whether this was done intentionally or accidentally.

definitions.en: An interactive system or computer program is designed to involve the user in the exchange of information.

examples.en: an interactive game/video
examples.zh: 交互式游戏／视频

examples.en: This is an interactive museum where children can actively manipulate the exhibits.
examples.zh: 这是一个互动式博物馆，孩子们可以亲手摆弄展品。

examples.en: interactive teaching methods
examples.zh: 互动式教学法

examples.en: Many educational and instructional programs are interactive.
The museum has many interactive exhibits.
Teachers often supplement class discussions with interactive software.
The museum has an interactive computer network that explains basic scientific themes.
Interactive displays have attracted many new visitors to the museum.

definitions.en: to stop and catch something or someone before that thing or person is able to reach a particular place

examples.en: Law enforcement agents intercepted a shipment of drugs from Central America.
examples.zh: 执法人员截获了一批从中美洲运来的毒品。

examples.en: Barry intercepted Naylor's pass and scored the third goal.
examples.zh: 巴里断下内勒尔的传球，射入了第3个球。

definitions.en: someone who carries messages between people who are unwilling or unable to meet

examples.en: The police negotiated with the gunman through an intermediary.
examples.zh: 警方通过调解人与持枪歹徒谈判。

examples.en: The former president has agreed to act as an intermediary between the government and the rebels.
examples.zh: 前总统已经同意在政府和叛军之间充当调解人。

definitions.en: being between two other related things, levels, or points

examples.en: There are three levels of difficulty in this game: low, intermediate, and high.
examples.zh: 这个游戏有3个难度级别：低级、中级和高级。

examples.en: This novel is too difficult for intermediate students of English.
examples.zh: 这部小说对于中等程度的英语学习者来说太难了。

examples.en: We can teach dancers up to intermediate level here.
The website is intended for intermediate and advanced French-language students and teachers.
The Grade VI music syllabus is classed as 'intermediate', and Grade VII is called 'advanced'.
He won the 110 metres hurdles event, taking his intermediate score in the competition to 5562 points.
As a fairly experienced walker, I think you might be able to cope with one of our intermediate-grade walking holidays.

definitions.en: not happening regularly or continuously; stopping and starting repeatedly or with periods in between

examples.en: intermittent rain
examples.zh: 阵雨

examples.en: an intermittent noise
examples.zh: 时断时续的噪音

examples.en: Although she made intermittent movie appearances, she was essentially a stage actress.
examples.zh: 她虽然断断续续地在电影中露面，但基本上还是一个舞台演员。

definitions.en: in a way that does not happen regularly or continuously; in a way that stops and starts repeatedly or with periods in between

examples.en: We've discussed this problem intermittently, but so far we've failed to come up with a solution.
examples.zh: 我们断断续续地讨论过这个问题，但目前还没有找到解决方案。

examples.en: Lightning flashed intermittently in an otherwise clear sky.
examples.zh: 闪电在原本晴朗的天空中断断续续地闪现。

examples.en: Wind energy supplies power only intermittently - when the wind blows.
The group has met intermittently for several years in an attempt to devise a long-term plan.
In the war-torn city, electricity is available only intermittently.

definitions.en: to put someone in prison for political or military reasons, especially during a war
definitions.zh: （尤指战时因政治或军事原因）拘禁，扣押，关押

examples.en: Many foreigners were interned for the duration of the war.
examples.zh: 战争期间许多外国人被拘禁。

definitions.en: a male or female doctor who is still training, and who works in a hospital
someone who is finishing training for a skilled job especially by getting practical experience of the work involved

examples.en: She worked in the White House as an intern.
examples.zh: 她在白宫做实习生。

definitions.en: to add something in the middle of a text, piece of music, etc.

examples.en: In his adaptation of the script he interpolated the words "tempus fugit" and the translation "time flies".
Coleridge-Taylor was keen on interpolating African-American spirituals into the classical music tradition.
"How very odd to say all that!" I interpolated.
The film-maker interpolates stills of Marlon Brando during the recital of the poem.
The church has always made practical judgments with respect to its attempt to interpolate Catholic principles into the civil law.
These median values were interpolated by using linear regression.
Yearly mean rainfall was obtained from 20-year data, and interpolated values for each square were used.

definitions.en: to add something in the middle of a text, piece of music, etc.

examples.en: In his adaptation of the script he interpolated the words "tempus fugit" and the translation "time flies".
Coleridge-Taylor was keen on interpolating African-American spirituals into the classical music tradition.
"How very odd to say all that!" I interpolated.
The film-maker interpolates stills of Marlon Brando during the recital of the poem.
The church has always made practical judgments with respect to its attempt to interpolate Catholic principles into the civil law.
These median values were interpolated by using linear regression.
Yearly mean rainfall was obtained from 20-year data, and interpolated values for each square were used.

definitions.en: the addition of something different in the middle of a text, piece of music, etc. or the thing that is added
definitions.zh: 插入;插入物;插入的文字（或音乐等）

examples.en: An actor reads the poems in English translation, with brief musical interpolations.
examples.zh: 一位演员朗读了诗歌的英文翻译，并配有简短的音乐插曲。

examples.en: The play is based on factual accounts with creative interpolation.
examples.zh: 这部剧在叙述事实的基础上插入了虚构成分。

examples.en: Sometimes the data were years apart and interpolations were made for all months in between.
examples.zh: 有时数据相隔数年，并且在其间的所有月份都进行了插值。

examples.en: For other years estimates were made by interpolation.
examples.zh: 其他年份的估算是通过插值进行的。

examples.en: They were not amused by his comical interpolations.
examples.zh: 他的插科打诨令别人不快。

examples.en: A suggested interpolation in one clause of the agreement was accepted.
This great moment in the opera is in fact an interpolation that was not in the original printed edition.
This sentence is regarded as later interpolation.
The atmospheric density is determined at each timestep by exponential interpolation from standard atmosphere tables.

definitions.en: to ask someone a lot of questions for a long time in order to get information, sometimes using threats or violence

examples.en: Thousands of dissidents have been interrogated or imprisoned in recent weeks.
examples.zh: 数千名持不同政见者在最近几周遭到审问或拘禁。

examples.en: The man was held and interrogated for five hours.
He believes the men who interrogated him were government agents.
The bill would require the CIA to abide by the certain rules when interrogating prisoners.
I was walking down the street doing nothing when the cops stopped and interrogated me.
She is endlessly curious about the world and other people, interrogating them about the details of their lives.

definitions.en: to get information from a computer

examples.en: The computer language was originally developed to make it faster to interrogate a database.
Twenty years ago people built fairly rudimentary systems to interrogate a database.
There are now more than 1,400 fund managers in our UK database, which is interrogated to find the award winners.
Online banking means the ability to interrogate your bank account by computer.

definitions.en: to stop a person from speaking for a short period by something you say or do

examples.en: She tried to explain what had happened but he kept interrupting her.
examples.zh: 她试图解释所发生的事情，但他不停地打断她。

examples.en: I wish you'd stop interrupting.
examples.zh: 但愿你别打断我的话。

examples.en: Please feel free to interrupt me if you don't understand anything.
Please go on with what you're doing and don't let us interrupt you.
The senator thoroughly squelched the journalist who tried to interrupt him during his speech.
He's very impatient and always interrupts me mid-sentence.
She rudely interrupted my speech.

definitions.en: to stop something from happening for a short period

examples.en: We had to interrupt our trip when we heard John's mother had had an accident.
examples.zh: 当我们听到约翰母亲发生意外后，不得不中断了我们的行程。

definitions.en: an occasion when two lines cross, or the place where this happens

examples.en: The intersection of the lines on the graph marks the point where we start to make a profit.
examples.zh: 图上几条线的交点就是我们的开始盈利点。

definitions.en: the place where two or more roads join or cross each other

examples.en: a busy intersection
examples.zh: 繁忙的十字路口

examples.en: Turn right at the next intersection.
examples.zh: 在下个十字路口向右拐。

definitions.en: to mix one thing in with another in a way that is not regular
definitions.zh: 无规律地混入;散布;点缀

examples.en: The documentary intersperses graphical animations with film clips of the actual event.
examples.zh: 该纪录片在生动的动画中穿插了一些真实事件的电影片段。

examples.en: Her handwritten notes were interspersed throughout the text.
examples.zh: 她的笔记散见于整篇课文中。

definitions.en: to intentionally become involved in a difficult situation in order to improve it or prevent it from getting worse

examples.en: The Central Bank intervened in the currency markets today to try to stabilize the exchange rate.
examples.zh: 中央银行今天对货币市场进行了干预，试图稳定汇率。

examples.en: The minister intervened personally to stop the museum from being closed.
examples.zh: 部长亲自干预以阻止博物馆的关闭。

examples.en: There is increasing demand for the United Nations to intervene in trouble spots throughout the world.
I'd like to help but I don't have the power to intervene in this dispute.
The army's potentiality to intervene in politics remains strong.
Their only hope now is that the outside world will intervene but it is an increasingly forlorn hope.
The Bank of England intervened this morning to defend the pound .

definitions.en: to happen between two times or between other events or activities

examples.en: Two decades intervened between the completion of the design and the opening of the theatre.
examples.zh: 从设计完成到戏院投入使用相隔20年。

definitions.en: the action of becoming intentionally involved in a difficult situation, in order to improve it or prevent it from getting worse

examples.en: Half the people questioned said they were opposed to military intervention in the civil war.
examples.zh: 半数被问到的人说他们反对对内战的军事干涉。

examples.en: Repeated interventions on the currency markets failed to prevent the currency's value falling.
examples.zh: 反复地干预货币市场并没有防止货币的贬值。

examples.en: A medical professional can advise family members of the most appropriate times to stage an alcohol intervention.
examples.zh: 医疗专业人士可以给家庭成员提供指导，建议组织干预活动的最恰当时机。

examples.en: He's been playing that video game all day. I think it's time for an intervention.
examples.zh: 他一整天都在玩电子游戏。我觉得是时候和他谈一下了。

examples.en: There is still some hope that the economic blockade will work and make military intervention unnecessary.
The government was reluctant to sanction intervention in the crisis.
The border dispute was used as a pretext for military intervention.
England have fallen so far behind in the championship that their only hope of victory is divine intervention.
Imposing sanctions is a moderate action when you consider that the alternative is military intervention.
His manager staged an intervention, forcing him into one of two rehab stays.

definitions.en: having, or being likely to cause, a very close friendship or personal or sexual relationship
definitions.zh: 亲密的;有性关系的;宜于密切关系的

examples.en: intimate relationships
examples.zh: 亲密关系

examples.en: The restaurant has a very intimate atmosphere.
examples.zh: 这家餐厅的氛围很怡人。

examples.en: He's become very intimate with an actress.
examples.zh: 他和一名女演员的关系非常亲密。

examples.en: The disease is spread through intimate sexual activity.
They shared an intimate physical experience.
The diary revealed several intimate details about his private life.
They had dinner in the very intimate surroundings of a local restaurant.
Her poetry deals with intimate personal experience.

definitions.en: (of knowledge or understanding) detailed, and obtained from a lot of studying or experience

examples.en: She has an intimate knowledge of Tuscany, where she has lived for 20 years.
examples.zh: 她在托斯卡纳区住了20年，对那里非常熟悉。

definitions.en: a friend you know very well

examples.en: Intimates of the star say that he has been upset by the personal attacks on him that have appeared in the press recently.
examples.zh: 这位明星的密友们说他最近正为报纸上出现的对他的人身攻击感到苦恼。

definitions.en: to make clear what you think or want without saying it directly

examples.en: She has intimated that she will resign if she loses the vote.
examples.zh: 她已经暗示如果选举落败她将辞职。

definitions.en: complicated details:
definitions.zh: 我喜欢这部电影，但情节中有些复杂的细节我弄不明白。

examples.en: I enjoyed the film, but I couldn't follow all the intricacies of the plot.
examples.zh: 我喜欢这部电影，但情节中有些复杂的细节我弄不明白。

examples.en: the intricacy of the needlework
examples.zh: 针线活的复杂精细

definitions.en: having a lot of small parts that are arranged in a complicated or delicate way
definitions.zh: 错综复杂的；复杂精细的；难理解的；难解决的

examples.en: The watch mechanism is extremely intricate and very difficult to repair.
examples.zh: 手表的机械结构非常复杂精细，很难修理。

examples.en: The tiled floor is installed in an intricate pattern.
It was a beautiful ornament, antique brass with an intricate design.
She taught her grand-daughter the intricate art of weaving grass baskets.
Following the intricate plotline can be a challenge.
He describes an intricate system for charting progress.

definitions.en: to interest someone a lot, especially by being strange, unusual, or mysterious
definitions.zh: （尤指因奇怪、不寻常或神秘而）使很感兴趣，迷住

examples.en: Throughout history, people have been intrigued by the question of whether there is intelligent life elsewhere in the universe.
examples.zh: 纵观历史，人们一直被这样一个问题吸引着：宇宙的其他地方是否也存在着有智力的生物？

definitions.en: (the making of) a secret plan to do something, especially something that will harm another person
definitions.zh: （尤指伤害他人的）密谋;阴谋，诡计

examples.en: a tale of political intrigue
examples.zh: 政治阴谋的传闻

definitions.en: being an extremely important and basic characteristic of a person or thing

examples.en: works of little intrinsic value/interest
examples.zh: 本身无甚价值／趣味的作品

examples.en: Maths is an intrinsic part of the school curriculum.
examples.zh: 数学是中小学校的基础课。

definitions.en: examination of and attention to your own ideas, thoughts, and feelings

examples.en: His defeat in the world championship led to a long period of gloomy introspection.
examples.zh: 世界锦标赛上失败后，他长期处于郁郁寡欢和自我反省之中。

definitions.en: someone who is in a place or situation where they are not wanted

examples.en: I feel like an intruder when I visit their home.
examples.zh: 我拜访他们家时总觉得自己像一名不速之客。

examples.en: Intruders had entered the house through a back window.
examples.zh: 盗贼从后窗闯入了住宅。

definitions.en: based on feelings rather than facts or proof

examples.en: an intuitive approach/judgment
examples.zh: 凭直觉的方法／判断

examples.en: Most people have an intuitive sense of right and wrong.
examples.zh: 大多数人具有基于直觉的是非感。

examples.en: Good agents are certainly extroverted, but are grounded, rather than intuitive.
He possesses an intuitive grasp of politics as theatre.
He was a lovely child: clever, and wonderfully intuitive.
This aspect of the software is intuitive and easy to learn.

definitions.en: in a way that is based on feelings rather than facts or proof

examples.en: I knew intuitively that something dreadful had happened to him.
examples.zh: 我直觉地知道他出了可怕的事情。

examples.en: Intuitively, it makes sense to start the project now.
examples.zh: 直观地说，现在开始这个项目是合适的。

examples.en: Intuitively, she knew that he was lying.
He intuitively understands the needs of his customers.
The best way to move intuitively is to lose yourself in the music.
This is one of the most intuitively designed wireless home audio systems we've seen.

definitions.en: to officially stop a document, ticket, law, etc. being legally or officially acceptable
a detailed list of all the things in a place

examples.en: A set of 24 gilded chairs appear on the inventory of the house for 1736.
examples.zh: 这幢房子1736年的物品清单上列有一套雕花镀金椅子，共24把。

examples.en: Our inventory of used cars is the best in town.
examples.zh: 我们卖的二手车是城里最好的。

definitions.en: to turn something upside down or change the order of two things

examples.en: In some languages, the word order in questions is inverted (= the verb comes before the subject of the sentence).
examples.zh: 在有些语言里，疑问句的词序是颠倒的。

definitions.en: to examine a crime, problem, statement, etc. carefully, especially to discover the truth

examples.en: Police are investigating allegations of corruption involving senior executives.
examples.zh: 警方正在调查对高级管理人员涉嫌贪污的指控。

examples.en: We are of course investigating how an error like this could have occurred.
examples.zh: 我们当然正在调查发生此类错误的原因。

examples.en: Investigating further, we discovered that Carol was the person who took the call.
Scotland Yard have been called in to investigate the murder.
The remit of this official inquiry is to investigate the reasons for the accident.
The government set up a commission to investigate the problem of inner city violence.
The UN is investigating new methods of population control .
I wonder what that noise was in the kitchen - I'd better go and investigate.

definitions.en: in a way that is impossible to see, or that is not noticed

examples.en: This crisis has been building invisibly for years.
examples.zh: 多年来，这场危机一直在无形中加剧。

examples.en: They swam almost invisibly in the shadows.
examples.zh: 他们游在阴影中，几乎完全看不见。

examples.en: They had servants who almost invisibly cleaned up after them.
Some cracks begin invisibly inside the wheels.
She lived quietly and almost invisibly in the United States.

definitions.en: the mention or use of something such as a law or an idea in order to explain or support what you are doing

examples.en: They disagreed with the administration's invocation of the laws of armed conflict in their treatment of these prisoners.
examples.zh: 他们不同意政府在对待这些囚犯时援引武装冲突法。

examples.en: Watch out for the increasing invocation of mantras like "loyalty", "patriotism", and "moral values".
examples.zh: 当心越来越多的口号被人使用，如“忠诚”，“爱国主义”和“道德价值观”。

examples.en: Ave Maria is an invocation to the Virgin Mary.
examples.zh: 万福玛利亚是对圣母玛利亚的祈祷。

examples.en: As an outspoken campus atheist, she didn't want to sit through a Christian invocation when she graduated.
examples.zh: 作为一个坦率的校园无神论者，她不想在毕业的时候坐着听完基督教的祈祷。

examples.en: The invocation of an outside threat to the nation's security is an old trick.
Lady Macbeth's famous invocation to the spirits of cruelty
"We have hardly any tears left to shed," the archbishop said in his invocation.

definitions.en: a list of things provided or work done together with their cost, for payment at a later time

examples.en: Invoices must be submitted by the 24th of every month.
examples.zh: 费用清单必须在每月的24日之前交上来。

definitions.en: to supply an invoice

examples.en: We'll invoice you for parts and labour.
examples.zh: 我们将给你一份零件和人工的费用清单。

definitions.en: in a way that is interesting, strange, or funny because of being very different from what you would expect
definitions.zh: 具有讽刺意味地;出乎意料地;令人啼笑皆非地

examples.en: Ironically, these technological tools that were supposed to liberate us have left us working longer hours than ever.
examples.zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，这些本应解放我们的技术工具却让我们的工作时间比以往更长。

examples.en: The tone of the novel is ironically comic.
examples.zh: 小说的基调是讽刺性的喜剧。

examples.en: She smiled ironically.
examples.zh: 她反讽地笑了笑。

examples.en: Ironically, many of the countries voicing concerns about the end of quotas argued for decades that they should be abolished.
The bid succeeded, ironically, because of his rival's attempts to stop it.
A lot of what I've written was meant ironically.
Fake Christmas trees were seen as ironically tacky at best.

definitions.en: in a way that is not possible to change or impossible to return to a previous condition
definitions.zh: 不可改变地，不可逆转地

examples.en: The monument has already been irreversibly damaged.
examples.zh: 纪念碑已经遭受了不可逆转的破坏。

examples.en: Our lifestyle choices will irreversibly affect life in the sea.
examples.zh: 我们生活方式的选择将不可逆转地影响海洋中的生命。

examples.en: Medication overdoses killed one patient and irreversibly damaged the heart of another.
The minister's reputation suffered, possibly irreversibly.
This invention will irreversibly alter the world of communications.

definitions.en: printed or written in italics

examples.en: italic type/print/script
examples.zh: 斜体铅字／印刷／字母

definitions.en: a place where criminals are kept to punish them for their crimes, or where people accused of crimes are kept while waiting for their trials

examples.en: the country's overcrowded jails
examples.zh: 该国过于拥挤的监狱

examples.en: a 13-year jail sentence/term
examples.zh: 被判13年监禁

examples.en: The financier was released from jail last week.
examples.zh: 那个金融家上周被释放了。

examples.en: They spent ten years in jail for fraud.
examples.zh: 他们因欺诈罪入狱10年。

examples.en: They don't throw anyone in jail for parking illegally, but they will tow your car and charge you a fine.
examples.zh: 他们不会因为违章停车而判人入狱，可是会拖走你的车还会罚款。

examples.en: Arrangements were made to move the prisoners to another jail.
He faces three years in jail for selling narcotics.
He's stayed out of trouble since he was released from jail last year.
There has been a mass breakout from one of Germany's top security jails.
He has been languishing in jail for the past 20 years.

definitions.en: to put someone in a jail

examples.en: He was jailed for three years.
examples.zh: 他被监禁了3年。

examples.en: He was jailed for four months for drink-driving.
Protestors were executed, jailed or otherwise persecuted.
He was jailed for 15 years for stabbing his wife to death.
California's "three strikes and you're out" bill means that from now on criminals found guilty of three crimes are jailed for life.
He was jailed for revealing secrets to the Russians.

definitions.en: a sweet, soft food made by cooking fruit with sugar to preserve it. It is eaten on bread.

examples.en: strawberry/raspberry jam
examples.zh: 草莓／覆盆子酱

examples.en: jam sandwiches
examples.zh: 果酱三明治

examples.en: She won first prize for her raspberry jam at the Mitchell county fair.
Gooseberries are used for making pies and jam.
We were served scones with butter and jam.
The recipe uses a whole jarful of jam.
She has toast with raspberry jam for breakfast.

definitions.en: → traffic jam

examples.en: We were stuck in a jam for two hours.
examples.zh: 我们堵车堵了两个小时。

definitions.en: something that is stuck in a machine, or that prevents the parts of a machine from moving
definitions.zh: 堵在机器中的东西;让机器部件无法运动的东西

examples.en: She fed the documents into the machine making sure that there were no paper jams.
examples.zh: 她把文件送进机器，确保没有纸张把机器卡住。

definitions.en: a difficult situation

examples.en: I'm in a jam - could you lend me some money till next week?
examples.zh: 我手头有点儿紧——你能借我点钱到下周再还吗？

examples.en: How are we going to get ourselves out of this jam?
examples.zh: 我们如何才能摆脱这一困境？

definitions.en: a situation in which a lot of people are in a small space

examples.en: It's a real jam inside - it took me ten minutes to get to the bar.
examples.zh: 里面太拥挤了——我用了10分钟才挤到吧台。

definitions.en: to be, or make something, unable to move

examples.en: The door jammed behind me and I couldn't get out.
examples.zh: 我进来后门随即卡住了，我出不去了。

examples.en: He jammed the window open with a piece of wood.
examples.zh: 他将窗户打开，用一块木头卡住。

examples.en: Foreign radio broadcasts were regularly jammed.
examples.zh: 外国的无线电广播经常受到干扰。

definitions.en: to play jazz or rock music with other people informally without planning it or practising together
definitions.zh: （与他人）即兴演奏爵士乐（或摇滚乐）

definitions.en: to push something forcefully or with difficulty into something else

examples.en: He jammed the boxes into the back of the car.
examples.zh: 他把箱子塞进汽车后面。

definitions.en: to fill a place completely

examples.en: The centre of town was jammed with cars moving at a very slow pace.
examples.zh: 镇中心堵满了车，移动缓慢。

examples.en: The motorway was jammed solid (= the traffic could not move) all morning.
examples.zh: 整个上午高速公路都被堵得严严实实。

definitions.en: special words and phrases that are used by particular groups of people, especially in their work

examples.en: military/legal/computer jargon
examples.zh: 军事／法律／计算机术语

definitions.en: to run at a slow, regular speed, especially as a form of exercise
definitions.zh: 慢跑（尤指作为健身锻炼的方式）

examples.en: "What do you do to keep fit?" "I jog and go swimming."
examples.zh: “你是怎样保持健康的？”“慢跑和游泳。”

examples.en: He was walking at a very quick pace and I had to jog to keep up with him.
examples.zh: 他走得很快，我得小跑才能跟上他。

definitions.en: to push or knock someone or something slightly, especially with your arm

examples.en: A man rushed past and jogged her elbow, making her drop the bag.
examples.zh: 一名男子匆匆走过，撞了她的胳膊肘，使她的包掉到地上。

definitions.en: to move forward slowly with a lot of shaking or movement up and down

examples.en: The horse and cart jogged down the rough track towards the farm.
examples.zh: 马车在崎岖的路上颠簸着驶向农场。

examples.en: We were getting more and more uncomfortable as we jogged along in the back of the truck.
examples.zh: 卡车一路颠簸前行，坐在后面的我们越来越感到不舒服。

definitions.en: a run that you do at a slow, regular speed, especially as a form of exercise
definitions.zh: 慢跑（尤指作为健身锻炼的方式）

examples.en: I haven't done much exercise all week, so I think I'll go for a jog this morning.
examples.zh: 我一周都没运动，所以今早我想去慢跑。

definitions.en: belonging to or shared between two or more people

examples.en: a joint bank account
examples.zh: 共有银行账户

examples.en: The project was a joint effort between the two schools (= they worked on it together).
examples.zh: 这一项目是两个学校共同合作的。

examples.en: The two Russian ice skaters came joint second (= they were both given second prize) in the world championships.
examples.zh: 两名俄罗斯滑冰运动员在世界锦标赛中并列第二。

examples.en: In court, the parents were awarded joint custody of their son (= the right to care for him was shared between them).
examples.zh: 法庭上这对父母获判对他们的儿子共同拥有监护权。

examples.en: He holds joint citizenship in Sweden and Peru.
The parents were given joint custody of the child.

definitions.en: a place in your body where two bones are connected

examples.en: an elbow/hip/knee joint
examples.zh: 肘／髋／膝关节

examples.en: As you become older, your joints get stiffer.
examples.zh: 随着年龄增加，关节就会变僵硬。

definitions.en: a place where two things are fastened together

examples.en: Damp has penetrated the joints in the wood panelling.
examples.zh: 潮气已渗透到木板饰面的接缝处。

definitions.en: a large piece of meat that is cooked in one piece

examples.en: a joint of beef/pork
examples.zh: 一大块烤牛肉／烤猪肉

examples.en: Fry four chicken joints in a pan with some mushrooms and garlic.
examples.zh: 用平底锅将4大块带骨鸡肉与一些蘑菇和大蒜一起煎。

definitions.en: a bar or restaurant that serves food and drink at low prices
definitions.zh: （出售廉价饮食的）酒吧（或餐馆）

examples.en: We had lunch at a hamburger joint and then went to see a movie.
examples.zh: 我们在一家汉堡包小店吃了午饭，然后去看电影。

examples.en: He owned several bars in the city and ran an illegal gambling joint.
examples.zh: 他在市里拥有好几家酒吧，还经营着一个非法赌场。

examples.en: We arrived at the club just before midnight and the joint was already jumping (= busy).
examples.zh: 我们午夜前夕到达俱乐部，那里已经人声鼎沸了。

definitions.en: a cigarette containing the drug cannabis
to cut meat into large pieces ready for cooking
definitions.zh: 把（肉）切成大块（供烹制用）

definitions.en: to throw several objects up into the air, and then catch and throw them up repeatedly so that one or more stays in the air, usually in order to entertain people

examples.en: We all watched in amazement as he juggled with three flaming torches.
examples.zh: 他拿3个燃烧的火把玩杂耍，我们都看呆了。

definitions.en: to succeed in arranging your life so that you have time to involve yourself in two or more different activities or groups of people
definitions.zh: 尽力同时应付（两种或两种以上不同的活动或人）

examples.en: Many parents find it hard to juggle children and a career.
examples.zh: 许多父母发现一边照顾孩子一边忙事业很难应付。

definitions.en: to change results or information recorded as numbers so that a situation seems to be better than it really is

examples.en: It won't matter if we juggle the figures - no one will know.
examples.zh: 我们改动这些数字没关系——没人会知道的。

definitions.en: a shish kebab
very interested, eager, or wanting (to do) something very much

examples.en: She's a keen tennis player.
examples.zh: 她热衷于打网球。

examples.en: Joan wanted to go to a movie but I wasn't keen (= I didn't want to go).
examples.zh: 琼想去看电影，可我不想去。

examples.en: keen to They were very keen to start work as soon as possible.
examples.zh: 他们渴望尽早开始工作。

examples.en: keen on She's keen on (playing) tennis.
examples.zh: 她很喜欢打网球。

examples.en: My son's mad keen on cycling.
examples.zh: 我儿子对骑自行车很着迷。

examples.en: He's rather keen on a girl in his school (= he is very attracted to her).
examples.zh: 他迷上了学校里的一个女孩子。

examples.en: Prince Charles is a keen polo player.
They've been keen walkers ever since they read about the benefits of exercise.
Doctors are short of time to listen and are therefore keen to dish out drugs whenever they can.
"What did he think of your suggestion?" "I don't think he was too keen."
With an election approaching, the prime minister is keen to maintain his popularity.

definitions.en: extreme or very strong

examples.en: keen interest Many people are taking a keen interest (= a very great interest) in the result of the vote.
examples.zh: 许多人对投票结果表现出浓厚的兴趣。

examples.en: a keen sense of smell
examples.zh: 灵敏的嗅觉

definitions.en: very sharp

examples.en: a keen knife
The tribe used obsidian to cut through whale skin, as the thick skin could dull even the keenest blade within a matter of minutes.

definitions.en: to make a loud, long, sad sound, especially because someone has died
the act of pressing down on a key on a computer

examples.en: I can add the date with a single keystroke.
examples.zh: 我按一下键就可以加上日期。

definitions.en: a round handle, or a small, round device for controlling a machine or electrical equipment
definitions.zh: 球形把手;（机器等的）旋钮

examples.en: a brass door knob
examples.zh: 铜质的门把手

examples.en: Turn/Twiddle the little knob to adjust the volume.
examples.zh: 转动小旋钮来调节音量。

examples.en: Turn the knob in a clockwise direction.
He pulled the knob and it just came off in his hand.
She turned the door knob and quietly opened the door.
Twiddle a knob on a radio in Britain and you may hear more voices speaking crackly French or German than English.
At the twiddle of a knob the operators can focus on a tiny amount of airspace or scan the whole area.

definitions.en: a small amount of something solid, especially butter

examples.en: Put a knob of butter in the frying pan.
examples.zh: 在煎锅里放一小块黄油。

definitions.en: a penis
US spelling of labour
the Labor Party, an Australian political party that believes in social equality and the rights of workers
a bomb that is put on or under the ground and that explodes when a person steps on it or a vehicle drives over it
a narrow road in the countryside or in a town
definitions.zh: 乡间小路；小巷，小街，胡同

examples.en: He drives so fast along those narrow country lanes.
examples.zh: 在那些狭窄的乡间小路上他把车开得飞快。

examples.en: I live at the end of Church Lane.
examples.zh: 我住在教堂巷的最里面。

examples.en: Turn left where the lane meets the main road.
The cottage is situated in the middle of a wood at the end of a narrow potholed lane.
The youths scuffled with the policeman, then escaped down the lane.
He climbed onto his horse and set off at a relaxed trot down the lane.
We were trotting along the lane when a car suddenly appeared from nowhere and almost made me fall off my pony.

definitions.en: a special strip of a road, sports track, or swimming pool that is used to keep vehicles or competitors separate
definitions.zh: 车道；（田径比赛的）跑道；泳道

examples.en: a bus/bike/cycle lane
examples.zh: 公共汽车／自行车车道

examples.en: The northbound lane is closed because of an accident.
examples.zh: 北行的车道因发生事故已经封闭。

examples.en: I find driving in the fast lane very stressful.
examples.zh: 我发现在快车道行驶时精神非常紧张。

examples.en: The German runners/swimmers are in lanes 4 and 6.
examples.zh: 德国径赛／游泳选手处在第4道和第6道。

examples.en: The English Channel is the busiest shipping lane in the world.
examples.zh: 英吉利海峡是世界上最繁忙的水运航道。

definitions.en: a temporary failure

examples.en: a lapse of concentration
examples.zh: 走神

examples.en: The management's decision to ignore the safety warnings demonstrated a remarkable lapse of judgment.
examples.zh: 管理层作出的对安全警告不予理会的决定是决策上的明显失误。

examples.en: a memory lapse
examples.zh: 一时想不起来

definitions.en: a period of time passing between two things happening

examples.en: a time lapse/a lapse of time
examples.zh: 时间间隔

examples.en: He turned up again after a lapse of two years.
examples.zh: 两年后他又露面了。

definitions.en: to end legally or officially by not being continued or made effective for a longer period

examples.en: The association needs to win back former members who have allowed their subscriptions to lapse.
examples.zh: 该协会需要重新把那些已经期满退出的会员吸引回来。

definitions.en: (a device that produces) a powerful, narrow beam of light that can be used as a tool to cut metal, to perform medical operations, or to create patterns of light for entertainment

examples.en: laser beam
examples.zh: 激光束

examples.en: laser surgery
examples.zh: 激光手术

examples.en: The tube that carries the laser is introduced into the abdomen through a small cut in the skin.
The laser beam heals the eye painlessly.
The laser guidance system dramatically increases the likelihood that the missile will strike home.
During surgery, doctors sometimes use a laser beam to vaporize tiny blood vessels.
There was a laser show by the harbour.

definitions.en: present but needing particular conditions to become active, obvious, or completely developed

examples.en: Recent developments in the area have brought latent ethnic tension out into the open.
examples.zh: 近来该地区的一些新情况导致民族之间潜在的紧张关系公开化了。

examples.en: We're trying to bring out the latent artistic talents that many people possess without realizing it.
examples.zh: 我们正在努力挖掘许多人自身具有但却没有意识到的潜在艺术才能。

definitions.en: the dirty clothes and sheets that need to be, are being, or have been washed
definitions.zh: 待洗（或正在洗涤、洗完）的衣物

examples.en: I've got to do (= wash) my laundry.
examples.zh: 我得把衣服洗了。

definitions.en: without much care, attention, or control

examples.en: The subcommittee contends that the authorities were lax in investigating most of the cases.
examples.zh: 小组委员会认为有关部门对多数案件疏于调查。

examples.en: He took a gun through baggage control to highlight the lax security.
examples.zh: 他携带一把枪通过了行李检查，以此引起大家对安全管理松懈这一问题的重视。

definitions.en: to (cause to) slope in one direction, or to move the top part of the body in a particular direction

examples.en: She leaned forward and whispered something in my ear.
examples.zh: 她俯身过来和我耳语了几句。

examples.en: I sat down next to Bernard, who leaned over to me and said "You're late."
examples.zh: 我在伯纳德身边坐下，他凑过身子对我说，“你来晚了。”

examples.en: Lean your head back a little.
examples.zh: 头向后仰一点。

examples.en: That fence is leaning to the right.
examples.zh: 那个栅栏向右倾斜。

examples.en: The post leans to the right.
I leaned over to pick up the letter.
He leaned forward to listen more closely.
The trees leaned in the wind.
The wall is leaning dangerously.

definitions.en: Lean meat has little fat.

examples.en: lean and fit
examples.zh: 瘦而健康

definitions.en: If a period of time is lean, there is not enough of something, especially money or food, at that time.

examples.en: It has been a particularly lean year for the education department.
examples.zh: 对教育部而言这是尤为不景气的一年。

definitions.en: A lean company or organization does not use too many people or spend too much money, so that there is no waste.

examples.en: Nowadays even efficient, lean, well-run industries are failing.
examples.zh: 如今即使是那些高效、精干、管理良好的行业也在亏损。

definitions.en: A leftover part of something is the part that has not been used or eaten when the other parts have been.

examples.en: some leftover chicken from last night's meal
examples.zh: 昨天晚餐剩下的一些鸡肉

definitions.en: food remaining after a meal:
definitions.zh: 这份食谱足够4人享用，剩下的可作冷食。

examples.en: This recipe can serve four easily, and the leftovers are just as good eaten cold.
examples.zh: 这份食谱足够4人享用，剩下的可作冷食。

definitions.en: allowed by law
definitions.zh: 合法的，正当的，法律允许的

examples.en: The army must give power back to the legitimate government.
examples.zh: 军队必须将权力交还给合法政府。

examples.en: He claimed that the restaurant bill was a legitimate business expense.
examples.zh: 他声称饭店账单是一笔合理的业务开支。

examples.en: Her passport seemed legitimate, but on closer inspection, it was found to have been altered.
They complained about excessive government intrusion into their legitimate activities.
Legitimate refugees should be treated with compassion.
Her complaints were quite legitimate.
He has a legitimate claim to the money.

definitions.en: to make something legal or acceptable

examples.en: The government fears that talking to terrorists might legitimate their violent actions.
examples.zh: 政府担心与恐怖分子谈判可能会使他们的暴力行为得到认可。

definitions.en: in a way that is allowed by law

examples.en: Most foreign visitors enter the country legitimately.
examples.zh: 大多数外国游客都是合法入境的。

examples.en: We support legitimately elected governments.
examples.zh: 我们支持合法的民选政府。

examples.en: There are times when you legitimately need to cancel travel plans.
examples.zh: 有时您有正当理由需要取消旅行计划。

examples.en: Medicine is not an exact science and specialists can disagree legitimately on the best course of action.
examples.zh: 医学不是一门精准科学，专家们在最佳治疗方案上出现分歧是很合理的。

examples.en: They had to decide whether the Marine had legitimately defended himself or committed a war crime.
In the 2000 election, in some precincts in Florida, people who were legitimately registered were turned away at the polls.
How can you be sure that a health records company will make your medical information available to people who legitimately need it while keeping it away from those who don't?
If you were under duress and in danger, you could legitimately lie.

definitions.en: a curved piece of glass, plastic, or other transparent material, used in cameras, glasses, and scientific equipment, that makes objects seem closer, larger, smaller, etc.

examples.en: a camera with a zoom lens
examples.zh: 变焦（镜头）照相机

definitions.en: the part of the eye behind the pupil (= the black hole at the front of the eye) that helps you to see clearly by focusing (= collecting) light onto the retina
respecting and allowing many different types of beliefs or behaviour
definitions.zh: 自由开放的，开放的，开明的

examples.en: a liberal society/attitude
examples.zh: 自由开放的社会／态度

examples.en: Her parents were far more liberal than mine.
examples.zh: 她父母比我父母开明得多。

examples.en: Espousal of such liberal ideas won't make her very popular around here.
Despite his apparent liberal views, he's really something of a puritan.
His natural supporters are the urban poor, and educated people of a liberal turn of mind.
He has very liberal attitudes towards sex.
My family are very liberal-minded.

definitions.en: believing in or allowing a lot of personal freedom, and believing that society should change gradually so that money, property, and power are shared more fairly
definitions.zh: （指政党或国家）自由的，开放的

examples.en: He absorbed the attitudes of his politically liberal parents.
Her politics have become more liberal over the past few years.
I'm sick of bleeding-heart liberal politicians.
She is interested in liberal policies.
He believes that the liberal agenda is damaging to our country.
Discussions on farming will quickly turn into a dispute with liberal free-marketeers.

definitions.en: giving or given in a generous way
definitions.zh: 慷慨的，大方的，毫不吝啬的

examples.en: He was very liberal with the wine.
examples.zh: 对于酒他毫不吝啬。

definitions.en: not exact; without attention to or interest in detail
definitions.zh: 不确切的，模糊的，笼统的

examples.en: a liberal interpretation of the law
examples.zh: 对法律的笼统解释

definitions.en: someone who respects many different types of beliefs or behaviour

examples.en: I am a liberal who believes in tolerance.
The city has a reputation for equality and tolerance, and has traditionally been a haven for liberals.
Liberals preach freedom of thought.
They are the sort of fake liberals who proclaim tolerance out of one side of their mouths and issue snobbish put-downs out of the other.

definitions.en: in a way that is involves large amounts of something

examples.en: Apply the cream liberally to the affected area.
examples.zh: 把药膏厚厚地涂在患处。

examples.en: Banks are lending money more liberally than ever.
examples.zh: 银行放贷比以往都宽松。

examples.en: The author sprinkles her English prose liberally with Spanish words.
Season the steak liberally before grilling.
Walt Whitman is liberally quoted in the piece.
Unlike US consumers, many of whom use credit liberally, Chinese workers opt to save.
Place the remaining stuffing on the top, and dot liberally with the butter.

definitions.en: in a way that is not exact or exactly correct
definitions.zh: 不确切地，模糊地，笼统地

examples.en: In describing him as an author I guess you're using the word very liberally?
examples.zh: 把他描述成一个作家，我想你这个用词很大方啊？

examples.en: Do concepts such as "resistance" have much force when employed so liberally?
He uses the description "soap opera" a little liberally.
In this context I'm using the word "compound" a bit more liberally.

definitions.en: in a way that allows a lot of freedom for different types of belief or behavior

examples.en: She was raised very liberally by her parents.
examples.zh: 她的父母对她的教育非常宽松。

examples.en: He mistakenly thought he could vote as liberally as he wanted in Washington.
examples.zh: 他误以为自己可以在华盛顿随心所欲地投票。

examples.en: Lincoln himself said after the victory, "I want no one punished; treat them liberally all around."
The MP condemned the decision to pardon the men as "deplorable" and said the four had been treated "far too liberally".
Polls show a big shift against racism and that we now think more liberally about race.

definitions.en: a cover on a container, that can be lifted up or removed

examples.en: Can you get the lid off this jar?
examples.zh: 你能把罐子的盖打开吗？

examples.en: Put a lid on the saucepan.
examples.zh: 给平底深锅盖上盖儿。

examples.en: She looked at him from under half-closed lids.
examples.zh: 她眯着眼睛看着他。

examples.en: The lid of the coffin had been nailed down.
I can't get the lid off this jar.
Screw the lid on tightly.
I prized the lid off with a spoon.
She had used a screwdriver to puncture two holes in the lid of a paint tin.

definitions.en: the chance that something will happen

examples.en: This latest dispute greatly increases the likelihood of a strike.
examples.zh: 最近的这场冲突极大地增加了罢工的可能性。

examples.en: There is every likelihood that more jobs will be lost later this year.
examples.zh: 今年晚些时候很可能会有更多的人失业。

examples.en: There is little likelihood now that interest rates will come down further.
examples.zh: 现在利率进一步下降的可能性几乎为零。

examples.en: In all likelihood, everything will go to plan.
examples.zh: 一切几乎肯定会按计划进行。

definitions.en: in the same way

examples.en: Just water these plants twice a week, and likewise the ones in the bedroom.
examples.zh: 这些植物一周浇两次水，卧室里的那些也一样。

examples.en: "I don't have time to spend hours preparing one dish!" "Likewise (= it's the same for me)."
examples.zh: “我没有功夫花好几个小时去做一个菜！”“我也一样。”

definitions.en: to take a long time to leave or disappear

examples.en: After the play had finished, we lingered for a while in the bar hoping to catch sight of the actors.
examples.zh: 戏演完后我们在酒吧里等了一会儿，希望能与演员见上一面。

examples.en: The smell from the fire still lingered days later.
examples.zh: 大火过后好几天还能闻到烧焦的味道。

examples.en: It's impossible to forget such horrific events - they linger (on) in the memory forever.
examples.zh: 这样恐怖的事件让人难以忘记——会永远留在人们的记忆中。

definitions.en: the existence or forming of connections between things
US spelling of litre UK
in a way that is not firmly held or attached

examples.en: The parcel had only been loosely wrapped, and the paper had come off.
examples.zh: 包裹没有包严实，上面的包装纸已经脱落了。

definitions.en: not exactly

examples.en: This phrase can be loosely translated as "Go away!"
examples.zh: 这个词组可以大概翻译为“走开”。

examples.en: The film is loosely based on the novel by Kundera.
examples.zh: 这部电影是根据昆德拉的小说的大致内容改编而成的。

definitions.en: not tightly

examples.en: The jacket hung loosely on his thin body.
examples.zh: 那件上衣穿在他那瘦小的身躯上显得有些松松垮垮。

definitions.en: involving the loss of data or of electrical energy
definitions.zh: 丢失数据的;损耗电子能量的

examples.en: Lossy file compression results in lost data and quality from the original version.
examples.zh: 有损文件压缩会丢失原始文件的数据的质量。

definitions.en: the large size or importance of something

examples.en: They don't seem to grasp the magnitude of the problem.
examples.zh: 他们似乎未领会问题的重要性。

definitions.en: the work needed to keep a road, building, machine, etc. in good condition
definitions.zh: 养护（保持道路、建筑、机器设备等状态良好的工作）

examples.en: Old houses need a lot of maintenance.
examples.zh: 老房子需要大量保养。

examples.en: There are thorough maintenance checks on each plane before take-off.
examples.zh: 每架飞机起飞前都要经过彻底的维修检查。

examples.en: The magazine offers tips on cutting your house maintenance costs.
examples.zh: 这本杂志教你一些降低房屋维修成本的妙招儿。

examples.en: The network will be down for an hour for routine maintenance.
The new ships, propelled by gas turbines, require less maintenance than older, steam-driven ones.
This course is designed to give drivers a grounding in car maintenance.
There is a risk that the debt-ridden airline that may be tempted to scrimp on maintenance or security.
