examples.zh: 毒品会改变你对现实的感知。

examples.en: She has extraordinary powers of perception for one so young.
examples.zh: 她年纪轻轻就有着非凡的洞察力。

examples.en: He's not known for his perception.
examples.zh: 他的洞察力并不怎么样。

definitions.en: (of a play, piece of music, dance, etc.) able to be acted, sung, played, etc. as entertainment

examples.en: The composer took another four years to flesh out the skeleton into a performable score.
examples.zh: 作曲家又花了四年时间将骨架充实起来，成为可演奏的乐谱。

examples.en: The dramatizations are impressive in their originality of approach and theatricality of conception, making them both eminently performable.
examples.zh: 这些戏剧化的作品在方法的独创性和概念的戏剧性上令人印象深刻，使它们都非常具有可表演性。

examples.en: The technique is performable if vascular surgeons and interventional radiologists work side by side in the operating theatre.
The executive branch has been assigned a function that in the past was performable by the Legislature itself.
It is no easy task to write music for children that is challenging rather than patronizing, and performable without being dull.
His conviction that the opera is hardly performable any more points to the potential problems of appreciating a work outside its original context.
The strict definition of robots requires that those manipulations be programmable, performable autonomously or by programmable teleoperation.
When TEM turns out not to be performable, an attempt with EMR will be done as well.

definitions.en: a magazine or newspaper, especially on a serious subject, that is published regularly

examples.en: She has written for several legal periodicals.
examples.zh: 她为几家法律期刊撰稿。

definitions.en: Something that is peripheral is not as important as something else.

examples.en: The book contains a great deal of peripheral detail.
examples.zh: 该书包含了大量无关紧要的细节。

examples.en: A figure came into my peripheral vision.
examples.zh: 一个身影从远处映入我眼帘。

definitions.en: a piece of equipment, such as a printer, that can be connected to a computer
definitions.zh: （计算机的）外围设备（如打印机）

definitions.en: lasting for a long time or for ever

examples.en: She is looking for a permanent place to stay.
examples.zh: 她正在寻找一个固定的住所。

examples.en: Are you looking for a temporary or a permanent job?
examples.zh: 你是想找一份临时的还是固定的工作？

examples.en: The disease can cause permanent damage to the brain.
examples.zh: 这种疾病可能导致大脑永久性损伤。

examples.en: A semi-permanent hair dye will wash out after about three months.
examples.zh: 半永固性染发剂大约3个月后开始褪色。

examples.en: He entered the United States in 1988 as a permanent resident because of his marriage to a U.S. citizen.
examples.zh: 他因和一位美国公民结婚而于1988年移居美国并成为永久居民。

examples.en: Mont Blanc has a permanent snow cap.
examples.zh: 勃朗峰的山顶终年积雪。

examples.en: Our office is in a permanent state of chaos.
examples.zh: 我们办公室里永远一团糟。

examples.en: The accident left him with permanent brain damage.
She took up permanent residency abroad.
The salaries of temporary employees ought to match those of permanent staff.
Getting some work experience now will help you when you apply for a permanent job.
This is a serious condition which can lead to permanent hearing loss.

definitions.en: a chemical process that makes your hair curly, or a hairstyle that is created in this way
always and for ever

examples.en: Smoking is likely to damage your health permanently.
examples.zh: 吸烟可能会永久性地损害你的健康。

examples.en: Michael and his family have settled permanently in the States.
examples.zh: 迈克尔和他的家人在美国永久定居下来。

examples.en: I seem to be permanently broke.
examples.zh: 我似乎永远一贫如洗。

examples.en: The explosion permanently deafened her in her right ear.
The mountain villages seem to be permanently shrouded in mist.
Three reconnaissance aircraft are permanently on patrol.
I started legal proceedings to try to have him taken away from his parents permanently.
His reputation was permanently tainted by the financial scandal.

definitions.en: for ever
lasting for a long time or difficult to get rid of
definitions.zh: 持续的;存留的;顽强存在的

examples.en: a persistent smell/skin rash
examples.zh: 持续不散的气味／顽固的皮疹

examples.en: Symptoms of the illness include a high temperature and a persistent dry cough.
examples.zh: 病症包括高烧和持久的干咳。

examples.en: There have been persistent rumours that the principal might take early retirement.
examples.zh: 不断有谣言说校长可能会提前退休。

examples.en: Be persistent - don't give up.
examples.zh: 要坚持不懈——别放弃。

examples.en: He has been a persistent critic of the president.
examples.zh: 他一贯对总统持批评态度。

examples.en: She is a persistent offender and has been arrested five times this year for shoplifting.
examples.zh: 她是个惯犯，今年已5次因在商店行窃被捕。

definitions.en: a particular way of considering something

examples.en: Her attitude lends a fresh perspective to the subject.
examples.zh: 她的态度为这一问题提供了新的视角。

examples.en: He writes from a Marxist perspective.
examples.zh: 他从一个马克思主义者的视角进行写作。

examples.en: Because of its geographical position, Germany's perspective on the situation in Russia is very different from Washington's.
examples.zh: 由于地理位置不同，德国对俄罗斯局势的看法与华盛顿大相径庭。

examples.en: You must keep things in perspective - the overall situation isn't really that bad.
examples.zh: 你必须正确看待问题——总体局势还没那么糟糕。

examples.en: Total investments for this year reached $53 million, and, to put this into perspective, investments this year were double those made in 2013.
examples.zh: 今年的总投资额达到5300万美元，客观地来看，这是2013年的两倍。

examples.en: I'd like to get someone else's perspective on this.
You're approaching the problem from a completely different perspective.
In a few years he might have a different perspective.
Why don't you think of this from a child's perspective?
When my Mum died it gave me a whole new perspective on life.

definitions.en: the way that objects appear smaller when they are further away and the way parallel lines appear to meet each other at a point in the distance

examples.en: In 15th-century Italy, artists rediscovered the rules of perspective.
examples.zh: 在15世纪的意大利，艺术家们重新发现了透视规则。

examples.en: Make sure that the figures are in perspective.
The painting had a naive, out-of-perspective style.

definitions.en: to be connected with a particular subject, event, or situation

examples.en: We are only interested in the parts of the proposals that pertain to local issues.
examples.zh: 我们只对议案中涉及当地问题的部分感兴趣。

definitions.en: When qualities, characteristics, or smells pervade a place or thing, they spread through it and are present in every part of it.

examples.en: The film movie is a reflection of the violence that pervades our culture.
examples.zh: 这部电影反映了充斥于我们文化中的暴力。

definitions.en: present or noticeable in every part of a thing or place
definitions.zh: 充斥各处的;弥漫的，遍布的

examples.en: The influence of Freud is pervasive in her books.
examples.zh: 弗洛伊德的影响在她的作品中随处可见。

examples.en: a pervasive smell of diesel
examples.zh: 到处弥漫的柴油味

examples.en: Reforms are being undermined by the all-pervasive corruption in the country.
examples.zh: 改革正在受到国内普遍存在的腐败现象的侵蚀。

definitions.en: annoying or causing trouble

examples.en: Those pesky kids from next door have let the air out of my car tyres again!
examples.zh: 隔壁那些讨厌的孩子又把我汽车轮胎的气放了！

definitions.en: a document signed by a large number of people demanding or asking for some action from the government or another authority

examples.en: I signed a petition against the proposed closure of the local hospital today.
examples.zh: 今天我在反对关闭当地医院的请愿书上签了名。

examples.en: She's filing a petition for divorce.
examples.zh: 她将要递交离婚诉状。

definitions.en: to make a formal request for something, especially in a law court

examples.en: They're petitioning for/about better facilities for disabled people.
examples.zh: 他们正在请愿，希望当局能为残疾人安装更好的设施。

examples.en: I think we should petition the government to increase the grant for the project.
examples.zh: 我认为我们应该请求政府增加对该项目的拨款。

examples.en: She is petitioning for a re-trial.
examples.zh: 她将上诉要求重审。

definitions.en: something that exists and can be seen, felt, tasted, etc., especially something unusual or interesting
definitions.zh: （尤指不寻常的或有趣的）现象

examples.en: Gravity is a natural phenomenon.
examples.zh: 重力是一种自然现象。

examples.en: Do you believe in the paranormal and other psychic phenomena?
examples.zh: 你相信超自然及其他通灵现象吗？

examples.en: There's evidence to suggest that child abuse is not just a recent phenomenon.
examples.zh: 有证据表明虐待儿童并不是近来才有的现象。

examples.en: paranormal phenomena
geophysical and geological phenomena
Johnson (op. cit., page 53) calls this phenomenon 'the principle of minimal effort'.
the El Nino weather phenomenon
This trend is certainly not a new phenomenon.

definitions.en: someone or something that is extremely successful, often because of special qualities or abilities
definitions.zh: 非凡的人（或事物）；奇才；奇迹

examples.en: The Beatles were a phenomenon - nobody had heard anything like them before.
examples.zh: 甲壳虫乐队真是个奇迹，在此之前人们从未听说过像他们那样杰出的乐队。

definitions.en: an attempt to trick someone into giving information over the internet or by email that would allow someone else to take money from them, for example by taking money out of their bank account
definitions.zh: “网络钓鱼”（骗取他人网上银行账户的有关信息从而盗取他人资金的行为）

definitions.en: objects positioned one on top of another

examples.en: a pile of books
examples.zh: 一摞书

examples.en: a pile of dirty clothes
examples.zh: 一大堆脏衣服

examples.en: a large pile of sand
examples.zh: 一大堆沙子

examples.en: I've got piles/a pile of things to do today.
examples.zh: 我今天有一大堆事情要做。

examples.en: Obviously, nobody intends to make a pile of terrible movies.
They hope that these new drugs will make them a pile of cash.
There are piles of people looking for jobs in this area.
We had to pay piles of money to get the work done.
He had a pile of papers on his desk.
Put those clothes in a pile for washing .
I have a pile of mending to do.
He found the piece of paper he had lost at the very bottom of the pile.
There's an enormous pile of washing-up in the sink.

definitions.en: a strong column or post of wood, metal, or concrete that is pushed into the ground to help support a building
the soft surface made by the ends of many short threads on a carpet or on cloth such as velvet
definitions.zh: （地毯或天鹅绒等布料的）绒面

examples.en: a luxurious deep-pile carpet
examples.zh: 豪华的长毛地毯

definitions.en: a large building

examples.en: They've got a great big Victorian pile somewhere out in the country.
examples.zh: 他们在乡下的某个地方有一座雄伟的维多利亚式的房子。

definitions.en: → haemorrhoids UK specialized

examples.en: Ask your pharmacist about the best treatment for piles.
They told us we'd get piles if we sat on the cold wall.
What's the best way to stop piles from itching?
The doctor told her she had piles.
Sometimes people need surgery to remove painful or very itchy piles.

definitions.en: to arrange objects into a pile

examples.en: We piled plenty of logs up next to the fire.
examples.zh: 我们在炉火旁堆了很多木柴。

examples.en: Please pile your homework books neatly on the table as you leave.
examples.zh: 离开时请把你们的作业本整齐地叠放在桌子上。

examples.en: Her plate was piled (high) with salad.
examples.zh: 她的盘子里盛满了色拉。

examples.en: Snow had piled up against the walls.
examples.zh: 雪在墙根堆积起来。

examples.en: The saucepans clashed as he piled them into the sink.
She had a most elaborate hairdo, all piled up on top of her head.
In the village market, the stalls are piled high with local vegetables.
The logs were neatly piled against the wall.
Knowing he had a good appetite, she passed him a plate piled high with mashed potato.

definitions.en: (of a group of people) to move together, especially in an uncontrolled way

examples.en: As soon as the train stopped, they all piled in/out.
examples.zh: 火车刚停下，他们就挤了进去／出来。

examples.en: A group of them had piled onto a truck, crowding on in such numbers that some were forced to sit on the roof.

definitions.en: a strong column made of stone, metal, or wood that supports part of a building

examples.en: A row of reinforced concrete pillars supports the bridge.
examples.zh: 一排钢筋混凝土柱子支撑着桥梁。

examples.en: a pillar of smoke/flame
examples.zh: 烟／火柱

examples.en: Mrs Maple is a pillar of the local church.
examples.zh: 梅普尔夫人是当地教会的骨干。

definitions.en: to find out or say the exact position in space or time of something
definitions.zh: 准确指出，确定（位置或时间）

examples.en: It is not possible to pinpoint precisely the time of death.
examples.zh: 准确地确定死亡时间是不可能的。

examples.en: Emergency workers at the site are still unable to pinpoint the cause of the explosion.
examples.zh: 现场的急救人员仍然无法确定爆炸的原因。

definitions.en: a very small spot of something

examples.en: a pinpoint of light
examples.zh: 光点

definitions.en: very exact

examples.en: The computer will calculate your position with pinpoint accuracy.
examples.zh: 计算机能够精确地计算出你的位置。

definitions.en: a large hole in the ground, or a slightly low area in any surface

examples.en: They'd dug a shallow pit and left the bodies in it.
examples.zh: 他们挖了一个浅坑把尸体扔在里面。

examples.en: These pits in my skin are from when I had chickenpox.
examples.zh: 我皮肤上的痘瘢是我出水痘时留下的。

examples.en: The coal-mining industry wants new pits to be opened.
examples.zh: 煤矿业需要开凿新的矿井。

examples.en: a gravel/chalk pit
examples.zh: 砾石／白垩坑

examples.en: I'm afraid my room is a complete pit!
examples.zh: 恐怕我的房间乱得一塌糊涂！

examples.en: The hotel we stayed in was the pits!
examples.zh: 我们住的宾馆实在太糟糕了！

examples.en: The unions fought hard against the government's programme of pit closures.
The plumber climbed down the inspection pit.
They had to dig a six-foot deep pit to sink the sewage pipes.
Can you tidy up that pit of a bedroom of yours!
They dumped his dismembered body in a pit.

definitions.en: a large, hard seed inside some types of fruit

examples.en: Peaches, plums, and olives all contain pits.
examples.zh: 桃子、李子和橄榄都有果核。

definitions.en: US for stone
a likely mistake or problem in a situation

examples.en: The store fell into one of the major pitfalls of small business, borrowing from suppliers by paying bills late.
examples.zh: 那家商店犯了一个小本经营的大错，即从供货商那里先赊账后付钱。

examples.en: There's a video that tells new students about pitfalls to avoid.
examples.zh: 有一部录像告诉新生应避免的错误。

definitions.en: a fixed point supporting something that turns or balances

examples.en: The former guerrilla leader has become the pivot on which the country's emerging political stability turns/revolves (= it depends on him).
examples.zh: 前游击队领导人成了决定该国政治稳定与否的关键人物。

definitions.en: to turn or twist

examples.en: She pivots her left foot.
examples.zh: 她扭了左脚。

examples.en: He pivoted on his heels and headed out.
examples.zh: 他脚跟一转，向外走去。

examples.en: Future deals will pivot on (= depend on) easing commercial conflicts.
examples.zh: 未来的协议将取决于疏缓商业冲突。

definitions.en: to cause worry, pain, or difficulty to someone or something over a period of time
definitions.zh: 不断困扰;折磨;使受煎熬

examples.en: Financial problems have been plaguing their new business partners.
examples.zh: 财政问题一直困扰着他们的新商业合作伙伴。

examples.en: My shoulder's been plaguing me all week.
examples.zh: 我的肩膀整个星期都很难受。

examples.en: The children plagued him with questions all through lunch.
examples.zh: 吃午饭时孩子们不停地问他问题，使他很烦。

examples.en: He's been plaguing me for a loan of the book.
examples.zh: 他总是缠着我向我借那本书。

definitions.en: a serious disease that kills many people, often used to refer to bubonic plague, a very infectious disease caused by bacteria spread mainly by fleas (= small insects that bite) on rats or other animals, that causes swelling, fever, and usually death in humans

examples.en: In 1349 almost half the townspeople of Cork died of plague after the Black Death arrived in the town.
The book looks at infectious diseases, from the plague to SARS, and at how treatment has changed over the centuries.
Somehow they had survived when the Plague came.
They described the historical plagues that had killed millions.
a plague of insects
examples.zh: 昆虫灾害

definitions.en: a soft sound like that of something solid dropping lightly into a liquid

examples.en: The stone fell into the water with a plop.
examples.zh: 那颗石子咚的一声掉进了水里。

definitions.en: to sit down or land heavily or without taking care, or to put something down without taking care
definitions.zh: 重重地坐下；随便地放下

examples.en: He came and plopped down next to me.
examples.zh: 他走过来，在我旁边重重坐下。

examples.en: Lynn plopped a paper cup down beside her.
examples.zh: 琳恩把一个纸杯随手放在她身边。

definitions.en: to fall with a soft sound

examples.en: I noticed drops of water plopping onto the carpet.
examples.zh: 我注意到水正嘀嘀嗒嗒往地毯上滴。

definitions.en: a small plastic or rubber object with two or three metal pins, attached to the end of a wire on a piece of electrical equipment and pushed into a special opening in a wall to connect the equipment to a supply of electricity

examples.en: a three-pin/two-pin plug
examples.zh: 三相／两相插头

examples.en: to fit/change a plug
examples.zh: 安装／更换插头

examples.en: If a plug is wired incorrectly, it can be dangerous.
examples.zh: 如果插头接线不对会很危险。

examples.en: Is there a plug in the bedroom that I can use for my hairdryer?
examples.zh: 卧室里有没有插座让我用一下电吹风？

examples.en: It's no wonder your shaver isn't working. There's a loose connection in the plug.
This cable should have a plug at one end and a socket at the other.

definitions.en: a small piece of rubber, plastic, wood, etc. that fits into a hole in order to close it

examples.en: a bath plug
examples.zh: 浴缸塞

examples.en: Put the plug in the sink and run some water.
examples.zh: 把洗涤槽排水孔塞上，然后放些水。

definitions.en: the act of telling people publicly about a product, event, etc.

examples.en: She never misses an opportunity to get in a plug for her new film.
examples.zh: 她从来不会错过向公众宣传自己新片的机会。

definitions.en: to advertise something by talking about it a lot or praising it, especially on the radio or television
definitions.zh: （尤指通过广播或电视）宣传，推广

examples.en: That interview was just a way for him to plug his new book.
examples.zh: 那次采访只是宣传他的新书的一种方式。

examples.en: They're plugging this new chocolate bar everywhere at the moment.
examples.zh: 此时他们正在到处宣传这种新巧克力。

definitions.en: to shoot someone with a gun

examples.en: Sure, boss, we plugged the guy (full of lead).
examples.zh: 是的，头儿，我们（乱枪）打死了那家伙。

definitions.en: to fill a hole with a piece of suitable material

examples.en: Have you plugged that leak (= stopped it by filling the hole) in the pipe?
examples.zh: 你把管子上的裂缝堵起来了吗？

examples.en: My nose was bleeding and I plugged it with cotton wool.
examples.zh: 我鼻子流血了，便塞了棉球止血。

definitions.en: a word or form that expresses more than one

examples.en: "Geese" is the plural of "goose".
examples.zh: geese 是 goose 的复数形式。

examples.en: "Woman" in the plural is "women".
examples.zh: woman 的复数形式是 women。

examples.en: To make the plural of "dog" you add the plural ending -s.
'Are' is the second person plural of the verb 'to be'.
The word 'teeth' is an irregular plural of the noun 'tooth'.
What's the plural of 'tooth'?
Words like "sheep" stay the same in the plural.

definitions.en: consisting of lots of different races or types of people or of different things

examples.en: We need to recognize that we are now living in a plural society.
examples.zh: 我们必须意识到我们现在生活在一个多元社会中。

examples.en: Very few countries allow people to have plural citizenship (= citizenship of more than one country).
examples.zh: 极少数几个国家允许公民拥有多重国籍。

definitions.en: of or relating to the form that expresses more than one

examples.en: "Cattle" and "trousers" are both plural nouns.
examples.zh: cattle 和 trousers 均为复数名词。

definitions.en: Something that is pointless has no purpose, and it is a waste of time doing it.

examples.en: This is a pointless exercise.
examples.zh: 这个练习毫无意义。

examples.en: It seemed pointless to continue.
examples.zh: 继续下去看来没有意义。

examples.en: It's pointless arguing with him.
examples.zh: 和他争辩是白费口舌。

definitions.en: a substance that can make people or animals ill or kill them if they eat or drink it

examples.en: The pest control officer put bowls of rat poison in the attic.
examples.zh: 虫害防治官员在阁楼里放了几碗鼠药。

examples.en: Her drink had been laced with a deadly poison.
examples.zh: 她的饮料里掺进了致命的毒药。

examples.en: Bottles of lemonade doctored with rat poison were discovered in the kitchen.
Forensic examination revealed a large quantity of poison in the dead man's stomach.
The giraffe was killed with a spear that had been tipped with poison.
Arsenic is a deadly poison that takes immediate effect.
a phial of poison

definitions.en: to kill a person or animal or to make them very ill by giving them poison

examples.en: Four members of the family had been poisoned, but not fatally.
examples.zh: 这家中有四口人被人投毒，但未致命。

examples.en: He said that someone had poisoned his coffee.
examples.zh: 他说有人在他咖啡里下了毒。

examples.en: The chemical leak had poisoned the water supply.
examples.zh: 化学品的泄漏污染了供水。

definitions.en: to spoil a friendship or another situation, by making it very unpleasant

examples.en: The long dispute has poisoned relations between the two countries.
examples.zh: 长期的纠纷损害了两国关系。

definitions.en: to push a finger or other pointed object quickly into someone or something

examples.en: You'll poke someone in the eye with that umbrella if you're not careful!
examples.zh: 你不小心的话伞尖会戳到别人眼睛的！

examples.en: Two kids were poking a stick into the drain.
examples.zh: 两个孩子在把一根棍子插到排水管里面去。

definitions.en: to (cause something to) appear or stretch out from behind or through something else

examples.en: Cathy poked her head round the door to say hello.
examples.zh: 凯茜从门后探出头来打招呼。

examples.en: The first green shoots are poking up through the soil.
examples.zh: 第一批绿芽破土而出。

definitions.en: to greet someone on a social networking website by leaving them a special short message
definitions.zh: （在社交媒体上）通过留言问候对方

definitions.en: the act of poking someone or something

examples.en: She gave me a poke in the stomach.
examples.zh: 她戳了戳我的肚子。

definitions.en: in a polite way

examples.en: He told them politely to leave him in peace.
examples.zh: 他礼貌地请他们让他一个人静静地呆着。

examples.en: The audience clapped politely.
examples.zh: 观众们礼节性地鼓了鼓掌。

examples.en: The waiter smiled politely as he handed me my bill/handed my bill to me.
"Where are we going?" he inquired politely.
Very politely the little boy asked if he might have another piece of cake .
He sensed that his guests were bored, although they were listening politely.
He smiled politely as Mary apologized for her drunken friends.

definitions.en: If an area is populated by people or animals, they live in that area.
definitions.zh: 构成…的人口（或动物的总数）

examples.en: The inner cities are no longer densely populated.
examples.zh: 市中心的人口密度已下降。

examples.en: The river is populated mainly by smaller species of fish.
examples.zh: 这条河里大部分是小型鱼类。

examples.en: The settlers began to move inland and populate the river valleys.
examples.zh: 移民开始迁往内陆，居住在河谷地带。

examples.en: There are several ways to populate the database.
examples.zh: 有几种方式给数据库自动增加资料。

definitions.en: a hard but delicate, shiny, white substance made by heating a special type of clay to a high temperature, used to make cups, plates, decorations, etc.

examples.en: a porcelain dish
examples.zh: 瓷碟

examples.en: The tea cups are (made of) porcelain.
examples.zh: 茶杯是瓷制的。

examples.en: He had a fine collection of Meissen porcelain.
examples.zh: 他收藏了很多精美的迈森瓷器。

definitions.en: a covered structure in front of the entrance to a building

examples.en: For details, see the poster in the church porch.
The front porch held pots of flowers, all looking healthy and strong.
We sat out on the porch to cool off.
examples.zh: 我们坐在外面的走廊上乘凉。

examples.en: A cheer rose from those assembled on the porch of the neighbour's house.
The two-bedroom cottage comes with a front porch.
He is hoping that folks on his mail route leave their porch lights on tonight.
We added a front porch with a wooden deck with exposed wooden support columns.
He now just sits on the porch of his farm.

definitions.en: the quality of being light and small enough to be easily carried or moved

examples.en: The advantage of the smaller model is its greater portability.
examples.zh: 较小型号的优势在于其更大的便携性。

examples.en: Campers like food pouches for their portability and long shelf lives.
examples.zh: 露营者喜欢食品袋的便携性和较长的保质期。

examples.en: There is a need for pension portability, to enable workers to take whatever pension credits they earn from job to job.
examples.zh: 养老金的可转移性是很有必要的，这样工人在换工作的时候能够带走他们已经获得的养老金积分。

examples.en: Health-savings accounts have been touted for their portability from job to job.
examples.zh: 健康储蓄账户的一大卖点是可以随工作转移。

examples.en: A projector's portability is important when you're carrying it through airport security.
Business travelers like the portability of jump drives like these.
Until recently, pension portability wasn't a big issue because so many teachers started and finished their careers in one system.
Number portability allows customers to keep their cellphone number when they change carriers.

definitions.en: a large, thin case used for carrying drawings, documents, etc.

examples.en: She's trying to build up a portfolio of work to show during job interviews.
examples.zh: 她正想整理出自己的代表作以便找工作面试时展示一下。

definitions.en: a collection of company shares and other investments that are owned by a particular person or organization
a particular job or area of responsibility of a member of a government
definitions.zh: （部长或大臣）职务;（部长或大臣）职责

examples.en: The prime minister offered her the foreign affairs portfolio.
examples.zh: 首相邀请她出任外交部长。

examples.en: He knows that life will not be simple when he takes up his post as minister without portfolio in the cabinet.

definitions.en: a part or share of something larger

examples.en: A large/major portion of the company's profit goes straight back into new projects.
examples.zh: 公司利润的一大部分／主要部分又直接投入了新的项目。

examples.en: I accept my portion of the blame.
examples.zh: 我承担部分责任。

examples.en: The portions are very generous in this restaurant.
examples.zh: 这家饭店每份菜量都很足。

examples.en: She donated a sizeable portion of her riches to children's charities.
A good rule of thumb is that a portion of rice is two and a half handfuls.
Just a modest portion for me, please.
The school would only receive a very small portion of the profits.
A very large portion of our income goes to pay the mortgage.

definitions.en: a large case for carrying clothes while travelling, especially one that opens out into two parts
consisting of a wide range of things that are considered as a single thing

examples.en: The Official Secrets Act was described as a piece of portmanteau legislation, covering everything from nuclear weapons to army boots.
examples.zh: 《官方保密法》被描述为一个内容庞杂的立法，涵盖了从核武器到军靴的一切东西。

definitions.en: to have or own something, or to have a particular quality

examples.en: I don't possess a single DVD (= I don't have even one DVD).
examples.zh: 我一张DVD光盘都没有。

examples.en: In the past the root of this plant was thought to possess magical powers.
examples.zh: 过去这种植物的根被认为具有神奇的力量。

examples.en: We're trying to bring out the artistic talents that many people possess without realizing it.
She had already sold everything of value that she possessed.
Ruth possessed great writing skills.
He was charged with possessing a fake passport.
"I'm arresting you on suspicion of illegally possessing drugs, " said the police officer.

definitions.en: (of a wish or an idea) to take control over a person's mind, making that person behave in a very strange way

examples.en: Whatever possessed him to wear that appalling jacket!
examples.zh: 他中了什么邪竟穿了那件怪异的夹克衫！

definitions.en: to delay an event and plan or decide that it should happen at a later date or time

examples.en: They decided to postpone their holiday until next year.
examples.zh: 他们决定将假期推迟到来年。

examples.en: We've had to postpone going to France because the children are ill.
examples.zh: 因为孩子们生病了，我们不得不推迟去法国的行程。

examples.en: The negotiations have been postponed indefinitely.
I was really angry when she asked me to postpone my trip to help her move house.
There's no point postponing the event just because the weather forecast is bad.
We decided to postpone the party until my parents had returned from America.
The exams have been postponed until next month.

definitions.en: solving problems in a sensible way that suits the conditions that really exist now, rather than obeying fixed theories, ideas, or rules
definitions.zh: 讲究实际的，重实效的;实用主义的

examples.en: In business, the pragmatic approach to problems is often more successful than an idealistic one.
examples.zh: 在商业活动中，解决问题用实际的方法往往比空想更有成效。

definitions.en: in a pragmatic way
definitions.zh: 讲究实际地，重实效地;实用主义地

examples.en: It is intended that these guidelines should be applied flexibly and pragmatically.
examples.zh: 这些指导原则在执行时应灵活运用，讲究实效。

definitions.en: to express admiration or approval of the achievements or characteristics of a person or thing

examples.en: He should be praised for his honesty.
examples.zh: 他应该因为诚实而获得表扬。

examples.en: My parents always praised me when I did well at school.
examples.zh: 我在学校里表现好时父母总是夸奖我。

examples.en: He was highly praised for his research on heart disease.
examples.zh: 他因在心脏病方面的研究而受到高度赞扬。

examples.en: His friends praised his generosity, wit, and learning.
He praised her professionalism and dynamism.
The police praised motorists for driving sensibly in the appalling conditions.
The speech praised those who had displayed gallantry in the liberation of their country.
I tried to boost his ego by praising his cooking.

definitions.en: to honour, worship, and express admiration for a god

examples.en: They sang hymns praising God.
examples.zh: 他们唱颂歌来赞颂上帝。

definitions.en: things that you say that express your admiration and approval of someone or something

examples.en: They deserve praise for all their hard work.
examples.zh: 他们如此辛苦地工作，理应受到表扬。

examples.en: His economic policies have won widespread praise for reducing government debt.
examples.zh: 他的经济政策因减少了政府债务而受到广泛赞誉。

examples.en: Praise from Adrian is (high) praise indeed. (= praise from him is particularly special because he rarely praises anyone.)
examples.zh: 能得到阿德里安的表扬可真是不容易。

examples.en: His boss showered him with praise.
The committee lavished praise on the project.
He is sparing with his praise .
His efforts are certainly deserving of praise.
A young child needs plenty of praise and encouragement.

definitions.en: an expression of respect and worship to a god

examples.en: As we give praise to God, let us remember those who are less fortunate than ourselves.
examples.zh: 在我们赞颂上帝时，让我们缅怀那些没我们幸运的人。

definitions.en: an action that is done to prevent something unpleasant or dangerous happening

examples.en: Many people have been stockpiling food as a precaution against shortages.
examples.zh: 为了预防食物短缺，很多人一直在大量储备食品。

examples.en: They failed to take the necessary precautions to avoid infection.
examples.zh: 他们未能采取必要的预防措施来避免感染。

examples.en: If you're going to have sex, make sure you take precautions.
examples.zh: 发生性行为一定要采取避孕措施。

examples.en: The inquiry into the disaster damns the company for its lack of safety precautions.
Safety precautions are observed as a matter of course.
Millions of travellers fail to take even the most elementary of precautions.
Doctors are legally obliged to take certain precautions.
The tighter security precautions include video cameras in the city centre.

definitions.en: to be or go before something or someone in time or space
definitions.zh: （指时间或空间上）处在…之前，先于

examples.en: Kofi Annan preceded Ban Ki-moon as the Secretary-General of the UN.
examples.zh: 科菲•安南在潘基文之前担任联合国秘书长。

examples.en: It would be helpful if you were to precede the report with an introduction.
examples.zh: 你给这份报告加上一段引言会更好些。

examples.en: This evening's talk will be preceded by a brief introduction from Mr Appleby.
Verbs usually precede objects in English.
The attacks were preceded by a period of unrest in the capital city.
He preceded Dennis Healey as the Chancellor of the Exchequer.
I'm not sure if I fully understand the sentence which precedes this one.

definitions.en: exact and accurate

examples.en: The bunker's precise location is a closely guarded secret.
examples.zh: 掩体的确切位置严格保密。

examples.en: He caught me at the precise moment that I fainted.
examples.zh: 他正好在我晕倒的那一刻抓住了我。

examples.en: There was a good turnout for the meeting - twelve of us to be precise.
examples.zh: 出席会议的人不少——确切地说有我们12个人。

examples.en: Let's not trouble ourselves about the precise details at the moment.
I can give no information at this precise moment in time.
We don't know the precise details of the story yet.
Machine-readable passports will permit precise identity-checking.
Do you think you could be a bit more precise?

definitions.en: very careful and accurate, especially about small details
definitions.zh: （尤指在细节上）严谨的，细致的

examples.en: Years of doing meticulous research had made her very precise in her working methods.
examples.zh: 多年来一丝不苟的研究使她养成了严谨的工作方法。

definitions.en: the quality of being exact

examples.en: Great precision is required to align the mirrors accurately.
examples.zh: 校准反光镜需要极高的精确性。

examples.en: Precision bombing was used to destroy enemy airbases and armaments factories.
examples.zh: 使用了精确轰炸来摧毁敌人的空军基地和军工厂。

definitions.en: the qualities of being careful and accurate

examples.en: His books are a pleasure to read because he writes with such clarity and precision.
examples.zh: 他的书读起来赏心悦目，因为他的写作清晰而又严谨。

definitions.en: to have existed or happened before another thing

examples.en: These cave paintings predate any others which are known.
examples.zh: 这些洞穴绘画早于其他任何已知的同类绘画。

definitions.en: to kill and eat another animal

examples.en: Some species of bat predate small mammals.
examples.zh: 某些种类的蝙蝠捕食小型哺乳动物。

examples.en: Possums will predate on baby birds and eggs.
examples.zh: 负鼠掠食小鸟的鸟蛋。

definitions.en: someone who had a job or a position before someone else, or something that comes before another thing in time or in a series
definitions.zh: 前任，前辈;原有事物，前身

examples.en: My predecessor worked in this job for twelve years.
examples.zh: 我的前任做这份工作做了12年。

examples.en: The latest Ferrari is not only faster than its predecessors but also more comfortable.
examples.zh: 最新款的法拉利赛车不仅比老款的更快，而且更舒适。

examples.en: Each new leader would blame his predecessor for all the evils of the past.
In her speech, she paid a glowing tribute to her predecessor.
She spoke scathingly of the poor standard of work done by her predecessor.
You need to learn from the mistakes of your predecessors.
This system is much more efficient than its predecessor.

definitions.en: to say that an event or action will happen in the future, especially as a result of knowledge or experience

examples.en: It's still not possible to accurately predict the occurrence of earthquakes.
examples.zh: 现在仍无法准确预报地震的发生。

examples.en: Who could have predicted that within ten years he'd be in charge of the whole company?
examples.zh: 谁能预料到10年内他会掌管整个公司？

examples.en: The hurricane is predicted to reach the coast tomorrow morning.
examples.zh: 飓风预计将于明天早晨登陆。

examples.en: No one can predict when the disease will strike again.
examples.zh: 没人能预见这种病什么时候会再次爆发。

examples.en: We can predict changes in climate with a surprising degree of accuracy.
I cannot predict what will happen next year.
The papers are predicting that the prime minister will call an election in the spring.
Heavy snowfalls are predicted for tonight and tomorrow.
It was predicted that a comet would collide with one of the planets.

definitions.en: to do or say something before someone so that you make their words or actions unnecessary or not effective
definitions.zh: 抢在…之前说话（或行动）;预先制止

examples.en: The minister held a press conference in order to pre-empt criticism in the newspapers.
examples.zh: 部长召开了记者招待会，旨在先发制人，预先防备各报纸的指责批评。

examples.en: You need to be prepared and preempt the impact of the change before it happens.
All the networks pre-empted their regular schedules to broadcast news of the hijacking.
examples.zh: 所有的电视网都取消了安排好的节目，转而播放劫机的新闻。

examples.en: For several days, this story preempted all others in the news schedules.

definitions.en: If something is pre-emptive, it is done before other people can act, especially to prevent them from doing something else.
definitions.zh: 抢先的；（尤指）先发制人的

examples.en: The Treasury has decided to raise interest rates as a pre-emptive measure against inflation.
examples.zh: 财政部决定加息，以此作为一项防止通货膨胀的预防性措施。

examples.en: The prime minister authorized a pre-emptive air strike against the rebels.
examples.zh: 首相批准了对叛乱分子进行空袭，先发制人。

examples.en: We strongly oppose this misguided preemptive war.

definitions.en: coming before a more important action or event, especially introducing or preparing for it

examples.en: Preliminary results show that the vaccine is effective, but this has to be confirmed by further medical trials.
examples.zh: 初查结果表明疫苗是有效的，但这有待于进一步医学实验的证实。

examples.en: We've decided to change the design based on our preliminary findings.
examples.zh: 我们决定依据初步调查的结果对设计方案进行更改。

examples.en: The preliminary results are very positive.
Our preliminary findings suggest an increased risk for those aged over 60.
Jackson is performing well in the preliminary stages of the competition.
The two officials will be meeting today in order to establish a preliminary agreement.
I'd like to make a few preliminary remarks before we begin the discussion.

definitions.en: an event or action that introduces or prepares for something else

examples.en: After a few polite preliminaries (= introductions), we stated our main ideas and intentions.
examples.zh: 几句开场白后，我们陈述了我方的主要观点和意图。

examples.en: The French team finished first in the competition preliminaries (= the first part of the competition).
examples.zh: 法国队在预赛中名列第一。

definitions.en: happening or done too soon, especially before the natural or suitable time
definitions.zh: 过早的;不成熟的;仓促的

examples.en: premature birth/death
examples.zh: 早产／夭折

examples.en: a premature baby
examples.zh: 早产儿

examples.en: Their criticisms seem premature considering that the results aren't yet known.
examples.zh: 鉴于结果尚未知晓，他们的批评似乎为时过早。

definitions.en: something that must exist or happen before something else can exist or happen
definitions.zh: 先决条件，前提，必备条件

examples.en: Passing a written test is a prerequisite for taking the advanced course.
examples.zh: 通过笔试是学习高级课程的前提。

examples.en: Public support is a prerequisite for/to the success of this project.
examples.zh: 公众支持是这一计划取得成功的必备条件。

examples.en: They had to agree to certain conditions as a prerequisite of being lent the money.
examples.zh: 他们不得不同意一些条件才能借到钱。

definitions.en: (of a doctor) to say what medical treatment someone should have

examples.en: The drug is often prescribed for ulcers.
examples.zh: 这种药通常是开来治疗溃疡的。

examples.en: I've been prescribed painkillers.
examples.zh: 医生给我开了些止痛药。

examples.en: The doctor prescribed some pills.
The doctor prescribed some medicine and told her to have a week's rest.
I hoped he would prescribe some stronger pain killers.
Is there anything stronger you can prescribe?
He wouldn't prescribe anything - just recommended rest.

definitions.en: to tell someone what they must have or do, or to make a rule of something

examples.en: Penalties for not paying taxes are prescribed by law.
examples.zh: 法律上规定了不交税的惩罚措施。

examples.en: The law prescribes that all children must go to school.
examples.zh: 法律规定所有孩子必须上学。

examples.en: Grammatical rules prescribe how words may be used together.
examples.zh: 语法规则对单词如何搭配使用作出了规定。

definitions.en: something that you are given, without asking for it, on a special occasion, especially to show friendship, or to say thank you
definitions.zh: （尤指表示友谊或致谢的）礼物，赠品

examples.en: a birthday/Christmas/wedding present
examples.zh: 生日／圣诞／结婚礼物

examples.en: They gave me theatre tickets as a present.
examples.zh: 他们送给我戏票作为礼物。

examples.en: We're having a collection for Tom's retirement present.
They got an entire set of silver cutlery as a wedding present.
I was looking for a birthday present for my mother but I didn't find anything suitable.
Aren't you going to unwrap your presents?
The children squealed in delight when they saw all the presents under the Christmas tree.

definitions.en: the period of time that is happening now, not the past or the future

examples.en: That's all for the present.
examples.zh: 暂且就讲这些。

examples.en: in the present The play is set in the present.
examples.zh: 这出戏以当代为背景。

examples.en: The verb in this sentence is in the present.
examples.zh: 这个句子中的动词是现在时。

examples.en: "Are you busy?" "Not at present."
examples.zh: “你忙吗？”“这会儿不忙。”

examples.en: I'm usually too immersed in the present to worry about the future.
Let's talk about the present.

definitions.en: in a particular place

examples.en: The whole family was present.
examples.zh: 全家人都在场。

examples.en: There were no children present.
examples.zh: 没有孩子在场。

examples.en: Mind your language - there are ladies present!
Is it necessary for all of us to be present at the meeting this afternoon?
Melanin is the dark brown pigment of the hair, skin, and eyes that is present in varying amounts in every human being.
More men are present at the births of their children these days.
There ought to be an adult present at all times, when there are young children in the pool.

definitions.en: happening or existing now

examples.en: I don't have her present address.
examples.zh: 我不知道她现在的地址。

examples.en: Please state your present occupation and salary.
examples.zh: 请说出你目前的职业和薪水。

examples.en: Charlie has a clever plan for getting us out of our present difficulties.
Unlike the present government, we believe in serving the community.
The present system of payment will remain in effect until the end of the rental agreement.
The present voting system distorts the wishes of the electorate.
Are you feeling frustrated in your present job?

definitions.en: to give, provide, or make something known

examples.en: The winners were presented with medals.
examples.zh: 获奖者被授予奖牌。

examples.en: The letter presented the family with a problem that would be difficult to solve.
examples.zh: 这封信给家人提出了一个难以解决的问题。

examples.en: The documentary presented us with a balanced view of the issue.
examples.zh: 这部纪录片向我们展示了对这个问题的公允看法。

examples.en: He presented the report to his colleagues at the meeting.
examples.zh: 他在会上向他的同事们宣读了这份报告。

examples.en: The classroom presented a cheerful busy atmosphere to the visitors (= appeared to them to have this).
examples.zh: 该教室向来访者呈现出一种欢快忙碌的气氛。

examples.en: The school is presenting (= performing) "West Side Story" as its end-of-term production.
examples.zh: 该校正在作期末表演：音乐剧“西区故事”。

examples.en: He has a lot more work to do before he can present the scheme to the public.
Ruth was astonished when he presented her with an engagement ring.
She presented a well-argued case for the banning of smoking in public places.
She graciously accepted the flowers that were presented to her.
The Duchess of Kent will be presenting the trophies.

definitions.en: to introduce a television or radio show

examples.en: She presents the late-night news.
examples.zh: 她主播深夜新闻。

examples.en: May I present Professor Carter?
examples.zh: 我可以向大家介绍卡特教授吗？

examples.en: Later on I'd like to present you to the headteacher.
examples.zh: 稍后我想把你介绍给校长。

examples.en: He presented himself at the doctor's at 9.30 a.m. as arranged.
examples.zh: 按照安排，他于上午9点30分到医生那里就诊。

examples.en: You should present yourselves at the front desk at 8 o'clock in the morning.
He presented himself to the Criminal Tribunal in The Hague.
She presented herself at the abbey to begin her life as a nun.
I presented myself to the supervisor, eager to begin work.
An opportunity suddenly presented itself.
examples.zh: 一个机会突然出现了。

examples.en: When a challenge to his authority presented itself, he dealt with it firmly.
She opted for cash over comfort whenever the choice presented itself.
I would be open to taking up a directorship should the right opportunity present itself.
After a couple of successful years, a new hurdle presented itself.
I'd like to present my grandson, Jackson Junior.
He was presented to the Queen and given a knighthood.
She used to present one of those holiday programmes but now she reads the news.
May I present Sir Bob Geldof?
What was that documentary called that she used to present?

definitions.en: to see a doctor, etc. when showing particular signs of an illness or medical condition

examples.en: Children presenting with acute respiratory infections were referred for a chest X-ray.
examples.zh: 出现急性呼吸道感染的儿童被送去做胸部X光检查。

examples.en: When patients present with chest pain, clinical examination and patient history are fundamental to determine the probable cause of pain.
Medical conditions might present differently during pregnancy.
Bone cancer generally presents as pain in the area of the tumour.
examples.zh: 骨癌一般表现为肿瘤区域的疼痛。

definitions.en: used to say what you think is the likely situation

examples.en: They can presumably afford to buy a bigger apartment.
examples.zh: 他们大概买得起一套大一点的公寓。

examples.en: Presumably he just forgot to send the letter.
examples.zh: 他可能是忘了寄信。

examples.en: Presumably they've decided to move back to Australia.
Presumably they can't afford a new car, or they'd have bought one by now.
You're going to go with her, presumably?
Her mother will presumably stay with them until she's made a full recovery.
You've bought all the materials you need, presumably?

definitions.en: to believe something to be true because it is very likely, although you are not certain

examples.en: I presume (that) they're not coming, since they haven't replied to the invitation.
examples.zh: 鉴于他们没有回复邀请，我认为他们不会来了。

examples.en: You are Dr Smith, I presume?
examples.zh: 我想你是史密斯博士吧？

examples.en: "Are we walking to the hotel?" "I presume not/so."
examples.zh: “我们走着去旅馆吗？”“我想不是／是的。”

examples.en: The boat's captain is missing, presumed dead (= it is believed that he is dead).
examples.zh: 这条船的船长失踪了，估计是死了。

examples.en: In British law, you are presumed innocent until you are proved guilty.
examples.zh: 按英国法律，在证明你有罪前，假定你是无辜的。

examples.en: The universe is presumed to contain many other planets with some form of life.
examples.zh: 据推测，宇宙中很多其他的行星上有某种形式的生命存在。

examples.en: You were sniffing a lot - I presumed you had a cold.
She said it in a whisper so I presumed it wasn't common knowledge.
"Is he coming?" "I presume so."
Under the Bill of Rights, a person is presumed innocent until proved otherwise.
A person accused of a crime is presumed innocent until proven guilty.

definitions.en: to do something although you know that you do not have a right to do it

examples.en: I wouldn't presume to tell you how to do your job, but shouldn't this piece go there?
examples.zh: 我不想冒昧地教你该如何工作，但这个零件不是应该装在那里吗？

examples.en: I don't wish to presume (= make a suggestion although I have no right to), but don't you think you should apologize to her?
examples.zh: 恕我冒昧，但你难道不觉得应该向她道歉吗？

examples.en: He presumes on her good nature (= takes unfair advantage of it).
examples.zh: 他利用了她的善良本性。

definitions.en: to behave as if something is true when you know that it is not, especially in order to deceive people or as a game

examples.en: He pretended (that) he didn't mind, but I knew that he did.
examples.zh: 他假装不在乎，但我知道他很介意。

examples.en: The children pretended (that) they were dinosaurs.
examples.zh: 孩子们装扮成恐龙。

examples.en: Were you just pretending to be interested?
examples.zh: 你只是在假装感兴趣吗？

examples.en: She's not really hurt - she's only pretending.
examples.zh: 她并不是真的受了伤——她只是在装。

examples.en: Of course I was angry - I can't pretend otherwise.
examples.zh: 我当然生气了——怎么可能会是装的。

examples.en: I don't pretend to be an expert on the subject.
examples.zh: 我不想妄称自己是该学科的专家。

examples.en: She'll pretend that she hasn't seen us - you watch.
Just for a laugh, I pretended that I'd forgotten it was his birthday.
He isn't really angry - he's just pretending.
She sidled past him, pretending that she had not seen him.
He even tried to pretend he was a film star's son - can you believe it?

definitions.en: imaginary or not real

examples.en: "Do you want a cup of tea?" she asks, offering me a pretend cup.
They knew the argument was only pretend, but they still got upset.
examples.zh: 他们知道这些争执是假装的，但还是觉得难过。

definitions.en: existing very commonly or happening often

examples.en: These diseases are more prevalent among young children.
examples.zh: 这些疾病在幼儿中更普遍。

examples.en: Trees are dying in areas where acid rain is most prevalent.
examples.zh: 在酸雨非常严重的地区，树木正面临枯死。

definitions.en: to stop something from happening or someone from doing something

examples.en: Label your suitcases to prevent confusion.
examples.zh: 在你的手提箱上贴上标签以防弄混。

examples.en: His disability prevents him (from) driving.
examples.zh: 他有残疾，不能开车。

examples.en: The police seem to be powerless to prevent these attacks.
She would go over the accident again and again in her mind, wishing that she could somehow have prevented it.
A block in the pipe was preventing the water from coming through.
Fierce winds prevented the race from taking place.
The law has done little to prevent racial discrimination and inequality.

definitions.en: an advantage that only one person or group of people has, usually because of their position or because they are rich
definitions.zh: （特定个体或群体的）特权，特别待遇

examples.en: Healthcare should be a right, not a privilege.
examples.zh: 医疗保健应该是权利，而非特权。

examples.en: Senior management enjoy certain privileges, such as company cars and health insurance.
examples.zh: 高级管理层享受公司配车和私人医疗保健等特权。

examples.en: I had the privilege of interviewing Picasso in the 1960s.
examples.zh: 我在20世纪60年代很荣幸地采访到了毕加索。

examples.en: It was a real privilege to meet her.
examples.zh: 见到她真是莫大的荣幸。

examples.en: a life of privilege
My thesis examines the role of societal privilege in the maintenance of health disparities.
Some men will even resort to violence to try to enforce and protect their male privilege.
diplomatic/parliamentary privilege
examples.zh: 外交／议会言论特权

examples.en: The leaders were often more concerned with status and privilege than with the problems of the people.
There are no special privileges for the managers.
It was a privilege to work with her and she will be sadly missed.
Frequent fliers receive travel privileges.
an abuse/misuse of privilege

definitions.en: taking action by causing change and not only reacting to change when it happens

examples.en: Companies are going to have to be more proactive about environmental management.
examples.zh: 公司将对环境管理采取更为积极主动的态度。

examples.en: a proactive approach/role
examples.zh: 积极的态度／作用

definitions.en: in a way that causes change by taking action, rather than reacting to changes

examples.en: We continue to work proactively with the authorities to identify any suspicious activity.
examples.zh: 我们积极配合当局排查任何可疑行为。

examples.en: The government must plan proactively instead of simply reacting to events.
examples.zh: 政府必须主动做规划而不只是应付各种状况。

examples.en: We must act proactively to ensure that passengers have the highest level of safety.
You need to make sure that you're proactively involved.
You should be ready to proactively take advantage of new opportunities.

definitions.en: full of problems or difficulties

examples.en: Getting everyone there on time might prove problematic.
examples.zh: 让每个人都准时到达可能会很难。

definitions.en: the fact of having the skill and experience for doing something

examples.en: The job ad said they wanted proficiency in at least two languages.
examples.zh: 招聘广告说他们希望至少精通两种语言。

examples.en: Photographs entered in the contest will be judged on originality and technical proficiency.
examples.zh: 参加比赛的照片将根据原创性和技术水平进行评判。

examples.en: The state will devote more money to helping students achieve proficiency in mathematics and reading.
Some providers recommended strategies for students with limited English proficiency.
He is self-taught to a high degree of proficiency.

definitions.en: felt or experienced very strongly or in an extreme way

examples.en: His mother's death when he was aged six had a very profound effect on him.
examples.zh: 他6岁时母亲的过世对他的影响很大。

examples.en: The invention of the contraceptive pill brought about profound changes in the lives of women.
examples.zh: 避孕药的发明给妇女的生活带来了深刻的变化。

examples.en: Those two lines of poetry express perfectly the profound sadness of loss.
examples.zh: 那两行诗很好地表达了失去亲人的深切悲痛。

examples.en: My grandfather has a profound mistrust of anything new or foreign.
examples.zh: 我祖父对任何新生或外来事物有一种根深蒂固的不信任。

examples.en: There was a note of profound irritation in his voice.
examples.zh: 他的语气中带有强烈的恼怒情绪。

definitions.en: showing a clear and deep understanding of serious matters

examples.en: profound truths/wisdom
examples.zh: 深奥的真理／博大的智慧

examples.en: The review that I read said that it was "a thoughtful and profound film".
examples.zh: 我看过的评论说它是一部“有创意、有深度的电影”。

examples.en: "Dying is easy - it's living that's the problem." "That was very profound of you, Steven."
examples.zh: “死很容易——活下去才是难题。”“史蒂文，你的话真深奥！”

definitions.en: gradually

examples.en: My eyesight has got progressively worse over the years.
examples.zh: 这些年来我的视力越来越差了。

definitions.en: to officially refuse to allow something

examples.en: Motor vehicles are prohibited from driving in the town centre.
examples.zh: 机动车辆禁止在市中心行驶。

examples.en: The government introduced a law prohibiting tobacco advertisements on TV.
examples.zh: 政府颁布了一项法律： 禁止在电视上播放烟草广告。

examples.en: Parking is strictly prohibited between these gates.
examples.zh: 这些大门之间严禁停车。

examples.en: The loudness of the music prohibits serious conversation in most nightclubs.
examples.zh: 大多数夜总会里音乐声嘈杂，根本没法进行严肃的谈话。

examples.en: Intermarriage between close relatives is prohibited in most societies.
Smoking is prohibited in the building.
A law has recently been passed prohibiting the consumption of alcohol in public places.
The government prohibits the passage of foreign troops and planes across its territory.
The law specifically prohibits acts of this kind.

definitions.en: a calculation or guess about the future based on information that you have

examples.en: The company has failed to achieve last year's sales projections by 30 percent.
examples.zh: 这家公司未能达到去年的预期销售目标，还差30%。

examples.en: Spending was well below projections.

definitions.en: the act of projecting a film or an image onto a screen or wall, or the film or image itself

examples.en: A few chairs and rear projections supplied the scene.

definitions.en: something that projects from a surface or above the edge of something
a drawing or image that represents a line, figure, or solid shown on a particular plane (= a flat or level surface that continues in all directions)

examples.en: CT scans can be reformatted in multiple projections, regardless of the original plane of scanning.

definitions.en: a part that comes at the beginning of a play, story, or long poem, often giving information about events that happened before the time when the play, story, or poem begins

examples.en: A series of internal struggles was the prologue to full-scale civil war.
examples.zh: 一系列内部斗争是爆发全面内战的序幕。

definitions.en: to make something last a longer time

examples.en: We were having such a good time that we decided to prolong our stay by another week.
examples.zh: 我们过得非常愉快，决定再多呆一个星期。

examples.en: She chewed each delicious mouthful as slowly as she could, prolonging the pleasure.
examples.zh: 她细嚼慢咽，尽情地享受每一口美味食品。

definitions.en: very well known and important

examples.en: a prominent Democrat
examples.zh: 民主党的重要人物

examples.en: a prominent member of the Saudi royal family
examples.zh: 沙特阿拉伯王室的重要成员

examples.en: The government should be playing a more prominent role in promoting human rights.
examples.zh: 政府应该在促进人权方面发挥更重要的作用。

examples.en: It's worrying that such a prominent politician is so easily led.
She is likely to play a prominent part in the forthcoming election campaign.
Lady Thatcher remained a prominent political figure.

definitions.en: sticking out from a surface

examples.en: She has a prominent chin/nose.
examples.zh: 她的下巴翘得厉害／鼻子很高。

examples.en: New books are displayed in a prominent position on tables at the front of the store.
examples.zh: 新书陈列在商店前面桌子上的一个显眼位置。

examples.en: The shop front occupies a very prominent position on the main street.
His photo stood in a prominent place on her desk.

definitions.en: the fact of being promiscuous
to encourage people to like, buy, use, do, or support something

examples.en: Advertising companies are always having to think up new ways to promote products.
examples.zh: 广告公司总是得想出新办法来促销。

examples.en: The Institute is intended to promote an understanding of the politics and culture of the Arab world.
examples.zh: 该协会旨在促进人们对阿拉伯世界政治文化的了解。

examples.en: Greenpeace works to promote awareness of the dangers that threaten our planet today.
examples.zh: 绿色和平组织致力于促使人们了解当前威胁地球的危机。

examples.en: It has long been known that regular exercise promotes all-round good health.
examples.zh: 经常锻炼能促进全面健康，这一点已久为人知。

examples.en: The government should be playing a more prominent role in promoting human rights.
The organization is working with young people to promote awareness of the dangers of unprotected sex.
The band are currently touring to promote their new album.
The city is trying to lose its industrial image and promote itself as a tourist centre.
Cutting the bush back in the autumn will help promote growth in the spring.

definitions.en: to raise someone to a higher or more important position or rank

examples.en: If I'm not promoted within the next two years, I'm going to change jobs.
examples.zh: 如果我在今后两年内得不到提升，我就换工作。

examples.en: She's just been promoted to senior sales rep.
examples.zh: 她刚刚被提升为高级销售代表。

examples.en: If Coventry City win this match, they'll be promoted to the Premier League.
examples.zh: 如果考文垂市队赢得了这场比赛，他们将晋升英超联赛。

examples.en: It is my considered opinion that he should be promoted.
He's deluding himself if he thinks he's going to be promoted this year.
She was very demotivated by being told she had little chance of being promoted.
What's her official designation now she's been promoted?
She has been promoted from deputy manager to senior manager.

definitions.en: quickly, without delay, or at the arranged time
definitions.zh: 迅速地，立即地;依照预定时间地

examples.en: We'll have to leave fairly promptly (= on time) if we want to catch that train.
examples.zh: 如果我们要赶那趟火车，就得准时出发。

examples.en: We try to answer readers' letters as promptly (= quickly) as we can.
examples.zh: 我们努力尽快答复读者来信。

examples.en: She promised she'd keep it secret and promptly (= immediately after) went and told Dad!
examples.zh: 她答应了要保密，随后却立马去告诉了爸爸！

examples.en: She came promptly on the dot of eleven.
The senator's weekend utterances were promptly rebutted by three of his colleagues on Monday.
The emergency services arrived promptly at the scene.

definitions.en: to produce a new plant using a parent plant

examples.en: Most house plants can be propagated from stem cuttings.
examples.zh: 大多数家养植物都能由切下来的茎繁殖。

examples.en: Plants need certain conditions to propagate.
examples.zh: 植物需要一定的条件才能繁殖。

examples.en: The female crab needs to be saved to propagate the species.

definitions.en: to spread opinions, lies, or beliefs among a lot of people

examples.en: The government have tried to propagate the belief that this is a just war.
examples.zh: 政府竭力宣扬这是一场正义之战。

examples.en: Such lies are propagated in the media.
examples.zh: 这样的谎言通过媒体传播开来。

definitions.en: to push or move something somewhere, often with a lot of force

examples.en: a rocket propelled through space
examples.zh: 在推力作用下穿越外层空间的火箭

examples.en: The Kon-Tiki sailed across the Pacific Ocean propelled by wind power.
examples.zh: “太阳神康提基号”帆船借助风力横渡了太平洋。

examples.en: The film propelled him to international stardom.
examples.zh: 这部电影使他成为了国际巨星。

definitions.en: If two amounts are proportional, they change at the same rate so that the relationship between them does not change.

examples.en: Weight is proportional to size.
examples.zh: 重量与大小是成比例的。

examples.en: How far you go is directly proportional to how fast you go (= as one thing increases, the other increases at exactly the same rate).
examples.zh: 走多远与走多快成正比（=一方增值，另一方亦以完全相同的比率增值）。

examples.en: There's a false idea that physical prowess and intellectual ability are inversely proportional (= as one gets bigger, the other gets smaller).
examples.zh: 有一种误解认为体力和智力成反比（=一个变大，另一个就变小）。

definitions.en: a suggestion, sometimes a written one

examples.en: Congress has rejected the latest economic proposal put forward by the President.
examples.zh: 议会否决了总统最近提出的经济计划。

examples.en: There has been an angry reaction to the government's proposal to reduce unemployment benefit.
examples.zh: 政府削减失业救济金的计划遭到了强烈的反对。

examples.en: Have you read Steve's proposals for the new project?
examples.zh: 你看了史蒂夫对新项目的建议了吗？

examples.en: There was anger at the proposal that a UN peacekeeping force should be sent to the area.
examples.zh: 有人对向该地区派遣联合国维和部队的建议很恼火。

examples.en: 50 people voted against the new proposal.
The members are agreed that the proposal should be rejected.
They declared their support for the proposal
I'll send you a fax with the details of the proposal.
The management has refused to back our proposals.

definitions.en: to offer or suggest a possible plan or action for other people to consider

examples.en: I propose that we wait until the budget has been announced before committing ourselves to any expenditure.
examples.zh: 我建议我们在预算没有宣布以前不要有任何支出。

examples.en: He proposed dealing directly with the suppliers.
examples.zh: 他提议直接和供应商打交道。

examples.en: She proposed a boycott of the meeting.
examples.zh: 她建议抵制这次会议。

examples.en: He proposed a motion that the chairman resign.
examples.zh: 他提出了一项让主席辞职的动议。

examples.en: To be nominated for union president you need one person to propose you and another to second you.
examples.zh: 要被提名为工会主席，你需要有一个推荐人和一个附议人。

examples.en: I remember the night your father proposed to me.
examples.zh: 我记得你父亲向我求婚的那个晚上。

examples.en: propose a toast to Now, if you'd all please raise your glasses, I'd like to propose a toast to the bride and groom.
examples.zh: 现在，请大家举起酒杯，让我们为新郎和新娘干杯。

examples.en: Someone proposed a motion to increase the membership fee to £500 a year.
Critics of the president have been pouring scorn on the plan ever since it was first proposed.
Now, if you'd all please raise your glasses, I'd like to propose a toast to the bride and groom.
She proposes an exchange of contracts at two o'clock.
What the government is proposing encroaches on the rights of individuals.

definitions.en: to intend to do something

examples.en: How do you propose to complete the project in such a short time?
examples.zh: 你打算怎样在这么短的时间内完成这个项目？

examples.en: How do you propose tackling this problem?
examples.zh: 你打算怎么解决这个问题？

examples.en: I do not propose to reveal details at this stage.
examples.zh: 我不打算在这个阶段透露细节。

examples.en: What we are proposing is a radical change in approach.
examples.zh: 我们打算在方法上来个彻底转变。

definitions.en: people who are expected to buy something, employ someone, become parents, etc.
definitions.zh: 潜在的客户／可能成为雇主的人／即将为人父母者等

examples.en: We've had three sets of prospective buyers looking at the house.
examples.zh: 已经有三批可能买我们房子的人来看过房子了。

examples.en: Given the fact that a prospective student is bombarded by prospectuses, selecting a suitable course is not easy.
Her father always wants to meet her prospective boyfriends.

definitions.en: to stick out from or through something

examples.en: A rotting branch protruded from the swamp like a ghostly arm.
examples.zh: 一根腐烂的树枝像鬼的手臂一样从沼泽里伸出来。

examples.en: protruding ears/teeth
examples.zh: 招风耳／龅牙

definitions.en: for the present time but likely to change

examples.en: a provisional government
examples.zh: 临时政府

examples.en: These dates are only provisional.
examples.zh: 这些日期只是暂定的。

definitions.en: to cut off branches from a tree, bush, or plant, especially so that it will grow better in the future

examples.en: She spent the afternoon pruning roses.
examples.zh: 她用下午的时间修剪了玫瑰。

examples.en: Arco has reacted to the loss in revenue by pruning (back) its expansion plans.
examples.zh: 大西洋里奇菲尔德公司通过缩减扩张计划以应对收益亏损。

examples.en: I felt his essay needed a little pruning.
examples.zh: 我认为他的文章需要稍作删节。

definitions.en: a dried plum
a piece of equipment that is used to cause liquid, air, or gas to move from one place to another

examples.en: a water/bicycle/fuel pump
examples.zh: 水泵／自行车打气筒／油泵

examples.en: a petrolpump
examples.zh: 汽油泵

examples.en: The pump is powered by a small electric motor.
Fish tanks often have a pump that oxygenates the water.
The pump is driven by steam.

definitions.en: a type of flat shoe, like a ballet dancer's shoe, worn by women
to force liquid or gas to move somewhere

examples.en: Our latest machine can pump a hundred gallons a minute.
examples.zh: 我们最新的机器每分钟能抽100加仑液体。

examples.en: The new wine is pumped into storage tanks.
examples.zh: 新酿的酒被抽送到储藏罐里。

examples.en: The heart pumps blood through the arteries/round the body.
examples.zh: 心脏通过动脉输送血液／将血液输送到全身。

definitions.en: to keep asking someone for information, especially in a way that is not direct

examples.en: She was pumping me for details of the new project.
examples.zh: 她在套问我有关新项目的详细情况。

definitions.en: a forceful hit with a fist (= closed hand)

examples.en: She gave him a punch on the nose.
examples.zh: 她朝他的鼻子打了一拳。

examples.en: The boxer was felled by a punch to the head.
He threw a punch at his attacker.

definitions.en: the power to be interesting and have a strong effect on people

examples.en: I felt the performance/speech/presentation lacked punch.
examples.zh: 我觉得那个表演／演讲／演出缺乏感染力。

definitions.en: a cold or hot drink made by mixing fruit juices, pieces of fruit, and often wine or other alcoholic drinks
definitions.zh: 宾治酒，潘趣酒（一种果汁、水果与酒勾兑成的冷饮或热饮）

definitions.en: a piece of equipment that cuts holes in a material by pushing a piece of metal through it

examples.en: a ticket punch
examples.zh: 剪票钳

examples.en: Have you seen the hole punch anywhere?
examples.zh: 你看到打孔器在哪里了吗？

definitions.en: to hit someone or something with your fist (= closed hand)

examples.en: He punched him in the stomach.
examples.zh: 他朝他的肚子上猛击了一拳。

examples.en: He was punched in the face.
The two youths punched the old man and snatched his wallet.
The boys in the cartoon were punching each other - wham, zap!
He punched me on the nose.
She punched her pillow in frustration.

definitions.en: to make a hole in something with a special piece of equipment

examples.en: I was just punching holes in some sheets of paper.
examples.zh: 我刚才在给几张纸打孔。

examples.en: This belt's too big - I'll have to punch an extra hole in it.
examples.zh: 这条皮带太长了——我得在上面再打一个洞。

definitions.en: (the use of) special symbols that you add to writing to separate phrases and sentences to show that something is a question, etc.
definitions.zh: 标点符号;标点符号用法

examples.en: His letter was completely without punctuation.
examples.zh: 他的信里一个标点也没有。

examples.en: You'll be marked down for poor spelling and punctuation.
He had written the letter in appalling handwriting with almost no punctuation.

definitions.en: a long, narrow boat with a flat bottom and square ends, moved by a person standing at one end and pushing on the bottom of a river with a pole
a way of kicking the ball in rugby or American football, by dropping it from your hands and kicking it before it hits the ground, or a powerful kick in football that causes the ball to go a long way
to risk money by buying or supporting something, in the hope of making or winning more money:
definitions.zh: 花点钱投资在科技股上也许是个好主意。

examples.en: It might be a good idea to take a punt on a few technology stocks.
examples.zh: 花点钱投资在科技股上也许是个好主意。

definitions.en: the standard unit of money used in Ireland before the introduction of the euro
definitions.zh: 爱尔兰镑（爱尔兰使用欧元前所采用的货币单位）

definitions.en: (in rugby or American football) to kick the ball after you have dropped it from your hands and before it touches the ground, or (in football) to kick the ball powerfully so that it goes a long way
definitions.zh: （在橄榄球或美式橄榄球中）踢（悬空球）;（足球中）将（球）踢得很远

examples.en: We were running out of time, so we decided to punt the sightseeing and just go shopping.
examples.zh: 我们时间不多了，所以决定不去游览只去购物。

definitions.en: to travel in a punt

examples.en: We punted up the river.
examples.zh: 我们乘方头平底船沿河逆流而上。

examples.en: It's a glorious afternoon - let's go punting.
examples.zh: 今天下午天气真好，我们去撑船吧。

definitions.en: to get rid of people from an organization because you do not agree with them

examples.en: Party leaders have undertaken to purge the party of extremists.
examples.zh: 政党领袖已采取行动清除党内的极端分子。

examples.en: Hard-liners are expected to be purged from the administration.
examples.zh: 主张强硬路线的人预计将从政府中清除出去。

definitions.en: to get rid of something unwanted, harmful, or evil

examples.en: Roman Catholics go to confession to purge their souls/themselves (from/of sin).
examples.zh: 罗马天主教徒前去忏悔以净化灵魂／涤罪。

examples.en: The new state governor has promised to purge the police force of corruption.
examples.zh: 新州长已保证要清除警察队伍中的腐败现象。

definitions.en: the act of getting rid of people from an organization because you do not agree with them
definitions.zh: 清洗（从一个组织中清除持不同政见者的行动）

examples.en: Between 1934 and 1938, Stalin mounted a massive purge of the Communist Party, the government and the armed forces in the Soviet Union.
examples.zh: 1934至1938年间，斯大林对苏联的共产党、政府和军队实行大清洗。

definitions.en: an activity that you spend time doing, usually when you are not working

examples.en: I enjoy outdoor pursuits, like hiking and riding.
examples.zh: 我喜欢户外活动，比如徒步旅行和骑马。

examples.en: I don't have much opportunity for leisure pursuits these days.
examples.zh: 我近来没有多少时间可以花在业余爱好上。

definitions.en: the act of following someone or something to try to catch him, her, or it

examples.en: Three people have been killed in high-speed pursuits by the police recently.
examples.zh: 有3人在最近警察的高速追捕中毙命。

examples.en: The robbers fled the scene of the crime with the police in pursuit.
examples.zh: 抢劫者逃离了犯罪现场，警察紧追不舍。

definitions.en: the act of trying to achieve a plan, activity, or situation, usually over a long period of time

examples.en: the pursuit of happiness
examples.zh: 对幸福的追求

examples.en: The company is ruthless in its pursuit of profit.
examples.zh: 公司为追求利润不择手段。

examples.en: The union is on strike in pursuit of (= trying to achieve) a ten percent pay increase.
examples.zh: 工会为了争取10%的加薪而罢工。

definitions.en: a situation that is difficult to understand
definitions.zh: 令人费解的情况;难题;谜

examples.en: Scientists have been trying to solve this puzzle for years.
examples.zh: 多年来科学家们一直试图解开这个谜。

examples.en: The situation remains a puzzle to all of us.
The building blocks are like the pieces of a puzzle.
a jigsaw puzzle
examples.zh: 智力拼图玩具

examples.en: She had a complex personality that was a real puzzle to me.
This puzzle has astronomers scratching their heads.
Planning the camera shots can be like putting together a puzzle.
Why don't you sit down and draw or do a puzzle?
I spent hours reasoning out the solution to the puzzle.

definitions.en: to cause someone to feel confused and slightly worried because they cannot understand something
definitions.zh: （使）感到迷惑;（使）感到为难，（使）感到困窘

examples.en: The findings of the survey puzzle me - they're not at all what I would have expected.
examples.zh: 调查的结果使我感到迷惑——它们根本不像我原本预料的那样。

examples.en: It puzzles me why she said that.
examples.zh: 我想不通她为什么那么说。

examples.en: The events of that night have been puzzling me for a long time.
Experts were puzzled by the reports.
What puzzles me is why he should have left without telling me.
The games have cunning solutions, designed to keep you puzzling for just long enough to be satisfying.
He had been puzzling for days about how to get into the files.

definitions.en: one of the points at which a large range of numbers, results, etc. is divided to make four groups of the same size

examples.en: We can then calculate the median or any other quantile.
The table compares the scores of students at various quantiles.
examples.zh: 该表比较了处于不同四分位段的学生的分数。

examples.en: We then looked at various quantiles in the distribution.
Table 1 gives some selected quantiles.
If natural cut-points are not apparent, then quantiles can be used.

definitions.en: an unusual habit or part of someone's personality, or something that is strange and unexpected

examples.en: You have to get used to other people's quirks and foibles.
examples.zh: 你得习惯别人的怪癖和小缺点。

examples.en: There is a quirk in the rules that allows you to invest money without paying tax.
examples.zh: 这些规章的奇怪之处在于其允许人们投资而不必缴税。

examples.en: By some strange quirk/By an odd quirk of fate (= unexpectedly), we ended up on the same train.
examples.zh: 说来奇怪，我们结果上了同一辆火车。

definitions.en: unusual in an attractive and interesting way

examples.en: He was tall and had a quirky, off-beat sense of humour.
examples.zh: 他个子高高的，有一种独特、另类的幽默感。

definitions.en: the smallest number of people needed to be present at a meeting before it can officially begin and before official decisions can be taken
a fixed, limited amount or number that is officially allowed

examples.en: The country now has a quota on immigration.
examples.zh: 该国现在对外来移民人数有限额。

examples.en: The class contains the usual quota (= number) of troublemakers.
examples.zh: 班上也有那么些个捣蛋鬼。

definitions.en: a frame or shelf, often formed of bars, that is used to hold things

examples.en: a vegetable rack
examples.zh: 摆放蔬菜的货架

examples.en: a plate rack
examples.zh: 盘碟架

examples.en: a luggage rack
examples.zh: 行李架

definitions.en: a large piece of meat cut from the neck of a sheep or pig

examples.en: The butcher prepared a rack of pork.
examples.zh: 那个屠夫准备了一块猪颈肉。

examples.en: We're having rack of lamb for dinner.
examples.zh: 我们晚餐吃羊颈肉。

definitions.en: a bar with tooth-like parts along one edge that fits into a pinion (= a wheel with tooth-like parts) allowing change between circular and straight-line movement
a state of decay:
definitions.zh: 整个农场逐渐变得破败不堪。

examples.en: go to rack and ruin The whole farm was going to rack and ruin.
examples.zh: 整个农场逐渐变得破败不堪。

definitions.en: to cause physical or mental pain, or trouble, to someone or something
definitions.zh: 使肉体（或精神上）受巨大痛苦;折磨

examples.en: Even at the end, when cancer racked his body, he was calm and cheerful.
examples.zh: 即使在最后，当他的身体备受癌症病魔摧残的时候，他仍然镇定自若、乐观开朗。

examples.en: The dog was already racked by/with the pains of old age.
examples.zh: 那条狗已经又老又病，痛苦不堪。

examples.en: He was racked by/with doubts/guilt.
examples.zh: 他被疑虑／内疚所折磨。

definitions.en: the system of transport that uses trains
definitions.zh: 铁路交通；铁路交通系统

examples.en: Environmentalists argue that more goods should be transported by rail.
examples.zh: 环保主义者们主张应该更加充分利用铁路来运输货物。

examples.en: A train left/went off the rails and crashed into the bank, killing several passengers.
examples.zh: 一列火车脱轨后撞到路堤上，造成数名乘客死亡。

examples.en: An inquiry was ordered into the recent rail disaster.
Government sources estimate a long-term 50% increase in rail fares.
The rail system is to put 20 million pounds into its modernization programme.
The question is whether road transit is cheaper than rail.
The resort is easily accessible by road, rail and air.

definitions.en: a horizontal bar fixed in position, especially to a wall or to vertical posts, used to close something off, as a support, or to hang things on
definitions.zh: （尤指固定在墙上或竖杆上的）横杆，栏杆，扶手

examples.en: Will spectators please stay behind the rail?
examples.zh: 观众们请站在栏杆后面好吗？

examples.en: Hold onto the rail so that you don't fall.
examples.zh: 抓牢扶手，这样就不会摔倒了。

examples.en: The (clothes) rail in her wardrobe was crammed full of dresses.
examples.zh: 她大衣橱里面挂满了衣服。

examples.en: The bathroom has a heated towel rail.
I watched an old woman pull herself up the stairs, holding on to a rail.
They leaned over the rails and peered down into the dizzying chasm below.
The rails were full of expensive clothes.
We have a heated towel rail in our bathroom.

definitions.en: to complain angrily

examples.en: He railed against/at the injustices of the system.
examples.zh: 他强烈谴责该制度的不公正。

definitions.en: the possible results of an action
definitions.zh: 可能的后果；衍生结果；派生影响

examples.en: Have you considered all the ramifications of your suggestion?
examples.zh: 你考虑过你的建议可能带来的各种后果吗？

definitions.en: (especially of governments or organizations) to make an agreement official
definitions.zh: （尤指政府或组织）正式批准，使正式生效，正式签署

examples.en: Many countries have now ratified the UN convention on the rights of the child.
examples.zh: 很多国家现在已经正式签署了联合国儿童权利公约。

examples.en: The decision will have to be ratified (= approved) by the executive board.
examples.zh: 这个决议必须要经过管理委员会的批准。

definitions.en: the reasons or intentions that cause a particular set of beliefs or actions

examples.en: I don't understand the rationale for these restrictions.
examples.zh: 我无法理解这些限制。

examples.en: He tried to explain the scientific rationale behind his work.
examples.zh: 他尝试解释他工作背后的科学原理。

definitions.en: to cut and collect a grain crop

examples.en: The serfs had to cultivate the lord's land, to make his hay, and reap and gather in his harvest.
He cleared away the forest and, with his own hands, planted, sowed, and reaped.

definitions.en: a machine that cuts and collects crops, or a person who cuts and collects crops by hand
to think again about something in order to decide if you should change the way you feel about it or deal with it
definitions.zh: 重新考虑;再次评价;重新评定

examples.en: We need to reassess our values as a nation.
examples.zh: 我们必须重新评价我们整个民族的价值观。

examples.en: The customer services department is reassessing its procedures for handling customer complaints.
examples.zh: 客服部正在重新评定其处理顾客投诉的程序。

examples.en: I've reassessed the situation and decided to stay.
examples.zh: 我重新估计了形势后决定留下来。

definitions.en: words of advice and comfort intended to make someone feel less worried
definitions.zh: 能消除疑虑的话;安慰，慰藉

examples.en: I felt I couldn't cope with the situation and was in desperate need of some reassurance.
examples.zh: 我觉得我应付不了这种局面，真是特别需要别人的安慰。

examples.en: Despite her father's reassurances, she was still frightened of the dark.
examples.zh: 尽管她父亲说了很多安慰的话，可她还是惧怕黑暗。

definitions.en: to repeat the main points of an explanation or a description
definitions.zh: 扼要重述;摘要说明;概括

examples.en: Finally, the teacher recapped the main points of the lesson.
examples.zh: 最后老师又概括了一下这一课的重点。

examples.en: To recap, our main aim is to increase sales by 15 percent this year.
examples.zh: 简要地说，我们的主要目标是把今年的销售额提高15%。

examples.en: We'll just recap briefly on what we did.
Let me just recap here in case we're losing track.
It is worth recapping the main developments over the past decade as background to this perspective.

definitions.en: a formal party at which important people are welcomed

examples.en: The president gave a reception for the visiting heads of state.
examples.zh: 总统举行招待会款待来访的外国元首。

examples.en: Her first book got a wonderful/warm/frosty reception from the critics.
examples.zh: 评论家对她的第一本书反应很好／热烈／冷淡。

examples.en: The new hospital was ready for the reception of its first patients.
examples.zh: 新医院已经可以用了，准备收治第一批病人。

examples.en: I went to see the sales manager but got a rather chilly reception.
The president had a hostile reception in Ohio this morning.
She was buoyed (up) by the warm reception her audience gave her.
Which firm will be catering at the wedding reception?
Soft drinks and hors d'oeuvres will be served during the reception and a cash bar will be available.

definitions.en: the place in a hotel or office building where people go when they first arrive
definitions.zh: （旅馆或办公楼的）接待处，服务台，前台

examples.en: Ask for me at reception.
examples.zh: 要找我的话，到前台问询。

examples.en: I signed in at the reception desk.
examples.zh: 我在接待处登了记。

examples.en: Please would you check in at the reception desk and sign your name in the book.
We would like to remind all our guests to leave their keys at reception before they depart.
New security measures require all visitors to sign in at reception and wear a visitor's badge.
Upon your arrival, please report to the reception desk.
New security measures require all visitors to sign in at reception and wear a visitor's badge.

definitions.en: the degree to which mobile phone, radio, or television signals are strong and clear
