definitions.zh: （收音机或电视信号）接收效果，接收质量

examples.en: The phone reception is really bad out here in the woods.
examples.zh: 电话信号在这儿的树林里特别差。

examples.en: We live on top of a hill and so we get excellent radio reception.
examples.zh: 我们住在小山顶上，所以收音机的收听效果特别好。

examples.en: A new digital antenna might improve your reception.
examples.zh: 安装一个新的数码天线可能会改善接受效果。

definitions.en: the first year of infant school

examples.en: a reception class/teacher
examples.zh: 学前班／学前班老师

definitions.en: a person who receives something

examples.en: Former Nobel Peace Prize recipients include Jimmy Carter and Barack Obama.
examples.zh: 过去获得过诺贝尔和平奖的包括吉米·卡特和巴拉克·奥巴马。

examples.en: He was a recipient of the Civilian Service Award.
examples.zh: 他被授予平民服务奖章。

definitions.en: to think or believe

examples.en: I reckon it's going to rain.
examples.zh: 我觉得天要下雨了。

examples.en: How much do you reckon (that) it's going to cost?
examples.zh: 你认为这会花多少钱？

examples.en: "Can you fix my car today?" "I reckon not/so (= probably not/probably)."
examples.zh: “你能今天把我的汽车修好吗？”“我想不行／大概可以。”

examples.en: "How old do I reckon she is? I'd say 38." "Spot on."
She's been promising to pay back the money for six months, but I reckon she's just stringing me along.
He reckons all policemen are fascists and bullies.
There was a man on the news last night who reckons we've been visited by beings from other worlds.
I paid for the tickets and you bought dinner so we're quits, I reckon.

definitions.en: to consider or have the opinion that something is as stated

examples.en: I don't reckon much to (US of) their chances of winning (= I do not think they will win).
examples.zh: 我认为他们获胜的希望不大。

examples.en: She was widely reckoned (to be) the best actress of her generation.
examples.zh: 人们普遍认为她是她那一代最优秀的演员。

definitions.en: to calculate an amount

examples.en: Angela quickly reckoned the amount on her fingers.
examples.zh: 安吉拉掰着手指头快速计算了一下数量。

examples.en: The inflation rate is now reckoned to be 10 percent.
examples.zh: 据计算，现在的通货膨胀率是10%。

definitions.en: to know someone or something because you have seen or heard him or her or experienced it before

examples.en: I hadn't seen her for 20 years, but I recognized her immediately.
examples.zh: 我20年没见她了，但我还是一眼就认出了她。

examples.en: Do you recognize this song?
examples.zh: “你听出这首歌来了吗？”

examples.en: Doctors are trained to recognize the symptoms of different diseases.
examples.zh: 医生受过训练可以辨识出不同疾病的症状。

examples.en: He put on a large hat and glasses as a disguise and hoped no one would recognize him.
His red hair and short stature made him easy to recognize.
As we drew alongside the black car, I suddenly recognized my ex-boyfriend at the wheel.
Many distant relatives, most of whom I hardly recognized, turned up for my brother's wedding.
He'd completely changed - I didn't recognize him.

definitions.en: to accept that something is legal, true, or important

examples.en: The international community has refused to recognize (= officially accept the existence of) the newly independent nation state.
examples.zh: 国际社会拒绝承认这个新独立的国家。

examples.en: He sadly recognized (that) he would die childless.
examples.zh: 他接受了这个悲哀的事实：他将无儿无女、孤苦伶仃地度过残生。

examples.en: You must recognize the seriousness of the problems we are facing.
examples.zh: 你必须认识到我们现在面临的问题有多严重。

examples.en: The Queen recognized his services to his country by awarding him an MBE.
examples.zh: 女王授予她大英帝国员佐勋章，以表彰她对国家的贡献。

examples.en: The country has de facto independence now, and it will soon be recognized de jure by the world's governments.
His pre-eminence in his subject is internationally recognized.
We need to recognize that we are now living in a plural society.
Alcoholics often have to hit rock bottom before they recognize that they have a problem.
Steiner was recognized as an original if unorthodox thinker.

definitions.en: to find a way in which two situations or beliefs that are opposed to each other can agree and exist together

examples.en: It is sometimes difficult to reconcile science and religion.
examples.zh: 有时很难调和宗教和科学之间的矛盾。

examples.en: It's difficult to reconcile such different points of view.
examples.zh: 差别如此悬殊的观点是很难达成一致的。

examples.en: How can you reconcile your fur coat and/with your love of animals?
examples.zh: 如果你爱动物，那怎么还穿毛皮大衣呢？

examples.en: be reconciled with They were finally reconciled with each other, after not speaking for nearly five years.
examples.zh: 他们差不多有5年的时间连话都不说，但最后终于重归于好。

definitions.en: a situation in which two people or groups of people become friendly again after they have argued

examples.en: It took hours of negotiations to bring about a reconciliation between the two sides.
examples.zh: 双方谈了好几个小时才达成和解。

definitions.en: to correct something or make something right

examples.en: I am determined to take whatever action is necessary to rectify the situation.
examples.zh: 我决心采取一切必要的措施来整顿局面。

examples.en: Every effort is made to rectify any errors/mistakes before the book is printed.
examples.zh: 在书付印前会尽力去审校修改书中的错误。

definitions.en: to make a substance pure
to change an electrical current from AC to DC
definitions.zh: 整（流）;把（电流）由交流变成直流

definitions.en: to happen many times or to happen again
definitions.zh: 反复出现；再次发生；重新产生

examples.en: The theme of freedom recurs throughout her writing.
examples.zh: 自由这一主题在她的写作中反复出现。

examples.en: If the pain/problem/trouble, etc. recurs, come and see me.
examples.zh: 如果疼痛复发／问题再出现／再感不适等，就来找我。

examples.en: Water, a symbol of life, recurs as an image throughout her poems.
If the symptoms recur, visit your doctor.
The theme of loss recurs throughout much of his writing.
If the problem recurred, we'd have to think again.
The same ideas recur throughout her books.

definitions.en: to remove words or information from a text before it is printed or made available to the public
definitions.zh: 在印刷（或公开）之前删减一些话（或信息）

examples.en: Officers' names are routinely redacted from any publicly released reports.
examples.zh: 在公开的材料中，军官们的姓名经常被隐去。

examples.en: Some parts of secret files are available to the public, but heavily redacted.
examples.zh: 有一些秘密文件公众可以看到，但是内容已被大量删减。

definitions.en: a situation in which someone loses their job because their employer does not need them

examples.en: The economic downturn has meant 10,000 redundancies in the Northeast.
examples.zh: 由于经济衰退，东北地区有一万人失业。

examples.en: She took voluntary redundancy.
examples.zh: 她选择了自愿裁汰。

examples.en: I live in dread of redundancy.
There's a lot of redundancy in the area.
He was so fed up at work that redundancy was starting to look quite appealing.
Some people would opt for redundancy rather than redeployment.
They got rid of 80 staff in the latest round of redundancies.

definitions.en: a situation in which something is unnecessary because it is more than is needed
definitions.zh: （尤指词、短语等的）多余，累赘，啰唆

examples.en: The aircraft has seven computer systems running in parallel, so as to provide enough redundancy to cope with computer breakdowns.
He said the symposium has been invaluable in reducing redundancy in research.
All the programs are being scrutinized for effectiveness, and there is an emphasis on seeking out and eliminating redundancies.
In the case of images, considerable redundancy can be found simply by locating parts of the image that are the same.

definitions.en: (especially of a word, phrase, etc.) unnecessary because it is more than is needed
definitions.zh: （尤指词、短语等）多余的，不需要的，累赘的，啰唆的

examples.en: In the sentence "She is a single unmarried woman", the word "unmarried" is redundant.
examples.zh: 在句子 She is a single unmarried woman 中，unmarried 一词是多余的。

definitions.en: having lost your job because your employer no longer needs you
definitions.zh: 失业的，被解雇的，被裁减的

examples.en: To keep the company alive, half the workforce is being made redundant.
examples.zh: 为了使公司生存下去，一半的员工要被裁掉。

examples.en: New technology often makes old skills and even whole communities redundant.
examples.zh: 新技术常常会使古老的技艺甚至是某个行业完全消失。

examples.en: The company was losing money and it had to make people redundant.
She was made redundant a year ago and hasn't found any work since.
They're threatening to make a third of the workforce redundant.
She was told she was being made redundant yesterday.
He was made redundant and given a big pay-off.

definitions.en: to avoid doing or stop yourself from doing something

examples.en: We refrained from talking until we knew that it was safe.
examples.zh: 我们缄默不语，直到确定安全了才开始说话。

examples.en: The sign on the wall said "Please refrain from smoking."
examples.zh: 墙上有个牌子，上写“请勿吸烟”。

definitions.en: a short part of a song or poem that is repeated, especially between the verses (= the separate parts)
definitions.zh: （尤指不同的节之间的）副歌，叠歌

examples.en: "Every vote counts" is a familiar refrain in politics.
examples.zh: “每一票都重要”是政治上的老生常谈。

definitions.en: to take or get possession of something again

examples.en: The government has regained control of the capital from rebel forces.
examples.zh: 政府已从叛军手中收复了首都。

examples.en: She made an effort to regain her self-control.
examples.zh: 她竭力控制住自己。

examples.en: The swimmers struggled to regain the shore.
examples.zh: 游泳者们挣扎着游回岸上。

examples.en: After a bad year, she has regained her form.
They regained their freedom after ten years of unjust imprisonment.
He looked embarrassed for a moment, then quickly regained his poise.
They regained the lead in the 21st minute.
He lost consciousness after his accident and never regained it.

definitions.en: the fact of an illness or its symptoms (= effects) becoming less severe

examples.en: The treatment was more effective than tamoxifen, which causes complete regression in only about 30% of cases.
examples.zh: 这种治疗比他莫昔芬更为有效，只有约30%的患者在接受他莫昔芬治疗后病症完全消退。

examples.en: In this group there was a significant regression in artery wall thickness.
examples.zh: 在这一组中，动脉壁厚度有显著减少。

examples.en: It is hoped that this regimen will result in clinical tumour regression in patients with metastatic melanoma.
Histological measurements performed at 3 months were striking, with signs of disease regression in all patients in the trial.
The frequency of such regressions in cancer is not yet known; however, it is generally accepted that they are extremely rare.

definitions.en: (of tax) lower on large amounts of money, so that the rich are less affected
definitions.zh: （税收）递减的（对富人的影响比对穷人的小）

examples.en: Regressive taxation means that poor people wind up paying a larger percentage of their income in tax than rich people do.
The single most regressive tax is the tax on food because everyone has to eat.

definitions.en: to control something, especially by making it work in a particular way
definitions.zh: （尤指使按照某种方式运作而）控制，管理，调节，调整

examples.en: You can regulate the temperature in the house by adjusting the thermostat.
examples.zh: 你可以通过调节恒温器来控制室温。

examples.en: Her mother strictly regulates how much TV she can watch.
examples.zh: 她妈妈严格控制她看电视的时间。

examples.en: Police can invoke the law of trespass to regulate access to these places.
This valve regulates the flow of water into the tank.
The travel industry is regulated by a set of guidelines.
The switch is used to regulate the speed at which the machine operates.
The government has introduced new laws to regulate the sale of firearms.

definitions.en: to make something stronger

examples.en: The pockets on my jeans are reinforced with double stitching.
examples.zh: 我牛仔裤的裤兜缝了两道线，很结实。

examples.en: The final technical report into the accident reinforces the findings of initial investigations.
examples.zh: 对事故作出的最终的技术性报告进一步证实了最初的调查结果。

examples.en: His behaviour merely reinforced my dislike of him.
examples.zh: 他的行为只能使我更讨厌他。

examples.en: The garrison is to be reinforced with/by another two battalions of soldiers.
examples.zh: 守备部队将得到另外两个营的增援。

examples.en: The foundations will have to be reinforced to prevent the house from sinking further into the ground.
The wooden beam is reinforced with a metal plate.
Caring for animals is not sentimentality - it reinforces our respect for life.
The builders used metal rods to reinforce the walls of the building.
He used hand gestures to reinforce what he was saying.

definitions.en: to say something again, once or several times

examples.en: The government has reiterated its refusal to compromise with terrorists.
examples.zh: 政府重申拒绝向恐怖分子妥协。

examples.en: She reiterated that she had never seen him before.
examples.zh: 她一再重申自己以前从未见过他。

definitions.en: to give up something such as a responsibility or claim

examples.en: He has relinquished his claim to the throne.
examples.zh: 他放弃了继承王位的要求。

examples.en: She relinquished control of the family investments to her son.
examples.zh: 她把家庭投资的掌控权移交给了儿子。

examples.en: She relinquished her hold/grip on the steering wheel.
examples.zh: 她松开了握着方向盘的手。

definitions.en: used for emphasizing how surprising or unusual something is

examples.en: It is a remarkably noisy and crowded city.
examples.zh: 这是座非常嘈杂拥挤的城市。

examples.en: Remarkably, she wasn't hurt in the crash.
examples.zh: 不可思议的是，她在撞车事故中竟然没有受伤。

examples.en: He's adopted a remarkably light-hearted attitude towards the situation.
She looked remarkably composed throughout the funeral.
Elizabeth is remarkably well-informed.
Her hearing is going, but otherwise she's remarkably fit for a 95-year-old.
We'd expected a few glitches, but everything's gone remarkably smoothly.

definitions.en: a successful way of curing an illness or dealing with a problem or difficulty
definitions.zh: 疗法，治疗;补救（办法），纠正（办法）

examples.en: an effective herbal remedy for headaches
examples.zh: 用草药治疗头疼的有效疗法

examples.en: The best remedy for grief is hard work.
examples.zh: 排解忧伤的最好办法就是努力工作。

examples.en: legal remedy for We have exhausted all possible legal remedies for this injustice.
examples.zh: 为了纠正这一不公正的行为，我们已经采取了一切法律手段。

examples.en: You can buy most cold remedies over the counter.
homeopathic remedies
There is a whole panoply of remedies and drugs available to the modern doctor.
Do you know of any remedies for sinus problems?
Love is a great remedy for unhappiness.

definitions.en: to do something to correct or improve something that is wrong

examples.en: This mistake must be remedied immediately.
examples.zh: 这一错误必须立即予以纠正。

definitions.en: making you remember a particular person, event, or thing
definitions.zh: 使人想起…的;使人回忆起…的

examples.en: That song is so reminiscent of my adolescence.
examples.zh: 那首歌勾起了我对青少年时代的无限回忆。

definitions.en: careless and not doing a duty well enough
definitions.zh: 疏忽的；懈怠的；玩忽职守的

examples.en: You have been remiss in your duties.
examples.zh: 你一直玩忽职守。

examples.en: It was remiss of me to forget to give you the message.
examples.zh: 我忘了把口信转达给你，是我的疏忽。

definitions.en: a person who has changed their feelings of support and duty from one political, religious, national, etc. group to a new one
definitions.zh: 叛徒；变节者；背叛者；叛教者

examples.en: A band of renegades had captured the prince and were holding him to ransom.
examples.zh: 一群叛变分子俘获了王子，将其扣押索要赎金。

definitions.en: the state of being famous

examples.en: a woman of great renown
examples.zh: 极富名望的女性

examples.en: Her renown spread across the country.
examples.zh: 她的名声传遍了全国。

definitions.en: famous for something

examples.en: The region is renowned for its outstanding natural beauty.
examples.zh: 该地区因其秀美壮丽的自然风光而享有盛誉。

examples.en: Marco Polo is a renowned explorer/is renowned as an explorer.
examples.zh: 马可‧波罗是著名的探险家。

definitions.en: the act of doing or saying something again

examples.en: His books are full of repetition.
examples.zh: 他的那些书都是在翻来覆去写同样的东西。

examples.en: We want to prevent a repetition of last summer's fires, which destroyed more than 500,000 acres of land.
examples.zh: 去年夏天的火灾毁了超过50万英亩地，我们要防止此类火灾再次发生。

examples.en: We hope to avoid a repetition of last year's fiasco.
We don't want a repetition of yesterday's scenes.
The job involved the endless repetition of two or three simple tasks.
Try to avoid unnecessary repetition when stating your points.
His speeches show a marked preference for repetition, that tedious rhetorical device.

definitions.en: to fill something up again
definitions.zh: 补充；把…再备足；重新装满

examples.en: Food stocks were replenished by/with imports from abroad.
examples.zh: 粮食储备通过从国外进口得到了补充。

examples.en: Does your glass need replenishing?
examples.zh: 你的酒杯要不要再斟满？

definitions.en: to make or do something again in exactly the same way

examples.en: Researchers tried many times to replicate the original experiment.
examples.zh: 研究者们作了很多次努力，试图重复这一实验。

examples.en: Chromosomes replicate before cells divide and multiply.
examples.zh: 染色体自我复制后，细胞便开始分裂、繁殖。

examples.en: Computer viruses replicate themselves and are passed along from user to user.
examples.zh: 计算机病毒自我复制并从一个用户的计算机传到另一个用户的计算机上。

definitions.en: able to be shown, done, or made again
definitions.zh: 能再生的;能再制造的;能再现的

examples.en: The method produces statistically reliable and reproducible results.
examples.zh: 该方法产生的结果具有统计学上的可靠性和可重复性。

definitions.en: to find a new use for an idea, product, or building
definitions.zh: 重新利用;改变…的用途;为…找到新用途

examples.en: The company's role is to repurpose print data for use on the Web.
examples.zh: 这家公司的职责是将印刷品资料转成电子版以供网络使用。

examples.en: Cinemas are harder to repurpose than ordinary shops.
examples.zh: 与普通店铺相比，电影院改作其他用途的难度要大得多。

definitions.en: the opinion that people in general have about someone or something, or how much respect or admiration someone or something receives, based on past behaviour or character

examples.en: The company has a worldwide reputation for quality.
examples.zh: 这家公司的产品质量誉满全球。

examples.en: She has the reputation of being a good doctor.
examples.zh: 作为一名优秀的医生，她很有声望。

examples.en: His reputation was destroyed when he was caught stealing some money.
examples.zh: 他偷钱时被当场抓住，这使他声名扫地。

examples.en: The hotel has a bad/good reputation.
examples.zh: 这家旅馆名声很好／坏。

examples.en: He earned/established/gained/acquired a reputation as an entertaining speaker.
examples.zh: 他因为演讲妙趣横生而赢得／享有／得到／获得盛名。

examples.en: Recent discoveries about corruption have done serious damage to the company's reputation.
The government's reputation has already been harmed by a series of scandals.
The newspaper has a reputation for being littered with spelling mistakes.
She has a reputation for open-mindedness and original thinking.
Actors' reputations have been made and unmade on this London stage.

definitions.en: to help someone or something out of a dangerous, harmful, or unpleasant situation

examples.en: The lifeboat rescued the sailors from the sinking boat.
examples.zh: 救生艇将水手们从正在下沉的船上救起。

examples.en: The government has refused to rescue the company from bankruptcy.
examples.zh: 政府已经拒绝援助这家濒于破产的公司。

examples.en: The management are putting together a plan to rescue the company.
They shouted for help, but nobody came to rescue them.
Six people were rescued by helicopter from a fishing boat in distress off the Cornish coast.
She showed enormous courage when she rescued him from the fire.
A goal just before half-time rescued the match from mediocrity.

definitions.en: the act of helping someone out of a dangerous or unpleasant situation

examples.en: Lifeboats carry out many rescues every month.
examples.zh: 救生艇每月都会执行很多救援任务。

examples.en: We huddled together on the cliff ledge, waiting for rescue.
examples.zh: 我们挤作一团蜷缩在悬崖边，等待救援。

examples.en: I didn't know anybody at the party, but the hostess came to my rescue (= helped me out of a difficult situation) by introducing me to a few people.
examples.zh: 在聚会上我谁也不认识，但女主人过来救了我的驾，把我介绍给几个客人。

examples.en: They were decorated for their part in the rescue.
We watched scenes of the dramatic rescue on the news.
Weather conditions have moderated, making a rescue attempt possible.
The central bank has pulled off one of the biggest financial rescues of recent years.
One man sustained severe burns during the rescue.

definitions.en: to look like or be like someone or something

examples.en: You resemble your mother very closely.
examples.zh: 你长得很像你妈妈。

examples.en: After the earthquake, the city resembled a battlefield.
examples.zh: 地震过后，这座城市看上去就像战场一般。

examples.en: The overlapping slates of the roofs in the mountain village resembled fish scales.
His latest sculpture resembles an enormous seashell.
That actor really resembles my brother-in-law.
The finished model should resemble the one in the picture.
The twins resembled each other more strongly when they were young.

definitions.en: to keep something for a particular purpose or time
definitions.zh: 把…专门留给;把…留作;保留

examples.en: I reserve Mondays for tidying my desk and answering letters.
examples.zh: 我把周一的时间留出来专门清理办公桌和回复信件。

examples.en: These seats are reserved for the elderly and women with babies.
examples.zh: 这些座位是专留给老人和抱小孩的妇女的。

examples.en: I reserve judgment on this issue (= I won't give an opinion on it now) until we have more information.
examples.zh: 在我们得到更多的信息之前，我暂不对此事发表意见。

examples.en: I reserved a double room at the Lamb Hotel.
examples.zh: 我在兰姆酒店预订了一个双人房。

examples.en: If you get there early, reserve me a seat/reserve a seat for me.
examples.zh: 如果你先到那儿，给我预订个座位。

examples.en: I'm awfully sorry, but we've forgotten to reserve you a table.
Could I reserve two seats for tomorrow evening's performance?
I've come to collect my tickets - I reserved them by phone yesterday in the name of Tremin.
"Had you forgotten about our anniversary?" "Certainly not! I've reserved a table at Michel's restaurant for this evening."
The worst of her criticism was reserved for journalists, photographers and others of their ilk.

definitions.en: the habit of not showing your feelings or thoughts

examples.en: I took her out for a drink and tried to break through her reserve.
examples.zh: 我带她出去喝了杯酒，想消除她的拘谨。

definitions.en: the act of keeping something or a supply of something until it is needed, or a supply that you keep

examples.en: She keeps a little money in reserve (= for use if and when needed).
examples.zh: 她留下了一些钱以备不时之需。

examples.en: The librarian has put the book on reserve for me (= will keep it for me when it becomes available).
examples.zh: 图书管理员已经替我预约了这本书。

examples.en: We still have a reserve of food/food reserves in case of emergency.
examples.zh: 我们仍然还有储备粮／粮食储备以应付紧急情况。

definitions.en: an area of land kept in its natural state, especially for wild animals to live in and be protected
definitions.zh: （尤指保护野生动物生存的）自然保护区，禁猎区，保护区

examples.en: a nature/game/wildlife reserve
examples.zh: 自然保护区／禁猎区／野生动物保护区

definitions.en: in sports, an extra player who is ready to play if needed

examples.en: We had two reserves in case anyone was injured.
examples.zh: 我们有两名替补队员，以防有人在场上受伤。

examples.en: Frank was a prolific scorer for the reserves before he graduated to the first team.
examples.zh: 在加入首选阵容之前，弗兰克在后备队比赛中进了许多球。

definitions.en: a group of people who are not permanently in the armed forces but are used only if needed:

examples.en: call up the reserves They will call up the reserves.
examples.zh: 他们将召集后备军。

definitions.en: a feeling of doubt about someone or something

examples.en: I can recommend him to you without reserve.
examples.zh: 我可以毫无保留地向您推荐他。

definitions.en: the lowest amount of money the owners will accept for something being sold, especially at auction (= public sale)
definitions.zh: （尤指拍卖中买方定的）底价，保留价格，最低价

examples.en: A rare Stradivarius violin failed to reach its reserve price (= no one offered to pay it) when put up for auction on Tuesday.
examples.zh: 星期二的拍卖会上，对一把稀有的斯特拉迪瓦里制造的小提琴的出价没能达到其拍卖底价。

examples.en: We set/put a reserve of €500 on the picture.
examples.zh: 我们为这幅画定的底价为500欧元。

definitions.en: Reserved people do not often talk about or show their feelings or thoughts.

examples.en: a quiet, reserved woman
examples.zh: 一位安静、矜持的女子

examples.en: The English have a reputation for being reserved.
examples.zh: 英国人以矜持闻名。

definitions.en: Reserved tickets, seats, etc. are ones that you have arranged to be kept for you
definitions.zh: 保留的，预订的（门票、座位等）

examples.en: May I sit here, or is this seat/table reserved?
examples.zh: 我可以坐这儿吗？还是这个座位／餐桌已经被预订了？

definitions.en: to live, have your home, or stay in a place

examples.en: The family now resides in southern France.
examples.zh: 这家人现居住在法国南部。

definitions.en: the ability to be happy, successful, etc. again after something difficult or bad has happened
definitions.zh: 复原力;恢复力;平复心情

examples.en: Trauma researchers emphasize the resilience of the human psyche.
examples.zh: 创伤研究人员强调人类心理的复原力。

examples.en: These are habits that can break down the resiliency you'll need to help you through difficult times.
The plant fibre has incredible strength and resilience.
examples.zh: 植物纤维有让人难以置信的强度和弹性。

examples.en: These new materials have additional resiliency and elasticity properties, as well as great strength.
Having a place that's theirs alone and that doesn't necessarily involve their mum or dad helps kids build resilience and problem-solving skills.
Resilience is likely to be tested during the coming weeks and months in the millions of people who witnessed the terror of last week.
What we are starting to see is the resilience of the country's economy.
The lads have shown great resilience considering the problems we've had.
Bone mineral density is the measure physicians use to determine bone strength and resilience.
"Good wool has a snap to it," she says, referring to the fibres' resilience.

definitions.en: able to quickly return to its usual shape after being bent, stretched, or pressed
definitions.zh: 有弹性的；能复原的；有复原力的；适应性强的

examples.en: This rubber ball is very resilient and immediately springs back into shape.
examples.zh: 这种橡胶球很有弹性，可以立即恢复原来的形状。

examples.en: resilient materials
We realised how resilient these women and children had to be.
In the study, resilient people were easily comforted by positive memories of their deceased partner.
Even my doctors were surprised at how resilient I was during and after treatment.
The exercise had helped make the company resilient to bad luck.

definitions.en: to fight against something or someone that is attacking you

examples.en: The soldiers resisted (the enemy attacks) for two days.
examples.zh: （面对敌人的进攻）士兵们抵抗了两天。

examples.en: The party leader resisted demands for his resignation.
examples.zh: 该党领导人拒不答应他辞职的要求。

examples.en: He tried to run away from the police and was charged with resisting arrest.
examples.zh: 警方逮捕他时，他企图逃跑，结果被控拒捕。

examples.en: The new hybrid crops are much better at resisting disease.
examples.zh: 这种新杂交作物抗病能力更强。

examples.en: I can never resist temptation/chocolate/the urge to laugh.
examples.zh: 我向来无法抗拒诱惑／我永远不可能抵挡巧克力的诱惑／我要是想笑，怎么也忍不住。

examples.en: She couldn't resist laughing at him in those clothes.
examples.zh: 看到他那副穿戴，她忍不住大笑起来。

examples.en: He couldn't resist embellishing the story of his accident a little.
The town had been besieged for two months but still resisted the aggressors.
They are bravely resisting subjugation by their more powerful neighbours.
General Rattigan summoned reinforcements to help resist the attack.
He usually resists attempts to introduce new procedures.

definitions.en: to produce, increase, or fill with sound, by vibrating (= shaking) objects that are near
definitions.zh: 发出回响;发出共鸣;回荡

examples.en: His voice resonated in the empty church.
examples.zh: 他的声音在空荡荡的教堂中回荡。

examples.en: The noise of the bell resonated through the building.
examples.zh: 铃声在大楼里回荡。

definitions.en: to be filled with a particular quality

examples.en: The building resonates with historic significance.
examples.zh: 这座房子富有历史意义。

examples.en: The significance of those great stories resonates down the centuries.
examples.zh: 这些著名故事的深远意义将世代流传。

examples.en: Her experiences resonate powerfully with me, living, as I do, in a similar family situation.
examples.zh: 她的家庭生活环境和我相似，所以她的经历在我心中引起强烈共鸣。

examples.en: What exactly is it about that music that resonates with you?

definitions.en: the start of something again after it has stopped

examples.en: The president called for an immediate ceasefire and a resumption of negotiations between the two sides.
examples.zh: 总统呼吁双方立即停火，回到谈判桌上。

definitions.en: to bring someone back to life

examples.en: Almost all Christians believe that Jesus was resurrected from the dead.
examples.zh: 几乎所有的基督徒都相信耶稣死而复生。

examples.en: Several members of the party have resurrected the idea of constitutional change.
examples.zh: 该党几位党员重新提出了修改宪法的主张。

examples.en: She has been busily trying to resurrect her Hollywood career.
examples.zh: 她一直奔忙，试图重振她的好莱坞演艺生涯。

definitions.en: the activity of selling goods to the public, usually in shops

examples.en: The job is open to applicants with over two years' experience in retail.
examples.zh: 这份工作面向有两年以上零售经验的求职者。

examples.en: The clothing company has six retail outlets (= shops) in Perth.
examples.zh: 这家服装公司在佩思开设了6家零售店。

examples.en: $13 off the manufacturer's recommended retail price
examples.zh: 比生产商的建议零售价便宜13美元

examples.en: The retail group outbid all three competitors for space in the shopping centre.
Shares in retail businesses were marked up on the news that consumer spending rose last month.
Christmas will not be enough to save the retail industry.
Large retail chains are usually only prepared to locate stores in areas of high population density.
It's the latest American retail giant to cross the Atlantic.

definitions.en: to sell goods to the public in shops, on the internet, etc.

examples.en: The company makes and retails moderately priced sportswear.
examples.zh: 这家公司生产和零售价格适中的运动服。

examples.en: This laptop retails at £850.
examples.zh: 这款计算机零售价为850英镑。

definitions.en: to keep or continue to have something

examples.en: She has lost her battle to retain control of the company.
examples.zh: 她被打败了，失去了继续掌管公司的权力。

examples.en: He managed to retain his dignity throughout the performance.
examples.zh: 在整个表演过程中，他设法保持了自己的尊严。

examples.en: She succeeded in retaining her lead in the second half of the race.
examples.zh: 在比赛的后半程中，她成功地保住了领先地位。

examples.en: I have a good memory and am able to retain (= remember) facts easily.
examples.zh: 我记忆力很好，记东西不费劲。

examples.en: The sea retains the sun's warmth longer than the land.
examples.zh: 海水储存太阳的热量的时间比陆地要长。

examples.en: He will retain the honorary title of non-executive chairman.
These plants need a rich soil which retains moisture.
When they divorced she retained a legal interest in the property.
The villagers retain a strong attachment to their traditional customs.
The management retains the right to refuse admission.

definitions.en: the continued use, existence, or possession of something or someone

examples.en: Two influential senators have argued for the retention of the unpopular tax.
examples.zh: 两位有影响的参议员主张保留这项不得人心的税收。

examples.en: The retention of old technology has slowed the company's growth.
examples.zh: 死守着旧技术不放减缓了这家公司的发展。

examples.en: water/heat retention
examples.zh: 水分保持／热量滞留

definitions.en: to take back an offer or statement, etc. or admit that a statement was false

examples.en: retract an invitation/confession/promise
examples.zh: 取消邀请／翻供／收回承诺

examples.en: When questioned on TV, he retracted his allegations.
examples.zh: 在接受电视采访时，他收回了他以前的说法。

examples.en: The wheels retract after the aircraft takes off.
examples.zh: 飞机起飞后轮子便收了起来。

examples.en: The cat retracted its claws.
examples.zh: 猫把爪子缩了回去。

definitions.en: to find and bring back something

examples.en: We taught our dog to retrieve a ball.
examples.zh: 我们教狗捡球。

examples.en: Computers are used to store and retrieve information efficiently.
examples.zh: 计算机用于快速高效地存储和检索信息。

examples.en: This software helps firms archive and retrieve emails.
They've managed to retrieve most of the data.
Did they manage to retrieve anything from the fire?
She bent over to retrieve her bag from where it had fallen under the seat.
Goodwill that is lost is extremely difficult to retrieve.

definitions.en: (of a law or decision) in a way that has effect from a date before it was approved
definitions.zh: 有追溯效力地;溯及既往地

examples.en: The courts cannot apply a new rule retroactively.
examples.zh: 法院不能追溯适用新规则。

examples.en: They imposed the safety rule retroactively.
examples.zh: 他们追溯施行安全规则。

examples.en: They passed a law retroactively banning the chemicals.
The ban was intended to apply retroactively.
The board retroactively fined the couple $2,700.

definitions.en: thinking now about something in the past

examples.en: In retrospect, I think my marriage was doomed from the beginning.
examples.zh: 现在回头看，我觉得我的婚姻从一开始就注定要破裂。

examples.en: I'm sure my university days seem happier in retrospect than they really were.
examples.zh: 我敢说，回顾我的大学时光，看起来肯定要比当时实际生活幸福。

definitions.en: to change or arrange something again, in order to improve it

examples.en: We revamped the management system, but the business is doing no better than it was before.
examples.zh: 我们对管理制度作了彻底改进，但是经营业绩并不比从前好。

examples.en: The walled garden was completely revamped last year.
This year, the system has been completely revamped and no problems have been reported so far.
Other manufacturers have recently revamped their manufacturing processes to eliminate lead.
The company has launched a revamped version of its commercial mortgages.

definitions.en: to make known or show something that is surprising or that was previously secret

examples.en: He was jailed for revealing secrets to the Russians.
examples.zh: 他因为向俄国人泄密而被监禁。

examples.en: Her biography revealed that she was not as rich as everyone thought.
examples.zh: 她的自传透露她并非像大家所想象的那样富有。

examples.en: He would not reveal where he had hidden her chocolate eggs.
examples.zh: 他就是不说他把她的巧克力蛋藏在哪里了。

examples.en: A gap in the clouds revealed the Atlantic far below.
examples.zh: 从云缝中可以看到远在下方的大西洋。

examples.en: The committee pressed him to reveal more information.
The journalist refused to reveal her sources.
The survey reveals that two-thirds of married women earn less than their husbands.
A close inspection revealed minute cracks in the aircraft's fuselage and wings.
The curtains parted, revealing a darkened stage.

definitions.en: an occasion at the end of a television programme, etc. when something that has been hidden or kept secret until then is shown to the audience

examples.en: The final reveal of the new kitchen was a huge success.
examples.zh: 最后大揭示的新厨房非常成功。

definitions.en: to look at or consider again an idea, piece of writing, etc. in order to correct or improve it

examples.en: His helpfulness today has made me revise my original opinion/impression of him.
examples.zh: 他今天所表现出的乐于助人的品质使我改变了对他的最初看法／印象。

examples.en: His publishers made him revise his manuscript three times.
examples.zh: 出版公司让他把手稿修改了3次。

examples.en: Casualty figures were revised downwards after the war had ended.
Conceivably, supporters of the law could filibuster to prevent it from being revised.
The cost of completion has been revised upwards again due to inflation.
The mechanism for collecting taxes needs revising.
He was asked to revise his proposal before submitting it again.

definitions.en: to study again something you have already learned, in preparation for an exam

examples.en: We're revising (algebra) for the test tomorrow.
examples.zh: 我们正在为明天的（代数）考试进行复习。

definitions.en: to make a recording go back towards the beginning

examples.en: Will you rewind that scene so we can watch it again?
examples.zh: 你能倒回到那个场景让我们再看一遍吗？

examples.en: It is now possible to pause and rewind live television.
examples.zh: 现在有可能在观看实况节目时暂停和倒回重放。

examples.en: She rewound to the beginning of the tape.

definitions.en: a long, narrow strip of material used to tie things together or as a decoration
definitions.zh: （捆扎或装饰用的）带子，丝带，缎带

examples.en: Sandra often wears a ribbon in her hair.
examples.zh: 桑德拉常在头发上扎条丝带。

examples.en: He tied up the present with ribbon.
examples.zh: 他用缎带将礼物包扎起来。

examples.en: A ribbon of road stretched ahead of us across the desert.
examples.zh: 沙漠中的一条路宛如一根细长的带子，在我们前方延伸。

examples.en: My typewriter needs a new ribbon.
examples.zh: 我的打字机得换色带了。

examples.en: A pink ribbon had been tied around the crown of the hat.
She shook her hair loose from its ribbon.
They bound the packages with brightly coloured ribbon.
She tied the ribbon tightly in a bow.
She wrapped the present and tied it with ribbon.

definitions.en: stupid or unreasonable and deserving to be laughed at

examples.en: Do I look ridiculous in this hat?
examples.zh: 我戴这顶帽子看着很可笑吗？

examples.en: Don't be so ridiculous! I can't possibly afford to stay in a hotel like that.
examples.zh: 别傻了！我才住不起那样的旅馆呢。

examples.en: It's ridiculous to expect a two-year-old to be able to read!
examples.zh: 指望一个两岁的孩子能阅读真是太荒唐了！

examples.en: Does he wear those ridiculous clothes to draw attention?
Quite frankly, I think this whole situation is ridiculous.
I've never heard anything so ridiculous.
He described the law as anachronistic and ridiculous.
There are countless arguments against this ridiculous proposal.

definitions.en: in a careful way so that every part of something is looked at or considered to make certain it is correct or safe

examples.en: The plutonium fuel cells had been rigorously tested.
examples.zh: 钚燃料电池已经过严格测试。

examples.en: Isabel thinks rigorously and creatively about many different things.
examples.zh: 伊莎贝尔对许多不同的事情都进行了严谨而富有创造性的思考。

examples.en: Manufacturers rigorously test new products to avoid skin reactions in those who use them.
The state should be rigorously checking these licensees.
The competition is rigorously even-handed in gender terms.

definitions.en: in a way that controls behaviour severely

examples.en: The rules were applied too rigorously.
examples.zh: 对规则的执行太教条了。

examples.en: Driving bans for serious offences should be rigorously enforced.
examples.zh: 应严格执行对严重违法的驾驶行为的禁令。

examples.en: The company has an extremely stringent and rigorously applied safety policy.
China has been rigorously enforcing the wearing of seat belts.
Impurities could stay in the soil for up to 16 years if not rigorously controlled.

definitions.en: the fact that people are made to follow rules in a very severe way

examples.en: They were punished with unusual rigour.
examples.zh: 他们遭到了非常严厉的惩罚。

examples.en: They survived the rigours of the winter.
examples.zh: 他们熬过了严冬。

definitions.en: the quality of being detailed, careful, and complete

examples.en: Her arguments lacked intellectual rigour.
examples.zh: 她的论点不够理智和严谨。

definitions.en: to pull apart; to tear or be torn violently and quickly

examples.en: His new trousers ripped when he bent down.
examples.zh: 他弯腰时把新裤子撑开了线。

examples.en: I ripped my shirt on a nail.
examples.zh: 我的衬衫被一颗钉子划破了。

examples.en: She excitedly ripped the package open.
examples.zh: 她兴奋地撕开了包裹。

examples.en: The wind ripped the flag to/into shreds (= into little pieces).
examples.zh: 风把旗子刮成了一缕缕的破布条。

examples.en: I wish the old fireplaces hadn't been ripped out.
examples.zh: 我真希望当时没有拆掉这些旧壁炉。

examples.en: We ripped up the carpets and laid a new wooden floor.
examples.zh: 我们拖走了地毯，铺了新木地板。

examples.en: I've ripped my trousers - can you stitch them up for me?
The sleeve of my jacket (got) caught on the door handle and ripped.
Rip the basil leaves into pieces and sprinkle them over the salad.
The eagle uses its beak to rip the meat from the carcass.
She ripped her dress on a nail that was protruding from the bench.

definitions.en: to copy pictures or sounds from a CD or DVD onto a computer
definitions.zh: （从光盘把图片或声音）撷取／复制（至电脑）

examples.en: How do I rip a DVD movie to my hard drive?
examples.zh: 怎样能把DVD电影拷到我的硬盘上？

definitions.en: a tear in a piece of cloth or paper

examples.en: Your sleeve has a rip in it.
examples.zh: 你袖子上裂了道口子。

definitions.en: a copy of a CD or DVD that you make on a computer
definitions.zh: （在计算机上制作的光盘）复制本，拷贝

examples.en: illegal rips of copyrighted music
examples.zh: 正版音乐的非法拷贝

definitions.en: abbreviation for rest in peace
definitions.zh: （墓碑铭文）愿其灵魂安息（rest in peace的缩写）

definitions.en: a way of doing something in which the same actions are done in the same way every time
definitions.zh: 例行公事，老规矩;（尤指）仪式

examples.en: Coffee and the newspaper are part of my morning ritual.
examples.zh: 喝咖啡和读报纸是我早晨必做的事情之一。

examples.en: The birds were performing a complex mating ritual.
examples.zh: 这些鸟在进行程序复杂的交配。

definitions.en: a person, group, etc. competing with others for the same thing or in the same area

examples.en: He beat his closest/nearest rival by 20 marks.
examples.zh: 他以领先20分的成绩打败了和他水平最接近的对手。

examples.en: The companies produce rival versions of the toy.
examples.zh: 这些公司生产的玩具跟我们的产品争夺市场。

examples.en: The company has decided not to go through with the takeover of its smaller rival.
The airline admitted being involved in a dirty tricks campaign to win customers from their rival.
She hired an assassin to eliminate her rival.
Big supermarkets can undercut all rivals, especially small high-street shops.
They beat several other rivals for the contract.

definitions.en: to be as good, clever, beautiful, etc. as someone or something else

examples.en: No computer can rival a human brain for/in complexity.
examples.zh: 没有任何计算机比得上人脑复杂。

examples.en: The beauty of the country is only rivalled by (= is as great in degree as) the violence of its politics.
examples.zh: 该国的美丽风光非同寻常，而其国内政治的残暴同样惊世骇俗。

definitions.en: to take money or property illegally from a place, organization, or person, often using violence

examples.en: The terrorists financed themselves by robbing banks.
examples.zh: 恐怖分子靠抢银行来获得资金。

examples.en: My wallet's gone! I've been robbed!
examples.zh: 我的钱包没了！我被偷了！

examples.en: They robbed the company of $2 million.
examples.zh: 他们从这家公司盗取了两百万英镑。

examples.en: A last-minute injury robbed me of my place on the team.
examples.zh: 我在最后一刻受了伤，失去了在球队中的位置。

examples.en: They were robbed of all their savings.
Police said the couple had been trussed up and robbed before being shot.
He was robbed while he was on holiday.
He was robbed of his wallet and car keys.
That was when I realized I'd been robbed.

definitions.en: behaving in ways that are not expected or not normal, often in a way that causes damage
definitions.zh: 异常的；行为失常的；常制造麻烦的

examples.en: a rogue state
examples.zh: 流氓国家

examples.en: rogue cells
examples.zh: 无赖细胞

definitions.en: to start behaving in a way that is not normal or expected, especially by leaving your group and doing something dangerous:
definitions.zh: 特种部队的一支小分队擅自离开，试图去解救人质。

examples.en: A Special Forces unit had gone rogue and was trying to free the hostage.
examples.zh: 特种部队的一支小分队擅自离开，试图去解救人质。

definitions.en: a person who behaves badly but who you still like

examples.en: "Come here, you little rogue!" chuckled my uncle.
examples.zh: “到这儿来，你这个小坏蛋！”叔叔咯咯地笑着说。

examples.en: The women all think he's a lovable old rogue.
examples.zh: 在所有女人眼里，他是个可爱的老活宝。

definitions.en: the act of making something, especially a product or service, available for the first time

examples.en: Since its rollout in fall of 1999, the online service has gained millions of members.
The improved product is scheduled for rollout in June.
The rollout of the new standards has gone well.
Thursday's release of statistics was the start of a two-year rollout of information from the Census Bureau.
The rollout marks the first time the company has developed a product for a specific ethnic group.
We ran into a few problems in the initial rollout .
The retailer is intensifying its charm offensive with the rollout of its new store format on Kensington High Street.
Today marked the proposal's first high-profile rollout.

definitions.en: the covering that forms the top of a building, vehicle, etc.

examples.en: The house has a sloping/flat/tiled/thatched roof.
examples.zh: 这所房子是斜顶／平顶／石板瓦顶／茅草（等）的。

examples.en: Put the luggage on the roof of the car.
examples.zh: 把行李放在车顶上。

examples.en: The roof (= upper surface) of the cave is 50 metres up.
examples.zh: 洞顶距地面有50米高。

examples.en: This cake is so dry that it sticks to the roof of your mouth (= upper surface of the mouth).
examples.zh: 这蛋糕很干，吃的时候会粘在上颚上。

examples.en: The slate roof differentiates this house from others in the area.
All I need is a roof over my head and a decent meal.
The roof tiles will need an overlap of several centimetres.
The garage has a pitched roof, not a flat one.
Water was splashing from a hole in the roof.

definitions.en: to put a roof on a building
definitions.zh: 给（建筑物）盖屋顶;给（建筑物）装屋顶

definitions.en: approximately

examples.en: There has been an increase of roughly 2.25 million.
examples.zh: 大概增长了225万。

examples.en: Roughly speaking, it's 2.25 million.
examples.zh: 大致来说，有225万。

examples.en: We have roughly similar tastes/roughly the same tastes.
examples.zh: 我们的口味大致相同。

examples.en: Chop up the onions and carrots roughly.
One box may look bigger than the other, but in fact they are roughly equal in volume.
The two scientists both made the same discovery independently, at roughly the same time.
The money I've saved corresponds roughly to the amount I need for my course.
The prices were very roughly calculated - it looked as though he'd done them on the back of an envelope.

definitions.en: without taking a lot of care to make something perfect
definitions.zh: 粗糙地；不精细地；简单地

examples.en: Roughly chop the tomatoes.
examples.zh: 把西红柿简单切成几大块。

definitions.en: in a violent or angry way

examples.en: He pushed the children roughly to one side.
examples.zh: 他粗暴地将孩子们推到一边。

examples.en: "And what's going on here?" he said roughly.
examples.zh: “这儿怎么回事儿？”他粗鲁地问道。

definitions.en: in a circular direction or position; around

examples.en: The moon goes round the earth.
examples.zh: 月球围绕地球转。

examples.en: We ran round (the outside of the house) to the back, looking for the dog.
examples.zh: 我们跑到房子后面去找狗。

examples.en: The idea has been going round and round in my head all day (= I can't stop thinking about it).
examples.zh: 这个想法一整天都萦绕在我心头。

examples.en: When one engine stopped, we had to turn round (= turn to face the opposite direction) and fly home.
examples.zh: 飞机的一台发动机熄火了，我们不得不掉头飞回。

examples.en: He tried to go round the keeper (= move with the football past a goalkeeper) but was forced wide.
examples.zh: 他想绕过守门员但是球被迫射偏了。

examples.en: The discussion kept going round in circles.
Christmas comes round so quickly!
She wears a gold cross round her neck.
It has always been my declared intention to sail round the world.
I'll just turn the car round and go back the way we've come.

definitions.en: in every part of a place, or in various parts of a place

examples.en: The landlord showed me round (the house).
examples.zh: 房主带我（在房子里）四处看了看。

examples.en: I had to go all round town to find a hotel that was open.
examples.zh: 我不得不在城中四处寻找还在营业的宾馆。

examples.en: This virus has been going round (the school) (= many people have had it) .
examples.zh: 这种病毒（在学校）四处传播。

examples.en: I walked round the town.
I'll take you round the grounds.
We went round the shops.
There's a rumour going round the office to that effect.
There's a stomach bug going round the school.

definitions.en: on all or some sides of something

examples.en: We sat round the fire.
examples.zh: 我们围着火炉坐着。

examples.en: The house has trees all round.
examples.zh: 这座房子四周都是树。

examples.en: The pyramid is 50 metres high and 100 metres round (the base).
examples.zh: 这座金字塔高50米，底部周长100米。

examples.en: Everyone for a mile round (= in the area) heard the explosion.
examples.zh: 方圆1英里之内的人都听到了爆炸声。

examples.en: They live in an idyllic country cottage, with roses round the door.
As soon as he appeared, reporters crowded round.
If we put the chairs a bit closer together , we should be able to get another one round the table.
There was a black line round the edge of the page.
The spectators had gathered round the green to watch the winning putt.

definitions.en: in a particular direction

examples.en: The garden is round the back (of the house).
examples.zh: 花园在（房子）后面。

examples.en: I used to live round (= near) here when I was a child.
examples.zh: 我童年时曾住在这一带。

examples.en: You must come round (to my house) sometime soon.
examples.zh: 你找个时间一定（到我家）来坐坐。

examples.en: We're going round (= to) the pub for a quick drink.
examples.zh: 我们要去酒馆抓紧喝上一杯。

examples.en: There's a restaurant round the corner that serves cheap and cheerful food.
Come round tonight and we'll watch a video.
There are two entrances - one at the front and one round the back.
He said he wouldn't pay up so I sent my brother round to put the frighteners on him.
Who's the head honcho round here?

definitions.en: shaped like a ball or circle, or curved

examples.en: Tennis balls and oranges are round.
examples.zh: 网球和橘子是圆的。

examples.en: a round hole/stone/table/window
examples.zh: 圆孔／石／桌／窗

examples.en: a round face
examples.zh: 圆脸

examples.en: round eyes
examples.zh: 圆眼睛

examples.en: The sculpture consists of two round sections connected by a series of wires.
They had a large round table in the dining room.
The bullet left a small round hole in the glass.
Use the cutter to cut the pastry into round shapes.
I'd like one of the round loaves, please.

definitions.en: (of a number) whole or complete; given to the nearest 1, 10, 100, etc. and not as exact amounts
definitions.zh: 整数的;用整数表示的;用约整数表示的

examples.en: 2.8 to the nearest round number is 3.
examples.zh: 与2.8最接近的整数是3。

examples.en: "I've got 95 bottles here for you." "Could you make it a round hundred, please?"
examples.zh: “我这儿有95瓶给你。”“能不能凑个整数，给100瓶怎么样？”

definitions.en: a number of things or group of events
definitions.zh: 一连串的事情;一系列的事件;一系列活动

examples.en: Russia and America will hold another round of talks next month.
examples.zh: 俄罗斯和美国将在下个月举行又一轮会谈。

examples.en: When we were young life was just one long round of parties/pleasure.
examples.zh: 我们年轻时，生活的内容好像就是一个又一个聚会／一件又一件令人开心的事情。

examples.en: It's your turn to buy a round.
examples.zh: 该你请大家都喝上一杯了。

examples.en: He bought a round of drinks to say thank you.
examples.zh: 他请大家都喝了一杯以表示谢意。

examples.en: He has a milk/paper round.
examples.zh: 他挨家送奶／报纸。

examples.en: Cooper was counted out in the final round.
He crashed out of the French Open in the second round.
We often play a round of golf at the weekend.
Persuading Chris to buy a round of drinks is like getting blood out of a stone.
She's through to the next round of interviews.

definitions.en: a part of a competition

examples.en: She was knocked out of the championship in the third round.
examples.zh: 她在锦标赛的第三轮被淘汰。

definitions.en: a bullet or other single piece of ammunition

examples.en: The soldiers had only 20 rounds left.
examples.zh: 士兵们只剩下20发子弹了。

definitions.en: to go around something

examples.en: Colin rounded the corner at high speed.
examples.zh: 科林急速拐弯。

examples.en: He rounded the keeper and scored.
examples.zh: 他把球带过了守门员射门得分。

definitions.en: a letter, usually of demands or complaints, that is signed by many people
definitions.zh: （通常指提出请求或投诉的）联名信，联名声明书

definitions.en: a competition in which all the players play against each other at least once
used for describing what often or usually happens

examples.en: Health and safety rules are routinely flouted/ignored.
examples.zh: 卫生和安全法规经常被忽视。

definitions.en: (of land) wild and not even; not easy to travel over
definitions.zh: （土地）荒芜崎岖的；崎岖难行的

examples.en: rugged landscape/terrain/hills/cliffs
examples.zh: 崎岖的地貌／高低不平的地形／陡峭的山丘／嶙峋突兀的悬崖

definitions.en: strong and simple; not delicate
definitions.zh: 坚固的；耐用的；结实的；简单皮实的

examples.en: Jeeps are rugged vehicles, designed for rough conditions.
examples.zh: 吉普车是种很坚固耐用的车，专为崎岖难行的路况而设计的。

examples.en: She fell for his rugged good looks.
examples.zh: 她为他英俊粗犷的相貌所倾倒。

definitions.en: to spoil or destroy something completely

examples.en: Huge modern hotels have ruined this once unspoilt coastline.
examples.zh: 庞然大物般的现代旅馆已经完全毁掉了这片曾经如世外桃源般的海岸。

examples.en: Her injury ruined her chances of winning the race.
examples.zh: 她受了伤，毁掉了赢得比赛的机会。

examples.en: Cheap imported goods are ruining many businesses.
examples.zh: 廉价的进口商品使众多公司正走向破产。

examples.en: If there's a scandal I'll be ruined!
examples.zh: 如果出了丑闻，我就会身败名裂！

examples.en: A modern extension on the old building would ruin its architectural integrity.
The poor sound quality ruined an otherwise splendid film.
Lucy has just ruined her new dress. That's children for you.
I think the new extension ruins the proportions of the building.
If you scuff your feet like that, you'll ruin your shoes.

definitions.en: the process or state of being spoiled or destroyed

examples.en: The car accident meant the ruin of all her hopes.
examples.zh: 这场撞车事故使她的一切希望都彻底破灭了。

examples.en: They let the palace fall into ruin.
examples.zh: 他们听任那座宫殿沦为一片废墟。

examples.en: Many companies are on the edge/brink/verge of ruin.
examples.zh: 很多公司濒临破产。

examples.en: Alcohol was my ruin (= the thing that spoiled my life)
examples.zh: 酒毁掉了我的一生。

examples.en: We visited a Roman ruin.
examples.zh: 我们游览了一处古罗马遗址。

examples.en: the ruins of the ancient city of Carthage
examples.zh: 迦太基古城遗址

examples.en: The town lay in ruins after years of bombing.
examples.zh: 数年的轰炸已把这座城镇变成一片废墟。

examples.en: The economy was in ruins after the war.
examples.zh: 战后经济完全陷于崩溃之中。

definitions.en: an unofficial interesting story or piece of news that might be true or invented, and quickly spreads from person to person

examples.en: Rumours are going round (the school) about Mr Mason and his assistant.
examples.zh: 有关梅森先生和他助手的流言蜚语（在学校里）满天飞。

examples.en: She's circulating/spreading rumours that the manager is going to resign.
examples.zh: 她到处散播谣言，说经理要辞职了。

examples.en: I heard a rumour that she'd been seeing Luke Harrison.
examples.zh: 我听到有传言说她一直跟卢克‧哈里森在一起。

examples.en: Rumour has it (that) you're going to be the next managing director. Is it true?
examples.zh: 大家都说你将成为下一任总经理，这是不是真的？

examples.en: I heard a rumour that she's leaving, but apparently there's nothing in it.
A rumour has surfaced that the company is about to go out of business.
The president has consistently denied the rumours.
He denied all responsibility for the rumours that have been circulating.
The company moved quickly to quash rumours that it is losing money.

definitions.en: a detailed report

examples.en: Here's a rundown on/of the activities of our ten biggest competitors.
examples.zh: 这儿有一份有关我们的10家最大竞争对手经营活动的详细报告。

definitions.en: a reduction in the size or quality of something

examples.en: the general rundown of the army
examples.zh: 军队的一般性减员

definitions.en: not thinking or worrying about any pain caused to others; cruel

examples.en: ruthless ambition
examples.zh: 冷酷无情的野心

examples.en: a ruthless dictator
examples.zh: 残忍的独裁者

examples.en: Some people believe that to succeed in this world you have to be ruthless.
examples.zh: 有些人认为，在这个世界上要想成功必须冷酷无情。

definitions.en: to give up something that is valuable to you in order to help another person

examples.en: Many women sacrifice interesting careers for their families.
examples.zh: 许多女性为了家庭牺牲了自己感兴趣的工作。

examples.en: She's had to sacrifice a lot for that relationship.
He's the world's best tennis player but he's sacrificed everything else in his life for it.
He has sacrificed his personal life for the sake of celebrity.
Many women sacrifice their own ambitions to put their family first.
It's the only bit of free time I get in the week and I'm not prepared to sacrifice it.

definitions.en: to kill an animal or a person and offer them to a god or gods
definitions.zh: 献祭;以（人或动物）为祭品

definitions.en: the act of giving up something that is valuable to you in order to help someone else

examples.en: We had to make sacrifices in order to pay for our children's education.
examples.zh: 为了支付孩子们的教育费用，我们不得不作出牺牲。

examples.en: They cared for their disabled son for 27 years, at great personal sacrifice.
examples.zh: 27年来，他们为照料有残疾的儿子作出了巨大的个人牺牲。

examples.en: You have to make sacrifices if you want to achieve your ambition.
No one raises a family without making a few sacrifices.
It's a career that leaves you with almost no free time and that's a sacrifice I'm not prepared to make.
She supported her husband throughout his 40-year career and at great personal sacrifice.
We make sacrifices now to give our children a better tomorrow.

definitions.en: the act of killing an animal or person and offering them to a god or gods, or the animal, etc. that is offered

examples.en: The people offered a lamb on the altar as a sacrifice for their sins.
examples.zh: 人们在祭坛上献祭一头羔羊作为赎罪的祭品。

definitions.en: in order to help or bring advantage to someone
definitions.zh: 为了（某人）的利益；为了帮助（某人）

examples.en: Please do it, for David's sake.
examples.zh: 为了戴维，请做这件事。

examples.en: Their parents only stayed together for the sake of the children.
examples.zh: 他们的父母仅仅是为了孩子才没有分开。

examples.en: I hope for both our sakes that you're right!
examples.zh: 为你我着想，我希望你是对的！

examples.en: I'm trying to keep life normal for the sake of my two boys.
They were very unhappily married but kept up appearances for the sake of their children.
I hope for your sake that she's happy with the arrangements.
I hope he's there for Paul's sake.
I knew I had to stay calm for Maria's sake.

definitions.en: because of, or for the purpose of something

examples.en: Let's not disagree for the sake of (= because of) a few dollars.
examples.zh: 咱们不要因为几块钱闹别扭。

examples.en: Let's say, just for the sake of argument/for argument's sake (= for the purpose of this discussion), that prices rise by three percent this year.
examples.zh: 为了便于讨论，我们假设今年的价格上涨3％。

examples.en: You're only arguing for the sake of arguing (= because you like arguing).
examples.zh: 你只是为争论而争论。

definitions.en: used to emphasize requests or orders when you are angry or have lost patience
definitions.zh: 做做好事吧，看在上帝份上，看在老天份上

examples.en: For goodness' sake don't let her know I told you!
examples.zh: 行行好，千万别让她知道是我告诉你的！

definitions.en: a Japanese alcoholic drink made from rice and usually drunk warm
having a healthy mind and not mentally ill
definitions.zh: 心智健全的，神智正常的;头脑清醒的

examples.en: In the doctor's opinion he was sane at the time of the murder.
examples.zh: 按照医生的看法，他在谋杀时神志清醒。

examples.en: The only thing which keeps me sane after a hard day in the office is jogging!
examples.zh: 在办公室辛苦工作一天后，唯一能使我保持头脑清醒的方法就是慢跑。

examples.en: It was a sane decision and one we all respected.
examples.zh: 这是一个明智的决定，也是我们都尊重的决定。

definitions.en: to make something completely clean and free from bacteria
definitions.zh: 给…消毒;对…进行卫生处理;使清洁

definitions.en: to change something in order to make it less strongly expressed, less harmful, or less offensive
definitions.zh: 净化;消除…中过激（或有害、令人不快的）内容

examples.en: The military wants to allow only a sanitized report/version of the incident to become public.
examples.zh: 有关该事件，军方希望只公开经过粉饰的报告。

definitions.en: to make something or someone completely wet

examples.en: The grass had been saturated by overnight rain.
examples.zh: 一夜的雨淋透了草地。

examples.en: He had cut his leg badly, and his trousers were saturated with/in blood.
examples.zh: 他划破了腿，伤势严重，裤子都被血浸透了。

definitions.en: to fill a thing or place completely so that no more can be added

examples.en: The police saturated (= a large number of police officers were sent into) the area.
examples.zh: 大批警察被派往该地区。

examples.en: Since the US market has now been saturated, drug dealers are looking to Europe.
examples.zh: 由于美国的市场现在已经饱和，毒贩们开始转向欧洲。

definitions.en: a saturated fat specialized
the act or result of filling a thing or place completely so that no more can be added

examples.en: market saturation
examples.zh: 市场饱和

examples.en: The airport is approaching saturation with more than 20 million passengers per year.
examples.zh: 该机场正接近饱和，每年有超过 2000 万乘客。

examples.en: The theme park industry has already reached its saturation point.
examples.zh: 主题公园行业已经达到饱和点。

definitions.en: the act of making something or someone completely wet
definitions.zh: 饱和（程度）;浸透，渗透，湿透

examples.en: Saturation of the soils is greatly increased by any increased level of the lake.
examples.zh: 土壤的饱和度随着湖水水位任何程度的升高而大大增加。

definitions.en: the degree to which something is dissolved in something else, compared with the maximum possible degree

examples.en: As the degree of saturation increases, the oil progresses towards being fully hydrogenated.
examples.zh: 随着饱和程度上升，油朝着完全氢化的方向变化。

examples.en: His blood oxygen saturation should be between 98 and 100.
examples.zh: 他的血氧饱和度应该在98和100之间。

definitions.en: Savoury food is salty or spicy and not sweet in taste.

examples.en: a savoury dish/sauce/flavour
examples.zh: 带咸味的一道菜／一种调料／一种味道

examples.en: savoury dumplings/pancakes
examples.zh: 咸汤圆／薄饼

examples.en: A pie can be sweet or savoury.
examples.zh: 馅饼可以是甜的或咸的。

examples.en: That hotel doesn't have a very savoury reputation.
examples.zh: 那家宾馆的声誉不太好。

definitions.en: not easy to find or get

examples.en: Food and clean water were becoming scarce.
examples.zh: 食物和洁净的水越来越少了。

examples.en: scarce resources
examples.zh: 稀缺资源

examples.en: With jobs so scarce, many young people have nothing to do.
In wartime, food is often scarce.
Research evidence in this area is scarce.
scarce water resources
Details are still scarce.

definitions.en: almost not

examples.en: I could scarce believe my eyes.
examples.zh: 我几乎不能相信我的眼睛。

definitions.en: frightening

examples.en: a scary movie/story
examples.zh: 恐怖电影／故事

examples.en: She's a scary woman.
I've always found him a bit scary.
When you think how much power these people have, it's scary.
She'd had a dream in which scary monsters were chasing her.
I really thought I was going to fall - it was a scary moment.

definitions.en: to (cause to) move far apart in different directions

examples.en: The protesters scattered at the sound of gunshots.
examples.zh: 听到枪声后抗议者便散开了。

examples.en: The soldiers came in and scattered the crowd.
examples.zh: 士兵进来把人群驱散了。

examples.en: Toss the salad and scatter the nuts on top.
Scatter the cake with a little caster sugar.
The wreckage of the car was scattered over the roadside.
Her ashes were scattered at sea.
Debris from the aircraft was scattered over a large area.

definitions.en: to cover a surface with things that are far apart and in no particular arrangement

examples.en: Scatter the powder around the plants.
examples.zh: 把这种药粉撒在植物周围。

examples.en: I scattered grass seed all over the lawn.
examples.zh: 我将草籽撒在整个草坪上。

examples.en: I scattered the whole lawn with grass seed.
examples.zh: 我在整个草坪上都撒下草籽。

definitions.en: a description of possible actions or events in the future

examples.en: There are several possible scenarios.
examples.zh: 有几种可能出现的局面。

examples.en: a horrific/nightmare scenario such as a Third World War
examples.zh: 可怕的／梦魇般的设想，如第三次世界大战

examples.en: The most likely scenario is that house prices rise again, forcing interest rates up.
The worst scenario is that we lose two or three thousand pounds.
There are several possible scenarios.
Imagine the scenario - David turns up and we're all there, waiting for him.
A doomsday scenario is not out of the question.

definitions.en: a written plan of the characters and events in a play or film
definitions.zh: 剧本提纲;剧情梗概;电影脚本

definitions.en: to cry or say something loudly and usually on a high note, especially because of strong emotions such as fear, excitement, or anger
definitions.zh: （尤指因恐惧、兴奋、愤怒等强烈情绪而）尖叫，惊呼

examples.en: A spider landed on her pillow and she screamed.
examples.zh: 一只蜘蛛爬上了她的枕头，她惊叫起来。

examples.en: Through the smoke, the rescuers could hear people screaming for help.
examples.zh: 透过烟雾，救援者可以听见人们在喊救命。

examples.en: He was screaming in/with pain and begging for anaesthetic.
examples.zh: 他疼得直叫，哀求用麻醉药。

examples.en: They screamed with laughter at her jokes.
examples.zh: 听了她的笑话，他们大笑了起来。

examples.en: Ken screamed (out) a warning telling people to get out of the way.
examples.zh: 肯大叫着警告人们别挡路。

examples.en: Mrs Brown screamed (= shouted angrily) at Joel for dropping the test-tube.
examples.zh: 布朗太太生气地冲乔尔大喊大叫，因为他把试管摔破了。

examples.en: I've never found screaming (and shouting) (= shouting angrily) at my staff to be very effective.
examples.zh: 我从来都认为冲着员工大喊大叫是没有什么用的。

examples.en: "I wish you were dead!" she screamed (= shouted angrily).
examples.zh: “我巴不得你去死！”她愤怒地叫道。

examples.en: I tried to apologize, but he just screamed abuse/obscenities at me.
examples.zh: 我想道歉，但他却冲我破口大骂。

examples.en: The cars screamed round the bend/past the spectators.
examples.zh: 汽车尖啸着转弯／从观众面前呼啸而过。

examples.en: The ambulance raced round the corner with its tyres screaming.
examples.zh: 救护车拐过街角，轮胎发出刺耳的声音。

examples.en: "Freeze or I'll shoot!" screamed the gunman.
He showed us a snake and Milly screamed.
He was greeted by an army of screaming teenagers.
I heard a woman in the house opposite screaming for help.
The audience screamed their appreciation.

definitions.en: If a word or image screams (out), it gets attention because it is very big or easy to notice.

examples.en: "Latest Plane Disaster!" screamed the newspaper headlines the next day.
examples.zh: “最新飞机失事！”，第二天报纸的头版头条用了这个令人触目惊心的标题。

definitions.en: a loud, high sound you make when very frightened, excited, or angry

examples.en: a scream of pain/rage/joy/laughter
examples.zh: 疼痛／愤怒／快乐／欢笑的尖叫

examples.en: No one heard their screams.
examples.zh: 没有人听到他们的尖叫。

examples.en: She let out a piercing/shrill scream.
examples.zh: 她发出了一声刺耳的尖叫。

definitions.en: a person, thing, or situation that is very funny
definitions.zh: 令人捧腹的人（或事物、情景）

examples.en: Jane's such a scream - her jokes have me in stitches.
examples.zh: 简真是个活宝，她讲的笑话让我捧腹大笑。

definitions.en: having a lot of experience of doing something and therefore knowing how to do it well

examples.en: a seasoned traveller
examples.zh: 经验丰富的旅行者

examples.en: a seasoned campaigner for human rights
examples.zh: 经验丰富的人权活动家

examples.en: Nobody, not even the most seasoned professional, can observe a child the way a parent can.
She is a seasoned observer of the state's political scene.

definitions.en: relating to or caused by an earthquake

examples.en: seismic activity/waves
examples.zh: 地震活动／地震波

examples.en: The news that the chairman would resign set off seismic waves in the business community.
examples.zh: 主席要辞职的消息在商界引发了剧烈震动。

definitions.en: the set of someone's characteristics, such as personality and ability, that are not physical and make that person different from other people

examples.en: The hero of the movie finally finds his true self (= discovers what his true personality and feelings are).
examples.zh: 电影的主角最终找到了真正的自我。

examples.en: When I saw them this afternoon they were more like their old/normal selves (= as they were in the past).
examples.zh: 我今天下午见到他们的时候，他们更像是平时的老样子了。

examples.en: a sense of self
examples.zh: 自我意识

examples.en: She's not her customary cheerful self today.
The Minister was her usual evasive self, skilfully dodging reporters' questions about her possible resignation.
He seemed ill at ease and not his usual self.
It was a long time after the accident before he seemed like his former self.
He wasn't his usual ebullient self.

definitions.en: interest only in your own advantage

examples.en: Her reply was typical of her constant regard for self.
examples.zh: 她的答复明显说明她一贯只想着私利。

definitions.en: of or by yourself or itself

examples.en: self-educated
examples.zh: 自学的

examples.en: a self-winding watch
examples.zh: 自动上发条的手表

definitions.en: containing or having everything that is needed within itself

examples.en: The apartment is small, but completely self-contained, with its own bathroom, kitchen, and living area.
examples.zh: 公寓很小，但完全独立，拥有自己的浴室、厨房和起居空间。

examples.en: She's very self-contained and isn't at all worried about moving to a big city where she won't know anybody.
examples.zh: 她非常独立，对于要搬到一个举目无亲的大城市去一点儿都不担心。

definitions.en: connected with the meanings of words
the symbol ; used in writing between two parts of a sentence, usually when each of the two parts could form grammatical sentences on their own. A semicolon can also separate the things in a list.
based on or acting on good judgment and practical ideas or understanding

examples.en: a sensible answer/approach/compromise/option
examples.zh: 明智的回答／方法／妥协／选择

examples.en: a sensible person
examples.zh: 理智的人

examples.en: I think the sensible thing to do is call and ask for directions.
examples.zh: 我觉得明智之举是打个电话问好路。

examples.en: It would be sensible to take an umbrella.
examples.zh: 带把伞是明智的。

examples.en: It could be cold and wet so pack some sensible clothes.
examples.zh: 天可能会又冷又潮，带上些合适的衣服。

examples.en: I'm sure Jenny can be relied on - she seems eminently sensible.
That would be a more sensible way of dealing with the problem.
Wouldn't it be more sensible to eat now before we go?
It would seem more sensible to do the research now before we start on the project.
That was a very sensible decision.

definitions.en: having an understanding of a situation
definitions.zh: 意识到的;知道的;觉察到的

examples.en: He did not appear to be sensible of the difficulties that lay ahead.
examples.zh: 他看上去并没有意识到前面的困难。

definitions.en: easily upset by the things people say or do, or causing people to be upset, embarrassed, or angry
definitions.zh: 神经过敏的;易被冒犯的;敏感的

examples.en: Her reply showed that she was very sensitive to criticism.
examples.zh: 她的回答表明她对批评很敏感。

examples.en: He was very sensitive about his scar and thought everyone was staring at him.
examples.zh: 他对自己的伤疤很敏感，以为每个人都在盯着他看。

examples.en: Sex education and birth control are sensitive issues.
examples.zh: 性教育和计划生育都是敏感的话题。

examples.en: The stolen car contained military documents described as very sensitive.
examples.zh: 被盗的汽车里有据称非常敏感的军事文件。

examples.en: Don't be so sensitive!
She's very sensitive about her weight.
He's very sensitive to criticism of his children.
I have to be very careful with what I say because she's so sensitive.
If I'd known how sensitive she was I wouldn't have made the remark.

definitions.en: understanding what other people need, and being helpful and kind to them
definitions.zh: 善解人意的;体贴的;体谅的

examples.en: Representatives of the company claim their plan will be sensitive to local needs.
examples.zh: 公司代表们说，他们的计划会照顾到当地的需要。

examples.en: In the movie, he plays a concerned and sensitive father trying to bring up two teenage children on his own.
examples.zh: 在影片中，他扮演了一个关怀备至、体贴入微的父亲，努力独自抚养两个十几岁的孩子。

examples.en: You have to be quite sensitive when you're dealing with adolescents.
It needs someone a bit sensitive to handle this sort of thing.
She's very sweet and sensitive.
He's not the most sensitive of people.
These are vulnerable people - you have to be sensitive to their needs.

definitions.en: easily influenced, changed, or damaged, especially by a physical activity or effect

examples.en: Some people's teeth are highly sensitive to cold.
examples.zh: 一些人的牙齿对寒冷高度敏感。

examples.en: sensitive skin
examples.zh: 敏感的皮肤

examples.en: The patient's responses are recorded on a sensitive piece of equipment which gives extremely accurate readings.
examples.zh: 病人的反应由一台灵敏仪器记录下来，该仪器能显示出极其精确的读数。

definitions.en: reacting to the presence of a particular substance or physical effect

examples.en: They have heat-sensitive infrared cameras.
examples.zh: 他们有热敏感红外摄像头。

examples.en: We use a pH-sensitive dye.
examples.zh: 我们使用ph敏感染料。

examples.en: After the exam, the patient's eyes are light-sensitive for two to four hours, which can cause blurred vision.
examples.zh: 检查后，病人的眼睛在两到四小时内对光敏感，有可能会导致视力模糊。

examples.en: Some types of debt are particularly risk-sensitive.
examples.zh: 有些债务风险敏感度尤其高。

examples.en: Anti-fraud features include heat-sensitive inks that are revealed if the prescription is photocopied.
The light-sensitive lenses of his sunglasses are turning black.
These findings suggest BPRP may be a glucose-sensitive protein.
We measured responses to dehydration in both desiccation-tolerant and dessication-sensitive material.

definitions.en: a person employed to guard something

examples.en: A policeman stood sentinel at the entrance.
examples.zh: 一个警察站在入口处放哨。

examples.en: the Fort Lauderdale Sun-Sentinel
examples.zh: 《劳德代尔堡太阳卫报》

definitions.en: existing or happening independently or in a different physical space

examples.en: The art department and the music department are in two separate buildings.
examples.zh: 艺术系和音乐系在两座不同的大楼中。

examples.en: I try to keep meat separate from other food in the fridge.
examples.zh: 我尽量把肉同冰箱里的其他食物分开放。

examples.en: I have my public life and my private life, and as far as possible I try to keep them separate.
examples.zh: 我有自己的公众生活和私人生活，并尽可能使两者分开。

examples.en: Three youths have been shot and killed in separate incidents this month.
examples.zh: 这个月有3名年轻人在不同的事件中被枪杀。

examples.en: We each have a separate bedroom but share a communal kitchen.
Householders are given four separate receptacles for their rubbish.
The waiter asked if we were all together so I explained that we were two separate parties.
He regarded the north of the country as a separate cultural entity.
The museums work closely together, but are separate legal entities.

definitions.en: to (cause to) divide into parts

examples.en: The north and south of the country are separated by a mountain range.
examples.zh: 这个国家的南北两部分被一条山脉隔开。

examples.en: You can get a special device for separating egg whites from yolks.
examples.zh: 你可以买一种把蛋白与蛋黄分开的专用器具。

examples.en: The top and bottom sections are quite difficult to separate.
examples.zh: 顶部和底部很难分开。

examples.en: It's sometimes hard to separate fact from fiction in what she says.
In many churches the side aisles are separated from the central aisle by a row of arches.
A narrow band of grass separated the greenhouse from the vegetable garden.
A grille separated the prisoners from their visitors.
I've separated the documents into three piles.

definitions.en: to make people move apart or into different places, or to move apart

examples.en: At school they always tried to separate Jane and me because we were troublemakers.
examples.zh: 在学校里，他们总是设法把简和我分开，因为我们是捣蛋鬼。

examples.en: Somehow, in the rush to get out of the building, I got separated from my mother.
examples.zh: 急急忙忙冲出大楼的时候，不知怎么的，我和妈妈走散了。

examples.en: Perhaps we should separate now and meet up later.
examples.zh: 或许我们现在应该分开，晚些时候再见面。

definitions.en: to consider two people or things as different or not related
definitions.zh: 分开考虑；认为（两者）不相关

examples.en: You can't separate morality from politics.
examples.zh: 你不能把道德与政治分开考虑。

definitions.en: If a liquid separates, it becomes two different liquids.
to start to live in a different place from your husband or wife because the relationship has ended

examples.en: My parents separated when I was six and divorced a couple of years later.
examples.zh: 我6岁的时候父母分居，几年之后他们便离婚了。

definitions.en: used to describe a person who repeatedly commits a similar crime or carries out a similar bad act, or the crime or act itself
definitions.zh: （重罪案犯或重案）连环的，连续的

examples.en: She wrote a thriller about a brutal serial killer/murderer.
examples.zh: 她写了部有关一名冷血连环杀手的惊悚小说。

examples.en: a serial adulterer/womaniser
examples.zh: 连续作案的强奸犯／沉湎女色难以自拔的人

examples.en: serial murders/killings
examples.zh: 连环谋杀案

examples.en: a serial abuser
serial abuse of children
a serial bomber
a serial sex offender
a serial murderer

definitions.en: broadcast or published in several separate parts, one after another

examples.en: She is currently filming a serial drama for the BBC.
examples.zh: 她正在为BBC拍摄一部系列剧。

examples.en: a serial novel
examples.zh: 系列小说

definitions.en: sending information one bit (= unit of information) at a time

examples.en: a serial cable
examples.zh: 串行电缆

examples.en: serial communication
examples.zh: 串行通信

definitions.en: arranged one after another

examples.en: He had difficulty in remembering the serial order of the tasks.
examples.zh: 他记不住任务的次序是什么。

definitions.en: a story on television or radio or in a newspaper, etc. that is broadcast or printed in separate parts
definitions.zh: （电视或广播）连续剧;（报纸上的）连载故事

examples.en: Most of her novels have been made into television serials at some time.
examples.zh: 她的大多数小说都已在某个时候被拍成了电视连续剧。

definitions.en: to reach a decision or an agreement about something, or to end a disagreement
definitions.zh: 确定;决定;解决，结束（争端等）

examples.en: Good, that's all settled - you send out the invitations for the party, and I'll organize the food.
examples.zh: 好，那就这么定了——你发聚会的请帖，我来准备吃的。

examples.en: They haven't yet settled when the wedding is going to be.
examples.zh: 他们还没有确定婚礼在什么时候举行。

examples.en: "The tickets are €40 each." "Well, that settles that - I can't afford that much."
examples.zh: “入场券每张40欧元。”“好吧，那就算了吧，我付不起那么多钱。”

examples.en: I'd like to get this matter settled once and for all (= reach a final decision on it).
examples.zh: 我想把这件事了结了。

examples.en: The details of the contract have not yet been settled.
examples.zh: 合同的细节问题仍然没有谈妥。

examples.en: Our lawyer advised us that it would be better to settle out of court (= reach an agreement in a legal case without it being decided in a court of law).
examples.zh: 我们的律师建议我们最好在庭外和解。

examples.en: It took months to settle (= bring to an end) the dispute/strike.
examples.zh: 用了好几个月的时间才解决这次争端／结束这次罢工。

examples.en: My father and I have agreed finally to settle our differences (= stop arguing).
examples.zh: 我和我父亲最终同意不再争论。

examples.en: They had to go to court to settle the argument.
They're still dithering about who to invite, but we really need to get it settled.
He still needs to settle the date of the meeting.
That's settled - I never want to see him again.
We haven't quite settled who's responsible for what.

definitions.en: to relax into a comfortable position
definitions.zh: （使）（舒舒服服地）坐下；安顿下来

examples.en: After dinner we settled in front of the television for the evening.
examples.zh: 晚饭后，我们舒舒服服地坐在电视机前。

examples.en: The dentist told her patient to settle back in the chair.
examples.zh: 牙医让病人靠在椅子上放松。

examples.en: He settled himself down with a newspaper, and waited for the flight to arrive.
examples.zh: 他安心坐下来看报纸，等候航班到来。

examples.en: I settled back and waited for the film to start.
We settled in our comfortable carriage and watched the scenery roll by.
I couldn't seem to settle the children in bed.
I settled myself with a cup of tea and thought about what had happened.

definitions.en: to go and live somewhere, especially permanently

examples.en: After they got married, they settled in Brooklyn.
examples.zh: 他们结婚后在布鲁克林定居下来。

examples.en: America was first settled by people who came across from Asia over 25,000 years ago.
examples.zh: 2.5万年前，从亚洲来的移民首次在美洲安家落户。

examples.en: We've moved all over the country, but I'd like to settle here now.
The Romans settled in Britain in the first century AD.
After decades of desert life, they settled in the mountains.

definitions.en: to move to a lower level and stay there; to drop

examples.en: The house had been empty for years, and dust had settled on all the surfaces.
examples.zh: 这座房子多年来一直空着，所有东西上面都落了一层灰。

examples.en: Do you think the snow will settle (= remain on the ground and other surfaces without melting)?
examples.zh: 你认为会积雪吗？

examples.en: The contents of this package may settle (= fall towards the bottom of the container and so seem to be less).
examples.zh: 包裹里的东西可以再压紧一些。

examples.en: The juice goes thick at the bottom when it settles.
Snow had settled on the rooftops.
Once dust settles in the gaps, it will not work as well.

definitions.en: to pay, especially money that you owe
definitions.zh: 支付（尤指欠款或被索款项）;偿付;结算

examples.en: Please settle your account/bill without further delay.
examples.zh: 请立即付清账款／账单，勿再拖延。

examples.en: It took the insurance company months to settle my claim.
examples.zh: 保险公司用了好几个月才付清我的理赔金。

examples.en: Payment of your account is now overdue, and we must ask you to settle (= pay the money you owe) immediately.
examples.zh: 您账上的款项已逾期未付，请您务必马上付清。

definitions.en: to become quiet and calm, or to make something or someone do this

examples.en: The weather is expected to settle towards the end of the week.
examples.zh: 天气预计会在周末时稳定下来。

examples.en: I'll call you back as soon as I've settled the kids for the night.
examples.zh: 我把孩子们安顿下来睡觉后，就马上给你回电话。

examples.en: Before a performance, she takes three deep breaths to settle her nerves.
examples.zh: 在演出之前，她会深呼吸3次来平定紧张的情绪。

examples.en: We're very busy this week, but things should settle (down) a bit after the weekend.
examples.zh: 我们这个星期很忙，不过周末之后事情应该会少一点儿。
