examples.en: Joe's parents are very worried about him because he doesn't seem to be able to settle to (= to give his whole attention to) anything.
examples.zh: 乔的父母很为他担心，因为乔似乎做什么事都不能够安下心来。

definitions.en: to reach and remain at a certain level or in a certain state

examples.en: The pound rose slightly against the dollar today, then settled at $1.53.
examples.zh: 英镑对美元的汇率今天略有上升，之后便停留在1.53美元的价位上。

examples.en: A peaceful expression settled on her face.
examples.zh: 她脸上现出一副平静的神色。

examples.en: After the recent riots, an uneasy calm has settled on the city.
examples.zh: 最近的暴乱平息后，城里笼罩着一种令人不安的平静气氛。

definitions.en: to break or separate, especially by cutting
definitions.zh: 弄断；分开；（尤指）切断，割断

examples.en: The knife severed an artery and he bled to death.
examples.zh: 刀子割断了动脉，他流血过多而死。

examples.en: Her foot was severed from her leg in a car accident.
examples.zh: 在一次车祸中，她的脚与腿断开了。

examples.en: Electricity cables have been severed by the storm.
examples.zh: 风暴弄断了电缆。

examples.en: The US severed diplomatic relations with Cuba in 1961.
examples.zh: 美国于1961年断绝了与古巴的外交关系。

examples.en: The company has severed its connection/links/relationship/ties with its previous partners.
examples.zh: 公司断绝了与前合作伙伴之间的联系／关系。

definitions.en: causing very great pain, difficulty, worry, damage, etc.; very serious
definitions.zh: 非常严重的；剧烈的；惨重的

examples.en: a severe chest infection/leg injury/toothache
examples.zh: 十分严重的胸腔感染／腿伤／牙痛

examples.en: This is a school for children with severe learning difficulties.
examples.zh: 这是一所为有严重学习障碍的孩子设立的学校。

examples.en: In parts of Africa there is a severe food/water shortage.
examples.zh: 非洲的一些地方存在严重的粮食／水资源短缺问题。

examples.en: There is expected to be a severe frost tonight.
examples.zh: 今晚预计会出现严重的霜冻。

examples.en: Severe cutbacks in public spending have been announced.
examples.zh: 已经宣布对公共开支进行大幅度削减。

examples.en: This will be a severe test of our strength.
examples.zh: 这将是对我们实力的严峻考验。

examples.en: They confessed, but only under severe constraint.
The latest trade figures have dealt a severe blow to hopes of an early economic recovery.
The stock market crash marked the start of a severe depression.
This year a severe drought has ruined the crops.
In cases of severe mental handicap, constant supervision is recommended.

definitions.en: not kind or showing sympathy; not willing to accept other people's mistakes or failures
definitions.zh: 不和善的;冷酷的;严厉的;苛刻的

examples.en: The headteacher spoke in a severe voice.
examples.zh: 校长说话的口吻很严厉。

examples.en: The president is currently facing severe criticism.
examples.zh: 总统目前正遭到严厉的批评。

examples.en: There are severe penalties for failing to declare all your income to the Inland Revenue.
examples.zh: 未向税务机关申报个人全部收入会受到严厉的处罚。

definitions.en: completely plain and without decoration
definitions.zh: 不加装饰的，朴素的，简朴的

examples.en: She wore a severe black dress, and plain black shoes.
examples.zh: 她穿了一件朴素的黑连衣裙和一双普通的黑鞋子。

examples.en: I don't like these severe modern buildings.
examples.zh: 我不喜欢这些没有任何装饰的现代建筑。

definitions.en: the whole of something, including everything that is connected with it

examples.en: The wedding's next week, but my parents are taking care of the whole shebang.
examples.zh: 下周举行婚礼，但整件事情都是我父母在张罗。

definitions.en: in the past, a large, flat object made of metal or leather that soldiers held in front of their bodies to protect themselves

examples.en: The police held up their riot shields against the flying rocks and bricks.
examples.zh: 警察举着防暴盾牌阻挡飞来的石头和砖块。

examples.en: The anti-personnel mines were laid as a protective shield around the town.
examples.zh: 城镇周围布设了杀伤人员地雷作为保护屏障。

examples.en: Anger can function as a shield against (= a way of avoiding) even more painful emotions of loss and hurt.
examples.zh: 愤怒可以起到避免更加痛苦的失落感和受伤害情绪的作用。

examples.en: The blazer has a shield sewn on the pocket.
examples.zh: 上装的口袋上缝着一个盾形徽章。

examples.en: When he retired from the police, he had to turn in his shield.
examples.zh: 当他从警队退休时，交还了盾形像章。

examples.en: Our school won the county basketball shield this year.
examples.zh: 我们学校赢得了今年全郡篮球赛盾形奖牌。

examples.en: All the police officers were equipped with shields to defend themselves against the rioters.
The front rank of the riot squad raised their shields.
They had a collection of shields from many centuries ago.
She's won various sporting trophies and shields.
He had his family shield on the wall.

definitions.en: to protect someone or something

examples.en: She held her hand above her eyes to shield them from the sun.
examples.zh: 她把手搭在眼睛上方以遮挡太阳。

examples.en: They are accused of trying to shield the General from US federal investigators.
examples.zh: 他们被指控试图包庇将军，使其逃脱美国联邦调查员的调查。

examples.en: We teach young players how to step in front of the defender and use their body to shield the ball.
examples.zh: 我们教年轻队员如何插到对方防守队员前面并用身体护球。

definitions.en: a small object or piece of material used between two parts of something to make them fit together, or to prevent them rubbing against each other

examples.en: A small metal shim will be used to keep the pedals from sticking.
examples.zh: 用一个小金属垫片来防止踏板卡住。

examples.en: Shims under the shoe cleats correct variances to foot tilt.
Before a race, he uses razor-thin shims to align the runners to .005 of an inch accuracy.
The repair involves installing a steel shim a couple of millimetres thick in the pedal assembly.
I was bolting them up using a bag full of shims.

definitions.en: to put a shim (= a small object or piece of material) between two parts of something to make them fit together, or to prevent them rubbing against each other
definitions.zh: 放置（金属、木、石等的）垫片，用填隙片

examples.en: The baseboards have been cut, shimmed, sealed, and nailed.
examples.zh: 踢脚板已经切割好、垫上填隙片、密封好并且钉牢。

examples.en: Be sure to shim the frames so they remain level and plumb after they're fastened in place.
To bore perfectly square, deep holes, shimming the table where it joins its column support casting is necessary.
The nut has been heavily shimmed in order to raise it.
Small, broken stones shim the base of a large monument bearing the family name.

definitions.en: a situation in which there is not enough of something

examples.en: There's a shortage of food and shelter in the refugee camps.
examples.zh: 难民营里缺少足够的食物和住处。

examples.en: The long hot summer has led to serious water shortages.
examples.zh: 这个夏天漫长而炎热，导致严重缺水。

examples.en: The government's programme to grow cash crops for export is a double-edged sword because it has created a local food shortage.
There's a shortage of cheap housing in the region.
Schools nationwide are experiencing a shortage of teachers.
There's a shortage of cheap accommodation .
The government admitted that there was a petrol shortage, but warned people against panic buying.

definitions.en: a system of fast writing that uses lines and simple signs to represent words and phrases

examples.en: Their conversations were taken down in shorthand by a secretary.
examples.zh: 他们谈话的内容由一位秘书速记了下来。

definitions.en: to become smaller, or to make something smaller

examples.en: Your sweater will shrink if you wash it at too high a temperature.
examples.zh: 用温度过高的水洗毛衣，毛衣会缩水。

examples.en: The company's profits have shrunk from $5.5 million to $1.25 million.
examples.zh: 公司利润从550万美元减少到了125万美元。

examples.en: The productivity improvements have shrunk our costs by 25 percent.
examples.zh: 生产率的提高使我们的成本下降了25%。

examples.en: My skirt shrank in the wash.
These trousers must have shrunk - I can't do the zip up.
Have you shrunk? You look so much thinner!
People shrink as they get older.
Unfortunately, the world population of Hawaiian geese has shrunk to very small numbers.

definitions.en: to move away from someone or something because you are frightened

examples.en: The child shrank behind the sofa as his father shouted at him.
examples.zh: 孩子在父亲冲他吼叫时退缩到了沙发后面。

examples.en: When she was younger she would shrink (away) from me whenever I spoke to her.
examples.zh: 她小的时候，我一和她说话她就会退缩。

definitions.en: a psychiatrist

examples.en: I was so depressed that I ended up going to see a shrink.
examples.zh: 我太郁闷了，最后不得不去找心理医生看看。

definitions.en: a brother or sister

examples.en: I have four siblings: three brothers and a sister.
examples.zh: 我有4个兄弟姐妹：3个兄弟，1个姐姐。

examples.en: There was great sibling rivalry (= competition) between Peter and his brother.
examples.zh: 彼得和他的弟弟之间争斗得很厉害。

definitions.en: to step to the side in order to avoid something, especially being hit
definitions.zh: 横跨一步躲开;侧步避让

examples.en: He sidestepped the blow/the tackle.
examples.zh: 他侧步避开了攻击／阻截。

examples.en: The speaker sidestepped the question by saying that it would take him too long to answer it.
examples.zh: 演讲者借口说回答要很长时间而回避了这个问题。

definitions.en: in a direction to the left or right, not forwards or backwards

examples.en: The fence is leaning sideways.
examples.zh: 栅栏向一边倾斜。

examples.en: If you would move sideways to the left, I can get everyone in the picture.
examples.zh: 如果你能向左移一点儿，我就可以把所有人都拍进照片里。

examples.en: It would be easier to get the bookcase through the doorway if we turned it sideways on .
Crabs walk with a sideways motion.
Can you move the chairs sideways a bit?
We turned the table sideways to make more room.

definitions.en: the ability to see

examples.en: If your sight is poor, you should not drive a car.
examples.zh: 视力不佳就不应该开车。

examples.en: The old woman has lost her sight (= has become blind).
examples.zh: 那位老妇人失明了。

examples.en: She suffers from a sight defect.
He has no sight in his left eye.
We will know in a couple of days if the operation to restore her sight was successful.
Doctors have restored his sight.
She lost her sight after a car accident.

definitions.en: something that is in someone's view

examples.en: The flowers at the annual flower show were a beautiful sight.
examples.zh: 年度花卉展上的鲜花是一道美丽的风景。

examples.en: You should always keep sight of your bags (= have them where you can see them) while you're at the airport.
examples.zh: 在机场里，应该时刻留意自己的行李。

examples.en: He was a real sight in his old clothes (= he looked messy or silly).
examples.zh: 你穿着这些旧衣服可真不像样！

examples.en: The child laughed at the sight of (= when she saw) the new toy.
examples.zh: 一看到玩具，孩子就笑了。

examples.en: The lawyer requested sight of (= to see) the papers.
examples.zh: 律师要求看文件。

examples.en: I don't dare let the children out of my sight (= go where I cannot see them) at the park.
examples.zh: 在公园里，我不敢让孩子们走出我的视线。

examples.en: The police officer was hidden out of sight (= where he could not be seen) behind a tree.
examples.zh: 那名警官藏在树后隐蔽的地方。

examples.en: The castle came into sight (= started to be able to be seen) as we went round a bend in the road.
examples.zh: 我们在路上拐了个弯，城堡就映入了眼帘。

examples.en: We're looking for a house which is within sight of (= from which it is possible to see) the mountains.
examples.zh: 我们正在找一所能看到群山的房子。

examples.en: After three years of campaigning, the end is finally in sight (= will happen soon) for Jon.
examples.zh: 经过3年的竞选，乔恩终于快看到结果了。

examples.en: I caught sight of (= saw for a moment) my former teacher while I was out shopping today, but she turned a corner and I lost sight of (= could no longer see) her.
examples.zh: 今天我去买东西时看到了以前的老师，但她转过街角，就看不见了。

examples.en: "Do you know David Wilson?" "I haven't met him, but I know him by sight (= I recognize him, but do not know him)."
examples.zh: “你认识戴维‧威尔逊吗？”“没有引见过，但我能认出他。”

examples.en: She hated/loathed the sight of (= hated) her former husband.
examples.zh: 她讨厌见到她的前夫。

examples.en: They used to be very good friends, but now they can't bear/stand the sight of (= hate) each other.
examples.zh: 他们曾经是好朋友，但现在一看到对方就烦。

examples.en: The question seemed easy at first sight (= when they first saw it), but when the students tried to answer it, they discovered how difficult it was.
examples.zh: 乍一看，这个问题似乎很简单，但学生们试图回答时，才发现有多难。

examples.en: We spent a week in Rome looking at all the sights.
examples.zh: 我们在罗马玩儿了一个星期，参观了所有的名胜。

examples.en: I never buy anything sight unseen.
examples.zh: 不亲眼看过实物我从来不会购买。

examples.en: The sky was a perfect blue - not a cloud in sight.
It was an affecting sight.
I cringed at the sight of my dad dancing.
The car decelerated at the sight of the police car.
He faints at the sight of blood.

definitions.en: a lot; much

examples.en: Food is a darn/damn sight more expensive than it used to be.
examples.zh: 食品比以前贵了很多。

examples.en: He's a sight better than he was yesterday.
examples.zh: 他比昨天好多了。

definitions.en: a part of a gun or other device through which you look to help you aim at something

examples.en: Make sure you line up the sights before you fire the gun.
examples.zh: 射击前要确保瞄好准星。

definitions.en: to suddenly see something or someone

examples.en: After days at sea, the sailors finally sighted land.
examples.zh: 在海上漂流数日后，海员们终于看到了陆地。

definitions.en: to be a sign of something; to mean

examples.en: Nobody really knows what the marks on the ancient stones signify.
examples.zh: 没人真正知道那些古代石头上的记号代表什么含义。

examples.en: The number 30 on a road sign signifies that the speed limit is 30 miles an hour.
examples.zh: 路标上的数字30表示时速限定为30英里。

definitions.en: to make something known; to show

examples.en: She signified her agreement by nodding her head.
examples.zh: 她点头表示同意。

examples.en: She signified (that) she was in agreement by nodding her head.
examples.zh: 她点头表示同意。

definitions.en: to have importance or to matter

examples.en: Don't worry about being late - it doesn't signify.
examples.zh: 别担心迟到——不要紧。

definitions.en: showing little thought or judgment

examples.en: Don't do that, you silly boy!
examples.zh: 不要那么干，你这个傻小子！

examples.en: a silly mistake
examples.zh: 愚蠢的错误

examples.en: It was silly of you to go out in the sun without a hat.
examples.zh: 你不戴帽子顶着太阳出门真是太傻了。

examples.en: I feel silly in this dress.
examples.zh: 穿这条裙子让我感觉很傻。

examples.en: She gets upset over such silly things.
examples.zh: 她会因为这样的小事而难过。

examples.en: We were served our drinks in these silly little glasses.
examples.zh: 我们的饮料是盛在这些不实用的小杯子里端上来的。

examples.en: What's the name of that guy on TV - you know, the American one with the silly voice?
They can't expect me to accommodate all their silly little likes and dislikes.
Why are you putting on that silly voice?
I screamed myself silly on the roller coaster.
Seriously now, did he really say that or are you just being silly?

definitions.en: in a way that is simultaneous (= happening or being done at exactly the same time)

examples.en: Two children answered the teacher's question simultaneously.
examples.zh: 两个孩子同时回答了老师的问题。

examples.en: The network was broadcasting four games simultaneously across the country.
examples.zh: 这家电视台在全国同时播放四场比赛。

examples.en: The opposing units opened fire simultaneously at close range and a 15-minute firefight ensued.
I was trying to do three things simultaneously.
She was cooking a meal while simultaneously holding a business meeting via her mobile phone.

definitions.en: of or relating to the form of a word used when talking or writing about one thing

examples.en: a singular ending/form/noun/verb
examples.zh: 单数词尾／形式／名词／动词

examples.en: The word "woman" is singular.
examples.zh: woman 这个单词是单数形式。

examples.en: The singular of 'mice' is 'mouse'.
Can you tell me the singular forms of this verb?

definitions.en: of an unusual quality or standard; noticeable

examples.en: It was a building of singular grace and beauty.
examples.zh: 这座建筑物非常雅致、漂亮。

examples.en: He showed a singular lack of skill in painting.
examples.zh: 他绘画技巧非常欠缺。

definitions.en: unusual or strange; not ordinary
the form of a word used when talking or writing about one thing

examples.en: The singular of "children" is "child".
examples.zh: children 的单数形式是 child。

examples.en: The word "teeth" is plural - in the singular it's "tooth".
examples.zh: 单词 teeth 是复数形式——单数形式是 tooth。

definitions.en: to cause something to be not straight or exact; to twist or distort

examples.en: These last-minute changes have skewed the company's results.
examples.zh: 这些最后一刻才做出的改变影响了公司的业绩。

definitions.en: not straight
the activity of legally owning other people who are forced to work for or obey you

examples.en: William Wilberforce campaigned for the abolition of slavery.
Those states still had slavery.
Beneath the surface of contemporary West Indian life lurk memories of slavery.
His African-American parents had fled from Kentucky to escape slavery.
St. Louis was one of the few Midwestern cities that had slavery.
Shackles are one of the most potent symbols of slavery.
The American Civil War was fought between the North and the South over the issue of slavery.
France abolished slavery on 27 April 1848.
Slavery still exists in many parts of the world.

definitions.en: the part of a piece of clothing that covers some or all of the arm

examples.en: short/long sleeves
examples.zh: 短／长袖

examples.en: The sleeves are too long for me.
examples.zh: 对我来说，这袖子太长了。

examples.en: You'd better roll your sleeves up or you'll get them dirty.
examples.zh: 你最好卷起袖子，否则会把它们弄脏的。

examples.en: The sleeve of my jacket got caught on the door handle and ripped.
You could always turn the sleeves up.
She wiped her nose on her sleeve.
One of his sleeves was ripped.
I had to unravel one of the sleeves because I realized I'd knitted it too small.

definitions.en: a cover to protect something

examples.en: Can you put the record back in its sleeve, please?
examples.zh: 你能把唱片放回封套里吗?

definitions.en: a tattoo (= permanent skin decoration) that covers the whole of someone's arm
definitions.zh: 花臂（纹满整个手臂的纹身）

definitions.en: a little

examples.en: She's slightly taller than her sister.
examples.zh: 她比她姐姐稍高一点儿。

examples.en: I'm slightly upset she forgot my birthday.
examples.zh: 她忘了我的生日，我有点儿不高兴。

examples.en: I'm slightly worried that she'll get lost on the way.
examples.zh: 我有点担心她会迷路。

examples.en: They were only slightly injured in the crash.
The sauce itself was light, fragrant and slightly sweet.
One of the occupants of the car was slightly injured.
The richness of the food made him feel slightly ill.
The pound rose slightly against the dollar today, then settled at $1.53.

definitions.en: Someone who is slightly built is thin and delicate.
very wet or liquid, often in a way that is unpleasant

examples.en: The batter was a bit sloppy so I added some more flour.
examples.zh: 面糊有点儿稀，于是我又加进了一些面粉。

examples.en: She covered his face with sloppy kisses.

definitions.en: not taking care or making an effort

examples.en: Spelling mistakes always look sloppy in a formal letter.
examples.zh: 在正式信函中出现拼写错误总让人感觉写信者草率马虎。

examples.en: Another sloppy pass like that might lose them the whole game.
examples.zh: 再来一次那样的草率传球，他们可能会输掉整场比赛。

examples.en: At home I tend to wear big sloppy sweaters and jeans.
examples.zh: 在家里，我一般穿宽松的套衫和牛仔裤。

definitions.en: expressing feelings of love in a way that is silly or embarrassing

examples.en: a sloppy love song
examples.zh: 庸俗伤感的情歌

definitions.en: moving or operating more slowly than usual and with less energy or power

examples.en: A heavy lunch makes me sluggish in the afternoon.
examples.zh: 丰盛的午餐使我一下午都懒得动弹。

examples.en: Something is wrong with the car - the engine feels sluggish.
examples.zh: 这辆车出了问题——引擎运转不太顺畅。

examples.en: The housing market has been very sluggish these past few years.
examples.zh: 过去这几年房地产市场一直很萧条。

definitions.en: (especially of a man's clothes or of his appearance) modern and stylish
definitions.zh: （尤指男子穿着或相貌）时髦的，漂亮的

examples.en: He's a snappy dresser.
examples.zh: 他是个穿着时髦的人。

examples.en: That's a very snappy new suit, Peter.
examples.zh: 你那套新衣服真是漂亮，彼得。

definitions.en: immediately effective in getting people's attention or communicating an idea
definitions.zh: 活泼明快的，短小易记的

examples.en: The magazine will be launched in September with a snappy new design.
examples.zh: 这本杂志将于9月以活泼明快的崭新设计样式发行。

examples.en: They're looking for a snappy slogan to communicate the campaign's message.
examples.zh: 他们在寻找一句短小精悍的口号来传达运动的要旨。

definitions.en: to smell something by taking air in through your nose

examples.en: He sniffed his socks to see if they needed washing.
examples.zh: 他嗅了嗅自己的袜子，看是否需要洗了。

examples.en: Dogs love sniffing each other.
examples.zh: 狗喜欢相互嗅闻。

examples.en: She sniffed at her glass of wine before tasting it.
examples.zh: 品尝那杯酒之前，她先闻了闻。

examples.en: Dogs are sometimes used at airports to sniff out (= find by smelling) drugs in people's luggage.
examples.zh: 有时候，狗被用来在机场嗅查人们的行李中是否有毒品。

examples.en: He was expelled from school for sniffing glue (= taking in the gas from glue because of the feelings of pleasure that this gives).
examples.zh: 他因吸食胶毒而被学校开除。

examples.en: You're sniffing a lot - do you have a cold?
examples.zh: 你不停地抽鼻子——你感冒了吗？

examples.en: "They didn't even serve wine at dinner!" she sniffed.
examples.zh: “他们在正餐时间竟然没有供应酒水！”她轻蔑地说。

examples.en: He sniffed the flowers.
The dog sniffed around his feet.
She sniffed the forkful and put it back on her plate.
As she turned away I saw him sniff furtively under his arm.
Could you blow your nose instead of sniffing?

definitions.en: a quick breath in through the nose to smell something, or to stop liquid in the nose from coming out

examples.en: Take a sniff of this medicine - it smells horrible, doesn't it?
examples.zh: 闻闻这种药——有一股让人作呕的味儿，对吗？

examples.en: "I don't think much of that idea," she said with a sniff (= an expression of a low opinion).
examples.zh: “我觉得这主意不怎么样。”她吸了吸鼻子轻蔑地说。

definitions.en: the part of a piece of equipment, especially electrical equipment, into which another part fits

examples.en: an electrical socket
examples.zh: 电插座

examples.en: a light socket
examples.zh: 灯头

examples.en: He had forgotten to plug the television into the mains socket.
examples.zh: 他忘了把电视机插头插进墙上的插座里。

examples.en: The air freshener plugs into a car's lighter socket.
examples.zh: 空气清新器插头插在汽车点烟器插孔里。

definitions.en: a part of the body into which another part fits

examples.en: a tooth/eye socket
examples.zh: 牙槽／眼窝

examples.en: a ball-and-socket joint like the hip joint
examples.zh: 像髋关节似的球窝关节

definitions.en: the situation of being alone without other people

examples.en: a life of solitude
examples.zh: 独居的生活

examples.en: After months of solitude at sea it felt strange to be in company.
examples.zh: 在海上独居了几个月之后，对有人陪伴感到有些不适应。

examples.en: It provides one with a chance to reflect on spiritual matters in solitude.
examples.zh: 它为人们提供了幽居独处、玄思冥想的机会。

definitions.en: a rolling movement or jump, either forwards or backwards, in which you turn over completely, with your body above your head, and finish with your head on top again

examples.en: Do you want to see me do a somersault?
She was so happy she turned three somersaults on the lawn.
examples.zh: 她太高兴了，在草坪上连翻了3个筋斗。

examples.en: He did his first somersault on a trampoline when he was two.
She attempted a backwards somersault.
He could do somersaults and cartwheels and he could walk on his hands.

definitions.en: a person who is sophisticated
definitions.zh: 老于世故的人，精明练达的人

definitions.en: a brand name for a type of meat sold in metal containers, made mostly from pork (= meat from a pig)

examples.en: spam fritters
examples.zh: 油炸斯帕姆午餐肉饼

definitions.en: If something is spare, it is available to use because it is extra.

examples.en: a spare key/tyre
examples.zh: 备用钥匙／轮胎

examples.en: spare sheets and blankets
examples.zh: 多余的床单和毛毯

examples.en: Do you have a spare pen?
examples.zh: 你有多余的笔吗？

examples.en: We have a spare room if you want to stay overnight with us.
examples.zh: 要是你想在我们这里过夜的话，我们有一个空房间。

examples.en: Could I have a word with you when you have a spare moment/minute?
examples.zh: 如果你有时间，我能否和你谈两句？

examples.en: "Do you want this cake?" "Yes, if it's going spare (= if no one else wants it)."
examples.zh: “你要这块蛋糕吗？”“如果没有别人要，就给我吧。”

examples.en: I like to paint in my spare time.
examples.zh: 我业余时间喜欢画画。

examples.en: I keep a spare tyre in the back of the car.
Do it in a spare moment at the weekend or whenever - it really doesn't matter.
We should be in when you arrive, but if the worst comes to the worst, the neighbours have a spare key and will let you into the house.
We were able to bunk down in a spare room for the night.
He bought an old engine and cannibalized it for spare parts.

definitions.en: tall and thin

examples.en: He had the spare build of a runner.
examples.zh: 他有着赛跑运动员的瘦长体型。

definitions.en: plain and not decorated

examples.en: The interior is spare, almost minimalist.
examples.zh: 内部是简朴的，几乎是最简单的。

definitions.en: to get very upset or angry

examples.en: She goes spare if I'm so much as five minutes late.
examples.zh: 如果我迟到5分钟，她就会很生气。

definitions.en: to not hurt or destroy something or someone

examples.en: They asked him to spare the women and children.
examples.zh: 他们要他放过妇女和儿童。

definitions.en: to prevent someone from having to experience something unpleasant

examples.en: Luckily, I was spared the embarrassment of having to sing in front of everyone.
examples.zh: 幸运的是，我躲过了在大家面前唱歌的尴尬。

examples.en: It was a nasty accident - but I'll spare you (= I won't tell you) the gruesome details.
examples.zh: 那是一起严重的事故——我就不跟你讲那些令人毛骨悚然的细节了。

definitions.en: to use a lot of effort, expense, etc. to do something

examples.en: We will spare no effort to find out who did this.
examples.zh: 我们会不遗余力，一定查出是谁做了这件事。

examples.en: She never spared herself in the pursuit of excellence.
examples.zh: 她竭尽全力追求卓越。

definitions.en: to give time, money, or space to someone, especially when it is difficult for you
definitions.zh: （尤指在自己困难的情况下）抽出，拨出，匀出

examples.en: Could you spare me £20?
examples.zh: 你能匀给我20英镑吗？

examples.en: I'd love to come, but I can't spare the time.
examples.zh: 我很想来，但恐怕没时间。

examples.en: Spare a thought for me tomorrow, when you're lying on a beach, because I'll still be here in the office!
examples.zh: 明天你躺在海滩上的时候，就想想我吧，因为我还在办公室里！

examples.en: If you have any woolyarn to spare when you've finished the sweater, can you make me some gloves?
examples.zh: 如果你织完毛衣还剩下毛线的话，可以给我织副手套吗？

examples.en: I caught the plane with only two minutes to spare.
examples.zh: 我赶上飞机时距离起飞只剩两分钟了。

examples.en: There's no time/We have no time to spare if we want to get the article written by tomorrow.
examples.zh: 如果我们想明天就把这篇文章写完，那么就没剩多少时间了。

definitions.en: an extra thing that is not being used and can be used instead of a part that is broken, lost, etc.
in small amounts, or without wasting any

examples.en: There wasn't enough coal during the war, so we had to use it sparingly.
examples.zh: 战争期间煤炭不够用，我们只好少用煤。

examples.en: She created a natural look, using makeup sparingly.
examples.zh: 她创造了一个自然的妆容，化妆品用得很少。

examples.en: City officials urged customers to use electricity sparingly to avoid more power failures.
Some families used soap sparingly because of cost.
Dieticians recommend eating red meat sparingly.

definitions.en: small in numbers or amount, often spread over a large area

examples.en: a sparse population/audience
examples.zh: 稀少的人口／观众

examples.en: sparse vegetation/woodland
examples.zh: 稀疏的植被／林地

examples.en: a sparse beard
examples.zh: 稀稀拉拉的胡子

examples.en: Information coming out of the disaster area is sparse.
examples.zh: 来自灾区的信息很少。

definitions.en: relating to the position, area, and size of things

examples.en: This task is designed to test children's spatial awareness (= their understanding of where things are in relation to other things).
examples.zh: 这一任务是为测试孩子的空间意识而设计的。

definitions.en: the set of colours into which a beam of light can be separated, or a range of waves, such as light waves or radio waves

examples.en: The colours of the spectrum - red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet - can be seen in a rainbow.
examples.zh: 光谱中的红、橙、黄、绿、蓝、靛、紫诸色都可以在彩虹中见到。

examples.en: The survey revealed a wide spectrum of opinion.
He is some way along the autistic spectrum.
He was held in great affection by people from the entire political spectrum.

definitions.en: a range of different positions, opinions, etc. between two extreme points

examples.en: He has support from across the whole political spectrum.
examples.zh: 他得到了所有政治派别的支持。

examples.en: The group includes students from both ends of the social spectrum (= range of social classes).
examples.zh: 这个团体包括了来自社会上、下两个阶层的学生。

examples.en: A wide spectrum of opinion was represented at the meeting.
examples.zh: 会上提出了一系列广泛的意见。

examples.en: My disease is nowhere near as bad as hers but they are on the same spectrum.
examples.zh: 我的病远没有她的那么严重，但处于同一范围内。

examples.en: the autism spectrum
examples.zh: 自闭症谱系

definitions.en: based on a guess and not on information

examples.en: The article was dismissed as highly speculative.
examples.zh: 该文被斥为纯属臆测。

examples.en: bizarre and speculative theories
His theory is too speculative for most of his colleagues to accept at this point.
This type of polling is less scientific and more speculative.
The numbers are high, but they're purely speculative.
There is another speculative but intriguing explanation.

definitions.en: bought or done in order to make a profit in the future

examples.en: The office block was built as a speculative venture.
examples.zh: 这栋写字楼的建造是一桩投机生意。

examples.en: Some of these buyers are speculative investors.
This is an aggressive, speculative market.
Most oil trades are speculative and do not result in physical delivery of oil.
After two decades of sustained economic growth and speculative residential building, places that were once farms and woodland have been transformed.

definitions.en: to force out the contents of the mouth, especially saliva

examples.en: Bob Ewell spat contemptuously right in the lawyer's face.
examples.zh: 鲍勃‧尤厄尔轻蔑地朝律师脸上吐了口唾沫。

examples.en: He spat the meat out in disgust.
examples.zh: 他厌恶地把肉吐了出来。

examples.en: They bought watermelons and ate them as they walked, spitting out the seeds.
examples.zh: 他们买了西瓜，边走边吃，一路吐着瓜子。

examples.en: "Get out!" she spat, when Ace appeared in the doorway.
examples.zh: 埃斯出现在门口时，她厉声说道：“出去！”

examples.en: He spat out the gristle.
I told her off for spitting at her brother.
I tried to offer friendship and he spat in my face.
It's disgusting to spit in the street.
Spit the mouthwash into this bowl.

definitions.en: to rain very slightly

examples.en: If it's only spitting (with rain), perhaps we don't need waterproofs.
examples.zh: 如果只是一点毛毛细雨，也许我们不用穿雨衣。

definitions.en: a long, thin, metal stick put through a piece of food, especially meat, so that it can be cooked above a fire
definitions.zh: （用于烧烤食物的）炙叉；（尤指）烤肉叉，烤肉扦

examples.en: Roast the lamb on a spit.
examples.zh: 把羊肉叉在烤肉叉上烤。

definitions.en: a long, thin, flat beach that goes out into the sea
saliva, especially when it is outside the mouth

examples.en: She used a little spit on a tissue to wipe the mirror clean.
examples.zh: 她在纸巾上吐了点唾沫，把镜子擦拭干净。

definitions.en: in a way that is natural, often sudden, and not planned or forced

examples.en: This story may prompt you to burst spontaneously into uncontrollable sobs.
examples.zh: 这个故事可能会让你不由自主地抽泣起来。

examples.en: They gave him a mike, and he spontaneously interviewed everyone.
My best friend, who doesn't have kids, always expects me to do things spontaneously with her.
Hundreds of people could spontaneously appear at my house for a party.

definitions.en: without any obvious outside cause

examples.en: The liquid spontaneously ignited.
examples.zh: 液体发生自燃。

examples.en: Blood clots can occur spontaneously in some people.
examples.zh: 在一些人身上可能自发产生血栓。

examples.en: They found that fat contained some cells that spontaneously divided into more specialized cells.
examples.zh: 他们发现脂肪中含有一些细胞，会自发分裂为分化程度更高的细胞。

examples.en: Nerve gases quickly affect the central nervous system. A person can't breathe, their eyes start blinking and their muscles move spontaneously.
About 90% of patients with acute low back problems recover spontaneously within a month.
Stem cells grown in petri dishes spontaneously try out new fates.

definitions.en: a funny and silly piece of writing, music, theatre, etc. that copies the style of an original work
definitions.zh: （对文章、音乐、戏剧等）滑稽模仿的作品

examples.en: They did a spoof on/of the Nine O'Clock News.
examples.zh: 他们搞了个“九点新闻”的滑稽模仿之作。

examples.en: It was a spoof cowboy film.
examples.zh: 那是对牛仔电影的滑稽模仿之作。

definitions.en: to try to make someone believe in something that is not true, as a joke
strange and frightening
definitions.zh: 怪异的;诡异的;阴森可怕的

examples.en: It was a spooky coincidence.
examples.zh: 这个巧合很诡异。

definitions.en: sometimes, but not regularly or continuously
definitions.zh: 偶尔发生地;阵发性地;断断续续地

examples.en: Fighting continued throughout the afternoon and sporadically into Friday night.
examples.zh: 战斗持续了整个下午，并零星地持续到星期五晚上。

examples.en: Though she likes to work, she has only done so sporadically.
examples.zh: 虽然她喜欢工作，但也只是偶尔做做而已。

examples.en: The security door buzzed sporadically as residents came and went.
They met at a nightclub and began dating sporadically .
They kept in touch sporadically until 2005.

definitions.en: to spread the arms and legs out carelessly and untidily while sitting or lying down

examples.en: I knocked into her in the corridor and sent her sprawling (= knocked her over).
examples.zh: 我在走廊里和她迎头相撞，把她撞了个四脚朝天。

definitions.en: (especially of a city) to cover a large area of land with buildings, especially gradually over a period of time
definitions.zh: （尤指城市）杂乱无序地扩展，散乱地延伸

examples.en: The refugee camps sprawl across the landscape.
examples.zh: 眼前杂乱地散落着许多难民营。

definitions.en: a position with the arms and legs spread out carelessly and untidily while sitting or lying down
a large area of land covered with buildings, especially ones that have been added gradually over a period of time
definitions.zh: （某地建筑的）无计划发展;没有城市规划

examples.en: the urban sprawl of South Florida
examples.zh: 佛罗里达州南部杂乱无序的城市扩展

definitions.en: no longer new or fresh, usually as a result of being kept for too long
definitions.zh: （通常指因久放）不新鲜的，走味的

examples.en: The bread/biscuits/cake had gone stale.
examples.zh: 面包／饼干／蛋糕已经不新鲜了。

examples.en: The morning after the party, their apartment smelled of stale cigarette smoke.
examples.zh: 聚会后的次日早上，他们的公寓里有一股污浊的烟味儿。

examples.en: stale jokes/news
examples.zh: 老掉牙的笑话／过时的新闻

examples.en: They had been working together for over five years and they had both become a little stale.
examples.zh: 他们两个已经在一起共事5年多了，彼此都有一点儿厌倦。

examples.en: A foul smell of stale beer permeated the whole building.
The room was filled with the reek of stale beer and cigarettes.
Have you got any stale cheese that I can bait the mousetrap with?
In the cathedral vaults the air was dank and stale.
Her breath was a little stale.

definitions.en: a large table or a small shop with an open front from which goods are sold in a public place

examples.en: In the village market, the stalls are piled high with local vegetables.
examples.zh: 村子集市的摊位上高高地堆着当地产的蔬菜。

examples.en: He runs a fruit and vegetable stall in the market.
She runs a stall in the market.
The stall sells drinks and snacks.
The gaily striped awnings of the little shops and market stalls made an attractive scene.
Some displayed their wares on stalls, while others had just spread them out on the pavement.

definitions.en: a small closed area within a farm building in which there is space for one animal to be kept
definitions.zh: （农场建筑中圈养单只牲畜的）隔栏，分隔间

examples.en: There was one bathroom with a shower stall in the corner.
examples.zh: 有一个洗手间，角落里有个淋浴的小隔间。

definitions.en: rows of fixed seats in a church, often with the sides and backs connected
definitions.zh: （边缘和背部常围起来的）靠背长凳座位

definitions.en: If an engine stalls, or if you stall it, it stops working suddenly and without you intending it to happen.

examples.en: A car may stall due to the driver braking too suddenly.
examples.zh: 司机踩刹车过猛可能会导致汽车熄火。

examples.en: I stalled the car twice during my driving test but still managed to pass.
examples.zh: 我在驾驶考试时两次把车弄熄火，不过还是勉强通过了考试。

definitions.en: to delay taking action or avoid giving an answer in order to have more time to make a decision or get an advantage

examples.en: She says she'll give me the money next week but I think she's just stalling (for time).
examples.zh: 她说下周会把钱给我，但我认为她只不过是在拖时间。

examples.en: I managed to stall him for a few days until I'd got enough money to pay back the loan.
examples.zh: 我设法拖住了他几天，直到我筹够了还贷款的钱。

examples.en: The thief broke into the office while his accomplice stalled off the security guard.
examples.zh: 小偷趁同伙拖住保安之际，闯进了办公室。

examples.en: Japan's economic growth has stalled, with industrial production contracting in June for the fourth straight month.
examples.zh: 日本经济陷入停滞状态，到六月份时工业产出值已连续四个月缩减。

examples.en: Commandos stalled the enemy attack by destroying three bridges.
examples.zh: 突击队破坏了3座桥，拖延了敌人的进攻。

examples.en: Fears are growing that a tax increase may stall economic recovery.
examples.zh: 人们越来越担心增税可能会阻碍经济复苏。

definitions.en: a small piece of paper with a picture or pattern on it that is stuck onto a letter or package before it is posted, to show that the cost of sending it has been paid

examples.en: I stuck a 50p stamp on the envelope.
examples.zh: 我在信封上贴了一枚50便士的邮票。

examples.en: a valuable stamp collection
She affixed a stamp to the envelope.
How much is a first-class stamp?
He sealed the envelope and put a stamp on it.
a stamp album

definitions.en: a tool for putting a mark on an object either by printing on it or pushing into it, or the mark made in this way

examples.en: A date stamp inside the front cover of a library book shows when it should be returned.
examples.zh: 图书馆藏书封面上的日期戳标明了应还书日期。

definitions.en: a small piece of paper worth a particular amount of money that you can buy several of, as a way of paying for something over a period of time

examples.en: vehicle licence stamps
examples.zh: 机动车牌照印花税票

definitions.en: a particular quality in something or someone, or a quality in something that shows it was done by a particular person or group of people

examples.en: Although this painting clearly bears the stamp of genius, we don't know who painted it.
examples.zh: 虽然这幅画明显是天才之作，但我们却不知其出自谁手。

examples.en: Each manager has left his or her own stamp on the way the company has evolved.
examples.zh: 每一位经理都在公司的发展道路上留下了自己的印记。

definitions.en: an act of putting the foot down on the ground hard, or the noise made in doing so
definitions.zh: （常指为表示生气）跺（脚）;重踏，重踩

examples.en: With a stamp of her foot she stormed out.
examples.zh: 她一跺脚，冲了出去。

definitions.en: to put a mark on an object either by printing on it or pushing into it with a small tool

examples.en: It is necessary to stamp your passport.
examples.zh: 护照上必须盖章。

examples.en: Every carton of yogurt is stamped with a sell-by date.
examples.zh: 每盒酸奶上都盖有最迟销售日期。

examples.en: The lawyer stamped the certificate with her seal.
Both books have 'property of Her Majesty's Government' stamped inside them.
She's stamped her name in all of her books.
Sell-by dates are stamped on eggs.
The dark red royal crest was stamped on the back of the envelope.

definitions.en: to put a foot down on the ground hard and quickly, making a loud noise, often to show anger
definitions.zh: （常指为表示生气）跺（脚）;重踏，重踩

examples.en: The little boy was stamping his foot and refusing to take his medicine.
examples.zh: 那个小男孩跺着脚不肯吃药。

examples.en: She stood by the road, stamping her feet to stay warm.
examples.zh: 她站在路边，跺着脚让自己暖和些。

examples.en: I wish those people upstairs would stop stamping (about/around).
examples.zh: 我希望楼上那些人别再乱跺脚了。

examples.en: Why did you stamp on that insect?
examples.zh: 你为什么要踩那只虫子？

definitions.en: to mark with a particular quality or show that someone has a particular quality

examples.en: Our new administrator seems to be trying to stamp her authority on every aspect of the department.
examples.zh: 我们的新主管似乎想在部门的方方面面都施展她的权威。

definitions.en: something that is always ready for use, especially if a regular one fails
definitions.zh: （尤指为应对出现问题或故障时的）备用设备，备用品，替代物

examples.en: Board games are a good standby to keep the children amused if the weather is bad.
examples.zh: 天气不好时，棋类游戏是个不错的哄孩子们开心的备用选择。

examples.en: There are standby generators but these usually only have to work for a few hours a year during power cuts.
examples.zh: 有备用发电机，不过通常一年只在断电时使用几个小时。

examples.en: Hospitals are on standby ready to deal with casualties from the crash.
examples.zh: 医院随时准备着接纳撞车事故的伤者。

definitions.en: a cheaper ticket sold just before a flight or a performance if there is a seat available
definitions.zh: （航班起飞前或演出开始前廉价出售的）余票

examples.en: Tickets start at about at about £7, with cheap standbys sometimes available to students on the evening of the performance.
About 850 standby tickets have been given to those hoping to take the place of no-shows.

definitions.en: a set of beliefs and ideas from which opinions and decisions are formed

examples.en: "I have to put aside my emotions," he says, "and consider it from a professional standpoint."
examples.zh: “我必须将个人感情搁在一边，”他说：“以专业的眼光去看它。”

definitions.en: a group of lines of poetry forming a unit
the state of having no food for a long period, often causing death

examples.en: 20 million people face starvation unless a vast emergency aid programme is launched.
examples.zh: 若不启动大型的紧急援助项目，两千万人将有饿死的危险。

examples.en: The animals had died of starvation.
examples.zh: 这些动物饿死了。

examples.en: They pay starvation wages (= not enough money to live on).
examples.zh: 他们付的工钱极少，不够维持基本的生活。

definitions.en: to (cause someone to) become very weak or die because there is not enough food to eat

examples.en: Whole communities starved to death during the long drought.
examples.zh: 在这场漫长的大旱中，整个村落的人都饿死了。

examples.en: From talking to former prisoners in the camps, an obvious conclusion is that they have been starved.
examples.zh: 通过和从集中营出来的囚犯谈话，一个很明显的感觉就是他们一直在挨饿。

examples.en: We can't stand by while millions of people starve.
When the aid stopped, thousands of people simply starved to death.
We can't leave these people to starve.
I don't mind her going on a sensible diet just as long as she doesn't starve herself.
She had to starve for 24 hours before the treatment.

definitions.en: not moving, or not changing

examples.en: a stationary car/train
examples.zh: 停着的汽车／火车

examples.en: The traffic got slower and slower until it was stationary.
examples.zh: 车流移动得越来越慢，最后完全不动了。

examples.en: The rate of inflation has been stationary for several months.
examples.zh: 通货膨胀率已经几个月保持不变。

definitions.en: information based on a study of the number of times something happens or is present, or other numerical facts

examples.en: Statistics show/suggest that women live longer than men.
examples.zh: 统计资料显示女性比男性寿命长。

examples.en: According to official statistics, the Japanese work longer hours than workers in most other industrialized countries.
examples.zh: 根据官方统计数字，日本人比其他大多数工业化国家工人的工作时间都要长。

examples.en: The city's most shocking statistic is its high infant mortality rate.
examples.zh: 这座城市最令人震惊的统计数字是其高婴儿死亡率。

examples.en: He has all the latest statistics at his fingertips.
It's difficult to interpret these statistics without knowing how they were obtained.
The opposition leader accused government ministers of manipulating the statistics to suit themselves.
The statistics reflect a change in people's spending habits.
According to official statistics, the Japanese work longer hours than workers in most other industrialized countries.

definitions.en: happening in a smooth, gradual, and regular way, not suddenly or unexpectedly
definitions.zh: 稳步的;持续的;有规律的

examples.en: The procession moved through the streets at a steady pace.
examples.zh: 队伍稳步穿过街道。

examples.en: Orders for new ships are rising, after several years of steady decline.
examples.zh: 新船的订单数量经过几年的持续下降之后开始增加了。

examples.en: Over the last ten years he has produced a steady flow/stream/trickle of articles and papers.
examples.zh: 过去10年中他连续发表了一系列文章和论文。

examples.en: Progress has been slow but steady.
examples.zh: 进展虽然缓慢，但却也在稳步向前。

examples.en: There has been a steady decrease in the number of visitors.
There's been a steady flow of visitors.
We're making slow but steady progress with the decorating.
The last few years have seen a steady increase in the number of infections.
There has been a steady improvement in the situation.

definitions.en: not moving or changing suddenly
definitions.zh: 固定不动的；稳固的，平稳的

examples.en: I'll hold the boat steady while you climb in.
examples.zh: 我把船稳住，你爬上来。

examples.en: Most rental prices have held steady this year.
examples.zh: 今年大部分租金保持不变。

examples.en: Young people assume that if you are in a steady relationship, you don't have to worry about HIV.
examples.zh: 年轻人想当然地认为，只要保持稳定的恋爱关系，就不必担心艾滋病病毒。

examples.en: Owning your own home and having a steady job will help when applying for a loan.
examples.zh: 拥有自己的住房而且工作稳定对申请贷款很有利。

definitions.en: under control

examples.en: a steady voice/look/gaze
examples.zh: 冷静的声音／表情／目光

examples.en: You need steady nerves to drive in city traffic.
examples.zh: 在市内开车需要沉着冷静。

examples.en: Painting these small details needs a steady hand.
examples.zh: 画这些小细节手要稳。

examples.en: a steady friend
examples.zh: 可靠的朋友

definitions.en: to make something stop shaking or moving

examples.en: He wobbled around on the bike and then steadied himself.
examples.zh: 他在自行车上摇晃了几下，随后便稳住了自己。

examples.en: He steadied his rifle on the wall and fired.
examples.zh: 他把步枪抵在墙上稳住，然后开火。

definitions.en: to become calm and controlled, or to make someone do this

examples.en: Some people say that a drink will steady your nerves.
examples.zh: 有些人说喝杯酒能稳定情绪。

definitions.en: to have a romantic relationship with one person for a long period
definitions.zh: （和某人）保持稳定的恋爱关系

examples.en: She's been going steady with Mike for six months.
examples.zh: 她和迈克确定关系已经有6个月了。

definitions.en: to take something without the permission or knowledge of the owner and keep it

examples.en: She admitted stealing the money from her employers.
examples.zh: 她承认偷了雇主的钱。

examples.en: The number of cars which are stolen every year has risen.
examples.zh: 每年失窃车辆的数量在上升。

examples.en: They were so poor they had to steal in order to eat.
examples.zh: 他们非常穷，为了填饱肚子只得去偷。

examples.en: Employees who steal are dismissed automatically.
He knew it was wrong to steal, but the money just lying there was too great a temptation.
They stole jewellery valued at £50 000 .
Thieves broke into the safe and stole everything in it.
She stole the shoes from right under the assistant's nose.

definitions.en: to do something quickly or without being noticed

examples.en: She stole a glance at her watch.
examples.zh: 她偷偷瞥了一眼自己的手表。

examples.en: He stole out of the room while no one was looking.
examples.zh: 他趁没人注意，偷偷溜出了房间。

definitions.en: a product that has a very low price, or a price that is much lower than the original cost

examples.en: I picked up a new iron in the sale - it was a steal.
examples.zh: 我在大减价时买了个新熨斗——非常便宜。

definitions.en: movement that is quiet and careful in order not to be seen or heard
definitions.zh: 偷偷摸摸;秘密行动;不声张的活动

examples.en: She moved across the field with the stealth of a cat on the prowl.
These thieves operate with terrifying stealth - they can easily steal from the pockets of unsuspecting travellers.
examples.zh: 这些窃贼偷起东西来神不知鬼不觉——他们轻而易举就能从毫无戒备的游客口袋中偷走东西。

examples.en: He can simultaneously run with the ball and cover ground with the stealth of a gazelle.
"It would be helpful to reduce calorific levels by stealth, so people don't notice there's been a change," he said.

definitions.en: a person whose job it is to organize a particular event, or to provide services to particular people, or to take care of a particular place
definitions.zh: （特定活动的）组织者，操办者；服务员；管家；管理员

examples.en: Stewards will be inspecting the race track at 9.00.
examples.zh: 组织者将在9点钟检查跑道。

examples.en: If you need help at any time during the conference, one of the stewards will be pleased to help you.
examples.zh: 会议期间您任何时候需要帮助，服务人员都会很乐意为您效劳。

examples.en: He's the steward of the City of Wakefield's Working Men's Club.
examples.zh: 他是韦克菲尔德市工人俱乐部的餐饮主管。

definitions.en: unwilling to spend money

examples.en: He's really stingy and never buys anyone a drink when we go out.
examples.zh: 他非常小气，我们一起出去时他从来不请喝酒。

examples.en: The landlords are so stingy - they refused to pay for new carpets.
examples.zh: 房东们太小气了——他们拒绝掏钱买新地毯。

examples.en: The state has been quite stingy with funding for public education.
He stated that the world's wealthiest countries were often stingy about donating foreign aid.
The prize money certainly isn't stingy.

definitions.en: to say exactly how something must be or must be done

examples.en: She agreed to buy the car, but stipulated racing tyres and a turbo-powered engine.
examples.zh: 她同意买这辆车，但指明要装赛车轮胎和涡轮动力引擎。

examples.en: The law stipulates that new cars must have seat belts for the driver and every passenger.
examples.zh: 法律规定新车必须为驾驶员和每位乘客配备坐椅安全带。

examples.en: We have signed a contract which stipulates when the project must be completed.
examples.zh: 我们已签署合同，规定了项目必须完成的时间。

definitions.en: a watch that can be started and stopped in order to measure the exact time of an event, especially a sports event
definitions.zh: （尤指用于体育赛事的）秒表，跑表

definitions.en: to cause something to reach, often as far as possible, in a particular direction

examples.en: I tripped on a piece of wire that someone had stretched across the path.
examples.zh: 我被不知是谁拉在路上的一根电线绊倒了。

examples.en: She stretched out her hand and helped him from his chair.
examples.zh: 她伸手将他从椅子上扶起来。

examples.en: "I'm so tired," she said, yawning and stretching.
examples.zh: “我太累了，”她打着呵欠伸了个懒腰说。

examples.en: It's a good idea to stretch before you do vigorous exercise.
examples.zh: 在进行剧烈运动前应该先伸展一下身体。

examples.en: The plastic cover was stretched tight across the tank.
I stretched out and listened to the sound of the rushing stream.
The cat was stretching in front of the fire.
Yoga is a great way to stretch your body.
I get up and walk around every half hour just to stretch my legs.

definitions.en: to spread over a large area or distance

examples.en: A huge cloud of dense smoke stretched across the horizon.
examples.zh: 一大团浓烟在地平线上蔓延开来。

examples.en: The Andes stretch for 7,250 km along the west coast of South America.
examples.zh: 安第斯山脉沿南美洲西海岸绵延达7250公里。

examples.en: Unsettled weather will stretch from the middle Mississippi Valley to the southern Middle Atlantic States.
examples.zh: 自密西西比河流域中部至濒大西洋中部诸州的南部天气均不稳定。

examples.en: The refugee camps stretch as far as the eye can see.
examples.zh: 难民营绵延不绝，一眼望不到头。

examples.en: The road stretched into the distance as far as the eye could see.
The mountain range stretched away into infinity.
A broad sweep of flat countryside stretched to the horizon in all directions.
On the eastern horizon, a huge cloud of smoke from burning oil tanks stretched across the sky.
A ribbon of road stretched ahead of us across the desert.

definitions.en: to go as far as or past the usual limit of something
definitions.zh: 到…的限度；超过…的限度；竭尽

examples.en: Many families' budgets are already stretched to breaking point.
examples.zh: 许多家庭的预算已经撑到了极限。

examples.en: We can't work any harder, Paul. We're already fully stretched.
examples.zh: 我们无法再更卖力了，保罗。我们已经竭尽全力了。

examples.en: This movie really stretches the patience of the audience to the limit.
examples.zh: 这部电影简直就是在考验观众的耐性。

examples.en: Normally, people under 18 aren't allowed, but I guess we can stretch the rules for you as it's your birthday tomorrow.
examples.zh: 我们通常不接纳未满18岁的人，但考虑到明天是你的生日，我想我们可以稍稍变通一下。

definitions.en: to (cause a material to) become longer or wider than usual as a result of pulling at the edges

examples.en: an exercise to stretch the leg muscles
examples.zh: 拉伸腿部肌肉的锻炼

examples.en: That elastic band will snap if you stretch it too far.
examples.zh: 那根橡皮筋拉得太长会断的。

examples.en: This substance stretches to any shape you want.
examples.zh: 这种物质可以拉伸成你想要的任何形状。

examples.en: to stretch fabrics
examples.zh: 弹力织物

definitions.en: to spread over a long period of time

examples.en: The dispute stretches back over many years.
examples.zh: 这场争论可追溯到许多年以前。

examples.en: Although we were supposed to finish this month, it looks like the work will stretch well into next year.
examples.zh: 虽然我们应该在本月完工，但看来工作要拖到明年了。

examples.en: I'd like to stretch my mortgage payments out over a longer period if possible.
examples.zh: 如果可能的话，我想把按揭贷款的期限延长一些。

definitions.en: If jobs or tasks stretch you, they make you learn new things that use your skill and experience more than before.
definitions.zh: （工作或任务）使施展本领，对…具有挑战性

examples.en: My present job doesn't stretch me, so I'm looking for something more demanding.
examples.zh: 我目前的工作不能让我尽展所长，所以我在寻找更有挑战性的工作。

definitions.en: a continuous area of land or water

examples.en: This particular stretch of coast is especially popular with hikers.
examples.zh: 在这一段海岸散步的人特别多。

examples.en: Traffic is at a standstill along a five-mile stretch of the ring-road.
examples.zh: 环城公路有5英里的路段交通陷入瘫痪。

examples.en: Some very rare birds inhabit our stretch of the river.
examples.zh: 一些非常珍稀的鸟类栖息在我们这个河段。

examples.en: She looked certain to win as she entered the final stretch.
examples.zh: 她进入最后一段跑道时似乎已经赢定了。

examples.en: He fell as he galloped down the home stretch (= towards the finish).
examples.zh: 他在冲向终点时跌倒了。

examples.en: There has been a collision on the southbound stretch of the motorway.
This stretch of land was once covered with luxuriant forest, but is now bare.
This stretch of water is heavily used by shipping.
There have been many shipwrecks along this dangerous stretch of coastline.
The car was dumped in a stretch of wasteland in the south of the city.

definitions.en: an act of stretching

examples.en: I always have a good stretch when I get up in the morning.
examples.zh: 我早上起床总会好好地伸展一下筋骨。

examples.en: This is a really good stretch for your hip flexors.
examples.zh: 这对你的髋部屈肌是很好的舒展活动。

definitions.en: the fact that something has gone past its usual limits
definitions.zh: 超过限度;过分;离谱;破例

examples.en: His idea may be a stretch, but it's not outside the realm of possibility.
examples.zh: 他的主意可能有点儿夸大其词，但并不是完全没有可能。

definitions.en: the degree to which a material can be made longer or wider by pulling

examples.en: This fabric doesn't have much stretch in it, does it?
examples.zh: 这种织物没什么弹性，是吗？

definitions.en: a continuous period of time

examples.en: The elderly generally need far less rest than the young, and tend to sleep in several short stretches.
examples.zh: 年长者一般需要的休息时间比年轻人要少得多，而且往往会分成几个小段时间睡觉。

examples.en: Her brother's doing a ten-year stretch for armed robbery.
examples.zh: 她弟弟因持械抢劫正在服刑，刑期10年。

examples.en: There's no way I could work for ten hours at a stretch.
examples.zh: 要我连续工作10个小时是不可能的。

definitions.en: a job, task, or role that is difficult for someone because it is very different from what they usually do
definitions.zh: （演员的）有难度的表演，拓宽戏路的表演

examples.en: Playing a budding opera star was not much of a stretch for this classically trained singer.
examples.zh: 饰演一位崭露头角的歌剧明星对这个受过古典音乐训练的歌手而言不是什么难事。

definitions.en: an important positive development

examples.en: The West made impressive strides in improving energy efficiency after the huge rises in oil prices during the 70s.
examples.zh: 70年代石油价格大幅上涨之后，西方国家在提高能源使用效率方面取得了显著进步。

examples.en: The group has made strides to expand internationally.
examples.zh: 这个集团在拓展国际市场方面取得了很大进展。

definitions.en: a long step when walking or running

examples.en: She attributes her record-breaking speed to the length of her stride.
examples.zh: 她认为她的速度之所以能打破纪录是因为步幅大。

definitions.en: trousers

examples.en: a new pair of strides
examples.zh: 一条新裤子

definitions.en: to walk somewhere quickly with long steps

examples.en: She strode purposefully up to the desk and demanded to speak to the manager.
examples.zh: 她果断地大步走到桌前，要求见经理。

examples.en: He strode across/into/out of the room.
examples.zh: 他大步走过／进／出房间。

definitions.en: (in baseball) the act making a batter (= person trying to hit the ball) fail to hit the ball three times

examples.en: He averaged 9 strikeouts per game last season.
examples.zh: 上个赛季他平均每场9次令对方击球员出局。

definitions.en: to remove, pull, or tear the covering or outer layer from something
definitions.zh: 除去，剥除，撕掉（外皮或表层）

examples.en: Because of the pollution, the trees are almost completely stripped of bark.
examples.zh: 由于受到污染，树木的表皮几乎完全脱落。

examples.en: The paintwork was so bad that we decided to strip off all the paint and start again.
examples.zh: 漆面太糟，我们决定铲去所有的油漆，重漆一遍。

examples.en: During the summer months, the sheep strip the mountains bare.
examples.zh: 夏天的几个月里，羊群把山上啃得光秃秃的。

examples.en: Huge areas of land have been stripped of all trees and vegetation.

definitions.en: to remove your clothing, or to remove the clothing of someone else

examples.en: The men were ordered to strip.
examples.zh: 那些男人被命令把衣服脱光。

examples.en: Suddenly he stripped off and ran into the sea.
examples.zh: 他突然脱掉衣服冲进大海。

examples.en: He had been stripped naked, beaten and robbed.
examples.zh: 他被脱光衣服，遭受殴打，东西还被抢了。

examples.en: The first time we see her character in the film, she is stripping on stage in a club.

definitions.en: to remove parts of a machine, vehicle, or engine in order to clean or repair it
definitions.zh: 拆卸，拆开（机器、车辆或引擎）

examples.en: I've decided to strip down my motorbike and rebuild it.
examples.zh: 我已经决定了把摩托车拆开重新组装。

definitions.en: a long, flat, narrow piece

examples.en: a narrow strip of land
examples.zh: 狭长地带

examples.en: He didn't have a bandage, so he ripped up his shirt into thin strips.
examples.zh: 他没有绷带，就把衬衫撕成细条。

examples.en: Protect the magnetic strip on your credit card from scratches, heat, or other damage.
examples.zh: 注意保护信用卡的磁条，防止划伤、受热及其他损伤。

definitions.en: the clothing worn by a football team that has the team's colours on it

examples.en: The team will be wearing its new strip at next Saturday's match.
examples.zh: 球队在下周六的比赛中将穿新队服亮相。

definitions.en: an entertainment in which the performer removes all his or her clothing

examples.en: He jumped up on the table and started to do a strip.
examples.zh: 他跳上桌子，开始跳起了脱衣舞。

definitions.en: to try very hard to do something or to make something happen, especially for a long time or against difficulties
definitions.zh: （尤指长期或不畏艰难地）努力，奋斗，力争

examples.en: Mr Roe has kindled expectations that he must now strive to live up to.
examples.zh: 罗先生激发了人们的期望，现在他必须努力不辜负这种期望。

examples.en: In her writing she strove for a balance between innovation and familiar prose forms.
examples.zh: 她在写作中力图在创新和传统散文形式之间求得平衡。

examples.en: We must strive to narrow the gap between rich and poor.
We strive to ensure that all children are treated equally.
We strive for perfection but sometimes have to accept something less.
We are constantly striving to improve our service.
The former leader now has to watch his successor unpicking much of what he strived so hard to achieve.

definitions.en: to step awkwardly while walking or running and fall or begin to fall

examples.en: Running along the beach, she stumbled on a log and fell on the sand.
examples.zh: 她沿着海滩跑步时绊到一块木头上摔倒了。

examples.en: In the final straight Meyers stumbled, and although he didn't fall it was enough to lose him first place.
examples.zh: 迈耶斯在最后的直道上绊了一下，虽然没有摔倒，但也足以使他丢掉第一的位置。

examples.en: We could hear her stumbling about/around the bedroom in the dark.
examples.zh: 我们可以听到她摸黑在卧室里跌跌撞撞地走动。

examples.en: He pulled on his clothes and stumbled into the kitchen.
examples.zh: 他披上衣服，摇摇晃晃地走进厨房。

definitions.en: to make a mistake, such as repeating something or pausing for too long, while speaking or playing a piece of music
definitions.zh: 犯错;磕磕巴巴地说，支吾;不顺畅地演奏

examples.en: When the poet stumbled over a line in the middle of a poem, someone in the audience corrected him.
examples.zh: 诗人在朗诵一首诗的过程中念错了一句，听众中有人纠正了他。

definitions.en: to divide something into smaller parts

examples.en: Each chapter is subdivided into smaller sections.
examples.zh: 每一章又被细分为几个小节。

definitions.en: having a lower or less important position

examples.en: a subordinate role
examples.zh: 配角

examples.en: subordinate status
examples.zh: 从属地位

examples.en: The individual's needs are subordinate to those of the group.
examples.zh: 个人需求从属于集体需求。

definitions.en: a person who has a less important position than you in an organization

examples.en: He left the routine checks to one of his subordinates.
examples.zh: 他把例行检查交给了一个下属负责。

definitions.en: a word whose meaning is included in the meaning of another word

examples.en: "Dog" is a subordinate of the category "pet".
examples.zh: “狗”是“宠物”的下义词。

definitions.en: to put someone or something into a less important position
definitions.zh: 使从属于，使处于次要地位

examples.en: Her personal life has been subordinated to her career.
examples.zh: 她将个人生活置于事业之下。

definitions.en: happening after something else

examples.en: The book discusses his illness and subsequent resignation from politics.
examples.zh: 书中讲述了他患病及随后隐退政坛的事。

examples.en: Those explosions must have been subsequent to our departure, because we didn't hear anything.
examples.zh: 那些爆炸一定是在我们离开后发生的，因为我们什么也没听到。

examples.en: The mistakes were corrected in a subsequent edition of the book.
In subsequent years he said he regretted his actions.
The press gave a lot of coverage to the break-up of his marriage and his subsequent affair with Taylor.
The following year saw his involvement in the arms affair and subsequent resignation from the company.
The union would keep up the pressure in subsequent months but to little avail.

definitions.en: to a large degree

examples.en: The new rules will substantially change how we do things.
examples.zh: 新规则会在很大程度上改变我们的行事方式。

examples.en: House prices have risen substantially over the last six months.
Their new house is substantially bigger than their previous one.
They charge substantially more than their competitors.
The quality of their products has improved substantially over the last five years.
We now have substantially reduced interest rates.

definitions.en: generally

examples.en: This model has leather seats, but the two cars are substantially the same.
examples.zh: 这一型号有几个附加装置，但两款车大体上是一样的。

definitions.en: to use something or someone instead of another thing or person

examples.en: You can substitute oil for butter (= use oil instead of butter) in this recipe.
examples.zh: 这道菜中你可以用食用油代替黄油。

examples.en: You can substitute the wheat flour with almond flour (= use almond flour instead of wheat flour).
He made his film debut when he was substituted for the actor who was originally cast.
I took out the words "he" and "his" and substituted them with "they" and "their".
Gas-fired power stations will substitute for less efficient coal-fired equipment.
examples.zh: 以煤气为燃料的发电站将会代替效率较低的燃煤装置。

examples.en: The soprano first appeared on stage when she substituted for someone who was ill.
You can always substitute tofu for meat, if you like.
I use that recipe but substitute the cream with coconut milk.
Is it possible for you to substitute someone else to do your work?
Computers can't substitute for human interaction.
Of course, no book or course of study can substitute for experience.

definitions.en: a thing or person that is used instead of another thing or person

examples.en: Tofu can be used as a meat substitute in vegetarian recipes.
examples.zh: 素食菜谱中豆腐可用以替代肉类。

examples.en: Vitamins should not be used as a substitute for a healthy diet.
examples.zh: 维生素不应用于替代健康饮食。

examples.en: Johnson came on as a substitute towards the end of the game.
examples.zh: 约翰逊在比赛接近尾声时作为替补队员上场。

examples.en: The manager brought on another substitute in the final minutes of the game.
examples.zh: 比赛还剩下最后几分钟时，主教练又换上了一位替补队员。

examples.en: You can read about other countries, but there's no substitute for visiting them yourself.
examples.zh: 你可以阅读有关其他国家的信息，但是什么也代替不了亲自到访体验。

examples.en: I'm allowed to eat ersatz chocolate made from carob beans, but it's a poor substitute for the real thing.
Lime zest can be used as a substitute for lime leaves.
Email is all well and good but it's a poor substitute for a hand-written letter.
This is not intended as a substitute for a lawyer's advice.
There is no substitute for proper training.

definitions.en: a substance or surface that an organism grows and lives on and is supported by
not loud, bright, noticeable, or obvious in any way
definitions.zh: 隐约的;暗淡的;不易察觉的，不明显的;微妙的

examples.en: The room was painted a subtle shade of pink.
examples.zh: 房间被刷成了淡粉色。

examples.en: The play's message is perhaps too subtle to be understood by young children.
examples.zh: 这部戏的内涵过于隐晦，小孩子可能难以理解。

examples.en: There is a subtle difference between these two plans.
examples.zh: 两个计划之间有一点细微的差别。

examples.en: a subtle plan/suggestion
examples.zh: 巧妙的计划／建议

examples.en: subtle questions
examples.zh: 巧妙的问题

examples.en: I could detect subtle variances in fragrance as we strolled through the garden.
Her conversation sparkled with her own subtle blend of wit and charm.
The film's direction is subtle and stylish.
The gradation in tempo in this piece of music is very subtle.
The directors managed to secure a good deal for the company with a bit of subtle manoeuvring.

definitions.en: in a way that is small and difficult to notice or describe, but important

examples.en: His expression changed subtly.
examples.zh: 他的表情微妙地变了。

examples.en: This discovery had subtly changed/altered the way I thought about myself.
examples.zh: 这一发现微妙地改变/改变了我对自己的看法。

examples.en: The lyrics have been subtly altered.
He subtly implied that she hadn't been happy at work.
Her appearance had changed, subtly but perceptibly.
If you're more subtly sexy you're still noticed but in a different way.

definitions.en: to remove a number from another number

examples.en: Four subtracted from ten equals six.
examples.zh: 10减去4等于6。

definitions.en: to try to destroy or damage something, especially an established political system
definitions.zh: 破坏，动摇，颠覆（尤指现有的政治体制）

examples.en: The rebel army is attempting to subvert the government.
examples.zh: 叛军试图推翻政府。

examples.en: Our best intentions are sometimes subverted by our natural tendency to selfishness.
examples.zh: 我们的良好意图有时会被自私的本性所破坏。

definitions.en: happening one after the other without any break

examples.en: It was the team's fourth successive defeat.
examples.zh: 那是球队接连第4次失利。

examples.en: He won the World Championship for the third successive year.
examples.zh: 他连续第3次赢得世界锦标赛的冠军。

definitions.en: said in a clear and short way; expressing what needs to be said without unnecessary words

examples.en: Keep your letter succinct and to the point.
examples.zh: 信一定要写得简明扼要。

definitions.en: to be enough

examples.en: I'm taking $400 - I think that should suffice.
examples.zh: 我带了400美元——我觉得应该够了。

definitions.en: enough

examples.en: McGeechan has not recovered sufficiently to play in the semifinal tomorrow.
examples.zh: McGeechan还没有恢复到可以打明天的半决赛。

examples.en: The case was sufficiently serious to warrant investigation by the police.
examples.zh: 这个案子严重到需要交由警方来搜捕调查。

examples.en: I was sufficiently close to hear what they were saying.
By this time she was sufficiently recovered to be back at work.
I am not confident that the measures in place are sufficiently rigorous.
It is thought that the feed was not exposed to sufficiently high temperatures to rid it of infection.
I'm not sufficiently versed in computers to understand what you're saying.

definitions.en: to mention an idea, possible plan, or action for other people to consider

examples.en: I suggested an Italian restaurant near the station for the party.
examples.zh: 我建议聚会去火车站附近的一家意大利餐馆。

examples.en: Might I suggest a white wine with your salmon, sir?
examples.zh: 我建议来一杯白葡萄酒配您的鲑鱼吧，先生？

examples.en: I suggest (that) we wait a while before we make any firm decisions.
examples.zh: 我建议我们在作出任何明确而肯定的决定之前先考虑片刻。

examples.en: Liz suggested (that) I try the shop on Mill Road.
examples.zh: 莉兹建议我去米尔街的那家店看看。

examples.en: I suggested putting the matter to the committee.
examples.zh: 我提议把这件事提交委员会讨论。

examples.en: Can you suggest where I could buy a dozen roses?
examples.zh: 你知道有什么地方我可以买一打玫瑰吗？

examples.en: I suggest we use Barkers as our main suppliers - they're good and furthermore they're cheap.
I suggested that we should meet, and they agreed .
She suggested half-jokingly that they should sell the family car and all buy bikes instead.
They'd laugh in your face if you suggested me for the post.
They suggested we meet up at Mustafa's.

definitions.en: to communicate or show an idea or feeling without stating it directly or giving proof

examples.en: There's no absolute proof, but all the evidence suggests (that) he's guilty.
examples.zh: 虽然没有确凿的证据，但所有证据都表明他有罪。

examples.en: Are you suggesting (that) I look fat in these trousers?
examples.zh: 你言下之意是说我穿这条裤子显得胖？

examples.en: Something about his manner suggested a lack of interest in what we were doing.
examples.zh: 他的举止流露出的某种东西表明他对我们正在做的事情并无兴趣。

examples.en: The latest opinion polls suggest that his days as leader are numbered.
Current demographic trends suggest that there will be fewer school leavers coming into the workforce in ten years' time.
There is no scientific evidence to suggest that underwater births are dangerous.
Studies suggest that regular intake of the vitamin significantly improves brain function.
Laboratory tests suggest that the new drug may be used to treat cancer.

definitions.en: to produce an idea in the mind

examples.en: Does anything suggest itself? (= Do you have any ideas about what we should do?)
examples.zh: 你想到什么好主意了吗？

definitions.en: a set of connected rooms, especially in a hotel

examples.en: The singer was interviewed in his hotel suite.
examples.zh: 这位歌手在宾馆套房里接受了采访。

examples.en: They have a whole suite of offices on the 34th floor.
examples.zh: 他们在34层有一整套办公室。

definitions.en: a set of furniture for one room, of matching design and colour

examples.en: I've ordered a new three-piece suite for the living-room.
examples.zh: 我为客厅订购了一套三件套的新家具。

examples.en: We're having a new bathroom/bedroom suite fitted at the weekend.
examples.zh: 周末我们要请人给浴室／卧室安装一套新家具。

definitions.en: a piece of music with several parts, usually all in the same key
a set of related software (= computer program) products
extremely good

examples.en: They are both super-duper spellers.
examples.zh: 他们都是超强的拼写者。

examples.en: He was one of these young guys who were just super-duper.
examples.zh: 他是这些超级优秀的年轻人中的一个。

examples.en: I've got a new super-duper computer.
They put the company's success down to their super-duper manager.
The super-duper French railways show up the many flaws in Britain's decrepit system.

definitions.en: extremely large or serious

examples.en: This is a super-duper problem.
examples.zh: 这是一个超级大问题。

examples.en: The song became a super-duper hit.
examples.zh: 这首歌成了超级大热门。

examples.en: If you want super-duper ease of use then you should choose this machine.
They're not going to notice a super duper traffic increase.

definitions.en: of excellent quality; very great

examples.en: He is a superb dancer.
examples.zh: 他是一个杰出的舞蹈家。

examples.en: Taylor scored a superb goal at the end of the first half.
examples.zh: 泰勒在上半场要结束的时候攻入了一个极为精彩的进球。

examples.en: The team's superb athleticism compensated for their lack of international experience.
The exhibition includes several superb nudes.
She gave a superb performance as Lady Macbeth.
A poor game was redeemed in the second half by a couple of superb goals from Anthony Edwards.
She gave a superb performance of the Beethoven.

definitions.en: (of a person) never thinking about things that are serious or important

examples.en: He's fun to be with, but he's very superficial.
examples.zh: 和他在一起很有趣，但是他见识很肤浅。

definitions.en: not complete and involving only the most obvious things

examples.en: I thought that article was written at a very superficial level.
examples.zh: 我认为那篇文章写得很肤浅。

examples.en: The documentary's treatment/analysis of the issues was very superficial.
examples.zh: 这部纪录片对这个问题的处理／分析非常肤浅。

examples.en: I only have a superficial (= slight) knowledge of French.
examples.zh: 我对法语只是略知皮毛。

definitions.en: appearing to be real or important when this is not true or correct

examples.en: There are superficial similarities between the two cars, but really they're very different in terms of performance.
examples.zh: 这两辆汽车表面上虽然相似，但事实上它们的性能相去甚远。

definitions.en: only on the surface of something

examples.en: superficial damage
examples.zh: 表面损伤

examples.en: The driver only received superficial injuries/cuts/wounds.
examples.zh: 司机只受了点皮外伤。

definitions.en: in a way that is not complete and involves only the most obvious things

examples.en: The arguments were very superficially discussed.
examples.zh: 这些论点得到了非常肤浅的讨论。

examples.en: Books about professional sports are usually superficially reported and poorly written.
examples.zh: 关于职业体育的书籍通常被肤浅地报道而且写得并不好。

definitions.en: in a way that only affects the surface of something

examples.en: Maize stems superficially resemble bamboo canes.
examples.zh: 玉米茎表面上类似于竹藤。

examples.en: Anyone who bathes regularly with soap and water may be considered superficially clean.
examples.zh: 任何经常用肥皂和水洗澡的人都可被认为是外表干净的。

definitions.en: more than is needed or wanted

examples.en: The report was marred by a mass of superfluous detail.
examples.zh: 这篇报告坏就坏在里面细枝末节的东西太多。

definitions.en: better than average or better than other people or things of the same type
definitions.zh: 好于平均的，好过其他的，优越的

examples.en: This is clearly the work of a superior artist.
examples.zh: 这显然是一位优秀艺术家的作品。

examples.en: She was chosen for the job because she was the superior candidate.
examples.zh: 因为她比其他应聘者更出色，所以她被选中做这份工作。

examples.en: For babies, breastfeeding is superior to bottle-feeding.
examples.zh: 对婴儿来说，母乳喂养比奶瓶喂养好得多。

examples.en: The government troops were superior in numbers (= there were more of them).
examples.zh: 政府军在人数上占优势。

examples.en: a superior manner/smile
examples.zh: 傲慢的态度／微笑

examples.en: I can't stand Amanda - she's so superior.
examples.zh: 我受不了阿曼达——她太高傲了。

examples.en: Although badly out-numbered by the enemy, we had vastly superior firepower.
She thinks she's morally superior to the rest of us.
This cheese is vastly superior to the other.
They only employ a very superior kind of nanny.
They make superior outdoor products.

definitions.en: higher in rank or social position than others

examples.en: The soldier was reported to his superior officer for failing in his duties.
examples.zh: 这个士兵失职之事被报告到了他的上级那里。

examples.en: The head is superior to the shoulder.
examples.zh: 头比肩离脚更远。

definitions.en: a person or group of people who are higher in rank or social position than others

examples.en: I will pass your complaint on to my superiors.
examples.zh: 我会把您的投诉转达给我的上司。

definitions.en: to replace something, especially something older or more old-fashioned
definitions.zh: 替代，取代（尤指陈旧或过时之物）

examples.en: Most of the old road has been superseded by the great interstate highways.
examples.zh: 大多数的旧公路都被宽阔的州际公路取代了。

definitions.en: something that is added to something else in order to improve it or complete it; something extra

examples.en: The doctor said she should be taking vitamin supplements.
examples.zh: 医生说她应该服用维生素补充剂。

examples.en: The money I get from teaching eveningnight classes provides a supplement to my main income.
examples.zh: 我晚上教课所挣的钱可以补充我的主要收入。

examples.en: We paid a supplement (= an extra amount of money) so that we could have a cabin on board the ship.
examples.zh: 我们多付了一些钱，这样我们在船上就可以有一个单独的小舱住了。

examples.en: The newspaper publishes a sports supplement every Monday.
examples.zh: 这家报纸每周一出版一份体育副刊。

examples.en: There is a supplement to the dictionary containing new words.
examples.zh: 这部词典有一个新词附录。

definitions.en: to add something to something to make it larger or better

examples.en: He supplements (= adds to) his income by working in a bar at night.
examples.zh: 他晚上在酒吧打工以增加收入。

examples.en: Some vegetarians like to supplement their diets with iron.
examples.zh: 有些素食主义者喜欢在饮食之外吃一些含铁的药片。

definitions.en: If an angle is supplementary to another angle, it forms 180° when combined with it.
extra

examples.en: a supplementary income
examples.zh: 额外收入

definitions.en: to provide something that is wanted or needed, often in large quantities and over a long period of time

examples.en: Electrical power is supplied by underground cables.
examples.zh: 电力是由地下电缆输送的。

examples.en: Three people have been arrested for supplying arms to the terrorists.
examples.zh: 有三个人因为向恐怖分子提供武器而被捕。

examples.en: The company has supplied the royal family (= provided them with something they need) for years.
examples.zh: 多年来该公司一直为皇室供货。

examples.en: At the beginning of termthe semester, students are supplied with a list of books that they are expected to read.
examples.zh: 在学期初，学生们拿到了一份阅读书目清单。

examples.en: A healthy diet should supply all necessary vitamins and minerals.
We supply systems for clients on the Pacific rim.
The proposed tidal barrage would generate enough electricity to supply between 60 000 and 80 000 homes.
All information supplied will be treated confidentially.
All our electric shavers are supplied with a free travel pouch.

definitions.en: an amount of something that is available for use

examples.en: Whenever she goes out with her baby, she always takes a large supply of baby food with her.
examples.zh: 每次她带孩子出门，总会带上大量的婴儿食品。

examples.en: In the capital, demand for cheap housing far outstrips supply (= what is provided).
examples.zh: 在首都，廉价住房远远供不应求。

examples.en: The refugees are urgently in need of food and medical supplies.
examples.zh: 难民急需食品和医疗用品。

examples.en: Extensive mining has reduced the supplies of coal in the area.
examples.zh: 大量开采，已减少了该地区的煤炭供应量。

examples.en: Someone has turned off the electricity supply.
examples.zh: 有人切断了供电系统。

examples.en: Strawberries are in short supply this summer.
examples.zh: 今年夏天草莓供应不足。

examples.en: The water supply is being tested for contamination .
The clinic provides a free supply of contraceptives on request.
The world's supply of agricultural land is shrinking fast.
If this bill is not paid within five days, your gas supply will be cut off.
He seems to think that I have an endless supply of money.

definitions.en: the outer or top part or layer of something

examples.en: Tropical rain forests used to cover ten percent of the earth's surface.
examples.zh: 以前，地球表面的10%都被热带雨林所覆盖。

examples.en: The marble has a smooth, shiny surface.
examples.zh: 大理石表面光滑亮泽。

examples.en: Neil Armstrong was the first person to set foot on the surface of the moon.
examples.zh: 尼尔‧阿姆斯特朗是第一个登上月球的人。

examples.en: The match will be played on an artificial/all-weather surface.
examples.zh: 这场比赛将会在人工／全天候场地上进行。

examples.en: a work surface
examples.zh: 工作台面

examples.en: Don't put anything wet on a polished surface, or it will leave a mark.
examples.zh: 别把任何湿东西放在抛光的台面上，不然会留下印痕的。

examples.en: She watched the sunlight dancing on the water's surface.
Pollution has decayed the surface of the stonework on the front of the cathedral.
There seems to have been some abrasion of the surface.
The surface had been filed smooth.
Roll out the pastry on a flat surface.

definitions.en: The surface of a situation or person is what they appear to be, or the features they have that are not hidden or difficult to see.

examples.en: On the surface, this seems like a difficult problem, but in fact there's an easy solution to it.
examples.zh: 表面上来看，这是一个难题，但事实上它很容易解决。

examples.en: Beneath/Below/Under the surface of contemporary West Indian life lurk memories of slavery.
examples.zh: 在西印度现代生活的背后，有关奴隶制度的记忆依然挥之不去。

examples.en: Suddenly, all her anger came/rose to the surface (= became obvious).
examples.zh: 她所有的愤怒一下子都爆发出来了。

definitions.en: to rise to the surface of water

examples.en: The submarine surfaced a few miles off the coast.
examples.zh: 潜艇在离海岸几英里的地方浮出了水面。

definitions.en: If a feeling or information surfaces, it becomes known.

examples.en: Doubts are beginning to surface about whether the right decision has been made.
examples.zh: 人们开始对这个决定是否正确表示质疑。

examples.en: A rumour has surfaced that the company is about to go out of business.
examples.zh: 有传言说这家公司要停业了。

definitions.en: to get out of bed

examples.en: He never surfaces until at least 11.00 a.m. on a Sunday.
examples.zh: 他星期天至少要睡到上午11点才起床。
