definitions.en: working or operating on the top of the land or sea, rather than under the land or sea, or by air
definitions.zh: 地表上作业的；海面上作业的

definitions.en: appearing in a particular way but not always showing the truth

examples.en: his surface appearance of calm confidence
examples.zh: 他表面上的冷静自信

definitions.en: a sudden and great increase

examples.en: An unexpected surge in electrical power caused the computer to crash.
examples.zh: 突然出现的电压剧增导致电脑死机。

examples.en: There has been a surge in house prices recently.
examples.zh: 最近房价飞涨。

examples.en: At the end of the game, there was a surge of fans onto the field.
examples.zh: 比赛结束后，大群的球迷涌向比赛场地。

examples.en: A tidal surge (= sudden and great rise in the level of the sea) caused severe flooding in coastal areas.
examples.zh: 潮水猛涨在沿海地区引发了严重水灾。

examples.en: She was overwhelmed by a surge of remorse.
examples.zh: 悔恨一下子涌上了她的心头。

examples.en: The surge in the power supply knocked out all the computers.
The surge in US share prices reverberated across the globe.
A special system prevents the circuitry being tripped accidentally by a power surge or lightning strike.
This new surge of interest in religion is perhaps a reaction to the the spiritual wasteland of the 1980s.

definitions.en: to increase suddenly and strongly

examples.en: The company's profits have surged.
examples.zh: 公司的利润激增。

examples.en: An angry crowd surged through the gates of the president's palace.
examples.zh: 愤怒的人群涌进了总统府的大门。

examples.en: A few metres before the end of the race, Jenkins surged into the lead.
examples.zh: 就在离终点几米的地方，詹金斯突然发力猛冲到最前面。

examples.en: She felt a wave of resentment surging (up) inside her.
examples.zh: 她感觉一阵悔恨涌上心头。

examples.en: The dollar surged against the yen in the final half hour of trading.
A feeling of love surged in his breast.
Sales of these cosmetics have surged in recent months.
The mob surged towards the castle.

definitions.en: to guess something, without having much or any proof

examples.en: The police surmise (that) the robbers have fled the country.
examples.zh: 据警方推测，抢劫犯已经逃离了这个国家。

definitions.en: a guess

examples.en: My surmise turned out to be right.
examples.zh: 最后证实我的推测是正确的。

examples.en: The article is pure surmise and innuendo.
examples.zh: 这篇文章完全是在主观臆测、含沙射影。

definitions.en: (an amount that is) more than is needed

examples.en: The world is now producing large food surpluses.
examples.zh: 世界上目前生产的食品大大过剩。

examples.en: We are unlikely to produce any surplus this year.
examples.zh: 我们今年不太可能有任何盈余。

examples.en: The government has authorized the army to sell its surplus weapons.
examples.zh: 政府已经授权军队出售多余的武器。

examples.en: The store is selling off stock that is surplus to requirements (= more than they need to have).
examples.zh: 这家商店正在甩卖多余的存货。

examples.en: a budget/trade surplus
examples.zh: 预算结余／贸易顺差

examples.en: Fortunately the company's bank account is currently in surplus.
examples.zh: 幸好目前公司的银行账户里还有结余。

examples.en: A surplus of corn has helped depress the grain market.
The chemical industry has a large and growing trade surplus.
There is no surplus capacity in our transport system.
We produce enough vegetables for our community, and any surplus is sold to local shops.

definitions.en: the careful watching of a person or place, especially by the police or army, because of a crime that has happened or is expected
definitions.zh: （尤指警察或军队的）监视，盯梢

examples.en: The police have kept the nightclub under surveillance because of suspected illegal drug activity.
examples.zh: 这家夜总会因涉嫌非法毒品活动而被警方监视。

examples.en: More banks are now installing surveillance cameras.
examples.zh: 现在更多的银行开始安装监控摄像机。

definitions.en: easily influenced or harmed by something
definitions.zh: 易受影响的，易受伤害的

examples.en: She isn't very susceptible to flattery.
examples.zh: 她不太听信别人的奉承。

examples.en: These plants are particularly susceptible to frost.
examples.zh: 这些植物很容易受霜冻的影响。

examples.en: Among particularly susceptible children, the disease can develop very fast.
examples.zh: 在特别易感的儿童中，这种疾病病情发展会非常迅速。

examples.en: They persuade susceptible teenagers to part with their money.
examples.zh: 他们劝说这些易被感动的年轻人捐出他们的钱。

definitions.en: (especially of an idea or statement) able to be understood, proved, explained, etc. in a particular way
definitions.zh: （尤指想法或陈述）能被理解（或证明、解释）的

examples.en: Shakespeare's plays are susceptible to various interpretations.
examples.zh: 莎士比亚的戏剧可以有多种诠释。

examples.en: The facts are susceptible of other explanations.
examples.zh: 这些事实可能还有别的解释。

definitions.en: the act of stopping something happening, operating, etc. for a period of time

examples.en: The suspension of fighting is to take effect at 6 a.m. on Monday.
examples.zh: 停战协定于周一早上6点生效。

examples.en: There have been calls for the drug's immediate suspension, following reports that it has dangerous side effects.
examples.zh: 有报道指出这种药物有危险的副作用后，人们呼吁立即停止使用该药。

examples.en: The union is protesting about the suspension of a restaurant worker.
examples.zh: 工会因一名饭店员工被停职而在进行抗议。

examples.en: The player is likely to receive a three-game suspension following an incident in yesterday's game.
examples.zh: 这位球员可能会因为昨天比赛中发生的事件而被停赛3场。

examples.en: There were calls for the suspension of parliament.
The suspension of hostilities allowed both sides to come to the negotiating table.
Staff are furious about a suspension of their flexible working scheme.
The school allows a partial suspension of its rules on the last day of term.

definitions.en: equipment attached to the wheels of a vehicle that reduces the uncomfortable effects of going over road surfaces that are not even
a liquid in which small pieces of solid are contained, but not dissolved

examples.en: a suspension of fine cornflour in corn oil
examples.zh: 悬浮在玉米油中的精玉米粉

definitions.en: making you feel that something illegal is happening or that something is wrong

examples.en: Her behaviour was very suspicious.
examples.zh: 她的举止非常可疑。

examples.en: The fire at the bank is being treated as suspicious.
examples.zh: 银行起火被认为非常可疑。

examples.en: It's a little suspicious that no one knows where he was at the time of the murder.
examples.zh: 有点儿可疑的是，没有人知道谋杀案发当时他在哪里。

examples.en: There were some suspicious characters hanging around outside.
examples.zh: 有些可疑的人在外面晃荡。

examples.en: There's a suspicious-looking van parked at the end of the street.
examples.zh: 路的尽头停着一辆看起来很可疑的送货车。

examples.en: His new book bears a suspicious resemblance to a book written by someone else. (= His book is so similar to the other book that it seems as if he has copied it.)
examples.zh: 他的新书与别人写的另外一本书内容雷同，令人生疑。

examples.en: She died in suspicious circumstances.
The public were warned to be on the alert for suspicious packages.
There was something furtive about his behaviour and I immediately felt suspicious.
His reticence about his past made them very suspicious.
He looks very suspicious hanging around by the bins - I'm sure he's up to something.

definitions.en: feeling doubt or no trust in someone or something
definitions.zh: 怀疑的，猜疑的;不信任的

examples.en: His colleagues became suspicious (= thought that there was something wrong) when he did not appear at work, since he was always punctual.
examples.zh: 他一向按时来上班，所以当他没有来上班时，他的同事们起了疑心。

examples.en: They are deeply/highly (= very) suspicious of one another/of each other's motives.
examples.zh: 他们对彼此的动机都很怀疑。

examples.en: My mother has a very suspicious nature (= does not trust people).
examples.zh: 我妈妈天生喜欢猜疑。

examples.en: Older people tend to be quite conservative and a bit suspicious of any supposed advances.
She allowed that she might have been too suspicious.
Of course I'm telling the truth - you've got such a suspicious mind!
I was becoming increasingly suspicious of his motives.
We became suspicious when the letter did not arrive.

definitions.en: to cause or allow something to continue for a period of time
definitions.zh: 保持，维持，使持续，使继续

examples.en: The economy looks set to sustain its growth into next year.
examples.zh: 看来经济的增长会持续到明年。

examples.en: He seems to find it difficult to sustain relationships with women.
examples.zh: 他似乎觉得和女人维持关系并不容易。

examples.en: The judge sustained (= accepted) the lawyer's objection.
examples.zh: 法官接受了律师的反对意见。

examples.en: The soil in this part of the world is not rich enough to sustain a large population.
examples.zh: 这里的土地不够肥沃，无法供养大量的人口。

examples.en: It proved particularly difficult to sustain the interest of the older children.
This year's harvest is insufficient to sustain a growing population.
Economic growth cannot be sustained without a rise in inflation.
This planet is unable to sustain human or plant life.
The team were finally victorious after a sustained effort to succeed.

definitions.en: to suffer or experience, especially damage or loss
definitions.zh: 经历，遭受（尤指破坏或损失）

examples.en: She sustained multiple injuries in the accident.
examples.zh: 她在这次事故中多处受伤。

examples.en: Most buildings sustained only minimal damage in the earthquake.
examples.zh: 在这次地震中，大多数建筑物受到的破坏都很小。

examples.en: The company has sustained heavy losses this year.
examples.zh: 公司今年遭受了严重的亏损。

definitions.en: to support emotionally

examples.en: She was sustained by the strength of her religious faith.
examples.zh: 宗教信仰的力量支撑着她。

examples.en: The love of my family and friends sustained me through my ordeal.
examples.zh: 家人和朋友的爱支撑着我渡过了难关。

definitions.en: to use words that are rude or offensive as a way of emphasizing what you mean or as a way of insulting someone or something

examples.en: It was a real shock, the first time I heard my mother swear.
examples.zh: 我第一次听见母亲骂脏话时觉得非常震惊。

examples.en: When the cab driver started to swear at him, he walked away.
examples.zh: 当出租车司机开口对他骂脏话时，他走开了。

examples.en: Please don't swear in front of the children.
I swear quite a lot but even I draw the line at saying certain words.
He was so angry he forgot himself and swore loudly.
We could hear him cursing and swearing as he tried to get the door open.
He was sent off for swearing at the referee.

definitions.en: to promise or say firmly that you are telling the truth or that you will do something or behave in a particular way

examples.en: I don't know anything about what happened, I swear (it).
examples.zh: 我发誓我对所发生的事一无所知。

examples.en: You might find it difficult to believe, but I swear (that) the guy just came up to me and gave me the money.
examples.zh: 你可能觉得这难以置信，但我发誓，那个人确实走过来把这些钱给了我。

examples.en: She swore blind (= promised definitely) (that) she didn't know what had happened to the money.
examples.zh: 她信誓旦旦地说她不知道钱哪儿去了。

examples.en: New gang members must swear to obey the gang leaders at all times.
examples.zh: 新的帮会成员必须发誓在任何时候都服从帮会头目。

examples.en: In some countries, witnesses in court have to swear on the Bible.
examples.zh: 在有些国家，出庭证人必须手按《圣经》宣誓。

examples.en: I swore an oath to tell the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.
examples.zh: 我发誓要陈述实情，言无不尽，绝无半点虚言。

examples.en: A few of us knew what was going to happen, but we were sworn to secrecy (= we were made to promise to keep it a secret).
examples.zh: 我们有几个人知道将要发生什么事，但我们已经发过誓要对此保密。

examples.en: I think his birthday is on the 5th, but I wouldn't/couldn't swear to it (= I am not completely certain about it).
examples.zh: 我想他的生日是5号，但我并不完全肯定。

examples.en: He swore he would avenge his brother's death.
He swore he'd pay her back for all she'd done to him.
Soldiers must swear allegiance to the Crown.
I swear to God I didn't know about it.
I want you to swear that you will never try to see her again.

definitions.en: involving two types of animal or plant in which each provides the conditions necessary for the other to continue to exist

examples.en: a symbiotic relationship
examples.zh: 共生关系

examples.en: This symbiotic relationship is echoed in the clams and other mollusks that also rely on the bacteria for food.
an experiment aimed at learning whether scientists can help corals adapt by providing them with symbiotic partners better prepared to cope with waters that are growing warmer
While having a caddie can improve a golfer's game, the relationship is symbiotic.
This small, symbiotic world of designers, editors and retailers influences what men will wear for the next few seasons.
Their work relationship is even more symbiotic. Not only do they edit the magazine together, they tend to edit manuscripts in tandem.

definitions.en: any feeling of illness or physical or mental change that is caused by a particular disease

examples.en: He's complaining of all the usual flu symptoms - a high temperature, headache, and so on.
examples.zh: 他说他出现了所有常见的感冒症状——高烧、头痛等。

examples.en: He's been HIV-positive for six years, but just recently he's started to develop the symptoms of AIDS.
examples.zh: 他 HIV 病毒检测呈阳性已有6年时间，但直到最近才开始出现艾滋病的症状。

examples.en: It's her feeling that the recent outbreaks of violence are a symptom of the dissatisfaction that is currently affecting our society.
examples.zh: 她觉得最近爆发的暴力事件表明目前不满情绪正在影响我们的社会。

examples.en: He had all the classic symptoms of the disease.
If the symptoms get worse, consult your doctor.
Tiredness, loss of appetite and sleeping problems are all classic symptoms of depression.
They claim it is a symptom of a deeper and more general malaise in society.
The first symptom of the disease is a very high temperature.

definitions.en: a word or phrase that has the same or nearly the same meaning as another word or phrase in the same language

examples.en: The words "small" and "little" are synonyms.
examples.zh: small 和 little 是同义词。

examples.en: 'Wrong' is a synonym for 'incorrect'.
English is a language which is rich in synonyms.

definitions.en: relating to the grammatical arrangement of words in a sentence

examples.en: syntactic analysis
examples.zh: 句法分析

examples.en: Readers use their syntactic and semantic knowledge to decode the text.
examples.zh: 读者使用他们的句法和语义知识来解读文章。

examples.en: Markup and content may be distinguished by the application of simple syntactic rules.
examples.zh: 标记和内容可以通过应用简单的句法规则来区分。

examples.en: These data suggest that even the earliest multi-word utterances adhere to syntactic constraints.
Is it to do with the syntactic structure or to the way in which this structure is lexically manifested?
She speculates that the reason is purely syntactic, being connected with the fact that make (unlike cause) can take a "small clause", as in "You make me angry."
This adjective can go in many syntactic slots.
The correct way to phrase the search query will depend on the syntactic peculiarities of the system.

definitions.en: the grammatical arrangement of words in a sentence

examples.en: The examples should always illustrate correct syntax.
The president's tortured syntax was often satirized.
He spoke using clumsy syntax and hilarious malapropisms.
Signing is a conceptual language, not based on English syntax.
E. E. Cummings was a poet famous for his use of lower case letters and quirky syntax.
The search engine uses the Windows Desktop Search query syntax.
REPLACE has the same syntax as INSERT, and works exactly like INSERT.

definitions.en: to try to deal with something or someone

examples.en: There are many ways of tackling this problem.
examples.zh: 处理这个问题有许多办法。

examples.en: I tackled him about his careless work.
examples.zh: 就他工作马虎一事我与他进行了交涉。

examples.en: The president is clearly in a dilemma about how to tackle the crisis.
There is a great deal of indecision over how to tackle the problem.
The manifesto includes tough measures to tackle road congestion and environmental pollution.
When is the government going to tackle the problem of poverty in the inner cities?
I felt that he just talked round the subject and didn't tackle the main issues.

definitions.en: (especially in football or hockey) to try to take the ball from a player in the other team, or (in rugby or American football) to do this by taking hold of the player and making them fall
definitions.zh: （尤指足球或曲棍球中）阻截;铲球;（橄榄球中阻截时）擒抱并摔倒（对方持球队员）

examples.en: He was tackled in the goalmouth.
He needs to tackle more often.
He tackled his opponent bravely and gained possession of the ball.

definitions.en: (especially in football or hockey) an act of tackling someone
definitions.zh: （尤指足球或曲棍球中）阻截;铲球

definitions.en: all the objects needed for a particular activity

examples.en: fishing tackle
examples.zh: 钓具

definitions.en: the male sexual organs
someone whose job is to repair, make, and adjust (= make changes to) clothes, especially someone who makes jackets, trousers, coats, etc. for men
to make or prepare something following particular instructions
definitions.zh: 专门制作，使适应特定需要

examples.en: We tailor any of our products to your company's specific needs.
examples.zh: 我们可以根据贵公司的特定需求定制产品。

definitions.en: (especially of animals) not wild or dangerous, either naturally or because of training or long involvement with humans

examples.en: After a few months' contact the monkeys become very tame.
examples.zh: 经过几个月的接触后，那些猴子变得非常温顺了。

definitions.en: not interesting or exciting

examples.en: It was a tame film in comparison to some that she's made.
examples.zh: 较之她拍摄的其他一些影片，这一部显得平淡乏味。

definitions.en: to make a wild animal tame

examples.en: He'll need to tame his temper if he wants to succeed.
examples.zh: 如果他想取得成功，就得改改自己的脾气。

definitions.en: a bicycle made for two people who sit one behind the other

examples.en: A man and his daughter cycled past on a tandem.

definitions.en: an untidy mass of things that are not in a state of order, or a state of confusion or difficulty

examples.en: a tangle of wires
examples.zh: 一团乱糟糟的电线

definitions.en: to become or form, or to make something into, an untidy mass
boring

examples.en: a tedious job
examples.zh: 单调乏味的工作

examples.en: The problem is I find most forms of exercise so tedious.
examples.zh: 问题是我觉得大多数形式的锻炼都很乏味。

definitions.en: to make someone want to have or do something, especially something that is unnecessary or wrong

examples.en: The offer of free credit tempted her into buying a new car.
examples.zh: 可享受免费贷款让她禁不住诱惑买了一辆新车。

examples.en: They tempted him to join the company by offering him a huge salary and a company car.
examples.zh: 他们许诺给他优厚的薪水和一辆公司汽车，以吸引他加入该公司。

examples.en: "Did you apply for that job?" "Well, I was very tempted, but I decided not to."
examples.zh: “你申请那个工作了吗？”“唔，我是很想申请，但最后还是决定放弃了。”

examples.en: I was sorely (= very) tempted to resign after my boss screamed at me for the second time.
examples.zh: 老板第二次对我大喊大叫了以后，我很想辞职。

examples.en: Perhaps some caviar can tempt your jaded palate.
I was really tempted to take the money.
They put out food to tempt the foxes into the traps.
Chocolate cake? - Don't tempt me!
I was tempted into ordering a whole new wardrobe.

definitions.en: the wish to do or have something that you know you should not do or have

examples.en: As a young actress, she resisted the temptation to move to Hollywood.
examples.zh: 作为一名年轻的女演员，她抵制住了前往好莱坞的诱惑。

examples.en: He knew it was wrong to steal, but the money just lying there was too great a temptation.
examples.zh: 他知道偷盗是不对的，但钱就摆在那儿，对他的诱惑实在太大了。

examples.en: I'm afraid I succumbed to temptation and had a piece of cheesecake.
I finally surrendered to temptation, and ate the last remaining chocolate.
It's very easy to yield to temptation and spend too much money.
The temptation is to take more food than you need.

definitions.en: the right to use land or live in a building in exchange for rent

examples.en: a two-year tenancy
examples.zh: 两年租赁期

definitions.en: consisting of three parts
using the number three as a base (= the number on which a counting system is built )
very unpleasant or serious or of low quality
definitions.zh: 糟糕的;可怕的;严重的;低劣的

examples.en: The weather was terrible.
examples.zh: 天气糟透了。

examples.en: We have just received some terrible news.
examples.zh: 我们刚刚收到一些可怕的消息。

examples.en: I felt terrible last night but I feel fine this morning.
That restaurant was terrible - I'm never going back there again.
Most of these women are very poorly paid and work in terrible conditions.
I've got no sense of rhythm, so I'm a terrible dancer.
I'll be blunt - that last piece of work you did was terrible.

definitions.en: very bad at doing something

examples.en: You're a terrible liar.
examples.zh: 你真不擅长说谎。

examples.en: I'm a terrible skier/swimmer/dancer.
examples.zh: 我不擅长滑雪／游泳／跳舞。

definitions.en: used to emphasize the great degree of something
definitions.zh: 极度的;厉害的;剧烈的;非常严重的

examples.en: This project is a terrible waste of money.
examples.zh: 这个项目完全是在浪费钱财。

examples.en: She's a terrible nuisance.
examples.zh: 她是个极讨厌的人。

examples.en: I had a terrible headache, but even so I went to the concert.
I find threading a needle a terrible fiddle.
She ran from the house in a terrible rage, her arms flailing in the air.
She's a terrible gossip.
I had a terrible hangover the next morning.

definitions.en: very badly

examples.en: I slept terribly last night.
examples.zh: 我昨晚睡得很不好。

examples.en: Tomato sauce stains terribly - it's really difficult to get it out of clothes.
She'd suffered terribly over the years but it hadn't made her bitter.
She suffers terribly in the winter when it's cold and her joints get stiff.
They were terribly injured.

definitions.en: very

examples.en: I'm terribly pleased to hear that you've got a job.
examples.zh: 听说你找到了工作，我高兴坏了。

examples.en: I was terribly sorry to hear about the death of your mother.
examples.zh: 听到你母亲去世的消息，我非常难过。

examples.en: She's terribly upset because her father passed away last week.
He came home after three months at college looking terribly scrawny.
I'm terribly sorry. I didn't mean to trip you up.
The form was terribly complicated and I had a lot of trouble with it.
I used to take plenty of exercise, but now I'm terribly unfit.

definitions.en: using few words, sometimes in a way that seems rude or unfriendly

examples.en: "Are you feeling any better?" "No!" was the terse reply.
examples.zh: “你觉得好点儿了吗？”“没有。”回答简短而生硬。

definitions.en: proof

examples.en: The detail of her wildlife paintings is (a) testament to (= proof of) her powers of observation.
examples.zh: 她画野生动物十分细致，这证明她有很强的观察能力。

definitions.en: a will that someone makes, saying what should be done with their money and property after they die

examples.en: This article will give clear instructions for making a last will and testament.
examples.zh: 这篇文章清楚介绍了如何立下遗嘱。

definitions.en: US spelling of theatre
connected with heat

examples.en: thermal conductivity (= ability of a substance to carry heat)
examples.zh: 导热系数

examples.en: It was the Romans who first recognized the medicinal benefits of Hungary's thermal springs (= ones which produce hot water).
examples.zh: 首先认识到匈牙利温泉的医疗效用的是罗马人。

definitions.en: a large column of hot air rising from the ground

examples.en: Paragliders can catch rides on these currents, hopping from thermal to thermal and covering many miles in a single day.
You will learn how to steer the glider into rising currents of warm air (thermals) to gain altitude and extend flying time.
The birds are magnificent fliers, capable of riding thermals for miles, like a glider.

definitions.en: clothes made of a material that is especially designed to keep you warm, especially those that are worn under other clothes
definitions.zh: 保暖内衣（thermal underwear 的非正式说法）

examples.en: It'll be really cold so remember to bring your thermals.
I'm okay - I have my thermals on.
You'll need your thermals.
You'll need several layers, starting with thermals.

definitions.en: a device that keeps a building, engine, etc. within a limited temperature range by automatically switching the supply of heat on and off
definitions.zh: 温度自动调节器，恒温器

examples.en: a central heating thermostat
examples.zh: 集中供暖温度调节器

definitions.en: a need for something to drink

examples.en: Hundreds of refugees collapsed from hunger and thirst.
examples.zh: 成百上千的难民饥渴难耐瘫倒在地。

examples.en: I woke up with a pounding headache and a raging (= extreme) thirst.
examples.zh: 我醒来时头痛欲裂，口渴难耐。

examples.en: I've got a terrible thirst after all that running.
examples.zh: 跑了那么长的路，我渴得要命。

definitions.en: a strong wish

examples.en: He's always had a thirst for adventure.
examples.zh: 他总是渴望冒险。

definitions.en: needing to drink

examples.en: I felt/was hot and thirsty after the basketball game.
examples.zh: 篮球比赛后我又热又渴。

examples.en: It's a curious paradox that drinking a lot of water can often make you feel thirsty.
All that exercise has made me thirsty.
Ask the children if they're thirsty.
After three hours in the heat, we were extremely thirsty.
All that singing makes you thirsty.

definitions.en: Someone who is thirsty for power, knowledge, etc. wants to have it very much.
detailed and careful

examples.en: a thorough revision of the manuscript
examples.zh: 对手稿的仔细修改

examples.en: They did a thorough search of the area but found nothing.
examples.zh: 他们仔细搜索了该地区，但一无所获。

examples.en: The arrival of the new baby caused a thorough shake-up of their family life.
They received a thorough briefing before they left the country.
Give the table a thorough wipe.
School gave me a thorough grounding in French.
We did a thorough spring clean of the house.

definitions.en: complete, very great, or very much
definitions.zh: 彻头彻尾的;完全的;十足的

examples.en: It was a thorough waste of time.
examples.zh: 这完全是浪费时间。

definitions.en: completely, very much

examples.en: I thoroughly enjoyed the performance.
examples.zh: 我非常喜欢这个演出。

examples.en: All the other teachers are thoroughly disillusioned with their colleague.
I thoroughly approve of what the government is doing.
She ought to be thoroughly ashamed of herself - talking to her mother like that!
The senator thoroughly squelched the journalist who tried to interrupt him during his speech.
They recognized that the country would revive only if it thoroughly disengaged from the chaos of the old regime.

definitions.en: in a detailed and careful way

examples.en: We went through the report thoroughly but couldn't find the information anywhere.
examples.zh: 我们仔细查看了该报告，但没有找到我们需要的信息。

definitions.en: to hit a person or animal hard many times as a punishment

examples.en: His father used to thrash him when he was a boy.
examples.zh: 他小时候，父亲常常责打他。

examples.en: He thrashed the horse with his whip.
examples.zh: 他用鞭子狠狠抽打这匹马。

definitions.en: to move from side to side in a violent or uncontrolled way
definitions.zh: 猛烈摆动;剧烈扭动;翻腾

examples.en: He was screaming and thrashing around on the floor.
examples.zh: 他大喊大叫，在地板上滚来滚去。

definitions.en: to defeat someone very easily in a game or sports competition

examples.en: We thrashed the visiting team 6–0.
examples.zh: 我们以6比0大败客队。

definitions.en: to thresh
the floor of an entrance to a building or room
the level or point at which you start to experience something, or at which something starts to happen

examples.en: I have a low/high boredom threshold (= I do/don't feel bored easily).
examples.zh: 我很容易／不容易感到厌倦。

examples.en: He earns £400 a month, well below the threshold for paying tax.

definitions.en: extremely happy about something

examples.en: I was thrilled that so many people turned up to the party.
examples.zh: 这么多人参加聚会，我太高兴了。

definitions.en: growing, developing, or being successful

examples.en: a thriving economy
examples.zh: 繁荣的经济

examples.en: The island has a thriving community and a rich and tragic history.
examples.zh: 这座岛上有繁荣的社区和丰富而悲惨的历史。

examples.en: The school was really thriving under her leadership.
The city has had a thriving art scene for more than 100 years.
Over the past century, the town has developed into a thriving commercial centre.

definitions.en: a valve which allows more or less fuel to go into an engine and so changes the power with which the engine operates
to press someone's throat very tightly so that they cannot breathe

examples.en: Sometimes he annoys me so much that I could throttle him.
examples.zh: 有时他真让我讨厌，我恨不得掐死他。

definitions.en: to prevent something from succeeding

examples.en: The reduction in funds is throttling the development of new programmes.
examples.zh: 经费的削减将阻碍新计划的开展。

definitions.en: from one end or side of something to the other

examples.en: They walked slowly through the woods.
examples.zh: 他们缓步穿过树林。

examples.en: The boy waded through the water to reach his boat.
examples.zh: 男孩涉水来到他的船上。

examples.en: He struggled through the crowd till he reached the front.
examples.zh: 他挤过人群来到前面。

examples.en: How long the journey takes will depend on how long it takes to get through the traffic.
examples.zh: 旅程要花多长时间将取决于穿过车流要花多长时间。

examples.en: Her words kept running through my mind/head (= I kept hearing her words in my imagination).
examples.zh: 她的话一直在我脑海里回荡。

examples.en: We drove through the tunnel.
examples.zh: 我们开车穿过隧道。

examples.en: I saw him drive through a red light (= he did not stop at the red traffic light).
examples.zh: 我看见他开车闯红灯。

examples.en: I'll put you through (= connect you by phone) (to the sales department).
examples.zh: 我给你接通（销售部的）电话。

examples.en: The teacher drew a diagram showing how the blood flows through the heart.
She walked through the city centre with its drab, grey buildings and felt depressed.
She heard the eerie noise of the wind howling through the trees.
Our new sofa doesn't fit through the door.
Some poisonous gases can enter the body by absorption through the skin.

definitions.en: from the beginning to the end of a period of time

examples.en: It rained all/right through June and into the first half of July.
examples.zh: 整个6月和7月的上半月一直在下雨。

examples.en: We sat through two of the speeches and then left.
examples.zh: 我们听完整整两个讲话，然后就离开了。

examples.en: She had just enough energy to get through the day.
examples.zh: 她硬撑着坚持了一天。

examples.en: She works Monday through Thursday (= from Monday to Thursday).
examples.zh: 她周一到周四工作。

examples.en: If I can just get through the training period, it won't be so bad after that.
We've gone through a few financially insecure years.
Unfortunately, I lost interest half way through the film.
Electricians worked through the night to localize the faulty switches.
The bells ring at regular intervals through the day.

definitions.en: as a result of

examples.en: The company lost the order through production delays.
examples.zh: 该公司由于生产延误失去了订单。

examples.en: Several political prisoners have been released through the intercession of Amnesty International.
If he fails it won't be through lack of effort.
The soil has been so heavily leached through intensive farming that it is no longer fertile.
A lot of water is wasted through leakage.
Four million hours were lost last year through stress-related illnesses.

definitions.en: by; using

examples.en: I got my car through my brother who works in a garage.
examples.zh: 我通过在汽车修理厂工作的兄弟买的车。

examples.en: We sold the bike through advertising in the local paper.
examples.zh: 我们通过在地方报纸上登广告卖掉了自行车。

examples.en: The performers tell the story through song and dance.
She met her husband through a dating agency.
The police entered the building through the side door.
She still controls the company indirectly through her son, who is the managing director.
Speaking through an interpreter, the president said the terms of the ceasefire were completely unacceptable.

definitions.en: having finished using or doing something

examples.en: I've got some work to do but I should be through in an hour if you can wait.
examples.zh: 我还有些工作要做，不过1小时内应该可以完成，你看能不能等一下。

examples.en: Are you through with that atlas?
examples.zh: 你看完那本地图册了吗？

definitions.en: to achieve success in an exam, competition, etc. and progress to the next stage or a higher level

examples.en: She's through to the next round of interviews.
examples.zh: 她通过了，可参加下一轮面试。

examples.en: "Has she heard about her entrance exams yet?" "Yes, she's through."
examples.zh: “她知道入学考试的成绩了吗？”“知道，她通过了。”

definitions.en: A through train or bus goes all the way from one place to another place without the passenger having to change trains or buses.
definitions.zh: （火车或公共汽车）直达的，中途不停的

definitions.en: in every part, or during the whole period of time

examples.en: People throughout the country are out of work.
examples.zh: 全国各地的人都失去了工作。

examples.en: He yawned throughout the performance.
examples.zh: 演出期间他一直在打哈欠。

examples.en: The school has been repainted throughout.
examples.zh: 学校各处都重新粉刷了一遍。

examples.en: She's been leading throughout the race, but it now looks as if she's tiring.
We'll update you on this news story throughout the day.
He stayed by her side throughout her long illness.
The room has been decorated in pastel shades throughout.
He was faithful to his wife throughout their 30-year marriage.

definitions.en: an amount of work, etc. done in a particular period of time

examples.en: We need to improve our throughput because demand is high at the moment.
examples.zh: 因为目前需求很大，我们需要提高生产能力。

definitions.en: made to be destroyed after use
definitions.zh: 一次性使用的，用后即弃的

examples.en: We live in a throwaway society (= one in which things are thrown away as soon as they have been used).
examples.zh: 我们生活在一个充斥着一次性物品的社会。

definitions.en: to stop something from happening or someone from doing something

examples.en: Our holiday plans were thwarted by the airline pilots' strike
examples.zh: 因为航空公司飞行员罢工，我的度假计划泡汤了。

definitions.en: one of several layers or levels

examples.en: We sat in one of the upper tiers of the football stands.
examples.zh: 我们坐在足球场看台的上层位置。

examples.en: My wedding cake had four tiers, each supported by small pillars.
examples.zh: 我的结婚蛋糕有4层，每层都由小柱子撑着。

examples.en: I don't understand why you think we need yet another tier of management.
examples.zh: 我不明白你为什么认为我们还需要一个管理层级。

definitions.en: to arrange or organize something in tiers

examples.en: The seats in the theatre were steeply tiered.
examples.zh: 剧院的座位呈陡直的阶梯状排列。

definitions.en: (held or kept together) firmly or closely
definitions.zh: 紧的；密封的（地）；牢固的（地）

examples.en: I can't untie the knot - it's too tight.
examples.zh: 我解不开这个结——太紧了。

examples.en: This lid is on very tight.
examples.zh: 这盖子盖得很紧。

examples.en: The people stood talking in tight groups.
examples.zh: 大家一群群地紧挨着站在那里说话。

examples.en: Hold on tight when we go around the corner.
examples.zh: 我们转弯时大家都要抓牢扶好。

examples.en: Check that windows and doors are shut tight (= completely closed) before you leave.
examples.zh: 走之前检查一下窗户和门是否关严了。

examples.en: The plastic cover was stretched tight (= stretched as much as it could be) across the tank.
examples.zh: 水箱上的塑料罩子绷得很紧。

examples.en: It is a tight squeeze on some trains, the General Manager acknowledged (= passengers are packed closely together).
Those trousers look a bit tight around your middle.
Hold on tight and don't let go!
He screwed his eyes tight shut against the bright light.
The cyclists were all dressed in tight lycra shorts and the official team vest.
I can't unscrew the top of this jar - it's really tight.

definitions.en: Clothes or shoes that are tight fit the body too closely and are uncomfortable.

examples.en: That jacket's too tight - you need a bigger size.
examples.zh: 那件夹克太紧身了——你需要号大一点的。

examples.en: Some people experienced tight chests after exercising in areas of high pollution.
If you have any symptoms such as itchy mouth or tight throat after eating nuts, stop.

definitions.en: Tight controls or rules severely limit what can happen.

examples.en: Rules on what can be taken on board an aircraft have become tighter.
tight security
examples.zh: 严密的安全措施

definitions.en: If time or money is tight, there is only just enough of it.

examples.en: I'm sorry I can't stay for a chat, time's really tight.
examples.zh: 对不起我不能停，时间真的很紧。

examples.en: They're raising three kids on one small salary so money is very tight.
examples.zh: 他们靠一份微薄的薪水抚养3个孩子，所以手头很紧。

definitions.en: unwilling to spend money
definitions.zh: 用钱不大方的，吝啬的（同 tight-fisted）

examples.en: He was too tight to pay for a taxi so we had to walk.

definitions.en: having drunk too much alcohol

examples.en: Jim, you're tight!
examples.zh: 吉姆，你醉了！

definitions.en: If two people are tight, they know each other very well and like each other a lot.
definitions.zh: 亲密的;个人关系密切的

examples.en: The two of them were pretty tight at school.
examples.zh: 他们俩在学生时代关系非常亲密。

definitions.en: (held or kept together) firmly or closely
definitions.zh: 紧的；密封的（地）；牢固的（地）

examples.en: I can't untie the knot - it's too tight.
examples.zh: 我解不开这个结——太紧了。

examples.en: This lid is on very tight.
examples.zh: 这盖子盖得很紧。

examples.en: The people stood talking in tight groups.
examples.zh: 大家一群群地紧挨着站在那里说话。

examples.en: Hold on tight when we go around the corner.
examples.zh: 我们转弯时大家都要抓牢扶好。

examples.en: Check that windows and doors are shut tight (= completely closed) before you leave.
examples.zh: 走之前检查一下窗户和门是否关严了。

examples.en: The plastic cover was stretched tight (= stretched as much as it could be) across the tank.
examples.zh: 水箱上的塑料罩子绷得很紧。

examples.en: It is a tight squeeze on some trains, the General Manager acknowledged (= passengers are packed closely together).
Those trousers look a bit tight around your middle.
Hold on tight and don't let go!
He screwed his eyes tight shut against the bright light.
The cyclists were all dressed in tight lycra shorts and the official team vest.
I can't unscrew the top of this jar - it's really tight.

definitions.en: Clothes or shoes that are tight fit the body too closely and are uncomfortable.

examples.en: That jacket's too tight - you need a bigger size.
examples.zh: 那件夹克太紧身了——你需要号大一点的。

examples.en: Some people experienced tight chests after exercising in areas of high pollution.
If you have any symptoms such as itchy mouth or tight throat after eating nuts, stop.

definitions.en: Tight controls or rules severely limit what can happen.

examples.en: Rules on what can be taken on board an aircraft have become tighter.
tight security
examples.zh: 严密的安全措施

definitions.en: If time or money is tight, there is only just enough of it.

examples.en: I'm sorry I can't stay for a chat, time's really tight.
examples.zh: 对不起我不能停，时间真的很紧。

examples.en: They're raising three kids on one small salary so money is very tight.
examples.zh: 他们靠一份微薄的薪水抚养3个孩子，所以手头很紧。

definitions.en: unwilling to spend money
definitions.zh: 用钱不大方的，吝啬的（同 tight-fisted）

examples.en: He was too tight to pay for a taxi so we had to walk.

definitions.en: having drunk too much alcohol

examples.en: Jim, you're tight!
examples.zh: 吉姆，你醉了！

definitions.en: If two people are tight, they know each other very well and like each other a lot.
definitions.zh: 亲密的;个人关系密切的

examples.en: The two of them were pretty tight at school.
examples.zh: 他们俩在学生时代关系非常亲密。

definitions.en: a group of connected cells in an animal or plant that are similar to each other, have the same purpose, and form the stated part of the animal or plant

examples.en: human tissue
examples.zh: 人体组织

examples.en: plant tissue
examples.zh: 植物组织

examples.en: brain/lung/muscle/fat tissue
examples.zh: 脑／肺／肌肉／脂肪组织

examples.en: His face is covered with scar tissue where he was badly burned.
examples.zh: 他脸上严重烧伤的地方满是疤痕。

examples.en: Nitric acid will dissolve most animal tissue.
Virus particles were eventually isolated from the tissue.
The nerves are protected by thin sheaths of fatty tissue.
Soft tissue, such as flesh, allows X-rays through.
The drugs stimulate the damaged tissue into repairing itself.

definitions.en: soft paper that is used for cleaning, especially your nose, and is thrown away after use, or a small rectangular piece of this
definitions.zh: （尤指擦鼻子用的）纸巾，手巾纸

examples.en: She handed me a tissue just before I sneezed.
examples.zh: 我快打喷嚏时她递给我一张手巾纸。

examples.en: I always keep a box of tissues in the car.
examples.zh: 我总是在车里放一盒纸巾。

examples.en: He used a piece of tissue to clean his sunglasses.
examples.zh: 他用一张纸巾擦太阳镜。

examples.en: The shop assistant wrapped the vase carefully in tissue paper.
examples.zh: 店员用薄纸将花瓶仔细地包好。

definitions.en: willingness to accept behaviour and beliefs that are different from your own, although you might not agree with or approve of them

examples.en: This period in history is not noted for its religious tolerance.
examples.zh: 历史上的这个时期在宗教上并不宽容。

examples.en: Some members of the party would like to see it develop a greater tolerance of/towards contrary points of view.
examples.zh: 党内的一些成员希望看到党对反面意见能更加宽容。

examples.en: The book is essentially an exhortation to religious tolerance.
My father never showed much tolerance towards us children.
He asked for tolerance of his habits.
The two peoples lived together in mutual tolerance.

definitions.en: the ability to deal with something unpleasant or annoying, or to continue existing despite bad or difficult conditions

examples.en: My tolerance of/for heat is considerably greater after having lived in the Far East for a couple of years.
examples.zh: 在远东生活了几年后，我的耐热能力大为增强。

examples.en: a greater tolerance of/to the drug
examples.zh: 对药物更强的耐受性

definitions.en: the amount by which a measurement or calculation might change and still be acceptable

examples.en: parts that are made to tolerances of a thousandth of an inch
examples.zh: 按千分之一英寸的公差加工的零件

definitions.en: a gravestone
the way the parts of something are organized or connected

examples.en: Globalization and the rise of information technology have changed the topology of the corporation.
examples.zh: 全球化和信息技术的崛起改变了公司的构造。

definitions.en: strong; not easily broken or made weaker

examples.en: These toys are made from tough plastic.
examples.zh: 这些玩具是由结实的塑料制成的。

examples.en: Children's shoes need to be tough.
examples.zh: 童鞋必须结实。

examples.en: Tough new safety standards have been introduced for cars.
examples.zh: 汽车生产采用了更加严格的新安全标准。

examples.en: There have been calls for tougher controls/restrictions on what online retailers are allowed to sell.
examples.zh: 一直有人呼吁要对网上零售商出售的商品种类严加控制／限制。

examples.en: I think it's time the police got tougher on/with (= treated more severely) people who drink and drive.
examples.zh: 我觉得警方该对酒后驾车者采取更加严厉的措施。

examples.en: The government is continuing to take a tough line on terrorism.
examples.zh: 政府继续对恐怖主义采取强硬的态度。

examples.en: I can't eat this meat - it's too tough.
Polyurethane varnish provides a tough, scratch-resistant finish.
We need a mayor who is tough enough to clean up this town.
You'll need some fairly tough footwear to go walking up mountains.
Several governments have adopted tough new anti-terrorist legislation in the wake of the attacks.

definitions.en: difficult to do or to deal with

examples.en: They've had an exceptionally tough life.
examples.zh: 他们的一生异常艰苦。

examples.en: They will be a tough team to beat.
examples.zh: 他们会是一支很难战胜的队伍。

examples.en: The company is going through a tough time at the moment.
examples.zh: 公司现在正值困难时期。

examples.en: We've had to make some very tough decisions.
examples.zh: 我们不得不作出了一些很艰难的决定。

examples.en: My boss has given me a tough job/assignment.
examples.zh: 我的老板给了我一个棘手的活儿／任务。

examples.en: Many homeless people are facing a tough winter.
examples.zh: 许多无家可归者正面临严冬的考验。

examples.en: After some tough bargaining, we finally agreed on a deal.
examples.zh: 经过艰苦的讨价还价，我们最终达成了一笔交易。

examples.en: It's been a tough six months and I feel I've earned a few weeks off.
We'll have to make some tough decisions - there are no easy options.
It's going to be a tough competition but I'm mentally prepared for it.
This course is really tough, - sometimes I feeling like packing it all in.
Now I'm going to ask you a really tough question - are you ready?

definitions.en: Tough food is difficult to cut or eat.
definitions.zh: （食物）老的，不易切开的，嚼不动的

examples.en: This steak is very tough.
examples.zh: 这块牛排很不好嚼。

examples.en: These apples have tough skins.
examples.zh: 这些苹果的皮很老。

definitions.en: likely to be violent or to contain violence; not kind or pleasant
definitions.zh: 粗鲁的;暴力的;不友善的

examples.en: a tough neighbourhood
examples.zh: 治安很差的社区

examples.en: Many of the country's toughest criminals are held in this prison.
examples.zh: 这个国家最凶残的罪犯有许多都关在这座监狱里。

definitions.en: unlucky

examples.en: "I have to work late today because I'm very behind on my work." "Oh, tough luck!"
examples.zh: “我今天必须加班，因为落下了许多活儿。”“哦，真倒霉！”

examples.en: It's tough on Geoff that he's going to miss the party.
examples.zh: 杰弗真倒霉，他要错过这次聚会了。

examples.en: "I don't have any money left." "Well, (that's just) tough - you shouldn't have spent it all on cigarettes."
examples.zh: “我的钱都花光了。”“哼，活该——你不该把钱都拿来买烟。”

definitions.en: a violent person

examples.en: Bands of armed toughs roamed the city.
examples.zh: 几群持有武器的暴徒在这座城市里游荡。

examples.en: There are some pretty tough children in his class.
He worked for a group of tough masters.
His bosses were really tough.

definitions.en: a box on wheels that is pulled by a car and is used for taking things from one place to another

examples.en: The car was pulling a trailer with a motorcycle on it.
examples.zh: 这辆汽车拖着一辆挂车，挂车上面载着一辆摩托车。

definitions.en: an advertisement for a film or a television or radio programme, consisting of short parts taken from it

examples.en: I saw a trailer for the new series of "Game of Thrones" last night.
examples.zh: 我昨晚看了新一季《权力的游戏》的预告片。

definitions.en: lasting for only a short time; temporary
definitions.zh: 短暂的，转瞬即逝的;暂时的

examples.en: A glass of whisky has only a transient warming effect.
examples.zh: 一杯威士忌只能使人暖和一会儿。

examples.en: The city has a large transient population (= many people who are living in it only temporarily).
examples.zh: 这个城市有大量的暂住人口。

definitions.en: someone who lives only temporarily in a place

examples.en: It's an organization set up to provide money and help for transients.
examples.zh: 这个组织建立的目的就是给暂住者提供钱款和帮助。

definitions.en: the movement of goods or people from one place to another

examples.en: It is possible to make an insurance claim for any goods lost or damaged in transit.
examples.zh: 为在运输中丢失或受损的货物索要保险赔偿是有可能的。

examples.en: The question is whether road transit is cheaper than rail.
examples.zh: 问题在于公路运输是否比铁路运输便宜。

examples.en: a rapid-transit train
examples.zh: 快运列车

definitions.en: a brand name for a type of road vehicle used especially for carrying goods
definitions.zh: （尤指用来运货的）一种小型车辆，“全顺”货车

examples.en: He was seen driving a white Transit van near Leeds.
examples.zh: 有人看到他在利兹附近开着一辆白色的“全顺”货车。

definitions.en: a change from one form or type to another, or the process by which this happens

examples.en: The health-care system is in transition at the moment.
examples.zh: 医疗保健制度目前正在变革中。

examples.en: There will be an interim government to oversee the transition to democracy.
examples.zh: 将会有一个临时政府来监督向民主政治的过渡。

examples.en: She began her transition from male to female almost seven years ago.
examples.zh: 大约七年前，她开始了从男性到女性的转变。

examples.en: The transition from a totalitarian state to a free country will be long and slow.
The transition from boyhood to manhood can be a confusing period.
There will be a transition period before Jenny takes over as boss.

definitions.en: If someone transitions, they make changes, which may be social, legal, or medical, that lead to them living as a person of a different gender to the one they were said to have at birth.

examples.en: Louise transitioned from male to female almost 20 years ago.
It wasn't easy transitioning gender in the military.
examples.zh: 在军队中转换性别并不容易。

examples.en: He said that they had transitioned many people off welfare very successfully.
The state this year transitioned to a different type of standardized test of educational achievement.
He recalled the turbulent years when the civil rights movement transitioned into demands for Black Power.

definitions.en: belonging or relating to a change, or the process of change, from one form or type to another

examples.en: a transitional government
examples.zh: 过渡政府

definitions.en: to move something, or to be moved, from one place or person to another

examples.en: The plants should be grown indoors until spring, when they can be transplanted outside.
examples.zh: 这些植物应该先种在室内，到了春天就可以移栽到外面。

definitions.en: a medical operation in which a new organ is put into someone's body

examples.en: a liver/kidney transplant
examples.zh: 肝脏／肾脏移植

examples.en: transplant surgery
examples.zh: 移植手术

examples.en: He had a heart transplant (= doctors gave him a different, healthier heart instead of his old one).
examples.zh: 他接受过心脏移植。

examples.en: His body accepted/rejected the transplant.
examples.zh: 他的身体对移植器官没有出现／出现了排斥反应。

examples.en: He was given a new liver in a transplant operation.
She received her transplant a month ago.

definitions.en: to change something from one position to another, or to exchange the positions of two things

examples.en: In their latest production they have reworked "King Lear", transposing it to pre-colonial Africa.
examples.zh: 在最新的制作中，他们改编了《李尔王》，把故事发生的地点改在了沦为殖民地之前的非洲。

examples.en: The confusion was caused when two numbers were accidentally transposed by someone in the Social Security office.
examples.zh: 这场混乱的起因是一个社会保障部门的职员无意中把两个数字调换了位置。

examples.en: The pianist transposed the song into C.
examples.zh: 钢琴家把这首歌改成了C调。

definitions.en: to move or travel through an area

examples.en: Stanley traversed the continent from west to east.
examples.zh: 斯坦利自西向东穿越了整个大陆。

examples.en: Bounded on the east by Lake Winnebago, the county is traversed by the Wolf and Fox rivers.
examples.zh: 该县东临温纳贝戈湖，沃尔夫河和福克斯河穿县而过。

definitions.en: very great in amount or level, or extremely good

examples.en: They were making a tremendous amount of noise last night.
examples.zh: 昨晚他们弄出的噪声震天响。

examples.en: She's been a tremendous (= very great) help to me over the last few months.
examples.zh: 过去几个月里她给我的帮助极大。

examples.en: You won? That's tremendous!
examples.zh: 你赢了？太棒了！

examples.en: It was a tremendous coup for the local paper to get an exclusive interview with Prince Charles.
She has every right to be proud of her tremendous achievements.
Heart surgery exacts tremendous skill and concentration.
Both countries took a tremendous hammering in the war.
He kicked up a tremendous fuss about having to wait.

definitions.en: the process of quickly examining patients who are taken to a hospital in order to decide which ones are the most seriously ill and must be treated first
definitions.zh: （医院）病人鉴别分类（以确定哪些病人情况最为严重须优先处理）

examples.en: The team's main job is to treat trauma victims at the scene of a disaster and perform triage.
The triage nurse told him he would have to wait.
examples.zh: 负责病人鉴别分类的护士让他等着。

examples.en: We are performing triage on funding requests.
A healthy project is in a constant state of triage between bugs and feature development.

definitions.en: a flat shape with three straight sides

examples.en: an equilateral/isosceles triangle
examples.zh: 等边／等腰三角形

examples.en: Which earrings did you buy in the end - the triangles or the circles?
examples.zh: 最后你买了什么样的耳环——三角形的还是圆的？

examples.en: Draw a perpendicular from the vertex of the triangle to its base.
A triangle is a shape with three sides.
The cloth was decorated with triangles of different colours.
We cut some triangles of cheese.
To make a star, put two triangles together.

definitions.en: If a piece of work or problem is tricky, it is difficult to deal with and needs careful attention or skill.

examples.en: It's tricky to learn to ride a skateboard, but you never forget how.
examples.zh: 学习滑板挺难的，但是一旦学会就不会忘记。

examples.en: I'm in a tricky situation - whatever I do I'll offend someone.
examples.zh: 我的处境真有点儿难办——我无论怎么做都会得罪人。

examples.en: The company's had some tricky problems to negotiate in its first year in business.
He asked a number of tricky questions that only a doctor could answer.
He has a deft touch with tricky painting jobs.
She's very easily offended, so working with her can be a bit tricky.
The switch is a bit tricky to reach.

definitions.en: likely to deceive people
definitions.zh: 欺骗性的;狡猾的;诡计多端的

examples.en: He's a tricky one.
examples.zh: 他是一个诡计多端的人。

examples.en: a/one trillion
examples.zh: 一万亿

examples.en: Four light years is 25 trillion miles.
examples.zh: 四光年相当于25万亿英里。

examples.en: trillions of dollars
examples.zh: 万亿美元

definitions.en: details or information that are not important

examples.en: She has an encyclopedic knowledge of sports trivia.
examples.zh: 她对体育运动的细枝末节无所不知。

examples.en: The museum celebrates the trivia of everyday life.
examples.zh: 这家博物馆收藏的是日常生活所用的各种小东西。

definitions.en: having little value or importance
definitions.zh: 微不足道的，琐碎的；没有价值的

examples.en: I don't know why he gets so upset about something so trivial.
examples.zh: 我不明白他因何为芝麻大的事儿也如此烦恼。

examples.en: Sexual harassment in the workplace is not a trivial matter.
examples.zh: 工作场所的性骚扰可不是件小事。

examples.en: Getting computers to understand human language is not a trivial problem.
examples.zh: 让计算机懂得人类语言可不是一个容易解决的问题。

examples.en: It was a badly researched documentary which glossed over important questions while pointing up trivial ones.
She gets completely stressed about trivial things.
The problems she faces are not trivial.
She has been suffering from a series of trivial illnesses.
The cost of the postage is trivial.

definitions.en: to make something shorter or quicker, especially by removing the end of it
definitions.zh: 截短，删节，缩短（尤指去尾）

examples.en: Television coverage of the event was truncated by a technical fault.
examples.zh: 对比赛的电视直播因技术故障而被缩短。

definitions.en: a series of musical notes, especially one that is pleasant and easy to remember

examples.en: He was humming a tune as he dried the dishes.
examples.zh: 他边哼着调子边擦干碗碟。

examples.en: a theme tune
examples.zh: 主题曲

examples.en: That's a very catchy tune (= easy to remember and pleasant).
examples.zh: 那是一首很容易上口的曲子。

examples.en: She can't even sing in tune.
I don't have a great voice but I can sing in tune.
At school, anyone who could sing in tune had to join the choir.
I find it almost impossible to play drums and sing in tune at the same time.
I tried so hard to play in tune.
Pipes can be beautiful when they're played in tune.
The piano is out of tune.
examples.zh: 恐怕这架钢琴走调了。

examples.en: Was it me or were they out of tune towards the end of that song?
Someone sitting behind me was singing out of tune.
I didn't want to be the person in church who sings out of tune.
They were both hopelessly out of tune.
I thought the violins were slightly out of tune.
The band played a familiar tune that had everyone clapping along.
As a songwriter, he combined great linguistic felicity with an ear for a tune.
I've forgotten how that tune goes - could you hum it for me?
That tune really brings back memories .
Hearing that tune again filled him with nostalgia.

definitions.en: If you are in tune with people or ideas, you understand or agree with them, and if you are out of tune with them, you do not.
definitions.zh: 与…协调／不协调，与…相／不相契合

examples.en: Much of his success comes from being in tune with what his customers want.
examples.zh: 他的成功很大程度上归功于他契合了顾客的需要。

examples.en: Her theories were out of tune with the scientific thinking of the time.
examples.zh: 她的理论与当时的科学思想不符。

examples.en: These are the sort of people who are in tune with the need for conservation.
Being a younger teacher, he's more in tune with the students.
These people are completely out of tune with public opinion.
Certainly with regard to these views, he is hopelessly out of tune with his party and his people.

definitions.en: to the stated amount

examples.en: The City Council had financed the new building to the tune of over four million pounds.
examples.zh: 市政会为建这座新大厦已经出资了400多万英镑。

examples.en: She is suing the company for breach of contract to the tune of $10 million.
It's reckoned they'll contribute to the local economy to the tune of £25 million.
There's been an annual decline in the population of this species to the tune of 3.8 percent between 2010 and 2020.
It's subsidized to the tune of approximately $30 million a year.

definitions.en: a tune-up
to change a part of a musical instrument so that the instrument produces the correct sounds when played

examples.en: Get into the habit of tuning your guitar every day before you practise.
examples.zh: 要养成习惯，每天练习前都给吉他调一下音。

examples.en: She tuned (up) her violin before the concert.
examples.zh: 音乐会前她给她的小提琴调了弦。

definitions.en: to move the controls on a radio, television, etc. so that it receives programmes broadcast from a particular station
definitions.zh: 给（收音机、电视等）调谐，为…调频率，调频道

examples.en: Press this button and the video will automatically tune itself to the next channel.
examples.zh: 单击这个按钮，录像机会自动调到下一个频道。

examples.en: His radio is constantly tuned to KROQ-FM, the local rock station.
examples.zh: 他的收音机总是锁定在当地摇滚乐台 KROQ-FM上。

definitions.en: to make slight changes to an engine so that it works as well as possible
definitions.zh: 把（发动机）调试到最佳运转状态

examples.en: The engine needs tuning, but there's nothing wrong with the car.
examples.zh: 发动机确实需要调试了，但汽车没毛病。

examples.en: Could you tune (up) the engine for me, please?
examples.zh: 请帮我调试一下发动机好吗？

definitions.en: to change something slightly, especially in order to make it more correct, effective, or suitable

examples.en: The software is pretty much there - it just needs a little tweaking.
examples.zh: 软件差不多好了——只需要一点点改进。

examples.en: You just need to tweak the last paragraph and then it's done.
examples.zh: 你只需要把最后一段稍作修改就可以了。

definitions.en: to pull and twist something with a small sudden movement

examples.en: Standing in front of the mirror she tweaked a strand of hair into place.
examples.zh: 她站在镜子前，把一缕头发捋好。

definitions.en: to move something repeatedly between your fingers, especially without any purpose
definitions.zh: （尤指无聊地反复用手指）摆弄，捻弄，旋弄

examples.en: She was twiddling (with) a pencil/her hair.
examples.zh: 她在摆弄铅笔／头发玩。

examples.en: Twiddle a dial/knob on a radio in the city and you may hear voices speaking Spanish, Chinese, Portuguese, or Russian.
examples.zh: 在这座城市里，如果旋弄收音机的旋钮，你可能会收听到西班牙语、汉语、葡萄牙语或俄语。

definitions.en: an act of turning something with the fingers

examples.en: At the twiddle of a knob the operators can focus on a tiny amount of airspace or scan the whole area.
examples.zh: 只要旋动旋钮，操作者就可局部观察一小块领空，或者扫描整个区域。

definitions.en: to turn something, especially repeatedly, or to turn or wrap one thing around another

examples.en: The path twists and turns for over a mile.
examples.zh: 这条小路弯弯曲曲，长达1英里多。

examples.en: She sat there nervously twisting the ring around on her finger.
examples.zh: 她紧张地坐在那儿，不停地转着手指上的戒指。

examples.en: She twisted her head (round) so she could see what was happening.
examples.zh: 她扭过头来，想看看发生了什么事。

examples.en: Twist the rope tightly round that post over there.
examples.zh: 把绳子紧紧缠在那根柱子上。

examples.en: She slipped on the ice and twisted her knee.
examples.zh: 她在冰上滑了一跤，结果扭伤了膝盖。

examples.en: "How do I get the top off this bottle?" "Push it down and twist it anti-clockwise."
She fell awkwardly when she was skiing and twisted her ankle.
After twisting and turning for a while, he managed to wriggle free.
We twisted the pieces of wire together.
He used supernatural powers to twist metal.

definitions.en: to change information so that it gives the message you want it to give, especially in a way that is dishonest

examples.en: This report shows how she twisted the truth to claim successes where none, in fact, existed.
examples.zh: 这份报告表明她歪曲事实，她所宣称的那些成就其实根本不存在。

examples.en: You're twisting my words - that's not what I said at all.
examples.zh: 你在歪曲我的话——我根本不是那样说的。

definitions.en: an act of twisting something

examples.en: She gave the cap another twist to make sure it was tight.
examples.zh: 她又拧了一下盖子，以确保盖紧了。

examples.en: an Elvis-style twist of the hips
examples.zh: 猫王式的臀部扭动

examples.en: a twist of hair
examples.zh: 麻花辫

examples.en: a twist of lemon
examples.zh: 柠檬片卷

examples.en: a path with many twists and turns
examples.zh: 蜿蜒曲折的小路

examples.en: Have you heard the latest twist in the saga of Gary's divorce?
All his stories have an unexpected twist at the end.
By a strange twist of fate, were both in New York on that day.

definitions.en: a change in the way in which something happens

examples.en: The story took a surprise twist today with media reports that the doctor had resigned.
examples.zh: 媒体报道该医生已经辞职，从而使事情在今天有了惊人的转折。

examples.en: The incident was the latest twist in the continuing saga of fraud and high scandal in banks and stock brokerages.
examples.zh: 此事件是银行和股票经纪公司持续不断的诈骗和高层丑闻的最新发展。

examples.en: But for a cruel twist of fate/fortune, he could now be running his own business.
examples.zh: 要不是时运不济，他现在可能正在经营自己的公司了。

examples.en: There's an unexpected twist in/to the plot towards the end of the film.
examples.zh: 电影结尾部分的情节出人意料。

examples.en: the twists and turns of fate
examples.zh: 命运的曲折变化

examples.en: It has proved very difficult to unravel the twists and turns and contradictions of the evidence.
examples.zh: 事实已经证明，要理清复杂且矛盾丛生的证据的确不容易。

definitions.en: a dance in which people stay in one place and twist their bodies from side to side to music
seeming to be everywhere
definitions.zh: 普遍存在的，似乎无处不在的

examples.en: Leather is very much in fashion this season, as is the ubiquitous denim.
examples.zh: 皮装在这个季节非常流行，当然牛仔服也似乎无处不在。

examples.en: The Swedes are not alone in finding their language under pressure from the ubiquitous spread of English.
examples.zh: 不仅仅是瑞典人感觉到了自己的语言正承受着英语无处不在所带来的压力。

examples.en: The mobile phone, that most ubiquitous of consumer-electronic appliances, is about to enter a new age.
examples.zh: 作为使用最普遍的消费电器，手机将进入一个新时代。

definitions.en: the fact that something or someone seems to be everywhere

examples.en: the ubiquity of fast-food outlets
examples.zh: 快餐店的无处不在

definitions.en: expressed in a way that makes it completely clear what is meant

examples.en: The minister promised a clear and unambiguous statement on the future of the coal industry.
examples.zh: 部长称她将尽快就煤炭工业的前途问题作一个清楚明确的表示。

definitions.en: in a way that makes completely clear what is meant

examples.en: Her lawyers stated unambiguously that she's not aware of any unethical conduct.
examples.zh: 她的律师团明确表示，她不知道有任何不道德的行为。

examples.en: The rules need to be unambiguously clear.
examples.zh: 规则需要清晰无歧义。

examples.en: She unambiguously stated that she wanted to do the laundry herself.
I oppose this bill because it is clearly and unambiguously unconstitutional.
His study shows unambiguously the impact of the media.

definitions.en: with different parts failing to work or move well together

examples.en: The marketing campaign was an uncoordinated effort by several different departments.
examples.zh: 促销活动是由几个部门一起搞的，彼此之间没有协调好。

examples.en: She was clumsy and uncoordinated as a girl.
examples.zh: 她这个女孩笨手笨脚的，协调能力差。

definitions.en: to make something that has been deleted (= removed from a computer) able to be seen again
definitions.zh: （在计算机中）恢复已被删除的文件

definitions.en: to fail to guess or understand the real cost, size, difficulty, etc. of something

examples.en: Originally the contractor gave me a price of €2,000, but now they say they underestimated and it's going to be at least €3,000.
examples.zh: 起初建筑商向我要价2000欧元，但现在他们说当初估价过低，至少要3000欧元。

examples.en: Don't underestimate the difficulties of getting both parties to the conference table.
examples.zh: 要让双方坐下来谈判，难度不可小觑。

examples.en: He miscalculated badly when he underestimated the response of the international community to the invasion.
They've seriously underestimated the cost of the building project.
The influence of the media should never be underestimated.
She had a tendency to underestimate her son's potential.
It seems that we've seriously underestimated just how long this process will take.

definitions.en: to fail to understand how strong, skilful, intelligent, or determined someone, especially a competitor, is
definitions.zh: 低估，轻视（尤指竞争对手）

examples.en: Never underestimate your opponent!
examples.zh: 永远不要低估你的对手！

definitions.en: to experience something that is unpleasant or something that involves a change
definitions.zh: 经历，经受（令人不快的事或变化）

examples.en: She underwent an operation on a tumour in her left lung last year.
examples.zh: 她在去年做了一次左肺肿瘤切除手术。

examples.en: Playing board games is undergoing a revival in popularity.
examples.zh: 棋盘游戏正经历着一场复兴。

examples.en: He seems to have undergone a change in attitude recently, and has become much more co-operative.
After the accident, he underwent reconstructive surgery to rebuild his face.
I had to undergo a medical examination when I started my pension scheme.
Athletes must undergo a mandatory drugs test before competing in the championship.
The company is undergoing a radical reorganization.

definitions.en: to give support, strength, or a basic structure to something
definitions.zh: 支撑;加强;巩固;构成（基础）

examples.en: He presented data to underpin his argument.
examples.zh: 他提供了一些数据来支持他的论点。

examples.en: Gradually the laws that underpinned (= formed part of the basic structure of) apartheid were abolished.
examples.zh: 以种族隔离制度为基础的法律被逐步废除了。

examples.en: When restoring the building, the first priority was to underpin the exterior walls by adding wooden supports along the foundations.
examples.zh: 修复这座建筑物时，首先要做的是沿着地基给外墙增加木支撑。

definitions.en: not level, equal, flat, or continuous
definitions.zh: 不平整的；参差不齐的；不平等的；不平坦的；不连续的

examples.en: Be careful on that path - the paving stones are uneven.
examples.zh: 走那条路时要小心——铺路石很不平。

examples.en: There is an uneven distribution of wealth across the country from the north to the south.
examples.zh: 全国的财富分配南北很不均衡。

examples.en: The contest was very uneven - the other team was much stronger than us.
examples.zh: 比赛很不平等——对方参赛队的实力比我们强很多。

examples.en: The patient's breathing was quick and uneven.
The uneven floorboards creaked when you walked across them.
His teeth were yellow and uneven.
an uneven distribution of financial resources
Their rates of development are uneven.

definitions.en: different in quality; often used to avoid saying bad

examples.en: Your work has been a little uneven this term, Matthew.
examples.zh: 马修，你的功课这学期很不稳定。

definitions.en: not known to you

examples.en: I noticed several unfamiliar faces in the meeting room.
examples.zh: 我注意到会议室里有好几张新面孔。

examples.en: His name was unfamiliar to me.
examples.zh: 他的名字我很陌生。

examples.en: Many older people are unfamiliar with computers.
examples.zh: 许多老年人不懂电脑。

examples.en: Many of the technical terms were unfamiliar to me.
She felt increasingly nervous as she walked across a room full of unfamiliar faces.
I'm afraid you'll have to explain things to me, I'm unfamiliar with this system.
He sounded very much like someone I knew, but his face was unfamiliar.
People can often be hostile to anything foreign or unfamiliar.

definitions.en: a particular set of clothes that has to be worn by the members of the same organization or group of people

examples.en: military/school uniform
examples.zh: 军装／校服

examples.en: a nurse's uniform
examples.zh: 护士服

examples.en: I love a man in uniform!
examples.zh: 我喜欢穿制服的男人！

examples.en: Photographs show him wearing the T-shirt and ripped jeans that were the student's uniform of the time.
examples.zh: 照片上的他穿着T恤衫和破洞牛仔裤，一幅当时学生的典型打扮。

examples.en: He was pictured as a soldier in full uniform.
She recalled the humiliation of exchanging her clothes for the prison uniform.
I never wear grey because it reminds me of my school uniform.
He thinks that wearing school uniform depersonalizes children.
I feel so drab in this grey uniform.

definitions.en: the same; not changing or different in any way
definitions.zh: 相同的；一律的；整齐划一的

examples.en: The office walls and furniture are a uniform grey.
examples.zh: 办公室的墙壁和家具一律都是灰色的。

examples.en: Small businesses are demanding that they receive uniform treatment from the banks.
examples.zh: 小企业要求银行给予它们一视同仁的待遇。

definitions.en: in a way that is the same everywhere or for everyone
definitions.zh: 相同地;一律地;整齐划一地

examples.en: Critics were uniformly enthusiastic about the production.
examples.zh: 评论家们全都对这部作品非常感兴趣。

examples.en: The impact of interest rates is not felt uniformly across the country.
examples.zh: 利率对全国各地的影响不尽相同。

examples.en: Growth prospects around the world are uniformly grim.
Economic news across the world was almost uniformly bad.
The streets were uniformly grey under the white winter sky.

definitions.en: to bring together; combine

examples.en: If the new leader does manage to unify his warring party it will be quite an achievement.
examples.zh: 如果新领导人能将党内互相争斗的各派力量整合起来，那将是个了不起的成就。

definitions.en: in a way that involves doing or deciding something without first asking or agreeing with another person, group, or country

examples.en: The law is part of a disturbing trend to act unilaterally without regard to the legitimate interests of others.
examples.zh: 这条法律展现了一种令人不安的趋势，即不顾他人的合法利益，单方面采取行动。

examples.en: Employers cannot unilaterally change your contract terms.
examples.zh: 雇主不能单方面改变你的合同条款。

examples.en: He unilaterally cancelled the project without any previous discussion with the board.
The region is seeking to unilaterally declare independence.
The rebels continue to attack from their side of the unilaterally determined border.

definitions.en: in a way that exists everywhere, or involves everyone

examples.en: It is universally acknowledged as one of the best new cars to be produced in recent years.
examples.zh: 它被普遍认为是近年来生产的最好的新车之一。

examples.en: He is universally recognised as the world's greatest living guitarist.
examples.zh: 他被普遍认为是世界上在世的吉他手中最伟大的。

examples.en: His theory was quite controversial and not universally accepted.
Sports TV ratings have been falling almost universally.
He was almost universally acclaimed for his decency and his modesty.

definitions.en: wrong and/or not deserved
definitions.zh: 错误的；不值得的；不必要的

examples.en: The defendant had supreme and, as it turned out, unjustified confidence in his own judgment.
examples.zh: 被告绝对相信自己的判断，尽管后来证明是错误的。

definitions.en: to suddenly release a violent force that cannot be controlled

examples.en: At worst, nuclear war could be unleashed.
examples.zh: 最坏的情况是，可能会爆发核战争。

examples.en: Rachel's arrival on the scene had unleashed passions in him that he could scarcely control.
examples.zh: 瑞秋的到来激起了他难以抑制的热情。

definitions.en: If a piece of knitted or woven cloth, a knot, or a mass of thread unravels, it separates into a single thread, and if you unravel it, you separate it into a single thread.

examples.en: You'd better mend that hole before the whole sweater starts to unravel.
examples.zh: 你最好把那个洞补上，免得整件毛衣脱线。

examples.en: I had to unravel one of the sleeves because I realized I'd knitted it too small.
examples.zh: 我不得不拆掉一只袖子，因为我发现它织得太小了。

definitions.en: If you unravel a mysterious, unknown, or complicated subject, you make it known or understood, and if it unravels, it becomes known or understood.
definitions.zh: 揭开，弄清，阐明（谜团或复杂问题）;被澄清;被解决

examples.en: We have a long way to go before we unravel the secrets of genetics.
examples.zh: 在揭开遗传学之谜前，我们还有很长的路要走。

definitions.en: If a process or achievement that was slow and complicated unravels or is unravelled, it is destroyed.

examples.en: As talks between the leaders broke down, several months of careful diplomacy were unravelled.
examples.zh: 由于领导人之间的会谈破裂，几个月精心策划的外交努力付诸东流。

definitions.en: not changed or spoiled in any way
definitions.zh: 未改变的;原封未动的;未受破坏的

examples.en: Most of the east coast remains untouched by tourism.
examples.zh: 东海岸的大部分地区还没受到旅游业的侵扰。

examples.en: She took a few spoonfuls of soup but left her main course untouched.
examples.zh: 她喝了几勺汤，但主菜却没有动过。

definitions.en: to remove a covering like a curtain from a new structure at a formal ceremony in order to show the opening or finishing of a new building or work of art

examples.en: The memorial to those who had died in the war was unveiled by the Queen.
examples.zh: 缅怀战争死难者的纪念碑由女王揭幕。

examples.en: The company recently unveiled a test version of its new search engine.
examples.zh: 这家公司最近公布了新搜索引擎的测试版。

definitions.en: speaking or behaving in a way that makes intentions and beliefs clear

examples.en: She's very upfront about why she wants the job - she'd earn a lot more money.
examples.zh: 她毫不掩饰自己想要获得这份工作的原因——她想挣更多的钱。

definitions.en: a great change, especially causing or involving much difficulty, activity, or trouble

examples.en: Yesterday's coup brought further upheaval to a country already struggling with famine.
examples.zh: 昨天的军事政变使这个正在饥荒中挣扎的国家更加动荡不安。

examples.en: I'm not sure it's worth the upheaval of moving to gain just a little more space.
examples.zh: 我怀疑费这么大力气搬家就为了得到多一点空间是不是值得。

definitions.en: Upscale goods and products are of very high quality and intended to be bought by people who are quite rich.

examples.en: an upscale brand name
examples.zh: 高档品牌

examples.en: The old hotel has gone upscale in recent years.
examples.zh: 这座老旅馆近年来变高档了。

definitions.en: completely or extremely

examples.en: What an utterly stupid thing to do!
examples.zh: 多么愚蠢的事情！

examples.en: She was utterly devastated when her husband died.
examples.zh: 当她的丈夫去世时，她完全奔溃了。

examples.en: The two boys walked into the room looking utterly miserable.
These accounts are utterly incomprehensible. Can you explain them to me?
I don't know why he gets so upset about something that is utterly trivial.
The whole situation was utterly ridiculous.

definitions.en: not clearly expressed, known, described, or decided

examples.en: I do have a vague memory of meeting her many years ago.
examples.zh: 我确实依稀记得多年前见过她。

examples.en: The patient had complained of vague pains and backache.
examples.zh: 患者自述说隐约感到疼痛和背酸。

examples.en: Through the mist I could just make out a vague figure.
examples.zh: 透过雾霭，我只能看到一个模糊的身影。

examples.en: My aunt is incredibly vague - she can never remember where she puts things.
examples.zh: 我的姨妈极其迷糊——她老是记不得自己把东西放在什么地方。

examples.en: Their report is studiously/deliberately vague on future economic prospects.
examples.zh: 他们的报告故意对未来经济前景含糊其词。

examples.en: The rules are vague and open to interpretation.
I have only a vague notion of what she does for a living.
I had a vague idea that she lived in Liverpool.
His writings are rather vague on the subject of his marriage.
Our teacher is so vague and disorganized.

definitions.en: without giving many details or facts, especially because you do not want to tell someone everything that you know

examples.en: "We have our sources," Miguel replied vaguely.
examples.zh: “我们有自己的消息来源，”米格尔含糊地回答。

examples.en: I was asked a few cursory questions about my job, which I answered as vaguely as possible.
examples.zh: 有人问了我一些关于我工作的粗略问题，我尽可能含糊地回答了。

examples.en: You are probably vaguely health-conscious.
examples.zh: 你可能大致有些健康意识。

examples.en: I put the ornaments vaguely back into position.
examples.zh: 我大致将那些装饰品放回了原位。

definitions.en: a substance made from the seeds of a tropical plant, used to give flavour to sweet foods
definitions.zh: （用于给食物调味的）香子兰精，香草精

examples.en: vanilla essence/extract
examples.zh: 香子兰精

examples.en: vanilla ice cream/yogurt
examples.zh: 香草冰激凌／酸奶

examples.en: a vanilla milkshake
examples.zh: 香草奶昔

examples.en: Add two teaspoons of vanilla and stir.
examples.zh: 加两茶匙香草精并搅拌一下。

examples.en: a vanilla pod (= seed container)
examples.zh: 香子兰荚

definitions.en: used to describe a product or service that is basic and has no special features

examples.en: I just want a vanilla bank account with low charges.
examples.zh: 我只是需要一个普通的低收费的银行帐号。

definitions.en: to disappear or stop being present or existing, especially in a sudden, surprising way

examples.en: The child vanished while on her way home from school.
examples.zh: 那个小女孩在放学回家的路上不见了。

examples.en: We ran out after the thief, but he had vanished into thin air (= had completely disappeared).
examples.zh: 我们冲出去追小偷，但他已经消失得无影无踪。

examples.en: Cheap rural housing is vanishing in the south of the country.
examples.zh: 廉价的乡间住房在该国南部行将绝迹。

examples.en: We watched the building vanish under a mantle of thick grey smoke as the fire swiftly moved through it.
One moment he was standing behind me, the next he had vanished in a puff of smoke.
He seems to have vanished without a trace .
All the food on the table vanished quickly.
Where are my keys? They can't just have vanished.

definitions.en: the fact that you are too interested in your appearance or achievements

examples.en: He wants the job purely for reasons of vanity and ambition.
examples.zh: 他想得到那份工作纯粹是为了满足虚荣和野心。

definitions.en: a piece of bedroom furniture like a table with a mirror and drawers
used to describe something that is done with the aim of getting praise, fame, or approval rather than for serious or good reasons

examples.en: The new bridge was quickly condemned as a "pointless vanity project", intended to enhance the reputation of the mayor.
examples.zh: 这座新桥很快就被谴责为意在提高市长声誉的“毫无意义的面子工程”。

examples.en: vanity publishing
examples.zh: 为了满足虚荣心而自己出钱出版作品

definitions.en: a change in amount or level

examples.en: Unemployment rates among white-collar workers show much less regional variation than corresponding rates among blue-collar workers.
examples.zh: 白领工作者的失业率与蓝领工人相比，地区性差异要小得多。

examples.en: The medical tests showed some variation in the baby's heart rate.
examples.zh: 医学检验表明婴儿的心率有些不齐。

examples.en: global temperature variations over the last 140 years
examples.zh: 过去140年间全球气温的变化

examples.en: A mother's ears are attuned to even the slightest variation in her baby's breathing.
There have been wild variations in the level of spending.
They are studying the variation in carbon emissions.
There is a lot of variation between regions when it comes to average income.
The instruments measure variations in the earth's temperature.

definitions.en: something that is slightly different from the usual form or arrangement
definitions.zh: 变种，变体；变化了的东西

examples.en: There are wide variations in the way pensioners have benefited from the system.
examples.zh: 养老金领取者从这个体制中受益的情况有很大差别。

examples.en: The films she makes are all variations on the same theme.
examples.zh: 她拍的电影围绕的都是同一主题。

examples.en: symphonic variations
examples.zh: 交响乐变奏曲

definitions.en: a type of arch that supports a roof or ceiling, especially in a church or public building, or a ceiling or roof supported by several of these arches
definitions.zh: （尤指教堂或公共建筑的）拱顶，穹顶

definitions.en: a room, especially in a bank, with thick walls and a strong door, used to store money or valuable things in safe conditions

examples.en: a bank vault
examples.zh: 银行金库

examples.en: She entered the vault with an armed guard.
examples.zh: 她同一名武装保安一起进入了金库。

examples.en: She was buried in the family vault.
examples.zh: 她被葬在家族墓室里。

definitions.en: to jump over something by first putting your hands on it or by using a pole

examples.en: He vaulted over the gate.
examples.zh: 他从那扇大门上翻了过去。

examples.en: She vaulted the wall and kept running.
examples.zh: 她跃过了那堵墙继续往前跑。

examples.en: He has vaulted 6.02 m in indoor competitions this year.
examples.zh: 在今年的室内比赛中他跃过了6.02米的高度。

examples.en: Last week's changes vaulted the general to the top, over the heads of several of his seniors.
examples.zh: 上周的变动使这位将军一下子超过他的几位上司而跃居最高层。

definitions.en: the speed at which an object is travelling

examples.en: Light travels at the highest achievable velocity in the universe.
examples.zh: 光的传播速度是宇宙中物体所能达到的最高速度。

examples.en: He always used high velocity lead bullets in his rifle.
examples.zh: 他的步枪一直使用高速铅弹。

definitions.en: deserving respect because of age, high position, or religious or historical importance
definitions.zh: 德高望重的;令人尊敬的;崇高神圣的

examples.en: a venerable tradition/company/family
examples.zh: 备受推崇的传统／久负盛名的公司／德高望重的家族

examples.en: She has worked at such venerable institutions as Boston's Museum of Fine Arts and the Metropolitan Art Museum in New York.
examples.zh: 她曾供职于一些久负盛名的机构，比如波士顿美术馆和纽约的大都会艺术博物馆。

definitions.en: used as a title for an archdeacon in the Church of England
definitions.zh: （对英国圣公会会吏长的尊称）尊者

definitions.en: in a way that uses exactly the same words as were originally used

examples.en: I don’t think I will read the whole thing verbatim to you.
She had an amazing memory and could recall verbatim quite complex conversations.
examples.zh: 她记忆力惊人，能够一字不差地回想起很复杂的谈话内容。

examples.en: I can remember lines from movies verbatim.
I know somebody who talks about their co-worker's private phone conversations verbatim.
