Orlov claimed that Khrushchev had lifted whole paragraphs verbatim from the Russian-language version of his book.

definitions.en: an opinion or decision made after judging the facts that are given, especially one made at the end of a trial
definitions.zh: （根据事实作出的）意见，决定;（尤指）判决，裁决

examples.en: The jury reached/returned a unanimous verdict of (not) guilty.
examples.zh: 陪审团一致判定有罪／无罪。

examples.en: Voters gave their verdict on the government's economic record last night by voting overwhelmingly for the opposition.
examples.zh: 昨晚选民们以压倒性的多数票支持反对党，由此可以看出他们对本届政府在经济上的作为的评判。

examples.en: The jury passed a verdict of guilty, with an appeal to the judge for clemency.
Protestors gathered outside the court to await the verdict.
After five days of deliberations, the jury decided on a verdict of not guilty.
The coroner recorded a verdict of death by misadventure.
Many politicians and members of the public expressed outrage at the verdict.

definitions.en: able to change easily from one activity to another or able to be used for many different purposes
definitions.zh: 多种用途的;万用的;多才多艺的

examples.en: He's a very versatile young actor who's as happy in highbrow dramas as he is in TV comedies.
examples.zh: 他是一个非常多才多艺的年轻演员，无论是高雅电影还是电视喜剧都一样得心应手。

examples.en: A leather jacket is a timeless and versatile garment that can be worn in all seasons.
examples.zh: 皮夹克是一种永不过时、适合多种场合的衣服，可以在任何季节穿。

examples.en: This jacket is very versatile - you can dress it up for the evening.
He is a very versatile actor.
We want all our staff to be versatile.
This is a very versatile recipe - you can double the portions for a party.
Teachers have to be versatile to cope with different ability levels.

definitions.en: used to say that one team or person is competing against another

examples.en: Tomorrow's game is Newcastle versus Arsenal.
examples.zh: 明天的比赛是纽卡斯尔队对阿森纳队。

examples.en: Abortion was legalized nationally in the United States following the Roe versus Wade case.
examples.zh: 在罗诉韦德案后，堕胎在美国全国变得合法化。

examples.en: private education versus state education
examples.zh: 私立教育与公共教育相比

examples.en: England versus Brazil - bring it on!
He gave the judgment in the Smith versus the Crown case.
OK, adults versus children for football!
When we were trying to get treatment for our son, we felt like it was us versus the medical establishment.

definitions.en: an official power or right to refuse to accept or allow something

examples.en: The president has the power of veto over any bill that comes across his desk.
examples.zh: 总统可以利用否决权来阻止任何议案的通过。

examples.en: In theory the British government could use its veto to block this proposal.
examples.zh: 理论上英国政府可以利用否决权来阻止这项议案通过。

examples.en: The Senate voted to override the president's veto of the proposed measures.
examples.zh: 参议院投票推翻了总统对拟议中的措施的否决。

examples.en: My parents put a veto on our watching television for more than two hours each evening.
examples.zh: 妈妈禁止我们每天晚上看电视超过两个小时。

examples.en: Opponents are unlikely to be able to muster enough votes to override the veto.
The president used his veto to override the committee's decision.
The chairwoman used her veto to block the appointment.

definitions.en: to refuse to allow something

examples.en: In 1961, President De Gaulle vetoed Britain's entry into the Common Market.
examples.zh: 1961年，戴高乐总统行使否决权，反对英国加入西欧共同市场。

examples.en: My boss vetoed my taking any more time off this year.
examples.zh: 我的老板不允许我今年再请假。

examples.en: The president has the power to veto the imposition of sanctions.
Managers vetoed proposals for a shorter working week.
My father vetoed our plans for a trip to Turkey.

definitions.en: able to work as intended or able to succeed
definitions.zh: 可以实施的；可望成功的

examples.en: In order to make the company viable, it will unfortunately be necessary to reduce staffing levels.
examples.zh: 很遗憾，为使公司继续运转下去，将不得不裁员。

examples.en: I am afraid your plan is not commercially/economically/financially/politically viable.
examples.zh: 恐怕你的计划在商业／经济／财政／政治上行不通。

examples.en: There is a continuing debate about the age at which a human foetus can be considered viable.
examples.zh: 关于人类胎儿在何时可被视为已有生命的问题仍处于争论中。

examples.en: The project is not financially viable .
We have concerns about whether the government will be able to provide viable social services for poorer families/provide poorer families with viable social services.
The government wants to encourage viable self-contained rural communities.
It was not viable to support ourselves on such as small plot of land.
We hope to provide a viable alternative to car travel.

definitions.en: energetic, exciting, and full of enthusiasm
definitions.zh: 活跃的;精力充沛的;热情洋溢的

examples.en: a vibrant young performer
examples.zh: 充满朝气的年轻演员

examples.en: a vibrant personality
examples.zh: 活泼的个性

examples.en: a vibrant city
examples.zh: 充满活力的城市

examples.en: The hope is that this area will develop into a vibrant commercial centre.
examples.zh: 希望这个地区会发展成为一个活跃的商业中心。

examples.en: He always uses vibrant colours in his paintings.
examples.zh: 他在画中经常运用鲜艳的色彩。

definitions.en: someone or something that has been hurt, damaged, or killed or has suffered, either because of the actions of someone or something else, or because of illness or chance
definitions.zh: 受害者;罹难者;牺牲者;牺牲品

examples.en: to provide financial aid to hurricane/flood, etc. victims
examples.zh: 为飓风／洪水等的受灾者提供经济援助

examples.en: victims of crime
examples.zh: 犯罪的受害者

examples.en: The children are the innocent/helpless victims of the fighting.
examples.zh: 孩子们是打架斗殴的无辜／无助的受害者。

examples.en: The new drug might help save the lives of cancer victims.
examples.zh: 这种新药也许可以用于挽救癌症病人的生命。

examples.en: We appear to have been the victims of a cruel practical joke.
examples.zh: 我们看来已经成了一个大恶作剧的受害者。

examples.en: Our local hospital has become the latest victim of the cuts in government spending.
examples.zh: 我们本地的医院成了政府削减开支的最新受害者。

examples.en: Sea turtles in this region often fall victim to the effects of pollution.
The company has fallen victim to increased competition.
examples.zh: 日益激烈的竞争让这家公司深受其害。

examples.en: The murderer had lured his victim to a deserted house.
She claimed to have been a victim of child abuse.
They're launching an appeal to raise money for famine victims.
Road accident victims make up almost a quarter of the hospital's patients.
The victim had received repeated blows to the head and body.

definitions.en: to compete with other people to achieve or get something

examples.en: Six candidates are currently vying for the Democratic presidential nomination.
examples.zh: 6名候选人目前正在争夺民主党的总统候选人提名资格。

examples.en: The two older children tend to vie with the younger one for their mother's attention.
examples.zh: 两个大一点的孩子正在和小的争宠，都想博得妈妈的注意。

examples.en: The two groups of scientists are vying to get funding for their research projects.
examples.zh: 两组科学家都在为各自的研究项目争取资金。

definitions.en: to break or act against something, especially a law, agreement, principle, or something that should be treated with respect
definitions.zh: 违反，违背，违犯（尤指法律、协议、原则等）

examples.en: They were charged with violating federal law.
examples.zh: 他们被控违犯了联邦法律。

examples.en: It seems that the troops deliberately violated the ceasefire agreement.
examples.zh: 那些军队似乎是故意违反停火协议。

examples.en: The doctor has been accused of violating professional ethics.
examples.zh: 那名医生被控违反了职业道德。

examples.en: The fishermen claimed that ships from another country had violated their territorial waters.
examples.zh: 那些渔民宣称另一个国家的船只侵犯了他们的水域。

examples.en: Questions of this kind violate my privacy and I am not willing to answer them.
examples.zh: 这种问题侵犯了我的隐私，我拒绝回答。

examples.en: She said that she had been treated so roughly by the hospital staff that she felt violated.
examples.zh: 她说医护人员待她非常粗暴，她感觉受到了侮辱。

examples.en: The courts struck down local segregation laws because they violated the federal constitution.
Such behaviour violates all the rules of a civilized society.
Invading tribes had violated their borders.
These clothes violate her sense of good taste.
His language violates any sense of decency.

definitions.en: almost

examples.en: Their twins are virtually identical.
examples.zh: 他们的双胞胎长得几乎一模一样。

examples.en: That wine stain on my shirt has virtually disappeared.
examples.zh: 我衬衣上的那块酒渍几乎已经看不见了。

examples.en: By midnight I was virtually comatose.
We can catch the vast majority of people, but hunting down every last tax dodger is virtually impossible.
Torn apart by war, its economy virtually destroyed, this country now faces a very uncertain future.
A generation ago, home computers were virtually unknown.
The hospital had virtually given her up for dead, but she eventually recovered.

definitions.en: using computer technology over the internet, and not involving people physically going somewhere

examples.en: Nowadays all these things can be experienced virtually.
examples.zh: 现在所有这些都可以通过虚拟方式体验。

examples.en: I've learned a lot, and met (virtually and in the real world) a lot of great people.
Building a relationship with new employees can be difficult when it's done virtually.

definitions.en: a good moral quality in a person, or the general quality of being morally good

examples.en: Patience is a virtue.
examples.zh: 耐心是一种美德。

examples.en: In the novel, Constanza is a paragon of virtue who would never compromise her reputation.
Her virtue would be proof against his charms.
Discretion is a virtue worth cultivating.
Tact is not one of his virtues.

definitions.en: an advantage or good thing

examples.en: Would there be any virtue in taking an earlier train?
examples.zh: 乘坐早一班的火车有什么好处吗？

examples.en: The actress has appeared in many TV ads extolling (= praising) the virtues of hair products.
examples.zh: 看见这位女演员在电视上吹捧美发产品的种种优点总是让人感觉怪怪的。

definitions.en: because of; as a result of

examples.en: She succeeded by virtue of her tenacity rather than her talent.
examples.zh: 她成功靠的是坚韧不拔，而不是天赋。

definitions.en: a part of a helmet that can be pulled down to cover the face
definitions.zh: （头盔上的）活动面罩;遮阳帽舌

examples.en: A helmet visor protects eyes from falling rain.
Knights lifted their visors to show they meant no harm.
The keys were tucked in the sun visor.
At the next red light, you can make a note on the Auto Memo Board, which attaches to your visor.
Greg's eyes were shaded by the visor of his cap.

definitions.en: necessary for the success or continued existence of something; extremely important
definitions.zh: 必不可少的；极其重要的

examples.en: A strong opposition is vital to a healthy democracy.
examples.zh: 有一个强大的反对党对于健康的民主制度来说非常重要。

examples.en: She had found out some information of vital importance.
examples.zh: 她已经查出了一些极为重要的信息。

examples.en: The kidney plays a vital role/part in the removal of waste products from the blood.
examples.zh: 肾脏对于排除血液中的废物起着至关重要的作用。

examples.en: It's absolutely vital that you do exactly as I say.
examples.zh: 完完全全按我说的去做，这至关重要。

examples.en: It is vital to get medical supplies to the area as soon as possible.
examples.zh: 要尽快把药品送往该地区，这至关重要。

examples.en: It was said that the police concealed vital evidence.
The exploration for new sources of energy is vital for the future of our planet.
Mr Clinton singled out education as a vital tool in bridging the gap between rich and poor.
These monuments are a vital part of the cultural heritage of South America.
Trust is a vital ingredient in a successful marriage.

definitions.en: energetic
definitions.zh: 生机勃勃的;充满生气的

examples.en: He had never felt so vital and full of life.
examples.zh: 他从来没有像现在一样感到如此精神焕发，充满活力。

definitions.en: likely to change suddenly and unexpectedly, especially by getting worse
definitions.zh: 不稳定的；易变的；易怒的，喜怒无常的

examples.en: Food and fuel prices are very volatile in a war situation.
examples.zh: 战争时期食品和燃料的价格极易波动。

examples.en: The situation was made more volatile by the fact that people had been drinking a lot of alcohol.
examples.zh: 人们一直在大喝烈性酒，这让情况变得更加不稳定。

examples.en: Earnings are very volatile in this business.
the danger of getting oil supplies from politically volatile parts of the world
For now, stock markets remain extremely volatile.
She was portrayed as over-sensitive and volatile.

definitions.en: A volatile liquid or solid substance will change easily into a gas.
definitions.zh: （液体或固体）易挥发的，易气化的

examples.en: The substance is highly volatile.
volatile compounds
There are different rules for ships carrying highly volatile cargo, such as oil.
Fruit release volatile compounds which attract animals and insects.

definitions.en: to be able from your knowledge or experience to say that something is true

examples.en: As a medical examiner I can vouch that his death was accidental.
examples.zh: 作为法医，我可以确定他是意外死亡。

definitions.en: If wood warps, or if water or heat warps it, it becomes damaged by bending or twisting.

examples.en: After being left in the damp garage, the wooden frame had warped.
examples.zh: 由于放在潮湿的车库里，木头框架已经变形了。

examples.en: The heat had warped the boards.
examples.zh: 热气已经让板子变形了。

definitions.en: to make a person or their behaviour strange, in an unpleasant or harmful way
definitions.zh: 使（人或人的行为）反常，使变得乖戾；扭曲

examples.en: Prison warps people. Had it warped Kelley enough that he would kill a stranger?
examples.zh: 监狱扭曲人性。它有没有使凯利变态到会杀死一个陌生人呢？

definitions.en: the threads that go along the length of a piece of cloth or a loom (= a special frame for making cloth)
to make a particular activity necessary

examples.en: Obviously what she did was wrong, but I don't think it warranted such a severe punishment.
examples.zh: 显然她做得不对，但我认为她不应该受到如此严厉的惩罚。

examples.en: It's a relatively simple task that really doesn't warrant a great deal of time being spent on it.
examples.zh: 这是一个相对来说比较简单的任务，不必在上面花费大量的时间。

definitions.en: used to say that you are certain about something

examples.en: He's to blame, I'll warrant (you).
examples.zh: 是他的错，我（跟你）保证。

definitions.en: an official document, signed by a judge or other person in authority, that gives the police permission to search someone's home, arrest a person, or take some other action
definitions.zh: （由法官或官员签署的）搜查令，逮捕状，令状

examples.en: a search warrant
examples.zh: 搜查令

examples.en: Judge La Riva had issued an arrest warrant/a warrant for his arrest.
examples.zh: 拉里瓦法官已经签署了一份逮捕状／逮捕他的令状。

definitions.en: a reason for doing something

examples.en: There's no warrant for that sort of behaviour!
examples.zh: 那种行为毫无任何根据！

definitions.en: not completely trusting or certain about something or someone

examples.en: I'm a little wary of/about giving people my address when I don't know them very well.
examples.zh: 如果不是太熟的话，我不会轻易告诉别人我的地址。

examples.en: All authors need to be wary of inadvertent copying of other people's ideas.
The legal system is full of snares for those who are not wary.
Tourists should be wary, as pickpockets are known to operate in this area.
Buyers should be wary of hidden costs when purchasing their holiday.
The teacher was wary of giving his pupils too much information at once.

definitions.en: very strange and unusual, unexpected, or not natural
definitions.zh: 怪异的；不寻常的；出乎意料的；超自然的

examples.en: He was sitting alone by a window with a weird contraption on the table in front of him.
examples.zh: 他独自坐在窗边，面前的桌子上摆着个奇怪的装置。

examples.en: Her boyfriend's a bit weird but she's nice.
examples.zh: 她的男朋友有点怪，不过她人倒很好。

examples.en: That's weird - I thought I left my keys on the table but they're not there.
examples.zh: 真奇怪——我记得把钥匙放在桌子上了，但现在却不见了。

examples.en: There is nothing to rival the weird and wonderful things that come out on the Rio streets at carnival time.
examples.zh: 没有什么可以和狂欢节期间里约热内卢大街上出现的那些奇妙而古怪的东西相媲美。

examples.en: She'd cooked up some weird scheme that was going to earn her a fortune.
Jimi Hendrix loved to fling his guitar around to get weird and wonderful sounds from the feedback.
Some people think I'm weird doing meditation, but it works for me and that's all that matters.
I heard some weird noises coming from outside.
The table was covered with all sorts of weird and wonderful food.

definitions.en: used after a negative phrase to add emphasis to the idea that is being expressed
definitions.zh: （用于否定句后表强调）无论怎样，丝毫，任何

examples.en: He has no respect for authority whatsoever.
examples.zh: 他对权威一点也不尊重。

examples.en: I can honestly say that I have no interest whatsoever in the royal family.
examples.zh: 我可以坦率地说我对皇室毫无兴趣。

examples.en: There is no evidence whatever to show that this is in fact the case.
examples.zh: 根本就没有任何证据表明这就是实际情况。

examples.en: "Did you have any idea what was happening at the time?" "None whatsoever."
examples.zh: “你知道当时发生了什么吗？”“完全不知道。”

definitions.en: during the time that, or at the same time as

examples.en: I read it while you were drying your hair.
examples.zh: 在你吹干头发的时候我读了它。

examples.en: While I was in Italy I went to see Alessandro.
examples.zh: 在意大利的时候，我去看望了亚历山德罗。

examples.en: I thought I heard him come in while we were having dinner.
examples.zh: 我想在我们吃饭的时候我听见他进来了。

examples.en: "I'm going to the post office." "While you're there can you get me some stamps?"
examples.zh: “我要去邮局。”“你去的时候帮我买些邮票好吗？”

examples.en: I thought I'd take advantage of the sports facilities while I'm here.
I found these photos while I was cleaning out my cupboards.
I'm acting as deputy while the boss is away.
I didn't drink at all while I was pregnant.
I gained a lot of weight while I was on holiday.

definitions.en: despite the fact that; although

examples.en: While I accept that he's not perfect in many respects, I do actually like the man.
examples.zh: 虽然我承认他在很多方面不是那么完美，但我实际上还是非常喜欢这个男人的。

examples.en: While I fully understand your point of view, I also have some sympathy with Michael's.
examples.zh: 尽管我完全能理解你的观点，但我在一定程度上也赞同迈克尔的观点。

examples.en: While your work has improved greatly, there are still some areas that concern me.
While I'd like to accept your offer, I'm unable to at the present time.
While I admire your courage, it's sometimes better not to get involved.
While I'd be willing to help you on this occasion, I may not always be able to in future.
While I'm reluctant to make a fuss, I feel I really must complain.

definitions.en: compared with the fact that; but

examples.en: He gets £50,000 a year while I get a meagre £20,000!
examples.zh: 他每年挣5万英镑，而我只有可怜的2万！

examples.en: Tom is very extrovert and confident while Katy's shy and quiet.
examples.zh: 汤姆性格外向，非常自信，而凯蒂却羞涩文静。

examples.en: I do all the housework while he just does the dishes now and again.
examples.zh: 家务活我样样都得干，而他却只是偶尔刷刷碗而已。

examples.en: Walnut is a hard wood, while pine is soft.
He likes football, while I prefer rugby.
I get 25 days holiday a year, while she gets over 50.
Simon will have steak, while I'll have the fish.
He wanted to go the party, while I wanted to stay in.

definitions.en: a length of time

examples.en: I only stayed for a short while.
examples.zh: 我就呆了一小会儿。

examples.en: You were there quite a while (= a long time), weren't you?
examples.zh: 你在那儿呆了很长时间，是不是？

examples.en: "When did that happen?" "Oh, it was a while ago (= a long time ago).
examples.zh: “那是什么时候发生的？”“噢，是很久之前了。”

examples.en: I haven't seen him for a while (= for a long time).
examples.zh: 我已经很久没见到他了。

examples.en: I'll be fine in a while (= soon).
examples.zh: 我很快就会好的。

examples.en: I lived on my own for a while but I missed the companionship of others.
I'm going to concentrate on my writing for a while.
We had tea, and afterwards we sat in the garden for a while.
We made a deliberate decision to live apart for a while.
My suitcase began to feel really heavy after a while.

definitions.en: a tall, spinning column of air that moves across the surface of the land or sea
A whirlwind event happens very fast, and often unexpectedly.

examples.en: They married three months after they met - it was a real whirlwind romance.
examples.zh: 他们认识3个月后就结婚了——真是旋风式的恋爱。

examples.en: a whirlwind tour/visit
examples.zh: 旋风式的旅行／拜访

definitions.en: a weather condition in which snow and clouds change the way light is reflected so that only very dark objects can be seen
definitions.zh: 乳白天空（一种大气光象）

definitions.en: existing or happening in many places and/or among many people

examples.en: There are reports of widespread flooding in northern France.
examples.zh: 有报道说法国北部到处洪水泛滥。

examples.en: Malnutrition in the region is widespread - affecting up to 78 percent of children under five years old.
examples.zh: 这个地区营养不良的现象十分普遍——5岁以下的儿童中78%营养不良。

examples.en: The campaign has received widespread support.
examples.zh: 这场运动得到了广泛的支持。

examples.en: There's widespread agreement that the law should be changed.
The next 36 hours will be even colder with snow showers becoming more widespread.
His economic policies have won widespread praise for reducing government debt.
The review exposed widespread corruption in the police force.
The designs for the new mosque have attracted widespread criticism.

definitions.en: to be happy to do something if it is needed

examples.en: If you're willing to fly at night, you can get a much cheaper ticket.
examples.zh: 如果你愿意夜里乘飞机，就能买到更便宜的机票。

examples.en: You said you needed a volunteer - well, I'm willing.
examples.zh: 你说过你需要一名志愿者——我愿意。

examples.en: Apparently John and Gabriel are willing for us to use their barbecue.
examples.zh: 很显然，约翰和加布里埃尔愿意让我们使用他们的烧烤架。

examples.en: a willing helper
examples.zh: 积极肯干的帮手

examples.en: He is not willing to concede any of his power.
You have to be willing to see other people's points of view.
They need an assistant who is willing to stay for six months.
The success of the talks turns on whether both sides are willing to make some concessions.
If you don't want the job, there's no end of people willing to take your place.

definitions.en: to slide something, especially a piece of cloth, over the surface of something else, in order to remove dirt, food, or liquid

examples.en: Do you have some paper towels that I can wipe the floor with?
examples.zh: 你有没有我可以用来擦地板的纸巾？

examples.en: I'll just get a sponge and wipe the crumbs off the table.
examples.zh: 我这就去找块海绵把桌子上的碎屑擦掉。

examples.en: Don't wipe your nose on your sleeve!
examples.zh: 别用袖子擦鼻子！

examples.en: Someone has wiped their dirty hands on my nice clean towel!
examples.zh: 有人在我干净漂亮的毛巾上擦过脏手！

examples.en: Don't wipe your grubby hands on my clean towel!
Please wipe your feet before you come into the house.
She wiped her floury hands on a cloth.
She wiped her eye make-up off with a cotton wool pad.
The nurse wiped my fevered brow.

definitions.en: a piece of soft, wet cloth or paper that you use for wiping

examples.en: baby wipes
examples.zh: 婴儿纸巾

examples.en: I'd better give the floor a quick wipe before someone slips on it.
examples.zh: 我最好赶快擦一下地板，省得有人滑倒。

definitions.en: shy and quiet and preferring to be alone rather than with other people
definitions.zh: 怕羞的;沉默寡言的;孤僻的

examples.en: Following her son's death, she became quiet and withdrawn and rarely went out.
examples.zh: 她儿子死后，她变得沉默寡言，并且很少外出。

definitions.en: a person who sees an event happening, especially a crime or an accident

examples.en: Police are appealing for witnesses to the accident to come forward.
examples.zh: 警方呼吁那场事故的目击者站出来。

examples.en: According to (eye) witnesses, the robbery was carried out by two teenage boys.
examples.zh: 据目击者说，抢劫案是两个十几岁的少年干的。

examples.en: She was witness to the tragic event.
examples.zh: 她目睹了这一悲剧。

examples.en: He signed the treaty in the presence of two witnesses.
examples.zh: 他在两位见证人面前签署了条约。

examples.en: They were married a year after they first met, with two friends acting as witnesses.
examples.zh: 初次见面一年之后，他们在两位朋友的见证之下结婚了。

examples.en: If you haven't got a witness, you haven't got a leg to stand on.
One by one the witnesses narrated the sequence of events which led up to the disaster.
The document was signed in the presence of two witnesses.
New witnesses have cast doubt on some of the evidence that sent the 19 year old to the gallows.
She bore witness to his patience and diligence.

definitions.en: a person in a law court who says what they know about a legal case or a particular person

examples.en: Ten witnesses are expected to testify at the trial today.
examples.zh: 在今天的审判中，将有两名证人出庭作证。

examples.en: The key witness for the prosecution was offered police protection after she received death threats.
examples.zh: 原告的关键证人在受到死亡恐吓后得到了警方的保护。

examples.en: A psychiatrist was called as an expert witness for the defence.
examples.zh: 一位精神病医生作为被告的专家证人被传作证。

definitions.en: to see something happen, especially an accident or crime
definitions.zh: 目击，看到（尤指事故或犯罪）

examples.en: Did anyone witness the attack?
examples.zh: 有人看到那次袭击了吗？

examples.en: They were staying in the capital at the time of the riots and witnessed several street battles.
examples.zh: 暴乱期间他们在首都，目击了好几起巷战。

examples.en: He arrived home just in time to witness his brother being taken away by the police.
examples.zh: 他到家时正好看到他兄弟被警察带走。

examples.en: Her will was witnessed by two colleagues.
examples.zh: 她的遗嘱由两名同事签名作证。

examples.en: I've never witnessed such extremes of wealth and poverty.
Recent years have witnessed a growth in religious fundamentalism.
What we are witnessing is the country's slow slide into anarchy.
His face registered extreme disapproval of what he had witnessed.
It was a terrible thing to witness.

definitions.en: When a place or period witnesses a particular event, the event happens in that place or during that period.

examples.en: This university has witnessed quite a few changes over the years.
examples.zh: 这所大学多年来经历过很多变革。

examples.en: Those years witnessed momentous changes throughout Europe.
examples.zh: 那些年间整个欧洲都发生了重大的变化。

definitions.en: to show or give proof of something

examples.en: This year's charity ball was the most successful one ever, as witnessed by the number of tickets sold.
examples.zh: 今年的慈善舞会是最为成功的一次，这可以从售出门票的数量上看出来。

examples.en: The programme aroused strong feelings - witness the number of letters received.
examples.zh: 这个节目激起了强烈的反响——来信的数量便是明证。

definitions.en: a man who is believed to have magical powers and who uses them to harm or help other people

examples.en: a computer wizard
examples.zh: 计算机高手

examples.en: Your mother's a wizard at Scrabble.
examples.zh: 你妈妈是一个拼字游戏的能手。

definitions.en: to (cause something to) shake or move from side to side in a way that shows poor balance

examples.en: That bookcase wobbles whenever you put anything on it.
examples.zh: 那个书架你一放东西就摇摇晃晃的。

examples.en: Don't wobble the table, please, Dan.
examples.zh: 丹，别晃桌子好吗？

examples.en: The company's shares wobbled with the news of a foreign takeover bid.
examples.zh: 这家公司的股票由于外国公司收购的消息而波动。

definitions.en: to be uncertain what to do or to change repeatedly between two opinions
definitions.zh: 拿不定主意；动摇；（在两种观点间）变来变去

examples.en: The government can't afford to wobble on this issue.
examples.zh: 如果政府在这个问题上不作出果断决策就会付出巨大代价。

definitions.en: a movement from side to side that shows poor balance

examples.en: I gave the poles a slight wobble and the whole tent collapsed.
examples.zh: 我轻轻晃了晃几根柱子，整个帐篷就倒了。

examples.en: The closure of the company's German subsidiary caused a sharp wobble in its profits.
examples.zh: 这家公司德国分部的关闭导致其利润急剧下降。

definitions.en: a feeling of not being certain about something
definitions.zh: 拿不定主意；动摇；（在两种观点间）变来变去

examples.en: She's having a bit of a wobble about the move to New York.
examples.zh: 她对是否搬到纽约还有点犹豫不定。

definitions.en: containing too many words

examples.en: As usual she gave a reply which was wordy and didn't answer the question.
examples.zh: 像往常一样，她的回答十分啰嗦，而且答非所问。

definitions.en: a way of dealing with a problem or making something work despite the problem, without completely solving it

examples.en: The software still has a few glitches but we've come up with a workaround.
examples.zh: 这个软件还是有一些小故障，但是我们已经想出了一个变通办法。

definitions.en: the group of people who work in a company, industry, country, etc.

examples.en: The majority of factories in the region have a workforce of 50 to 100 (people).
examples.zh: 该地大多数工厂都拥有50至100名工人。

examples.en: Much of the workforce in the banking sector is/are affected by the new legislation.
examples.zh: 银行部门的大部分员工都受到了新法律的影响。

definitions.en: a small line in the skin caused by old age

examples.en: fine wrinkles around the eyes
examples.zh: 眼晴周围的细纹

examples.en: anti-wrinkle creams
examples.zh: 抗皱霜

definitions.en: a problem, usually a small one

examples.en: There are still a few wrinkles to iron out (= solve) before the agreement can be signed.
examples.zh: 在协议签订之前还有一些小问题需要解决。

definitions.en: If skin or material wrinkles, or if something wrinkles it, it gets small lines or folds in it.

examples.en: Sunbathing can prematurely age and wrinkle the skin.
examples.zh: 日光浴会使皮肤提早老化、产生皱纹。

examples.en: She wrinkled up her nose at the strange smell coming from the kitchen.
examples.zh: 闻到厨房里飘来的怪味，她皱起了鼻子。

examples.en: Amy wrinkled her nose in disapproval.
examples.zh: 艾米皱起鼻子以示反对。

definitions.en: to pull something forcefully with a quick movement

examples.en: He tripped over the cord and yanked the plug out.
examples.zh: 他绊到了电线，一下子把插头拽了出来。

examples.en: She yanked open the cupboard door and everything fell out.
examples.zh: 她猛地打开橱柜，所有东西都掉了出来。

examples.en: I was yanked out of school and forced to go to work.
examples.zh: 我被赶出了学校，只好去找工作。

definitions.en: a person from the US

examples.en: The place was full of Yanks.
examples.zh: 这地方全是美国佬。

definitions.en: used to show that you are very pleased about something

examples.en: "We won." "Yay!"
examples.zh: “我们赢了。”“好耶！”

definitions.en: so; to a particular degree

examples.en: It was about yay long.
examples.zh: 那个大约这么长。

definitions.en: to supply or produce something positive such as a profit, an amount of food or information

examples.en: an attempt to yield increased profits
examples.zh: 提高利润的努力

examples.en: The investigation yielded some unexpected results.
examples.zh: 调查得出了一些出乎意料的结果。

examples.en: Favourable weather yielded a good crop.
examples.zh: 好天气带来了好收成。

examples.en: The process yields oil for industrial use.
Burning waste yields energy that can be used for electric power or heating.
The excavation yielded some superb artifacts.
Early radio equipment yielded poor sound quality.
The experiments yielded some surprising results.

definitions.en: to give up the control of or responsibility for something, often because you have been forced to

examples.en: They were forced to yield (up) their land to the occupying forces.
examples.zh: 他们被迫将领土割让给占领军。

examples.en: Despite renewed pressure to give up the occupied territory, they will not yield.
examples.zh: 尽管有新的压力要求放弃被占领的土地，他们就是不愿屈服。

examples.en: They refused to yield power.
They had to yield control of the river area.

definitions.en: to bend or break under pressure

examples.en: His legs began to yield under the sheer weight of his body.
examples.zh: 他的双腿连自己的体重也承受不住，开始弯曲。

definitions.en: to stop in order to allow other vehicles to go past, especially before you drive onto a bigger road
definitions.zh: 停车让道（以让其他车辆通过）

examples.en: If you're going downhill, you need to yield to bikers going uphill.
examples.zh: 下山的时候，你需要停车让上山的骑自行车的人先行。

definitions.en: an amount of something positive, such as food or profit, that is produced or supplied

examples.en: Crop yields have risen steadily.
examples.zh: 农作物产量稳步增长。

examples.en: Yields on gas and electricity shares are consistently high.
examples.zh: 天然气与电力股票的收益一直很高

examples.en: This year's yield was very large.
Scientists have greatly increased the yield of crops such as wheat, rice and corn.
Farmers add fertilizer to increase the yield.
The fertile soil enabled farmers to produce abundant yields.
Plant breeding has increased yields and has improved the nutritional value of several crops.

definitions.en: of a long time ago

examples.en: This was once a Roman road in days of yore.
examples.zh: 很久以前这里曾是古罗马的一条道路。

definitions.en: a small magazine that is produced cheaply by one person or a small group of people, and is about a subject they are interested in
